metadata.title: Rebranding and communication for Selfish, reinforcing its positioning

content.description:
Rebranding and communication for Selfish, reinforcing its positioning

Selfish is one of the brands within the Starfoods group, with its own positioning in the restaurant industry, focused on fish, freshness, and flavor.

At LV, we recently supported the brand through a refresh process aimed at clearly reinforcing the freshness of its fish. The work included updating the visual identity, simplifying the brand language, and strengthening its positioning, making the brand more contemporary, direct, and aligned with its audience.

We maintained the irreverence that has always defined Selfish, but introduced a cleaner, more confident, and consistent image, elevating the perception of quality and authenticity.

Today, Selfish operates in two distinct formats:– the shopping mall version, faster and more functional– and the Selfish with a Twist concept, located at Freeport Alcochete, offering a more experiential and differentiated approach.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A comprehensive brand refresh for Selfish, a fish-focused restaurant, aimed at reinforcing its core positioning of freshness, flavor, and quality through an updated visual identity and simplified brand language, while retaining its irreverent character.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Diners who value fresh, quality fish and authentic flavors, seeking contemporary and direct dining experiences, likely those in urban settings or frequenting shopping malls, who appreciate a brand with an irreverent yet confident image.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Updating visual identity (logo, branding elements), simplifying brand language, and implementing a 360° communication strategy across various touchpoints to present a cleaner, more confident, and consistent brand image.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reinforce Selfish's positioning, clearly communicate the freshness and quality of its fish, elevate brand perception, and align the brand more effectively with its target audience.

content.description: ‘Kaprao’ the dish the whole nation takes seriously.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: KFC Thailand playfully challenges or incorporates the universally beloved and 'serious' Thai dish, Kaprao, likely through a new product offering that combines KFC's chicken with the iconic Kaprao flavor profile.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Thai street food enthusiasts, particularly those aged 18-45 who appreciate Kaprao and are open to modern, fast-food interpretations of traditional dishes.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across major digital platforms like YouTube, Facebook, and TikTok, complemented by traditional TV advertising to maximize reach.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate significant buzz and drive trial for a new Kaprao-inspired menu item, thereby increasing sales and reinforcing KFC's relevance within the local Thai culinary landscape.

content.description: Comic Relief kicked off its Red Nose Day 2026 campaign, Take Yourself Funny For Money, with a one-night-only projection of celebrities’ cringeworthy ‘Best Worst Photos’ onto Battersea Power Station. Developed and launched by Ogilvy UK, the campaign is inviting people to embrace Britain’s self-deprecating sense of humour by sharing their own awkward snaps with the hashtags #BestWorstPhoto and #rnd26 on social media.Claudia Winkleman’s smudged eyeliner look at the 2013 GQ awards and Miranda Hart’s infamous school photo lit up the iconic Battersea Power Station, alongside other national treasures Davina McCall, Big Zuu and La Voix, which Ogilvy UK collaborated with immersive digital experience specialist Pixel Artworks to execute. Branded content was also displayed in Manchester’s flagship entertainment complex Printworks.The PR stunt is now being amplified through hashtag-driven user-generated content and PR across national and broadcast media, drawing mass awareness to the beginning of Comic Relief's legendary fundraising campaign which will culminate on Red Nose Day, Friday 20th March 2026.Ogilvy UK was appointed Comic Relief’s creative and strategic partner for Red Nose Day 2026 following a competitive pitch last spring. The agency was briefed to create an attention-grabbing ‘big idea’ that would serve as a hero moment for the launch of the fundraising campaign.Samir Patel, CEO, Comic Relief:“Nothing brings people together quite like laughter, especially when we’re laughing at ourselves. We’re kicking off the campaign this year with a fun, silly and simple way to get involved, inviting the public to post their own #BestWorstPhoto, to help us build the energy and excitement as we ramp up to Red Nose Day on Friday 20 March.”Jules Chalkley, Chief ECD, Ogilvy UK, says:“One night, one landmark and a few laughs at the willing expense of celebs. By making the campaign purpose-built for capturing, sharing and participating across social media, last night’s spectacle will once again cement Comic Relief firmly in the nation’s hearts and drive awareness of a fundraising campaign that helps to change and saves lives. Charlie Coney, Creative Strategy Officer, adds: “We’ve all got a photo we’re embarrassed about - AND laugh about. After all, self-deprecating humour is what makes Brits, British. Rather than a traditional launch for Comic Relief’s fundraising campaign, we wanted an irresistible bit of cultural theatre. What better way to take yourself funny for money?"

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Comic Relief launched its Red Nose Day 2026 campaign by projecting celebrities' 'Best Worst Photos' onto Battersea Power Station, inviting the public to share their own awkward snaps on social media to embrace British self-deprecating humor.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: UK general public, particularly those who appreciate self-deprecating humor, are engaged with celebrity culture, and active on social media.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: OOH Outdoor projection onto Battersea Power Station, social media call to action using #BestWorstPhoto and #rnd26, collaboration with immersive digital experience specialist Pixel Artworks, and branded content display in Manchester.
ai_enrichment.objective: Drive public engagement and participation for Red Nose Day 2026 by leveraging a humorous, self-deprecating social media challenge.

content.description:
In an era of high-decibel brand activations, Audi UK and BBH London are betting on the power of the unsaid for the 2026 BRIT Awards.

Music and automotive culture share a natural moment: the start of a journey. Great bands, artists and journeys often start in the same place - the garage. A humble space where musicians find their rhythm, tune their instruments, and take the first steps toward something extraordinary. It’s where rhythm is found, and where engines are tuned.

By highlighting this shared origin, BBH connects the BRITs and Audi UK in a way that feels authentic, iconic, and culturally fluent. Because every great journey starts somewhere.

Tony Moore, Head of Marketing at Audi UK said: “This campaign celebrates the shared philosophy of Audi and the BRITs: that every great performance begins with dedication, practice, and attention to detail. By showing rather than telling, it captures our partnership in a way that feels natural, culturally fluent, and unmistakably Audi.”

Set at night, the scene is a quiet garage bathed in moody, cinematic lighting. The night-time atmosphere adds intimacy and focus, letting the audience pause, lean in, and absorb the parallels between the journeys of music and automotive. There’s no headline, no explanation, no dramatic call-to-action. Just the Audi and BRITs logos. It’s confident, understated, and unmistakably Audi, proving that sometimes the most impactful storytelling is the one the audience completes themselves.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Audi UK and BBH London connect the shared origin of musical journeys and automotive journeys – the garage – to highlight that every great performance and journey starts somewhere.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals interested in premium automotive brands and music culture, particularly those who appreciate authentic narratives of origin and ambition.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: OOH Outdoor advertising, leveraging subtle messaging to connect Audi and the BRIT Awards.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand perception and strengthen the authentic and culturally fluent association between Audi UK and the BRIT Awards.

content.description:
For many, eating and scrolling on their phone are activities that go hand in hand. In fact, the survey unveiled that almost all Americans (96%) have used their phone while eating, and 66% of Americans use their phones often while eating at least one of their daily meals, according to a new study by Angus Reid commissioned by bibigo™ .

That’s why bibigo created ScrollSticks - chopsticks featuring a custom dual-ended design, with one side for enjoying your meal, and the other has touchscreen tips for scrolling.The re-imagined chopsticks are born from foodie behaviour and developed with multitasking consumers in mind to allow audiences to snack and scroll — without the mess.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Bibigo created "ScrollSticks," chopsticks with a dual-ended design featuring a traditional eating end and a touchscreen-compatible end. This innovation allows consumers to comfortably snack and scroll on their phones without making a mess, catering to the widespread habit of using phones while eating.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Multitasking consumers who frequently eat Asian cuisine or Bibigo products and commonly use their smartphones while eating, seeking convenience and mess-free dining.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Product innovation (ScrollSticks) launched as a tangible solution, supported by commissioned market research revealing widespread eating and scrolling habits, likely disseminated through PR, social media, and digital content showcasing the product's utility.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate brand awareness and engagement by demonstrating Bibigo's understanding of modern consumer behavior and offering an innovative, relatable solution to a common dining dilemma.

content.description:
This Valentine’s Day, Zales is blending nostalgia with jewelry in a playful new campaign that puts the focus on self-love, Forever Sweethearts by Zales. Developed in partnership with its agency of record, Anomaly, the brand has partnered with iconic candy Sweethearts® to create a limited-edition collection that transforms the classic candies into wearable keepsakes: a 10k yellow gold heart charm, featuring lab-grown diamonds, engraved with one of three classic Sweethearts® messages: OOO LA LA, AMORE, or XOXO.

The charms were available for purchase on Zales.com - and sold out within 27hrs of launching. The 10k Yellow Gold heart-shaped charms were boxed in a Sweethearts®-inspired package, echoing the familiar candy packaging, and featured a surprise message inside - true to the element of surprise that makes the candies so beloved.

The blind-box format was encouraging to self-gifters, inviting consumers to treat themselves to a “sweetheart” they can keep forever. The campaign leveraged the nostalgia of Sweethearts® candies and the emotional power of jewelry to create a playful, memorable experience. The campaign expands on Zales’ ‘Own It’ platform, empowering consumers to celebrate themselves daily. The work is live now across owned channels and paid social with influencer support

 

Zales + Vera Wang Love Presents Furever Love

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Zales collaborated with Sweethearts® candy to create a limited-edition collection of 10k yellow gold, lab-grown diamond heart charms, engraved with classic Sweethearts® messages, blending nostalgia with self-love for Valentine's Day.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adults celebrating Valentine's Day, particularly those interested in nostalgic brand collaborations, unique limited-edition jewelry, and messaging focused on self-love.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Limited-edition product launch exclusively available for purchase on Zales.com, featuring custom 10k yellow gold heart charms with lab-grown diamonds and Sweethearts® engravings, presented in Sweethearts®-inspired packaging.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive sales and generate significant consumer demand for a unique, nostalgic, and self-love themed jewelry collection during the Valentine's Day period.

content.description:
Since 1837, Tiffany & Co. has celebrated the world’s greatest love stories through cinematic storytelling, meaningful design and exceptional craft. In its 2026 Valentine’s Day campaign, the House offers a poignant narrative around joy, human connection and the strength love affords us. The film is directed by Justyna Obasi, an Emmy ® -nominated commercial and music video director represented by Love Song, and stars actress Adria Arjona. Tiffany’s exceptional craft becomes a lens for storytelling, a narrative where love lives in the small details that make it real. Love takes strength, and HardWear from Tiffany is a beautiful expression of this spirit.

Honoring the strength of that bond with a gift that embodies the myriad qualities of love, several HardWear pieces are at the center of Tiffany & Co.’s 2026 Valentine’s Day campaign. Equally intimate and genuinely romantic, the film grounds itself in a couple who engage in an irreverent bit in which they meet again as married strangers, with the man lovingly describing his wife, played by Arjona, as she sits across from him. Obasi directs this film with delicate choreography to accentuate the actors’ natural instincts, making the on-screen relationship feel palpably real.

Radiating elegant simplicity, the story elevates the beautiful craft behind Tiffany & Co.’s iconic HardWear collection. A cinematic experience made up of organic moments, genuine chemistry and striking visuals, the House’s 2026 Valentine’s Day campaign is a love letter to the real, complex and enduring commitment of truly loving someone.

“A good love story isn’t one note; it’s multifaceted and layered,” said director Justyna Obasi. “There’s humor, patience and vulnerability, and ultimately, inner strength, the facet of love that HardWear represents. We wanted to show how this strength translates within a relationship.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses cinematic storytelling to portray love as a powerful force, highlighting joy, human connection, and the strength it affords. Tiffany's exceptional craft, especially the HardWear collection, serves as a metaphor for the strength found in love and its intricate, real-life details.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Affluent individuals and couples aged 25-55, particularly those seeking meaningful, high-end jewelry gifts to celebrate love and enduring connections for Valentine's Day.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Development and release of a cinematic campaign film, directed by Emmy®-nominated Justyna Obasi and starring actress Adria Arjona, likely distributed across premium digital platforms and broadcast television.
ai_enrichment.objective: To strengthen Tiffany & Co.'s brand association with celebrating profound love stories, enhance emotional connection with the brand, and drive desirability for the HardWear collection as an embodiment of love's strength.

content.description:
Stimorol launches new “Chew Bold” brand platform with film starring a boxing sloth.

Perfetti Van Melle chewing gum brand, Stimorol, is targeting Gen-Zers with a new platform, “Chew Bold.” Created by AMV BBDO, the platform launches with a film of a boxing sloth, directed by Emmy-winning and Grammy-nominated director Jake Scott at RSA.

Inspired by Gen Z’s belief that you don’t need to blend in to belong, “Chew Bold” encourages audiences to be confident, carefree and true to yourself, no matter who you’re with or where you are.

The “Chew Bold” platform is embodied by the sloth who stars in the launch film. Not fitting the stereotype of a fitness fiend, he’s working the speedbag like a pro. Having finished his workout, he cooly nods at curious gymgoers and nonchalantly saunters to the door. As our sloth leaves the gym it’s revealed he is actually human 20-something who Chews Bold and doesn’t need to blend in to belong.

“Chew Bold” marks a key milestone in Perfetti Van Melle’s ongoing strategic investment in growing and modernising its gum portfolio and is the first major new brand campaign and platform for Stimorol since Perfetti Van Melle took ownership of the brand in late 2023 as part of its acquisition of Mondelēz International’s gum business in the US, Canada and Europe.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Stimorol's new 'Chew Bold' platform encourages Gen Z to be confident and true to themselves, launching with a film featuring a boxing sloth that defies stereotypes.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Gen-Zers who value authenticity, self-expression, and believe they don't need to blend in to belong.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A launch film starring a boxing sloth, directed by Emmy-winning and Grammy-nominated director Jake Scott.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand awareness and establish the 'Chew Bold' brand platform and its message of authenticity among Gen-Zers.

content.description:
This Chinese New Year, Marrybrown celebrates the quiet beauty of traditions passed down through generations. Through the delicate art of paper cutting, every snip carries love, memories, and blessings.

True prosperity is the legacy we pass on.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Marrybrown celebrates Chinese New Year by portraying the quiet beauty of traditions and intergenerational love, symbolized through the intricate art of paper cutting.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Families and individuals celebrating Chinese New Year who cherish cultural traditions, intergenerational connections, and the values of love and blessings.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film, showcasing the delicate art of paper cutting, distributed across digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media) and potentially television during the Chinese New Year period.
ai_enrichment.objective: To foster brand affinity and emotional connection with consumers by aligning Marrybrown with the warmth and significance of Chinese New Year traditions and family heritage.

content.description:
Music has always been on the table. We cook with playlists and dine with songs. But if music is always present, why haven´t we ever tasted it? Create the world’s first restaurant where music is not just heard it’s tasted. With Spotify, we’ll search for synesthetic chefs who can transform entire albums into menus. This will give fans a unique experience, and for those who think a certain genre "isn’t their thing," the chance to try it with their palate.

This  student campaign titled 'The Tracktaste' was published in Colombia in November, 2025. It was created for the brands: Musica and Spotify, by ad school: Bendita Carpeta. This Design, Digital, and Experiential media campaign is related to the Food and Music industries and contains 2 media assets. 

Advertising Agency (School): Bendita CarpetaArt Director: Fredy BeltránArt Director: Anderson MuñozCopywriter: Juan SepulvedaCopywriter: Juliana OsorioCopywriter: Felipe BerrioCopywriter: Sergio TorresVIdeo Producer: Neil Muñoz

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The world's first restaurant, 'The Tracktaste,' where music is tasted through menus created by synesthetic chefs who transform entire albums into dishes, in partnership with Spotify.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Music enthusiasts, adventurous foodies, and individuals open to unique sensory experiences, including those who may be hesitant about certain music genres, likely young adults to middle-aged (18-45).
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Establishment of an experiential restaurant ('The Tracktaste') where synesthetic chefs convert music albums into tasting menus; collaboration with Spotify to identify and showcase these chefs; digital promotion of the restaurant concept and experience; design elements for the restaurant environment.
ai_enrichment.objective: To create a unique, multi-sensory brand experience that fosters engagement, challenges perceptions of music genres, and showcases innovation for both Musica and Spotify.

metadata.title: Smell Like Your Ex(foliating Body Wash)

content.description:
Suave has launched Smell Like Your Ex(foliating Body Wash), a new social-first campaign created in partnership with global social-first creative agency Born Social. The push supports the launch of Suave’s new exfoliating body wash collection, marking the 89-year-old brand’s inaugural social-first campaign.

The campaign signals a clear evolution in Suave’s marketing playbook to social- and culture-first storytelling to power the launch of its newest Exfoliating Body Wash. The work spotlights the brand’s commitment to quality and accessibility, bringing Suave’s There’s More To It positioning to life within this fast-growing product category.

Built on a cultural insight gleaned from social listening, the campaign plays into the shared experience of exes and the lingering clothing and scent that instantly brings old relationships back to life. The central idea encourages letting go of your ex - starting with their hoodie - and replacing it with a scent that’s actually worth keeping. It sits at the intersection of emotional baggage, Gen Z’s interests in thrift culture, and the exfoliating, scent-led benefit of the product. The thrift concept also nods to Suave’s long-standing focus on everyday affordability.

“This campaign marks an important moment for Suave as we launch our new exfoliating body wash collection and take a modern approach to connecting with Gen Z by hacking into their feeds through entertainment,” said Tina Ghory, Senior Director, Consumer Engagement at Suave. “Smell Like Your Ex(foliating Body Wash) reflects how we’re evolving as a brand and showing up differently, speaking the language of this audience and bringing our There’s More To It positioning to life in a way that feels relevant, accessible and native to social.”

The campaign launches with a hero social film and three vignettes set inside a local thrift store called The Ex-Change. Shot like a throwback, homemade thrift-store commercial, the film follows a deadpan store representative as she wanders the aisles addressing the camera. Customers line up clutching items left behind by their exes, from stained overalls to oversized novelty items, as tongue-in-cheek sections categorize different “ex” archetypes. The film culminates with a customer trading in her ex’s hoodies and receiving a fresh hoodie and Suave Exfoliating Body Wash, ending on a knowingly awkward product reveal that positions the body wash as an empowered upgrade to smelling like your ex.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A social-first campaign that humorously leverages the relatable shared experience of lingering scents and memories of ex-partners to promote Suave's new exfoliating body wash collection.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Socially active young adults and millennials who engage with culturally relevant, humorous content and can relate to experiences about past relationships.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Digital and social media platforms using social-first content and cultural storytelling, developed in partnership with a social-first creative agency.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive awareness and engagement for Suave's new exfoliating body wash collection, while evolving the brand's marketing strategy towards social- and culture-first storytelling.

content.description:
In Colombia, every customer has a unique and peculiar way of relating to money.Some still preserve ancestral rituals and pay in cash. Others have taken a first evolutionary leap and migrated to the world of transfers… although their money still takes hours to arrive. Further along, we find those who move their money from app to app, without much skill. And finally, at the top of this evolutionary chain, a new species appears: the BBVA customer.

A customer who has already discovered how to receive their money instantly and send it for free to any person, bank, or digital wallet through Instant Transfers from the BBVA App.

 

• Agencia • DDB Colombia• CEO • Xavier Serrano• CCO & President • John Raúl Forero• VP Creative • Juan Carlos Chaves• VP Planning • Juan David Franco• Planners •  Camilo Barragán• Head of Art • Javier Soler• Creative Director • Antonio López• Creative Director Associate • Nicolás Franco• Brand Lead Account • Tatiana Castellanos• Senior Account Executive • Maria Camila Guarnizo• Art Directors • Sara Nieto /  Juan Daniel Bustos• Copywriters • José Guillermo Rojas / Pedro Gonzáles Villegas• Motion Designer • Edward Fabian Rojas Villamizar• Production • Primo / Dragonfly• Director • Gina Medina• Photography • Lucila Blumencweig• Client • Diana Padilla / Juana Vergara

Una tarjeta para hoy, un banco para lo que vendrá.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign humorously depicts the evolution of money transfer methods in Colombia, positioning BBVA's instant, free transfers as the ultimate, most advanced solution for modern financial transactions.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Colombian individuals currently using traditional or less efficient money transfer methods (e.g., cash, slow bank transfers) who are seeking faster, more convenient, and free digital transaction solutions.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across digital platforms like YouTube and social media, potentially supplemented by television broadcasts, to showcase the BBVA App's Instant Transfers feature.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase awareness and drive the adoption of BBVA's Instant Transfers feature within the BBVA App by demonstrating its superiority in speed and cost over existing methods.

content.description:
In a world that keeps moving faster and faster, Freddy’s invites you to slow down. There’s something special about the way a juicy cooked-to-order steakburger, crispy fries, or freshly churned frozen custard can make time itself feel like it’s standing still. 3Headed Monster’s new campaign for Freddy’s captures that feeling, literally, creating a world where time slows as guests savor every bite, every laugh, and every second together. Because at Freddy’s, the good stuff isn’t just the food… it’s the moments you take to enjoy it.

 

Advertising Agency: 3Headed Monster, Texas, USAPost-Production Company: Republic, Texas, USAProduction Company: Humming Bird Content, California, USAChief Creative Officer: Shon RathboneCreative Director: Pete VoehringerCreative Director: Kevin WalshAccount Director: Stephanie HillasHead of Production: Matt FarisProducer: Loreal JohnsonDirector: Bobby WhiteDirector: Brig WhiteManaging Director: Olivia BerlinExecutive Producer/ Director of Sales: Becky JungmannExecutive Producer: Megan PfaffenrothProducer: Jay BreauxEditor: Dean Pelton / RepublicAssistant Editor: Austin CrossExecutive Producer: Carrie CallowayFlame/ FX Supervisor: Bryan BayleyPost Producer: Michael WagnerMusic: Jon Slott / Slottman Music

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign visually depicts time slowing down as guests enjoy Freddy's food, emphasizing that the 'good stuff' includes not just the food but also the cherished moments spent savoring it.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and families seeking a pause from fast-paced life, who value quality time and appreciate classic American fast-casual dining experiences.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film/commercial (medium: Film) broadcast across relevant channels, illustrating the concept of time slowing down during the Freddy's experience.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build an emotional connection with consumers by positioning Freddy's as a place where people can slow down, savor their food, and enjoy quality moments, thereby enhancing brand perception and loyalty.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A visually driven campaign establishing 'The Iron Standard' as Dunlop's benchmark for unparalleled durability, strength, and reliability, likely showcased through compelling cinematic storytelling.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Consumers seeking high-performance and extremely durable products, particularly in the automotive or sports equipment sectors, who value reliability and superior quality, aged 25-60.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film and supporting video assets produced for digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media) and potentially broadcast television, complemented by integrated visual design elements across various touchpoints.

content.description:
If you’ve turned on network or cable TV in the last few months, you’ve likely witnessed the ongoing Clash of the Carriers between AT&T and T-Mobile. The two brands have been lobbing a steady stream of confusing, convoluted ads at one another — staged on dusty dirt roads — all in service of a familiar, cliche contest: comparing whose network is bigger.

But judging by the lawsuit filed between the companies, the only thing the dirt has been good for is something they can drag one another through.

In an era when nationwide coverage should be a given, smart consumers know these campaigns are just a distraction from what really matters: great coverage at low prices, backed-up by industry-leading customer service.

Consumer Cellular launched a very different kind of salvo with a Super Bowl spot this past Sunday that shifts the focus away from coverage chest-thumping and toward service — the thing customers interact with long after the signal bars fade from view. To make the point, they also found a nice dirt road to shoot the aptly titled, “Dirt Road” on, just so everyone would know they’re watching a wireless carrier spot.

The difference is who’s doing the talking. Rather than a celebrity or a scripted pitchman, they asked a conveniently named real store manager, Rhine Reynolds, to explain why Consumer Cellular stands out. More on Mr. Reynolds coming soon.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Consumer Cellular's 'Dirt Road' campaign spoofs the ongoing, confusing 'Clash of the Carriers' ads by AT&T and T-Mobile, positioning Consumer Cellular as the smart alternative focused on great coverage, low prices, and customer service, rather than engaging in network size comparisons.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Smart consumers who are tired of misleading and convoluted carrier ads, prioritizing actual value, which includes great coverage, low prices, and excellent customer service.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A Super Bowl film spot (TV commercial) designed to contrast with competitor campaigns running on network and cable TV.
ai_enrichment.objective: To differentiate Consumer Cellular from major network carriers, reposition the brand as the intelligent choice for value, and attract new customers by highlighting its core value proposition of superior coverage, low prices, and customer service.

content.description:
Quebrar o Silêncio, the first Portuguese non-profit organisation dedicated to providing specialised support to men and boys who are survivors of sexual violence and abuse, has just launched a new awareness campaign.The campaign takes a surprising approach to addressing sexual violence against men, starting from the discovery, inside a classroom, of the person who is believed to have been the victim of sexual abuse the previous day.

 

Advertising Agency: Clube RecriativoProduction Company: Mosaic Media HouseCreative Directors: Kiko Pedreira, Vasco GasparArt Director: Carlos GasparCopywriter: Kiko PedreiraDirector: Phillip ReeseDOP: Duarte DomingosPA: João Pedro Soares1o AC: Filipe Pantana2o AC: Daniel FerreiraGaffer: Sérgio PontesKey Grip: Afonso SantosDolly Grip: Ricardo AbrantesBest Boy: Tiago ValenteEdit: Mosaic Media HouseColorist: The Yellow Color / Marco AmaralVFX: Glimpse VFXSound & Mix: Salva Studios

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses a surprising and intriguing narrative device, beginning with the discovery of a presumed sexual abuse victim in a classroom, to challenge perceptions and initiate a conversation about sexual violence against men and boys.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General public, particularly those unaware of or holding misconceptions about male sexual violence survivors, and potentially male survivors, their families, and support networks in Portugal.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film (commercial/short film) developed by Mosaic Media House, featuring a dramatic and mysterious classroom scenario.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness about sexual violence against men and boys and the specialized support offered by Quebrar o Silêncio.

content.description:
Heineken® this week launches Fans Have More Friends, a new global sponsorship platform built on the belief that fandom is one of the quickest ways to spark a connection between strangers.

Rooted in Heineken®’s long-standing belief in the importance of sociability, and inspired by the book of the same name (Fans Have More Friends) by Ben Valenta and David Sikorjak, the platform reflects how shared passions help people connect naturally. It’s the first time Heineken® is bringing together its sponsorships across football, Formula 1® and music festivals, using fandom as both a social catalyst and a way to elevate social experiences in a world that can feel increasingly lonely.

New research commissioned by Heineken® shows that three-quarters (75%) of fans say their fandom has helped them meet new people, making it one of the fastest ways to create bonds at a time when many people are missing IRL connections more than ever before.

Three in five (59%) say it's helped them forge some of their closest friendships and 75% say watching sports with other fans, even if they're not fans of the same team, makes the experience better. Football topped the list as one of the greatest social connectors with 72% of fans saying language is not a barrier when it comes to making football mates in a bar.

To put the findings to the test, Heineken® partnered with Zac Alsop to carry out a social experiment designed to test whether fans would really show up for one another when it mattered. The experiment followed Joe, an Australian living in New York, who found himself without anyone to watch Liverpool play Qarabağ FK in the final group match of the UEFA Champions League. Like many people, despite having a life and routine in a big city, he was facing the prospect of watching one of football’s biggest nights alone.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign posits that shared passions and fandom are powerful catalysts for sparking genuine social connections between strangers, leveraging this insight to combat feelings of loneliness.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Fans of major global sports like football and Formula 1, as well as music festival attendees, who are seeking authentic social connections and opportunities to combat loneliness.

ai_enrichment.objective: To reinforce Heineken's long-standing brand association with sociability and connection, and to elevate social experiences for fans by fostering interaction through shared passions.

content.description:
As housing decisions feel harder to make, whether buying, renting or planning what comes next, Zillow® is evolving how it shows up as a brand. The company is launching "Someday Starts Today," a new national platform, campaign and tagline designed to support people at every stage of the home journey, from those ready to move now to those still only imagining what's possible.

The campaign debuts Feb. 1 in connection with Zillow advertising that airs during the Grammy Awards broadcast, and it reflects a strategic expansion of Zillow's brand approach. Rather than focusing only on moments of transaction, the approach is designed to resonate across the full home journey, supporting people who are actively moving today while also building belief and confidence among future movers who may be browsing, dreaming or planning what comes next.

"Everyone's home journey looks different, but progress is what moves it forward," said Beverly W. Jackson, vice president of brand and product marketing at Zillow. "'Someday Starts Today' reflects our commitment to meet people where they are and recognize every step forward, because even small progress matters."

Speaking to the in-between momentAffordability challenges and economic uncertainty have reshaped how people approach housing, stretching the time between moves and changing consumer behavior. While some consumers are actively buying, renting or selling, millions of people use Zillow every day to scroll listings, compare rentals, track home values and save ideas long before they're ready to make a move.

"Someday Starts Today" is designed to speak to that in-between moment. The campaign reframes browsing, planning and dreaming as meaningful parts of the home journey — not passive behavior, but early signals of intent that build over time. Zillow believes that for many future movers, their "someday" may be closer than they think.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Zillow's "Someday Starts Today" campaign redefines its brand to support individuals at all stages of their home journey, from immediate movers to future dreamers. It shifts focus from solely transaction moments to building belief and confidence across the full home journey.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals at every stage of the home journey, including active movers ready to transact now and future movers who are still imagining what's possible.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: TV advertising, specifically debuting during the Grammy Awards broadcast, as part of an integrated campaign.
ai_enrichment.objective: To strategically expand Zillow's brand approach, evolving its positioning to resonate across the full home journey and build belief and confidence among future movers.

metadata.title: GP Access – Tap it, type it and we’ll take care of it

content.description:
GP Access – Tap it, type it and we’ll take care of it

NHS England has launched GP Access – Tap it, type it and we’ll take care of it, a new multichannel national campaign created by M+C Saatchi Group UK, designed to encourage people to contact their GP practice via its website rather than automatically picking up the phone.

The campaign supports a wider NHS ambition to recover access to primary care by reducing pressure on GP phone lines for those who need them most, particularly during the 8am rush, and by helping patients get the right help in a more convenient way. It focuses on increasing awareness, understanding and use of online forms available through GP practice websites, improving both access and overall patient experience.

At the heart of the campaign is a significant behavioural shift. For many people, the deeply ingrained belief is that the ‘best’ and ‘right’ way to contact a GP is by phone, and that calling is how GP practices want to hear from them. The work is designed to challenge that assumption and create a new memory structure for audiences, reframing online access as an easy, practical way to get help.

With that in mind, the creative needed to connect with a broad range of people while clearly demonstrating the ease and convenience of the service. The campaign centres on everyday members of the public tapping away on laptops and mobiles as they enter their symptoms on their GP practice website. This simple, familiar action is shown in different settings and situations, making the process feel quick, accessible and achievable.As the campaign unfolds, the sound of typing gradually builds into a recognisable rhythm, forming a shared melody that reflects a nation coming together and embracing a new way of accessing care. The edit and tempo begin slowly before building in pace and energy, culminating in a flurry of synchronised tapping. A 30-second film, directed by Simon Ratigan at Rogue Films, brings together sound, picture and emotion to challenge perceptions and, ultimately, help change habits.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign, 'GP Access – Tap it, type it and we’ll take care of it,' encourages the public to use online forms on GP practice websites instead of phone calls for more convenient access and to reduce pressure on phone lines.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: The general public in England who need to contact their GP practice, particularly those who typically default to phone calls.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Multichannel national campaign, with film as a key medium.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive behavioral change by increasing awareness, understanding, and use of online GP practice forms, thereby reducing pressure on phone lines and improving patient access and experience.

content.description:
Agencies:Isla Agency•Rebolucion AI, About Entertainment

The new short film by Rebolucion AI, About Entertainment and creative agency ISLA explores what happens when relationships are no longer altogether human.

Happy Valentine’s DA.I. is a short film developed through a collaboration with Rebolucion AI, About Entertainment, and ISLA. The film reflects how relationships change when technology starts taking over their intimacy.Set in the near future, in an AI-created world, and during Valentine’s Day, its inhabitants feel compelled to express their true feelings.

Ezequiel De Luca and Nicolas Diaco pointed out: “We believe the most insightful way of understanding what AI can contribute to creativity is to put it to the test —not only on a technical level, but also an emotional one. This short film is an excuse to explore new ways of telling stories, incorporating technology as part of the process.”

Happy Valentine’s DA.I. is a short film directed by Sebastian Lopez, developed over a ten-month period and the result of a collaborative work involving Rebolucion, About, and ISLA. It was a cross-team project encompassing conceptual development, scriptwriting, production and post-production. The film was conceived as a non-profit artistic, cultural and experimental piece, and it’s now available on YouTube.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The short film 'Happy Valentine’s DA.I.' explores the evolving nature of intimate relationships in a near-future, AI-created world, where technology impacts human connection during Valentine's Day.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Creative professionals, advertising agencies, and brands interested in the intersection of AI, technology, and storytelling.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Release of a short film, likely distributed online (e.g., YouTube, Vimeo, agency websites) and potentially through film festivals or industry events.
ai_enrichment.objective: Showcase the collaborating agencies' (Isla Agency, Rebolucion AI, About Entertainment) innovative capabilities in AI-driven creativity, thought leadership, and ability to explore complex emotional themes through film.

content.description:
For years, Bulgarians were told that sleeping is for the weak.In a culture that glorifies exhaustion, rest had a bad reputation.

To challenge that belief, isleep created a job no one had ever heard of: Chief Sleep Officer — and gave it to someone who knows that recovery builds champions: Olympic sprinter Ivet Lalova.

Launched on World Sleep Day, the appointment turned sleep into a national conversation, sparking massive earned media attention across TV, podcasts, social platforms and expert forums.

What started as a symbolic role became a cultural shift — reframing sleep from laziness into strength, and proving that even the strongest need rest to perform.

 .

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: isleep appointed Olympic sprinter Ivet Lalova as the first Chief Sleep Officer to challenge Bulgaria's culture of glorifying exhaustion, positioning sleep as a vital component for peak performance and recovery.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: The Bulgarian general public, particularly adults and high-achievers influenced by a culture that glorifies exhaustion and views sleep as a weakness.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Launch of a hero film announcing Olympic sprinter Ivet Lalova as the Chief Sleep Officer, amplified by massive earned media across TV, podcasts, social platforms, and expert forums, strategically launched on World Sleep Day.
ai_enrichment.objective: To challenge and shift cultural perceptions of sleep in Bulgaria, reframing it from laziness into strength, and to generate widespread conversation and media attention around the importance of rest.

content.description:
Toyota launched the third season of GRIP to show that technology should enhance the thrill of driving rather than replace the driver. While the industry focused on autonomous systems that remove the human element, Toyota used an anime series to demonstrate how tech and human instinct work together.

The story followed a team of drivers facing a mass technology takeover by an elite hacker named Baron Shadow. By diving into the team's origins, the campaign showed the battle to keep humanity's spirit alive against a rogue mainframe.

The season successfully built brand momentum, driving a 6% increase in both brand favorability and consideration. Ultimately, GRIP Season 3 proved that true innovation happens when tech and humans unite to ensure human driving is never erased.

 

Advertising Agency: Intertrend CommunicationsProduction Company: Lawrence Fells ProductionsPR Agency: Meehan Business Advisers

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Toyota's GRIP Season 3 utilized an anime series to advocate for technology that enhances human driving thrills, not replaces them, by depicting a battle against an AI takeover to preserve humanity's spirit.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Automotive enthusiasts and tech-savvy younger audiences (e.g., 18-45) who appreciate anime and value the human element in driving.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Launch of a multi-episode anime series as the core 'film' content.
ai_enrichment.objective: Increase brand favorability and consideration for Toyota USA.

content.description:
Today, Coach introduces “Explore Your Story,” the brand’s Spring 2026 campaign shaped by global listening to Gen Z and co-created with communities worldwide. Spanning cultural partnerships from the United States to China, the campaign features Coach’s iconic Tabby bag and introduces a new collection of readable book charms.

Inspired by a renewed cultural embrace of long-form storytelling, “Explore Your Story” taps into a growing tension: Gen Z lives in a short-form, fast-paced digital world, yet is increasingly turning back to books as a way to explore identity, self-expression and possibility. Through the campaign, Coach champions storytelling as a way to express all of who you are and have the Courage to Be Real, creating room for connection through shared experiences, from books and book clubs to digital communities and campus conversations.

“One of the clearest insights we heard from Gen Z globally is how central storytelling is to how they make sense of who they are and who they’re becoming,” said Joon Silverstein, Coach Chief Marketing Officer. “In a world shaped by fragmentation, digital overload and constant acceleration, many described books and long-form storytelling as a refuge, a way to slow down, reflect and feel a sense of belonging. ‘Explore Your Story’ grew from that insight, and from our desire to show up alongside this generation as they find the confidence to write their own narratives.”

Six boundary-breaking members of the Coach community star in the two films that anchor the campaign. Returning global ambassadors include Academy Award-nominated actress and producer Elle Fanning, Emmy-winning actress and producer Storm Reid, Korean artist, singer-songwriter and producer SOYEON, and Japanese singer-songwriter Lilas Ikuta. They are joined by Paige Bueckers, now in an expanded role with the brand, and breakthrough Chinese singer Shan Yichun, who joins Coach for the first time. Together, they span film, music and sport, each embodying Coach’s grounding purpose: The Courage to Be Real.

Directed by Marcus Ibanez, the two campaign films bring storytelling to life through intimate vignettes that show how a powerful story can shift perspectives, spark possibility and foster community. As each cast member reads a book, the world transforms around her, showing how stories can inspire courage and connection. Each book featured is mirrored by a matching, readable book charm, part of Coach’s new collection, which hangs from each protagonist’s Tabby bag. The films are complemented by still imagery captured by award-winning photographer Elaine Constantine.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Coach's 'Explore Your Story' campaign connects its iconic Tabby bag and new book charms with Gen Z's renewed interest in long-form storytelling and self-expression, despite their digital lives. It champions storytelling as a way for individuals to express their full identity and foster authentic connections.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Gen Z globally, who are navigating a fast-paced digital world but increasingly seeking identity, self-expression, and connection through storytelling, including traditional mediums like books.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Integrated campaign spanning cultural partnerships from the United States to China, featuring Coach’s iconic Tabby bag and introducing a new collection of readable book charms, likely involving digital content, community engagement, and experiential activations around storytelling and shared experiences.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand connection and engagement with Gen Z globally by championing authentic self-expression through storytelling, while promoting Coach's iconic Tabby bag and new book charms collection.

content.description:
Today, pioneering automotive brand OMODA launches Everything is Essential, a bold new campaign for the OMODA 7 featuring thousands of real flowers, three tons of water and a clowder of live cats. Marking the first work with Uncommon Creative Studio, the campaign signals a confident step into the UK market — showcasing OMODA’s innovation and design through craft-led, culturally fluent storytelling.

Created by Uncommon, the campaign takes a social-first visual approach, pairing distinctive, almost GIF-like visuals with ASMR-inspired sound design. In doing so, it deliberately moves away from the category’s familiar tropes of rugged terrain and high-speed performance, offering a more playful, sensory take on automotive advertising.

The work comprises three striking films, each spotlighting a different element of the OMODA 7’s in-car experience — from intelligent fragrance settings and insulated noise-reducing glass to a 745-mile hybrid range, 14 speakers and 256 light modes.In a world that prizes minimalism — a car packed with this much innovation, technology and unique features is counter cultural. The work leans into this maximalist nature celebrating how everything really is essential to satisfy our human need for more.

EVERYTHING CAPTURED IN-CAMERATogether, the films present the OMODA 7 as a car defined by atmosphere and sensory detail, created for a style-conscious audience. Each execution was captured entirely in-camera placing physical craft at the centre of the storytelling.The first film explores the vehicle’s 745-mile hybrid range in one tank of fuel and charge — brought to life by subjecting the car to every weather condition in the UK you might encounter to show its range. Over the course of the shoot, three tons of water were dropped from ten meters custom-engineered rigs, fans and manual releases, while additional mechanisms generated rain, dust, leaves and snow.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A bold, sensory, and visually distinctive campaign for the OMODA 7, using unconventional elements like flowers, water, and live cats, combined with GIF-like visuals and ASMR sound, to offer a playful take on automotive advertising.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: UK consumers interested in innovative automotive design, who are receptive to modern, sensory, and culturally fluent storytelling, likely engaging with content on social media platforms.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A social-first film campaign comprising three striking films, featuring distinctive, almost GIF-like visuals and ASMR-inspired sound design, designed to move away from traditional automotive tropes.
ai_enrichment.objective: To confidently introduce OMODA to the UK market and showcase its innovation and design through culturally fluent storytelling.

metadata.title: Apuesta con los mejores en BetPlay

content.description:
Dentro de la estrategia de la marca BetPlay, la conexión con figuras deportivas ha sido un pilar importante en la consolidación del liderazgo en el mercado; después de trabajar con grandes deportistas llega un momento clave en la marca, vincular a la compañía al futbolista más grande de la historia del país, James Rodríguez.

Cuando pensamos en James, pensamos en una historia de gloria y también de frustración, pero, detrás de cada momento de celebración y de trabajo en su carrera siempre hubo una constante, el apoyo de un país. James para Colombia representa esa apuesta que nunca falta, esa alegría infinita dada por un talento excepcional y junto a eso, un pueblo que nunca dejó de apostar por él. Así nace apuesta con los mejores, una estrategia de comunicación 360° en la que dimos la gran noticia, ahora James era parte del equipo más grande de Colombia, el equipo BetPlay, y mediante una serie de contenidos en diferentes medios (Televisión, Digital, Radio, OOH, PR, POP) generamos conversación nacional, fortalecimos el vínculo con la marca y dejamos claro que Colombia hoy y siempre seguirá apostando por James en la plataforma de apuestas más grande, BetPlay.

 

CEO: Henry CanoCreative VP: William MichellyExecutive Creative Director: Diego SánchezCreative Director: Óscar LópezCreative Director: Luis RamónMotiongraphic Director: David ArenasCreative Copywriter: Carlos CalderónGraphic Designer: Andrés CorredorGraphic Designer: Andrés PlazaGraphic Designer: Esteban OvalleContent Manager: Laura RomeroContent Manager: Juan Pablo MorenoAccount Executive: Laura GonzálezProduction company: Parche Films

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign links BetPlay with iconic Colombian footballer James Rodríguez, using his journey of talent, glory, and national support to position the brand as one that partners with 'the best' and encourages users to 'bet with the best'.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Sports betting enthusiasts and football fans in Colombia, particularly those who admire national sports figures like James Rodríguez.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A comprehensive 360° communication strategy centered around the strategic partnership with James Rodríguez.
ai_enrichment.objective: To consolidate BetPlay's market leadership by associating the brand with the country's most significant sports figures and leveraging their popular appeal.

ai_enrichment.target_audience: Modern sports fans, likely aged 25-55, who watch games at home, are interested in smart home technology, and seek to enhance their viewing environment with immersive and customizable lighting experiences.

content.description: adam&eve\TBWA is proud to unveil “Calling All Heroes,” a bold new campaign created for and in partnership with Merlin Entertainments to celebrate the launch of Galacticoaster, LEGOLAND® Resorts’ newest high-tech indoor rollercoaster coming to LEGOLAND® Florida and LEGOLAND® California Resorts on Feb. 27 and March 6, respectively.Designed to rally heroes of all sizes, the campaign invites families to step up and save LEGOLAND from an out-of-control asteroid in a thrilling mission woven directly into the new ride’s storyline.The campaign spotlights Merlin’s record $90m investment in the groundbreaking space-themed attraction, a first of its kind indoor coaster where guests can design their own spacecraft using LEGO® elements- resulting in more than 625 unique spacecraft combinations- before seeing themselves “riding” in the vehicle they designed before blasting off to face the Asteroid of Probable Destruction heading their way and save LEGOLAND.More a recruitment drive than a traditional advertising campaign, Calling All Heroes serves as a universal call to arms, encouraging families to build, customize, train and save the day together as a crew of LEGOLAND space heroes.The hero launch film, directed by Gary Freedman through MJZ, opens on Mission Control, where Lead space engineer Tex Hardstone and his team have run out of answers for how to stop an asteroid threatening to destroy LEGOLAND. Until an unexpected hero steps forward: 8-year-old Jenny, ready to take charge. As she outlines her imaginative spacecraft vision, the engineers scramble to keep up, racing through LEGO dials, reference books and communication gadgets as Jenny leads the mission through space.The film will run through the end of March, with additional activations planned throughout the year. Versions will air in 60, 30 and 15-second formats across online video, connected TV, radio, social and out of home channels, with campaign media planning and buying led by OMD.Miranda Hipwell, CEO of adam&eve\TBWA, spoke of the creation:“Working with Merlin on our first major campaign post pitch has been a blast from start to finish. What an honor to launch this LEGOLAND work into the stratosphere."Toby Horry, SVP of Global Marketing at Merlin Entertainments, said:“We’re incredibly excited to unveil our new advertising campaign for Galacticoaster, opening at LEGOLAND in Florida and California in the coming weeks. The campaign captures the exhilaration of blasting through space on our newest indoor family coaster while showcasing one of the ride’s most unique features—the chance for every young space explorer to design their very own spacecraft… It’s a perfect example of how families get to build bigger together at LEGOLAND Resorts.”Matt Gay, Executive Creative Director at adam&eve\TBWA, added:“Launch a space ride. At LEGOLAND. An absolute belter of a brief and so much fun to create with our new clients at Merlin Entertainments.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The 'Calling All Heroes' campaign invites families to become heroes and save LEGOLAND from an asteroid, a thrilling mission woven directly into the storyline of the new Galacticoaster ride.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Families with children, particularly those interested in theme parks, LEGO, and interactive immersive experiences.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Promotional campaign around the launch of a new interactive indoor rollercoaster, integrating the campaign's heroic mission directly into the ride's storyline and guest experience; film as a primary medium for promotion.
ai_enrichment.objective: To celebrate the launch of the new Galacticoaster ride and generate excitement and visitation for LEGOLAND Resorts Florida and California.

content.description:
Agencies:72andSunny•BUTTER Music and Sound

The NFL's Inspire Change platform continues its purpose-driven storytelling with You Are Special, a cause spot celebrating the power of feeling seen, valued, and supported.

Inspired by the timeless warmth of Mr. Rogers and created by 72andSunny, with the cover created by BUTTER Music and Sound, the spot features Michael Strahan, Cam Heyward, and Christian McCaffrey alongside children from Harlem Children’s Zone, the Boys & Girls Clubs of America, and SMASH, singing along to the iconic song “You Are Special.” All three organizations are Inspire Change grant partners supported by the NFL.

“You Are Special’ is a powerful expression of what Inspire Change embodies — it’s a reminder to every young person that they matter,” said Sarah Bishop, Vice President of Global Brand and Consumer Marketing for the NFL. “By leveraging the influence of our players to spotlight our incredible Inspire Change partner organizations—and the barriers those partners work tirelessly to break down—this creative builds toward a Super Bowl moment that celebrates community and opportunity for all.”

The campaign has rolled out in multiple versions across key moments of the postseason:A 30-second Inspire Change version launched during Weeks 15 and 16A 60-second general cause version debuted during the NFL’s Christmas Day games and was aired again during the Conference Championship GamesA 30-second general cause version will air during the Super BowlTogether, these placements reflect the NFL Family’s ongoing commitment to investing in youth and communities nationwide, using the league’s biggest moments to spotlight purpose-driven storytelling.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A cause spot for the NFL's Inspire Change platform, inspired by Mr. Rogers, featuring NFL players and children from partner organizations singing 'You Are Special' to celebrate the power of feeling seen, valued, and supported.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young people, their parents, and the broader community interested in youth development, social justice, and the NFL's community initiatives.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Production of a multi-platform cause spot/film featuring NFL players and children, distributed across integrated channels including TV and digital platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To inspire and empower young people by affirming their value and sense of belonging, while raising awareness for the NFL's Inspire Change platform and its grant partners.

content.description:
Ollie, the human-grade fresh dog food company and leader in the dog wellbeing revolution, introduces today its new brand platform and largest national brand campaign to date, Feed the ObsessionTM. The campaign celebrates the deep, joyful devotion dog parents have for their dogs and Ollie’s obsession with delivering the highest quality experience for parents and pets.

Feed the Obsession is born out of a simple truth: Dog parents are obsessed with their dogs. In fact, according to a recent survey of American dog parents 1, a whopping 89% consider their pup their best friend and more than two-thirds (68%) admit they’d take five years off their own life to add five years to their dog’s. It’s that selfless obsession that fuels Ollie’s team of veterinarians, chefs, food scientists, and tech experts to develop the best possible food, experience, and technology that redefine how we care for our dogs. And in turn, feeds dogs’ obsession with their food.“At Ollie, obsession isn’t a flaw —  it’s our driving force,” said Allison Stadd, CMO of Ollie. “Our veterinarians, engineers, in-house chef, and the rest of our deeply dog-obsessed team use science and technology to build a truly tailored experience, all in service of the one thing that matters most: your dog’s wellbeing. Feed the Obsession puts a flag in the ground. It celebrates the people who read ingredient lists, track poop quality, and reorganize their lives around a dog they love like family, and shows how Ollie fits right into that level of care.”Feed the Obsession features many of the “if you know, you know” habits that make the dog-obsessed a special breed. From perfectly organizing your dog’s food and accessories with custom labels to post-meal mouth kisses and even dedicated dog parent tattoos — the national campaign showcases the tight-knit bond that only humans and their dogs have.

Directed by Josefine Cardoni and created in partnership with creative agency nice&frank, Feed the Obsession launches March 2nd with a series of video spots across streaming platforms like Peacock, Paramount+, and Hulu, as well as broadcast networks like HGTV, The Food Network, NBC, and ESPN — all set to Kylie Minogue’s iconic track, Can’t Get You Out of My Head, which underscores the joyful, obsessive bond between dog parents and their pups.

content.description:
Vileda, a global leader in home cleaning, announces the launch of “Flexible Pets”, the new integrated campaign created by Herezie Milano. At the heart of the communication is Flex & Catch, the innovative dust wand featuring a super-flexible head designed to reach even the most difficult corners, making it the ideal ally—especially for pet owners.The creative concept is based on an immediate visual parallel: the ability of dogs and cats to flex and squeeze into tight spaces, just like the new Vileda duster. Through a series of three print subjects, the campaign showcases the extreme flexibility of pets, providing a perfect mirror for the versatility of the new Flex & Catch.To ensure maximum authenticity, the production chose to rely exclusively on the lens of Ale Burset. The award-winning photographer worked without any recourse to AI, seeking the exact moment when the animal’s natural pose perfectly expressed the concept of flexibility.“We wanted the campaign to speak a language of truth. The flexibility of Flex & Catch is real and tangible, just like that of the animals living in our homes,” explained Andrea Stillacci, CEO and President of Herezie. “Relying on Ale Burset allowed us to elevate the product to a superior aesthetic, celebrating nature without filters or technological shortcuts.”The campaign is currently on air in Italy and Eastern Europe. In addition to the print subjects, the communication unfolds across the brand's social channels with a series of video snippets featuring the animal protagonists captured in their most fluid and surprising movements.The ecosystem is further enhanced by influencer marketing activities involving creators and pet owners, invited to test the effectiveness of Flex & Catch against the daily challenges of pet hair and stubborn dust.

 

Agency: Herezie MilanoCEO: Andrea StillacciCreative Directors: Federico Anelli, Francesca NepoteSenior Account Executive: Manuel CalloriPhotographer: Ale BursetDirector: Ale Burset / Mariano Legname GarciaDigital retoucher: Eduardo NavarroExecutive Producer: Marcela MoracciProducer: Ariel Ticona Molina (Wolf Credo)VILEDA GLOBAL:Markus Dietz: Senior Marketing DirectorMaike Mocikat: Social Content ManagerVILEDA ITALIA:Marta Alderighi: Marketing DirectorEddy Chiaberge: Senior Digital Marketing ManagerArianna Di Giorgio: Digital Marketing Manager

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign visually compares the extreme flexibility of pets squeezing into tight spaces with the super-flexible head of Vileda's new Flex & Catch dust wand, emphasizing its ability to reach difficult corners.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Pet owners who seek effective cleaning solutions for hard-to-reach areas in their homes.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A series of three print advertisements featuring authentic photography by Ale Burset.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive awareness and highlight the unique selling proposition (USP) of Vileda's new Flex & Catch dust wand upon its launch.

content.description:
This Chinese New Year, everyone has their own idea of getting richer.

Some are chasing the next big win. Some are glued to their screens, calculating, planning, hoping. Some are dreaming of the angpow that will change everything.

But sometimes, wealth isn’t where you expect it to be…

This Chinese New Year, may Ma Shang Fa Cai bring more than prosperity. May it bring warmth, presence, and the kind of richness that no angpow or bonus could ever buy.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign redefines wealth for Chinese New Year, shifting focus from material gain to the invaluable richness of warmth, presence, and family connections.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and families celebrating Chinese New Year, particularly those who shop at AEON BIG, valuing traditional festive sentiments and emotional richness over purely material wealth.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across digital platforms like YouTube and social media, potentially including TV broadcast, supported by in-store promotions and digital content.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand affinity and emotional connection with consumers during Chinese New Year by repositioning the concept of wealth beyond material gain, thereby strengthening AEON BIG's brand image as a community-focused retailer.

content.description:
To announce the arrival of Fugglers in Argentina, we turned Buenos Aires into the stage for a playful and irreverent FOOH invasion. Together with Vulcanita, we developed a digital film where the brand’s chaotic collectible monsters take over iconic city landmarks, blending humor, surprise, and pop culture at an urban scale.

Fugglers appear roaming through emblematic locations such as the Obelisk and Puerto Madero, interacting with the city as if they truly belonged there. The piece leans into the absurd logic of FOOH content, using visual shock and scale to blur the line between fiction and reality.

Staying true to the characters’ DNA, the film embraces the Fugglers’ most recognizable trait: their provocative, irreverent, and unapologetically crude humor. The result is highly shareable social content designed to entertain first, positioning the brand through laughter and controlled chaos rather than traditional advertising.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A playful and irreverent FOOH (Fake Out Of Home) digital film where collectible monster toys, Fugglers, appear to invade and interact with iconic landmarks in Buenos Aires.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young adults and teens in Argentina, particularly those interested in quirky collectibles, pop culture, irreverent humor, and viral social media content.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A digital film employing FOOH techniques to depict Fugglers in iconic Buenos Aires locations, designed for high shareability across digital and social media platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To announce the arrival of Fugglers in Argentina and generate widespread buzz and awareness for the new collectible toy line through engaging and shareable content.

content.description:
Start Your Path is a statewide, multilingual public service campaign designed to address Washington’s urgent behavioral health workforce shortage. Commissioned by the Washington Health Care Authority and developed by Quinn Thomas, the campaign expands awareness of behavioral health careers and guides diverse audiences toward attainable, community-rooted career paths.

Grounded in extensive research, including bilingual focus groups, and community input,  the campaign uncovered a key insight: many young adults and career changers have personal lived experience with mental health or substance use but don’t realize this often-stigmatized experience can qualify them for meaningful roles in the behavioral health field. Confusing education requirements, financial concerns, and fear of emotional strain frequently prevent them from exploring these paths.

Start Your Path reframed what’s possible. Through clear messaging, culturally resonant creative, and low-barrier resources, the campaign demonstrated that impactful healthcare careers can begin with accessible entry-level roles where lived experience is not only valued but essential.

The integrated media approach prioritized authenticity and reach across English- and Spanish-speaking audiences. Key executions included short-form videos, paid search, Reddit conversation ads, transit placements, digital media and DOOH across Washington, sponsored podcast audio, and a guided career quiz on StartYourPath.org.

From September to December 2025, the campaign delivered 14.9 million impressions, 51% above plan, along with 88,000 website sessions and Spanish-language CTRs more than double expectations and industry benchmarks. To date, Start Your Path has generated over 250 million impressions and nearly one million site sessions, offering a scalable model for rebuilding Washington’s behavioral health workforce.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The core creative idea is to reframe personal lived experiences with mental health or substance use from a stigmatized experience into a valuable qualification for meaningful behavioral health roles.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young adults and career changers across Washington state, particularly those with lived experience in mental health or substance use, and diverse audiences interested in community-rooted career paths.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A statewide, multilingual public service campaign utilizing a 360° medium to reach diverse audiences, including through extensive research and bilingual focus groups.
ai_enrichment.objective: The objective is to expand awareness of behavioral health careers and guide diverse audiences towards attainable career paths, thereby addressing Washington’s urgent behavioral health workforce shortage.

content.description:
Nestlé Compleat has launched “Project Inchstone,” a new purpose-driven platform during U.S. Feeding Tube Awareness Week. The campaign challenges traditional pediatric “milestones,” replacing them with "inchstones" — the small, hard-won steps that matter most to families who tube feed. Built as a long-term platform, Project Inchstone invites caregivers to share their own inchstones, helping shape future tools, resources, and advocacy through 2026.

 

Haythem Shoukry, Senior Director of Brand MarketingPriyanka (Pri) Malhotra, Director of Brand MarketingAshleigh Ricevuto, Senior Manager of Brand MarketingNicole (Nikki) O’Brien, Brand Manager, Compleat

Chief Creative Officer: Rich LevyEVP, Executive Creative Direction: Jillian Baum / Klick HealthVP, Group Creative Director: Mark Scott / Klick HealthVP, Group Creative Director: Dan Mingle / Klick HealthAssociate Creative Director, Copy: Liz Allemang / Klick HealthAssociate Creative Director, Art: Bryan Thompson / Klick HealthBrand Strategy: Gina Nasuti / Klick HealthGroup Account Director: Colleen Small / Klick HealthProgram Manager: Alex Light / Klick HealthAgency Producer: Oliver Featherman / Klick Health
