ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Nestlé Compleat's 'Project Inchstone' reframes traditional pediatric milestones as 'inchstones' for families who tube feed, celebrating their unique progress and building a long-term platform for community and advocacy.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Caregivers and families of children who require tube feeding, primarily in the U.S.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A purpose-driven platform launched with a film, inviting caregivers to share their stories, and ongoing development of community resources, tools, and advocacy initiatives through 2026.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build community and advocacy for caregivers of children who tube feed by reframing progress through 'inchstones' and fostering long-term engagement.

content.description:
On February 1, SALOMON launches Shaping New Futures, its latest global brand campaign, positioning innovation as the brand’s cultural engine: a way to shape sport, influence style, and design a modern mountain sport culture, while staying true to Salomon’s roots.

Over the past three years, SALOMON has followed a clear and purposeful brand trajectory. In 2024, Welcome Back to Earth reaffirmed its deep connection to nature and outdoor culture. In 2025, Invented. ReInvented. extended that vision to the world’s leading cities, showcasing how Salomon’s heritage continually adapts to new environments, practices and communities.

In 2026 Salomon opens new chapter with a campaign dedicated to the mindset that has always set it apart: innovation. Fully asserting its role as a force that shapes both sport and culture.

Guillaume Meyzenq, CEO, Salomon: “2026 marks a significant step in a journey we began in the French Alps 80 years ago.Our mission is to connect people to nature and to shape the future of how the world moves. Whether on a mountain peak or a pavement, we design boldly, ensuring each innovation propels the sport ahead. We are moving from a modern mountain sport equipment brand into a modern mountain sports culture - creating a future where performance & responsibility go hand in hand. "

The new tagline, Shaping New Futures, reflects Salomon’s belief that the future is not something to predict, but something to create. Fueled by curiosity and experimentation, innovation is seen not as a destination, but as an ongoing, unfinished process — driven by bravery, creativity and a constant desire to challenge boundaries.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Salomon's 'Shaping New Futures' campaign positions innovation as the brand's cultural engine, aiming to influence sport, style, and modern mountain sport culture while honoring its heritage.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Globally, active individuals and urban trendsetters interested in outdoor sports, progressive design, and a modern mountain sport lifestyle, from core enthusiasts to those adopting outdoor aesthetics in urban settings.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Global film campaign, likely distributed across digital platforms (e.g., YouTube) and potentially traditional broadcast media.
ai_enrichment.objective: To strengthen Salomon's brand positioning by asserting innovation as its fundamental cultural engine and a driving force in shaping modern sport and style.

metadata.title: The Case of the Missing Idli

content.description:
The ChallengeFor most Indians, giving up carbs means giving up comfort.And for millions, that comfort is shaped like a soft, round, perfectly steamed idli.But when fitness fads began villainizing carbs, idlis disappeared from breakfast plates, victims of the new “protein revolution.”

The InsightWhat if we could bring idlis back, not as a cheat meal, but as a smart one?Prolicious launched its new protein-rich idli mix in packaging conceptualised by Creative Khichdi, featuring India’s first front-of-pack nutrition label that proudly puts protein content up front. A bold move that made people see their favourite comfort food in a whole new, guilt-free light.

The IdeaTo launch it, we turned the idli’s disappearance into a national mystery. A dramatic, tongue-in-cheek campaign that began with one simple question: “Where have all the idlis gone?”

The ExecutionWe kicked off with teasers showing idlis vanishing from plates and kitchens across the country. Then came vox pops with real people lamenting their loss, from breakfast lovers to gym-goers trying to cope. Finally, we dropped the big reveal: a breaking-news style launch film announcing that the missing idli had been found, only this time, it came packed with protein.

The OutcomeIndia’s most loved breakfast made a comeback, healthier, happier, and stronger than ever.The campaign sparked conversation, revived nostalgia, and delivered a visible spike in sales for Prolicious’ protein-packed idli range. Because sometimes, to make people fall in love again, you first have to make them miss it.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign turned the traditional idli's disappearance due to low-carb trends into a national mystery, using a dramatic, tongue-in-cheek approach to launch a new protein-rich idli mix.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Health-conscious Indians, particularly those who enjoy idlis but have avoided them due to carb concerns or are influenced by protein-focused fitness trends.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Digital campaign focusing on a 'national mystery' narrative, leveraging product packaging with India’s first front-of-pack protein nutrition label.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reframe idlis as a healthy, protein-rich breakfast option and drive sales of the new Prolicious protein-rich idli mix by addressing consumer guilt around carbs.

content.description:
In early 2024, thirty6 were asked to help lead a global insight development project to ascertain the core role of the Financial Times within current, lapsed and likely readers, across the globe. The output of this project led to reinforce the FT’s positioning as the most trusted source of quality news, due to its balanced and unbiased approach to reporting – a unique and powerful stance in a news landscape plagued by divided political allegiances and obscured agendas. This then led to the FT and OPC developing SOURCE:FT as the creative articulation of that strategy, with first work in-market in September 2024.

Fast forward a year, with further work in-market back in January, and here we are at the third instalment. The campaign has certainly moved on, stripping back some of the more overt elements that featured at the launch stage, but are no longer needed now that the platform has become more established.

 

Brand: Financial TimesManaging Director, Consumer Revenue: Fiona SpoonerConsumer Marketing Director: Graham MacFadyenHead of Creative Strategy: Chris StoreySenior Performance Marketing Executive: Alessandra French

Agency: thirty6Creative Partner: Nigel RobertsClient Partner: Simon HewittStrategy Director: Marie MaurierCreative: Gavin Kellett / David Hillyard / Rob JackDesign Director: Jimmy HunterDesign Director: Sam HenryDesigner: Kate StephensonMotion Graphics Designer: Ignacio RodriguezAgency Producers: Amy Sworder / Gemma Hose

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The SOURCE:FT campaign articulates the Financial Times' strategic positioning as the most trusted source of quality news, emphasizing its balanced and unbiased reporting.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Current, lapsed, and likely global readers who seek and value trusted, balanced, and unbiased quality news.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A multi-phase film campaign, with initial work in-market in September 2024, followed by further instalments in January and an ongoing third phase.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reinforce the Financial Times' positioning as the most trusted source of quality news based on its balanced and unbiased approach.

content.description: From a childhood ride in the '90s to the driver's seat today, the Toyota RAV4 reminds us that the superhero belt comes full circle, and the journey is defined by the people beside us

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign creates an emotional narrative spanning generations, connecting childhood memories in a '90s vehicle to present-day driving, using the metaphor of a 'superhero belt' (seatbelt) to symbolize life's full circle and the enduring importance of family journeys. It positions the Toyota RAV4 as a constant companion through these significant life stages.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adults likely aged 30-50 who grew up in the 1990s, are now drivers and potentially parents themselves, who value nostalgia, family connections, and are seeking a reliable vehicle for their own evolving family journeys.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film, distributed across digital platforms like YouTube and potentially broadcast television, showcasing the multi-generational story and emotional journey, likely supported by shorter social media cuts.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand affinity and strengthen emotional connections with the Toyota RAV4 model by evoking nostalgia and highlighting its role in family milestones across generations.

content.description:
As anticipation builds for the Winter Olympic and Paralympic Games Milano Cortina 2026 on Feb. 6 and the Big Game on Sunday, Feb. 8, Starbucks is introducing a new ad campaign.

‘The Coffee Run’ ad will debut during the Olympic Winter Games Milano Cortina 2026 opening ceremonies on Feb. 6, with additional placements before and after the Big Game. It will also run on Feb. 8 during the pre-and post-post Super Bowl game.

Starbucks is the Official Coffee Partner of Team USA for the Winter Games, and the ad features Team USA Olympic and Paralympic athletes––including Amber Glenn, Elana Meyers Taylor, Oksana Masters and Aaron Pike.

InspirationA coffee run is simple, yet meaningful universal ritual that inspires connection. It’s more than a quick errand; it’s a gesture of care that strengthens bonds among friends, colleagues, and loved ones, whether shared in a café, at work, or even on the slopes.The campaign reinforces Starbucks role in the rituals that bring people together, from everyday moments to once-in-a-lifetime global events.Details‘The Coffee Run’ reimagines the familiar concept of a coffee run as an epic, cinematic, Olympic-scale journey.The film follows a man on a sweeping journey through a snowy mountain town, zipping through streets on a scooter greeting locals and dropping off Starbucks coffees to members of the Winter Games community and Team USA winter athletes.Along the way, coffee is handed off to members of the extended Team USA community, including Olympic athletes (Elana Meyers Taylor and Amber Glenn) and Paralympic athletes (Oksana Masters and Aaron Pike) and the many people working behind the scenes to support them.Set to a playful, a cappella–inspired track, the film builds in repetition and scale as the runner repeats the journey for one more coffee, ending with a final request at the top of a mountain before cutting to his return to Starbucks, welcomed by a knowing smile from a barista who recognizes him.Highlighting the feeling of warmth and joy when someone brings you a coffee, ‘The Coffee Run’ is a celebration of the dedication, craft, and human connection that goes into every cup, reminding us that a little coffee goes a long way.Directors and Production NotesDirector duo: Julian & QuentinDebuting during one of the busiest advertising moments of the year––Olympics & Super Bowl weekend––a deliberate point of distinction in this film is the analog approach, crafted to feel cinematic rather than commercial, and leading with storytelling.To honor the craft at the heart of The Coffee Run, the film was put through a film out process, transferring the 90-second film to physical film and back introduced texture, grain and imperfection that couldn’t be replicated digitally, grounding the work in the lineage of authentic Italian cinema and giving it a tactile, timeless quality.MusicThe music choice was led by directors Julian & Quentin, who were drawn to iconic American composer Burt Bacharach––famous for his unconventional, highly crafted arrangements.The song “South American Getaway” brings that sense of action and chase that make the film so catchy.

Anomaly is the creative agency that Starbucks partnered with us on the ad campaign.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign positions a 'coffee run' as a universal ritual of care and connection, linking this sentiment to the spirit of the Winter Olympics and Super Bowl by featuring Team USA Olympic and Paralympic athletes.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: A broad audience of sports enthusiasts, daily coffee drinkers, and individuals who value connection and care, particularly those tuning into major national sporting events like the Olympics and Super Bowl.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film ad campaign broadcast on national television, premiering during the Winter Olympic Games opening ceremonies and running during pre- and post-Super Bowl broadcasts, leveraging Starbucks' official partnership with Team USA.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand awareness and strengthen Starbucks' association with universal rituals of connection, care, and national pride, especially during major high-visibility sporting events.

content.description:
Leading online personalised greeting cards and gifts brand, Moonpig, is launching a special, interactive Valentine’s Day campaign in partnership with Global, the Media & Entertainment Group.

Sharing the love in the run up to February 14th, Moonpig and Heart, the UK's biggest radio brand, have created a co-branded campaign at Tottenham Court Road station, featuring a takeover of every digital screen on the Elizabeth line platforms.

The creative encourages customers to upload an image and message to wish someone special a Happy Valentine’s Day and spread the message that “Everyone Deserves a Moonpig”.

The campaign forms part of Moonpig’s national marketing campaign with Global, which includes radio and online activity across Heart and social media activity on Moonpig’s Instagram, TikTok and LinkedIn. It’s a unique and really fun opportunity to celebrate your best mate, your pet, or (more traditionally) the love of your life!

Rachael Halliday, Marketing Director at Moonpig, said: “At Moonpig, we’re all about helping people create more personal, meaningful connections - and is there a better time to share the love than Valentine’s Day? This campaign brings real love stories to life in a fun and joyful way, turning everyday commutes into moments that make you smile. Our Tottenham Court Road takeover celebrates all kinds of love with genuine messages from real people, putting heartfelt connections centre stage. We’re thrilled to be working with Global, the perfect partner to bring these moments to life both on air and on screen.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: An interactive Valentine's Day campaign co-branded with Heart, encouraging commuters to upload personalized messages and images to be displayed on digital OOH screens at Tottenham Court Road, promoting the idea that 'Everyone Deserves a Moonpig'.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals celebrating Valentine's Day, particularly those looking to send personalized greetings and gifts, who are commuters in London (specifically Tottenham Court Road) and engaged with Heart radio, Instagram, and TikTok.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Digital Out-Of-Home (OOH) screen takeover on Elizabeth line platforms at Tottenham Court Road station, supported by national radio and online activity across Heart, and social media engagement on Moonpig's Instagram and TikTok.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase brand awareness and engagement for Moonpig during the Valentine's Day period by encouraging user participation and reinforcing the brand's association with personalized gift-giving.

content.description:
Young people are increasingly shaped by extreme worldviews online and often feel pressured or misunderstood. Let’s not leave them alone in this — together, we can help prevent radical actions that begin behind screens.

 

Agency: MADE BY VACULIKChief Creative Officer: Milan BielikCEO: Alexandra MartinákováStrategy Director: Michaela MartonováStrategic Planner: Alexandra Remo, Terezia ŠabováAssociate Creative Director: Ivana PolohováCopywriter: Andrej Hrivnák, Veronika RybárováArt Director: Daniel Vojtek, Naďa Lukáčiková, Michal NagyAccount Director: Michala ZezulováAccount Manager: Linda KadlicováSocial Media Manager: Kristína Kačániová, Natália Klieštiková, Monika RezníčkováProduction House: Stink PragueStink President/Executive Producer: Daniel BergmannExecutive Producer: Klára KrálíčkováProducer: Martin MelicharProduction Manager: Tereza FeketeProduction Coordinator: Martin SommerCasting: Casting Dorantová, Casting - BarrandovDirector: Dominik StröhleDOP: Nico SchrenkPhotographer: Aleš KrálEditor: Filip MalásekGrading: Daniel Gallego/PFXSound Production: NAPA RecordsSet Designer: Mariana HädlerCostume Designer: Kateřina KrobováMusic supervision: TAYSTMusic composing: TAYST, Wolfsrudelmusic

content.description:
On Valentine’s Day, we all know what’s getting the spotlight. And after a highly intense activity, hunger always follows. That’s why many couples make sure to order their Valentina in advance, forgetting that the delivery might… interrupt the intimate moment.

metadata.title: New, game-changing electric SUV: the Volvo EX60

content.description:
New, game-changing electric SUV: the Volvo EX60

AKQA and Volvo Cars have continued their creative partnership with the launch campaign for the highly anticipated Volvo EX60. A fully electric SUV that can drive up to 810 km on a single charge.Not only does the EX60 match the range of many petrol cars, it can also fast charge up to 340 km in ten minutes. Being the first of its kind, it’s positioned to overcome the remaining barriers that hold people back from choosing electric – known to consumers as range anxiety. Its innovations not only prove that sustainable travel is no longer a compromise, but mark a new era for electric vehicles.

AKQA, known for pioneering the application of new technology for brands with powerful and impactful storytelling, was proud to be chosen to collaborate with Volvo Cars’ in-house teams to showcase the innovative design and engineering that the EX60 boasts.

The collaboration between Volvo Cars and AKQA ranges from positioning, design system, tease and social assets, to, print, DOOH, digital activations, a “one-shot” film, a series of documentary-styled films, as well as the reveal event, broadcasted live from Sweden.Held at Artipelag, in the Stockholm archipelago on January 21, the reveal brought together industry experts and media for an unexpected live event, where the Volvo record-range SUV was seen driving non-stop on stage. The technological feat was achieved in collaboration with AKQA’s Architecture and interior design practice, Universal Design Studio, FutureDeluxe and Volvo Cars engineers. It required customised sets of robotic, motion-controlled rims, a special-built 26-metre screen, three turntables and a range of meticulously calibrated environments, digitally crafted from Swedish landscapes.Throughout the show, the bespoke content was presented on the surrounding LED screen in sync to the car’s movement, transporting both car and audience to the various environments, demonstrating the EX60’s class-leading capabilities in real time.

The launch campaign continued the theme of uninhibited movement with a “one-shot” film directed by Filip Nilsson and produced by PINE. The film explores the car’s ground-breaking features while celebrating its impressive range as it traverses vast landscapes and dynamic urban environments without a single cut. The film rolled out across social and digital, with shorter formats running on TV. It was accompanied by a suite of other campaign assets, including OOH, DOOH, social and radio.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign introduces the Volvo EX60, a new electric SUV positioned as a game-changer that eliminates range anxiety with its exceptional range and fast-charging capabilities, proving sustainable travel is no longer a compromise.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Consumers interested in electric vehicles but held back by concerns about range and charging infrastructure (range anxiety), seeking a reliable and high-performance sustainable transportation option.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A launch campaign utilizing film as the primary medium, developed in partnership with AKQA, focusing on impactful storytelling and showcasing the vehicle's innovative features.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive consideration and adoption of the Volvo EX60 by directly addressing and overcoming consumer range anxiety, thereby marking a new era for electric vehicles and accelerating their mainstream acceptance.

content.description:
Three stories, one dreamer: Jung von Matt crafts character-led automotive storytelling.

Jung von Matt has developed and produced a global product campaign marking the first major international product push for MINI under its new creative lead agency. Built around a modular storytelling system, the work showcases three distinct vehicles through the imaginative lens of a single, unlikely protagonist, a car wash worker who dreams himself behind the wheel of each MINI model that enters his bay.

The narrative follows a car wash attendant whose daily routine takes a cinematic turn as he begins to fantasize about the lives he might live in each of the three MINI Cooper models he washes. Each vehicle triggers a different story, reflecting the unique personality of the car:

With The MINI Cooper 5-Door, he imagines himself as part of a close-knit band of friends on their way to a gig — laughter, energy, and music filling the car as they cruise through the city.The arrival of The all electric MINI Cooper sparks a joyful family moment, as he sees himself driving through an urban landscape with his daughter beside him.And when The MINI Cooper 3-Door pulls in, he shifts gears into full go-kart mode: tearing through a parking garage with sharp corners, tight turns, and pure driving thrill.Together, these three daydreams form a playful yet powerful expression of the MINI Cooper range. Each model offering a different way to experience the road, and a different version of yourself.

Modular storytelling for global marketsDesigned to meet the complex content needs of markets across three continents, the campaign structure allows for the flexible use of product-led and emotionally driven storytelling elements, while also integrating the witty MINI tone of voice. Although they are unified by a single creative idea, each model narrative can be adapted or expanded to emphasise regional differences.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A car wash attendant dreams himself into different adventurous lives, each inspired by a distinct MINI Cooper model, showcasing the cars' unique personalities through a single, imaginative protagonist.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Aspiration-driven individuals, likely urban dwellers, who value distinctiveness, creativity, and seek a premium compact car that reflects their imaginative lifestyle.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A global film campaign, distributed across digital platforms (e.g., YouTube) and broadcast TV, utilizing a modular storytelling system to highlight three specific MINI models.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate global awareness and intrigue for MINI's new product lineup and to define the brand's fresh creative direction under its new lead agency.

metadata.title: Own Your Flow
tags: A Clio Health Initiative - Women's Health Platform

content.description:
Kotex launches its global platform ‘Own Your Flow’ with an empowering anthem film directed by Juan Cabral via MJZ. Using tennis as a metaphor for pressure and self-doubt, the spot debuts bold new branding and a reworked classic by Tears for Fears, challenging period taboos with striking confidence.

 

How we won the hearts of Israeli teenage girls

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Kotex's global 'Own Your Flow' platform uses an empowering anthem film, featuring tennis as a metaphor for overcoming pressure and self-doubt, to challenge period taboos with new branding and striking confidence.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Global young women and teenage girls, with a specific focus on Israeli teenage girls.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: An empowering anthem film, directed by Juan Cabral and featuring a reworked classic song, was launched as part of an integrated campaign.
ai_enrichment.objective: To launch a new global brand platform and branding, challenge period taboos, and build brand affinity and empowerment, especially among teenage girls.

content.description:
Introducing the first ever music-streaming urn from Liquid Death x Spotify.

Finally death is a lot less boring. With the Eternal Playlist Urn, now the dead can listen to their favorite jams for all of eternity. There are only 150 in existence. Get your own before they are gone forever.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Liquid Death collaborated with Spotify to introduce the "Eternal Playlist Urn," a limited-edition music-streaming urn designed to make death less boring by allowing the deceased to listen to their favorite music perpetually.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adults who appreciate dark humor, unconventional marketing, and the edgy brand identity of Liquid Death, including existing fans and potentially collectors of unique, limited-edition items.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film introduces the novel product, likely distributed across digital video platforms and social media, with a direct-to-consumer sales model for the limited 150 units.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate significant brand awareness, media buzz, and reinforce Liquid Death's distinctively irreverent and unconventional brand identity through a humorous and surprising product collaboration.

content.description:
'Tavern Collaborates with Faccia Brutto to Create a One-of-a-Kind Liquor'

To celebrate its third year in business, global creative agency Tavern partnered with its Brooklyn neighbors, Faccia Brutto, to create a one-of-a-kind liquor: a 30-month barrel-aged Fernet Pianta. Fewer than 450 bottles exist. The collaboration brings together two Brooklyn brands and one shared heritage to create something that honors the ritual of Italian drinking traditions.

A collaboration born from Brooklyn craft and friendship

Faccia Brutto has been a long-time collaborator of Tavern. Tavern helped to build the Faccia Brutto brand on the ground – from bar activations to local hype. Its Fernet Pianta is widely considered one of the best Fernets on the market, beloved by bartenders and obsessives alike.

Taking its signature Fernet Pianta, Faccia Brutto placed it in white oak barrels and aged it for 30 months exclusively for Tavern. This is Faccia Brutto’s first-ever aged Fernet, made specifically for this collaboration and never to be reproduced.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A global creative agency, Tavern, collaborated with Brooklyn-based Faccia Brutto to create a limited-edition, 30-month barrel-aged Fernet Pianta, celebrating shared heritage and Italian drinking traditions.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Spirits connoisseurs, bartenders, and enthusiasts of craft, limited-edition liquors and Italian drinking traditions, particularly those who appreciate premium, small-batch offerings and are familiar with the Faccia Brutto brand.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Leveraging an integrated approach, execution likely includes targeted bar activations, local public relations, and word-of-mouth 'hype' within the spirits community, especially given the extremely limited bottle count.
ai_enrichment.objective: The objective is to enhance brand prestige and affinity for Faccia Brutto, celebrate the ongoing collaboration between Tavern and Faccia Brutto, and generate buzz within the spirits community for this exclusive, limited-edition product.

content.description:
Life’s most treasured moments may not always be picture-perfect. But they are the stories we can laugh about when we come together as a family. Watch what happens when this family is greeted with a ‘burst of luck’ at their reunion dinner!

Wishing everyone a Hay-ppy and prosperous Year of the Horse!

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A humorous narrative centered around a family's 'burst of luck' during a reunion dinner, highlighting how imperfect family moments create cherished, lasting memories, set against the backdrop of the Chinese New Year celebration.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Singaporean families, particularly those of Chinese descent, celebrating Chinese New Year.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A festive film or commercial depicting a family's humorous reunion dinner, likely distributed across television and digital platforms (e.g., YouTube) during the Chinese New Year period.
ai_enrichment.objective: To foster positive family values, promote community spirit, and spread goodwill during the Chinese New Year celebrations, consistent with a public utility's role in society.

content.description:
Created to launch BOH’s new Masala Chai Latte and Cocoa Tea Latte, the video celebrates the beauty of diverse tastes and personalities. Just as people with different interests can live harmoniously, the campaign positions BOH’s new flavours as a reflection of this diversity—distinct yet complementary. Through a light and relatable narrative, the video reinforces BOH’s role in bringing people together, proving that even the most different preferences can share the same cup of connection.

 

Director: Nor Syakira bt Mohd PauziSenior Digital Art Director: Yee Sook YanSenior Digital Designer: Anson Tan Her YiiDigital Designer: Rebecca Thoo Hui YingDigital Designer: Lim Khe YingProducer: Lai Mei YenAssistant Producer: Luqman HamdaniSenior Digital Copywriter	: Sarah Daniel Jacob a/p Paul DanielProject Manager: Angeline Ong Ai XuanAccount Manager: Ooi Yi WenActor: Layla Sania Binti KhairuzikriActor: Avanyeesh Karti GesuActor: Lim Ke XinActor: Lim Xin WayActor: Lim Kah HongHead of Production: Andrew Tristan BastianDoP: Ku Muhammad Akif bin Ku AzmanWardrobe Stylist: Jessica Lee Ying YingEditor: Fan Shi Qi, AmeliColourist: Chin Yik WeiMotion : Lew Khai Yen

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign introduces BOH's new Masala Chai and Cocoa Tea Lattes by celebrating the beauty of diverse tastes and personalities, positioning the distinct flavors as a reflection of how different preferences can harmoniously connect.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Consumers interested in exploring new tea latte flavors, particularly those who appreciate themes of diversity, connection, and relatable social narratives.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Digital video content, primarily distributed across online platforms and BOH's digital channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To launch and drive awareness for BOH’s new Masala Chai Latte and Cocoa Tea Latte, while reinforcing the brand’s positioning as a unifier of diverse preferences.

content.description:
In “The Game of Your Life,” cyclists and e-bike riders of all ages step into a TV game show where the stakes couldn’t be higher — their own safety. In a studio packed with hidden traps, they ride a giant treadmill, convinced they’re fully in control.But the road — even here — has other plans.

While focusing on a specific task, each contestant faces sudden, unexpected traffic challenges pulled straight from real life. A car door swings open. A pedestrian steps out. Another cyclist appears out of nowhere. Every test mirrors the unpredictability of the streets outside.Because in traffic — just like in this show — confidence can be dangerous.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A simulated TV game show on a giant treadmill challenges cyclists and e-bike riders with sudden, real-life traffic dangers, demonstrating that overconfidence can be perilous due to road unpredictability.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Cyclists and e-bike riders of all ages, particularly those who may overestimate their control and safety on the road.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film showcasing the 'Game of Your Life' simulation, distributed across digital platforms like YouTube and social media, potentially as a public service announcement (PSA) on television.
ai_enrichment.objective: To educate cyclists and e-bike riders about the unpredictability of road traffic and the dangers of overconfidence, thereby promoting increased awareness and safer riding behaviors.

content.description:
ConceptCeres, a small Italian beer brand in a world of giants, breaks traditional Italian marketing rules. In a TVC launched for Italy’s most-watched TV event, Sanremo Festival, the brand toasts with what appears to be its most famous competitor— the Green One, as an authentic gesture celebrating friendship beyond differences, because Nobody’s perfect.

Strategic InsightIn a market dominated by major players, Ceres has always stood out for its sincere, irreverent tone. Rather than ignoring competitors, the brand reflects real life: at any party, not everyone drinks the same beer. The campaign turns a potential “mistake” into a message of inclusion and radical tolerance.

The SpotSet to the nostalgic notes of Reality by Richard Sanderson, the film captures the start of an unlikely friendship. At a party, a woman struggles to open her Ceres Strong Ale but is too shy to ask for help. A stranger notices and silently opens it with a spectacular gesture.

This moment sparks a daydream: she imagines a future with her new best friend through surreal claymation scenes. The dream ends abruptly when she sees her friend grab an awkward, “blurred” green beer—you know the one. After a brief pause, acceptance wins. She chooses not to let different tastes get in the way, and they toast anyway. The final scene shows that friendship doesn’t require agreement.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Ceres, a smaller Italian beer brand, launches a TVC during the Sanremo Festival depicting an unlikely friendship where it toasts its main competitor, 'the Green One.' The core idea is to celebrate friendship and inclusion, acknowledging that 'Nobody's perfect' and reflecting real-life social dynamics.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Italian beer drinkers, likely young to middle-aged adults, who appreciate authentic, inclusive brand messaging and an irreverent, non-traditional approach to competition.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film (TV Commercial) broadcast during Italy's most-watched TV event, the Sanremo Festival, leveraging high national visibility.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand image and strengthen brand preference by positioning Ceres as an authentic, inclusive, and irreverent brand that understands real-life social dynamics beyond traditional competitive norms.

content.description:
BAPTIST HEALTH AND 52. A DREAM COMPANY PRESENT “THE COMEBACK”Baptist Health South Florida, the largest healthcare organization in the region, with 12 hospitals, more than 29,000 employees, 4,500 physicians and 200 outpatient centers, urgent care facilities and physician practices spanning Miami-Dade, Monroe, Broward and Palm Beach counties, together with 52. A Dream Company / Miami, the agency founded and led by Tony Waissmann, present their new work under the concept: “What really matters is living life to the fullest because an injury can take you out of the game, but Baptist Health puts you back in”

Elizabeth Novick Wenger, Director of Brand Management at Baptist Health South Florida, shared her thoughts on the project:

“Seeing Matthew Tkachuk publicly thank our physicians who helped him return to the ice was a powerful reminder of why we do what we do. At Baptist Health, caring for elite athletes and caring for our community are one and the same. It’s about delivering exceptional care with compassion, so every patient can get back to the moments that matter most. 52. A Dream Company, understood that from the very beginning. Their team brought creativity, agility and true partnership to the process, helping us tell this story in a way that feels both authentic and impactful”

Tony Waissmann, Founder & CCO of 52. A Dream Company, commented: The truth is, the first thing I have to say is that it’s a pleasure to work with Baptist Health because of their professionalism and their human quality. This is the first project we’ve done together, and we’re already working on the second. What I find very interesting about this work is that everything is real. We started from a real injury suffered by Matthew Tkachuk, assistant captain and one of the Florida Panthers’ stars, and what happened after his recovery at Baptist Health, when he returned to play and won the Stanley Cup with the team. At that very moment, he thanked the doctors, and everything started from there, leading to this concept, which applies not only to him but to everyone who has a passion for playing a sport or even going for a run. ‘An injury can take you out of the game but Baptist Health, puts you back in.’ It speaks about living life to the fullest, enjoying sports without fear because you know who’s behind you taking care of you. It’s an optimistic message about living and enjoying life.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign centers on the idea that while injuries can disrupt life, Baptist Health empowers individuals to make a full comeback and live life to the fullest.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Active individuals and their families across Miami-Dade, Monroe, Broward, and Palm Beach counties who value their health and seek expert care to recover from injuries.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film, leveraging storytelling and potentially featuring real-life testimonials (e.g., Matthew Tkachuk), distributed across various media channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To strengthen brand awareness and trust, positioning Baptist Health South Florida as the leading healthcare organization dedicated to helping patients recover and return to their lives.

metadata.title: The Story Of Guests
tags: Ads of the World Highlighted Ramadan Campaigns

content.description:
Ramadan is a season of gathering, where hosting becomes the highlight of every evening. It’s a time when homes open their doors and hospitality takes center stage.

Every family knows the familiar characters who arrive for Iftar or Suhour — the early guest who never misses the call to prayer, the cheerful guest who lifts the mood, the quiet observer who enjoys the moment, and the lively storyteller who keeps the table entertained. Different personalities, different arrivals, yet each one welcomed with the same warmth and generosity.

The Story of Guests celebrates these cherished Ramadan moments — the joy of hosting, the beauty of togetherness, and the spirit of welcoming everyone just as they are.

Inspired by this tradition of heartfelt hospitality, IHG Hotels & Resorts extends the same spirit beyond the home, opening its doors to every guest with warmth, care, and a genuine sense of belonging throughout the holy month.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign celebrates the diverse personalities of guests during Ramadan gatherings, highlighting the joy of hosting and IHG's commitment to extending this same heartfelt hospitality to everyone.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals observing or participating in Ramadan traditions, including hosts and guests, who value heartfelt hospitality and are seeking accommodation or experiences that reflect this spirit.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A film showcasing the various guest archetypes and the warmth of Ramadan hospitality, distributed on digital platforms and potentially television.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand affinity and emotional connection with the target audience by aligning IHG's hospitality values with the cherished traditions of Ramadan.

content.description:
Viettel 5G’s “Uncle Badass,” created by The Friday, reimagines Lunar New Year storytelling by flipping the traditional homecoming narrative. When his family can’t return for Tết, an uncle from northern Vietnam travels across the country to visit them instead. The humour-led road trip captures everyday encounters with drag performers, tattoo artists, gamers, and floating market vendors — communities rarely seen in mainstream holiday ads. By reframing Tết from an act of returning to one of reaching out, the campaign reflects modern Vietnamese family life while showcasing the nationwide connectivity of Viettel 5G in a warm, human way.

 

Creative Agency: The FridayCreative Director: Khoa PhamManaging Director: Trâm LêArt Director: Hy VõCopywriter: Hà Đàm, Huy HoàngDesigner: Việt LongAccount team: Tùng Phong, Thảo Hồ, Nghĩa Trần, Mai Thư, Thảo Trân, Sương PhạmPlanner: Thanh Phạm, Mai Linh

Re-energize - daily solution for a long-active day

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign reimagines Lunar New Year by flipping the traditional homecoming narrative, featuring an uncle traveling across Vietnam to visit family, showcasing nationwide 5G connectivity through a humor-led road trip.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Modern Vietnamese families and individuals, particularly those seeking reliable nationwide mobile connectivity, who appreciate inclusive storytelling and humor.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film deployed across mainstream holiday advertising channels, likely including TV and digital platforms like YouTube, featuring a narrative road trip.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance Viettel 5G's brand image, increase brand awareness, and demonstrate the widespread connectivity of their 5G network in a relatable and human manner.

content.description:
The "Outdo It with Intuit Quickbooks" campaign uses an unexpected but universally recognized cultural icon -- the Tooth Fairy -- to illustrate a familiar small business reality: Running a small business can feel incredibly lonely and overwhelming. Customers consistently say there aren’t enough hours in the day and they wish they could scale themselves.Created by BBDO New York, the campaign asks what the world loses when an essential business owner is pulled away from their work, and shows how QuickBooks helps them continue doing the work that people value and rely on. The work stands apart in a crowded field of AI business tools by approaching both the problem and the solution from the small business owner’s point of view.The message: With Intuit Quickbooks, doing it yourself doesn't mean doing it alone. With a team of AI agents and human experts at your side, customers can outdo financial uncertainty, outdo time constraints, and outdo the competition.This campaign marks a shift from positioning QuickBooks solely as “do-it-yourself” software to offering “done-for-you” experiences. Intuit helps small businesses -- even the Tooth Fairy -- outdo what feels possible.

 

Brand: Intuit/QuickbooksChief Marketing Officer: Thomas RaneseDirector, Global Brand Marketing: Gemini BablaGroup Manager, Global Brand Marketing: Natalie AliaVP of Creative, Brand & Storytelling: Katie PotochneyPrincipal Brand and Marketing Designer: Suncica LukicGroup Creative Director, Design: Christian WidlicGroup Creative Director, Campaigns: Curtis MuellerStaff Marketing Manager, Global Brand Marketing: Angela LeungStaff Marketing Manager, Global Brand Marketing: Vicky ShreflerAgency: BBDO New YorkChief Creative Officer: Michael AimetteEVP, Executive Creative Director: Gary ReschSVP, Creative Director: Lance ViningSVP, Creative Director: Gary Du ToitSVP, Head of Production: Nick WilliamsExecutive Producer: Jack PatrickSenior Producer: Alina CarrelSenior Producer: Rhea PhippsSVP, Group Account Director: Diana CashSVP, Group Account Director: Katie BartasevichVP, Account Director: Brandon RodriguezAccount Supervisor: Mariana SuarezSr Project Manager: Vida EhnHead of Business Affairs: Cindy HoffmanCEO: Emma ArmstrongChief Strategy Officer: Todd SussmanProduction Company: Morton Jankel ZanderDirector: Matthijs van HeijningenPresident/EP: David ZanderSr. Executive Producer: Eriks KruminsHead Of Production: Robert HelphandLine Producer: Donald TaylorService Company: FilmgroupExecutive Producer: Nathan Miles, Renee PoulinHead of Production:  Alexandra WilsonStaff Producer: Kerrigan CartnerIn-House Production Coordinator: Vianney MedinaProducer: Daniel HellmannEditorial: Union EditorialEditor: Jono GriffithExecutive Producer: Melissa LubinAnimation: Post EFX (PARIAH)VFX Supervisor: Patrick MurphyExecutive Producer: Michael SteinmannHead of Production: Persis ReynoldsProducer: Andrew RosenbergerProducer: Lily HernandezColor: Company 3Colorist: Tim MasickAudio Engineer: Sonic UnionHead of Production: Pat SullivanEngineer: Mike MarinelliMusic: HumanMD/EP: James Dean WellsCreative Lead: Morgan ViscontiComposer: Ava Matasavage

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses the universally recognized Tooth Fairy to symbolize the often lonely and overwhelming reality of small business ownership, illustrating how Intuit QuickBooks empowers these entrepreneurs to overcome challenges and continue their essential work.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Small business owners who feel overwhelmed, isolated, and stretched for time, seeking solutions to manage their operations, scale their business, and maintain their valuable contribution.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Primarily a film-based campaign, utilizing hero films for distribution across channels such as TV and digital video platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive consideration and adoption of Intuit QuickBooks among small business owners by demonstrating its value in alleviating operational burdens and enabling them to focus on their core business.

content.description:
The film depicts a shredded rendition of a potentially perilous deal that unfolds amidst the chaos of a dimly lit room. Within its confines, four illicit characters engage in a precarious transaction involving cocaine, counterfeit currency, and weaponry. With a myriad of unexpected twists, the narrative is deliberately fragmented, leaving viewers grappling to reconstruct the shattered pieces of the story.

 

Director - Raylin VallesExecutive Producer - Apratim BhatnagarCCO - Rahul MathewGCD - Sourabh DubeyGCD - Gagan BindraCreative Director - FatemaProducer - Ashwarya CCostume - PratishrutiDA - Kunal Jadhav

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A deliberately fragmented and suspenseful film portraying a perilous illicit transaction, compelling viewers to actively piece together its shattered narrative.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Viewers aged 25-50 who enjoy dark, suspenseful crime thrillers, non-linear storytelling, and mentally engaging narratives.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A short film released on digital video platforms (e.g., YouTube, Vimeo) and potentially promoted via social media teasers and content partnerships.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate significant viewer engagement and online discussion through its challenging, fragmented storyline, fostering intrigue for the "Steadfast Shredders" brand.

content.description:
KAYAK, a leading travel search engine, today unveiled a new brand platform and tagline, “Got That Right,” alongside a new campaign designed to tap into the most stressful moment of travel planning: the decision-making.

While 66% of travelers say booking travel makes them feel stressed, most travel brands continue to focus on selling the thrill of the trip itself. KAYAK is taking a different approach by owning the moment before the trip, when uncertainty and second-guessing peak – and confidence matters most. The new platform positions KAYAK as the tool that cuts through complexity and replaces doubt with confidence, so travelers can book knowing they got it right.

“Our research underscores the reality that travelers don’t need more information, they need reassurance,” said Carolina Montenegro, SVP of Global Brand Marketing at KAYAK.  “As travel search has become increasingly crowded and commoditized and people are bombarded by reels, reviews, stories and more - information overwhelm is real. ‘Got That Right’ is about giving travelers the reassurance that they’ve made the best choice, so they can avoid second-guessing.”

Created in partnership with independent agency Rethink, the campaign is aimed at travelers aged 25-45, dramatizing the very real obstacles standing in the way of booking a trip.

At the center of the effort are two comedic 30-second spots.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Kayak's 'Got That Right' campaign aims to alleviate the stress and uncertainty of travel planning by positioning itself as the definitive tool that provides confidence in booking decisions, owning the moment before the trip where doubt peaks.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Travelers who experience stress, uncertainty, or second-guessing during the travel planning and booking process, seeking reassurance in their decisions.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Film (online and potentially television) promoting the new brand platform and tagline.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand perception and drive usage by positioning Kayak as the solution for stress-free, confident travel booking, addressing a common pain point for travelers.

metadata.title: The biggest moments deserve the best viewing experience.

content.description:
The biggest moments deserve the best viewing experience.

 

Housewives, Birthday, Nun, Child, Birthday

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: XFINITY positions itself as the provider of an unparalleled viewing experience for all of life's significant and memorable moments, appealing to a diverse audience through relatable scenarios.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Households and individuals of all ages who seek a premium entertainment viewing experience for everyday enjoyment and special life events.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film or series of commercials, distributed across television and digital video platforms, showcasing diverse people and their 'biggest moments' (e.g., birthdays, family gatherings).
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand perception and preference by associating XFINITY with providing the highest quality viewing experience for life's most cherished moments.

content.description:
Today, HomeGoods is unveiling “Never Shop the Same,” a modern evolution of the brand including both a new visual ID and a new campaign. The work is a vibrant invitation back to the way shopping should be—a tactile and visual feast where you can find unique selections of top brands at up to 60% off, curated for people who believe that quality and value are deeply personal.

To reintroduce this "category of one" story, the campaign leans into a strategic musical framework. Each spot uses the music of a global superstar to reinforce HomeGoods’ "IRL" experience:

“We’re reintroducing HomeGoods as an experience that simply can’t be replicated online or in other stores,” says Vanessa  Crooks, VP Director of Marketing at HomeGoods “‘Never Shop the Same’ is a rallying cry to put down the phone, leave task-driven and boring shopping behind, and enjoy discovering something new and different every time you walk through our doors. We’ve modernized the look and feel of the brand to match the premium, ever-changing assortment our customers love.”

“We’ve lost the art of shopping. We shouldn’t be buying pillows and candles on our phones. They’re meant to be touched and smelled," says Leslie Shaffer, CCO at Fallon. "HomeGoods is here to remind everyone of the magic of shopping, and they have an experience like no other. Their inventory changes all the time. They have great quality items at killer prices. It’s not transactional, it’s emotional.”

For the visual identity, HomeGoods is introducing an updated holistic visual style and aesthetics, including a new color palette.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign reintroduces HomeGoods as a unique, vibrant, and tactile in-store shopping experience that offers curated, high-value selections, emphasizing that it cannot be replicated online.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Consumers who appreciate unique finds, believe quality and value are deeply personal, and enjoy the physical, discovery-driven shopping experience.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Primarily through film (TV commercials and online video) featuring spots with music from global superstars to reinforce the in-real-life (IRL) shopping experience.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reintroduce the HomeGoods brand, elevate its unique 'category of one' physical shopping experience, and differentiate it from online or other retail competitors.

content.description:
Bay Area-based independent media and ad agency H/L partnered with Toyota and Palisades Tahoe to deliver a pulse-pounding promo, “Real Drive,” which premiered during the opening ceremony of the Winter Olympic Games and aired during Super Bowl LX across the Northern California region. The spot pairs Olympic gold medalist Jonny Moseley with 15-year-old freestyle moguls skier Beau Rohlen, an aspiring Olympian, to launch the all-new 2026 Toyota RAV4.

“H/L’s latest work in its Toyota/Palisades Tahoe partnership is about meeting this exciting moment with the momentum made by world-class athletes on their sports’ biggest stages, an iconic Northern California destination, and the most advanced RAV4 Toyota has ever built,” said Dallas Baker, Executive Creative Director at H/L.

Toyota, The Official Vehicle of Palisades Tahoe, is captured in motion in high-impact, energetic scenes, slicing through the frame as Rohlen and Moseley take to the resort’s snowy terrain. Conceived by H/L and produced by its integrated in-house production team, Wolfhouse, the fast-paced, action-driven spot captures Palisades Tahoe in pristine bluebird conditions, elevated by bold motion graphics, VFX, dynamic sound design, and music crafted for the biggest stages in sports. The antidote to traditional “car in snow” footage, “Real Drive” starts with accomplished humans doing what they do best and features Toyota’s fully redesigned hybrid 2026 RAV4, reflecting athletic excellence on the slopes.

“Real Drive” leverages Toyota’s partnership with Palisades Tahoe and the US Ski & Snowboard Team to harness the excitement for the Winter Olympics and the NFL in Northern California. Anchoring the film in shots of Rohlen and Moseley in action, cutting down the slopes at Palisades Tahoe, and seeing Toyota vehicles breaking through the frame energetically puts the vehicles on the same playing field as these professional athletes.

“From the moment I saw the boards for ‘Real Drive,’ I knew this campaign was going to be a wild ride,” said Grey Rosado, Senior Editor at Wolfhouse. “The close collaboration between H/L and the Wolfhouse production team—from concept to post—was key to ensuring every dynamic transition and detail landed seamlessly.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign pairs Olympic gold medalist Jonny Moseley with an aspiring Olympian, 15-year-old Beau Rohlen, to showcase the all-new 2026 Toyota RAV4 against the backdrop of a renowned Northern California ski destination, symbolizing momentum and performance.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Automotive consumers in the Northern California region interested in SUVs, active lifestyles, winter sports, and aspirational performance, potentially families and younger individuals looking for a reliable and advanced vehicle.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A promotional film titled “Real Drive” premiered during the opening ceremony of the Winter Olympic Games and aired during Super Bowl LX, both across the Northern California region.
ai_enrichment.objective: To launch and build awareness for the all-new 2026 Toyota RAV4.

content.description:
To demonstrate how C Spire powers our connected lives, Lewis created two spots that work as one and ran them back-to-back during the Super Bowl. Together, they depict a fast-paced, energetic day in the life of a connected woman who uses technology to live like a boss.

The spot ran in Mississippi, Birmingham, AL and Nashville, TN across TV, OLV, CTV, Social, Programmatic.

 

LewisRyan Gernenz, VP Creative DirectorJason Headrick, Associate Creative Director/Art DirectorCedrick Bearss, Associate Creative Director/Copy WriterBrian Curtin, Creative Director, ProductionKyle Daniel, Video Production Director/EditorJacob Garner, Sr. ProducerJoy Honeycutt, VP Account ServiceGillian Lake, Account SupervisorProduction PartnerGear Seven, NashvilleMatthew Underwood, Director

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: C Spire's 'Like a Boss' campaign utilizes two back-to-back Super Bowl spots to portray a connected woman's energetic daily life, powered by C Spire technology, illustrating how it enables an efficient lifestyle.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Technology-reliant individuals and modern professionals within C Spire's service regions (Mississippi, Birmingham, AL, and Nashville, TN) who seek seamless connectivity and digital empowerment in their daily lives.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Two film spots aired back-to-back during the Super Bowl, supported by broad digital distribution across TV, Online Video (OLV), Connected TV (CTV), Social media, and Programmatic advertising.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand awareness and demonstrate C Spire's integral role in powering seamless, connected lives through its technology services.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign captures the delight of staying home, transforming everyday home experiences—from kitchen activities to lounge relaxation—into vibrant 'staycation' moments with Chicken Licken.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and families seeking convenience, comfort, and an enjoyable fast-food experience to enhance their home-based activities and 'staycation' relaxation.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across television and digital platforms like YouTube, potentially supported by shorter cut-downs for social media engagement.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase brand affinity and drive sales by positioning Chicken Licken as the ideal meal for comfortable, fun, and memorable at-home dining experiences.

content.description:
While other brands compete for pre-game and in-game attention, we’re focusing on the moment that’s ownable to McDonald’s–the morning after any big night, especially the Super Bowl.

Everything we do starts with a fan truth. This idea came from a pretty universal one: There’s nothing like McDonald’s Breakfast to help you recover after a big night.

We found that millions of Americans planned to take the Monday after the Super Bowl off from work. Some say it should be a national holiday for…recovery. Even if you still have work, your body wants nothing more than to be horizontal. So, we thought, let’s give that opportunity to our fans.

The idea:  “The horizontal breakfast”. While the country either literally is horizontal or dreams to be, we flipped our communications this match their orientation – billboards, the TV spot, our social channels all turned horizontal – because that’s the posture many of our fans will be in as they recover from the big game. McDonald’s breakfast, specifically the hot honey sausage + egg biscuit is the thing that will help you get vertical again (or at least reason enough to get vertical for a moment).

The breakfast features the hot honey sausage + egg biscuit, hash browns, and a coke.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign, 'The Horizontal Breakfast,' positions McDonald's Breakfast as the ultimate recovery meal for the morning after a big night, particularly Super Bowl Monday, by encouraging consumers to enjoy their meal while being as horizontal as they desire.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals recovering from a big night, especially Super Bowl viewers taking or wishing for a day off, who are seeking comfort and convenience in their morning meal.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film (TV, digital video) with communications creatively designed to visually align with a 'horizontal' orientation.
ai_enrichment.objective: To establish McDonald's Breakfast as the essential recovery solution for the morning after a big night, thereby strengthening brand relevance and preference for this specific occasion.

content.description:
Fake images to avoid real traps.At a time when artificial intelligence (AI) has become fraudsters' favorite tool, Visa and its agency Marcel have created “The Feathered Lamb,” a campaign designed to raise awareness about online fraud. On social media, 99% of scams originate from fake ads. Thanks to AI, these scams are multiplying and becoming increasingly difficult to detect. Phishing, for example, surged by 1,265% in 2025 (source: SlashNext). In response to this growing threat and to reinforce its role as a leader in payment security, Visa has chosen to tackle the weakest link in AI-driven fraud: people.

The creative idea: turning fraudsters' tactics against them“The Feathered Lamb” campaign borrows the very tactics used by fraudsters and flips them for awareness purposes. Absurd yet highly realistic AI-generated images like a lamb covered in feathers are shared on social media to grab attention. Once users take the bait, the trick is revealed and a clear educational message is delivered.

“We wanted to use the same psychological triggers as fraudsters, but in reverse,” explains Julian Clobus, Brand & Product Marketing Director, France. “Transaction security is at the core of our mission, but the main vulnerability remains human. The idea is to attract attention, then immediately defuse the trap by reminding people of simple best practices and the solutions Visa provides.”This awareness campaign unfolded in two phases:

The trap: first, we posted AI-generated fake photos and videos on social media through an anonymous account called « Petit Pamplemousse ». We then let users react to the posts for two weeks.

The reveal: everyone who believed the fake news received a message with a personalized video revealing that everything was staged. This allowed us to share simple tips to help them avoid fraud and encourage them to learn more at visa.fr/protectionfraude.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign turns fraudsters' AI-driven tactics against them by creating absurd, fake content to demonstrate how easily people can be fooled by online scams and raise awareness.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General internet users, particularly those active on social media, who are susceptible to online fraud, phishing, and fake ads.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Integrated campaign, heavily leveraging social media platforms where fake ads proliferate, to deploy deliberately absurd and deceptive content mimicking scam tactics.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness about the pervasive threat of AI-driven online fraud and reinforce Visa's leadership in payment security.

content.description: No matter how life unfolds, family remains our truest blessings

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign leverages a heartwarming narrative centered on the enduring importance of family as life's greatest blessing, aiming to emotionally connect McDonald's with these universal values.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Malaysian families, particularly parents and adult children, who cherish family bonds and appreciate shared moments.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across digital platforms like YouTube and social media, potentially supplemented by television broadcasts to maximize reach and emotional impact.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand affinity and emotional connection with consumers by associating McDonald's with the universal value of family and blessings.

content.description:
Driven by the same progressive spirit that has defined the agency for over four decades, BBH (Bartle Bogle Hegarty) today unveils a refreshed visual identity that honours its storied heritage while sharpening its focus on the future of creative excellence.

Since its 1982 founding by John Bartle, Sir Nigel Bogle, and Sir John Hegarty, the agency has operated under a singular, defiant mantra: “When the world zigs, zag.” This philosophy, famously paired with the iconic black sheep in their inaugural Levi’s campaign, remains the cornerstone of the brand’s DNA.

BBH still believes that creativity and difference are the biggest drivers for commercial growth. So to move the brand on, without moving it off, BBH’s Felipe Serradourada Guimarães and Adam Buckland, supported by the rest of the agency, set out the vision.

At the heart of the new identity are three bespoke typefaces, created in collaboration with Studio DRAMA, the London-based type studio behind the Heinz typeface. Each one honours one of BBH’s three founders, capturing a distinct creative instinct that continues to shape the agency today:

BBH Bartle: designed for clarity, with broad, confident letterformsBBH Bogle: built for conviction, echoing the heavier pen strokes of the original BBH logoBBH Hegarty: crafted for creativity — expressive and unconventional, embodying the spirit of the Zag

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: BBH is refreshing its visual identity to reflect its enduring 'When the world zigs, zag' philosophy and black sheep heritage, while simultaneously sharpening its focus on future creative excellence. This move aims to evolve the brand without straying from its core DNA of creativity and difference.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Prospective clients, industry professionals, and potential recruits in the advertising and creative industries.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign is described as 'Integrated,' suggesting a multi-channel rollout for the new visual identity. Specific tactics are not detailed in the provided information.
ai_enrichment.objective: To refresh BBH's brand identity, reinforcing its core values and heritage while signaling a forward-looking commitment to creative excellence and continued relevance within the industry.

content.description:
Expedia sends Mattel’s Ken doll on his first-ever adventure in a playful Super Bowl spot produced by BUCK with stop-motion studio Bix Pix. Marking Ken’s 65th birthday, the film sees him grow restless with life at the beach and set off in search of somewhere new, landing on advertising’s biggest stage as a wide-eyed first-time traveller.

Titled *Going Places with Ken*, the ad follows him as Expedia guides his journey to destinations including Italy, Japan and Mexico City, highlighting flexible bookings, rewards and savings. The launch is supported by Ken-themed Super Bowl sweepstakes, giving away $1,000 in OneKeyCash to 65 winners.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Expedia celebrates Ken doll's 65th birthday by sending him on his first-ever global adventure, showcasing the ease and benefits of booking travel with Expedia through a playful Super Bowl film.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adults with an interest in travel and pop culture, particularly those seeking flexible booking options, rewards, and savings for their journeys.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film launched as a Super Bowl commercial, supported by Ken-themed Super Bowl sweepstakes offering OneKeyCash.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase brand awareness for Expedia, inspire travel among a broad audience, and highlight the benefits of flexible bookings, rewards, and savings available through the platform.

metadata.title: Rooted in the Past. Inspired by the Present. Built for the Future.

content.description:
Rooted in the Past. Inspired by the Present. Built for the Future.

For its Winter 2026 campaign, premium Canadian outerwear brand Quartz Co. reaffirms what has defined its DNA for nearly three decades: expert craftsmanship, a deeply considered approach to sustainability, and a modern design language forged by Nordic winters.

At a moment when fashion and marketing are rapidly transformed by technology, Quartz Co. turns inward, and backward, to move forward, setting a new benchmark for what modern heritage can look like.

Founded in 1997 as Quartz Nature and reimagined in 2015 as Quartz Co., the year 2025 marked the brand’s 10th anniversary in its current form; a milestone that prompted a period of reflection.

Drawing directly from the brand’s 1990s archives, the creative team revisited original lookbooks, production notes, and early visual identity. The result is a campaign that feels both nostalgic and contemporary. Each silhouette is presented in a pared-back setting, shot primarily on analog film, embracing grain, texture, and imperfection. “There’s something honest about film,” adds Guindon. “It captures texture, depth, and emotion in a way that feels increasingly rare today. We wanted the imagery to feel lived-in, just like the garments”.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign celebrates Quartz Co.'s nearly three-decade legacy by blending archival inspiration from the 1990s with modern design and a commitment to sustainability, redefining what modern heritage outerwear embodies.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Affluent, design-conscious consumers aged 30-60 who prioritize expert craftsmanship, sustainability, and quality in their premium outerwear purchases, and appreciate brands with a rich heritage.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: An integrated campaign utilizing visual storytelling across digital platforms, premium print media, in-store activations, and potentially experiential elements to showcase the brand's archives, craftsmanship, and modern design language.
ai_enrichment.objective: To strengthen Quartz Co.'s brand positioning and equity by reaffirming its foundational DNA of expert craftsmanship, sustainability, and modern design, setting a new benchmark for modern heritage in the fashion industry.

content.description:
A new multichannel campaign Tell DWP, which aims to encourage customers claiming universal credit to report changes of circumstances has been created by M+C Saatchi Group UK for The Department for Work and Pensions (DWP).

The push supports the Department for Work and Pensions’ ongoing efforts to reduce the annual cost of fraud and error, by increasing the number of customers who update the department when their circumstances change, to avoid errors in payments. It also aims to raise awareness of the penalties and consequences of not keeping claims up to date, while improving understanding of which changes need to be reported.

While most people understand in principle that they need to keep DWP informed, reporting changes in circumstances often falls down the to-do list of everyday life. Changes are not always obvious either – as a result, incorrect self-reporting can happen unintentionally.

Tell DWP tackles this problem by using relatability and humour to create cut-through around a topic that is rarely front of mind. In two 30-second films, directed by Jim Archer at Mindseye, everyday people are shown chatting about their lives in familiar, everyday situations. As they talk through moments of change, a DWP Work Coach unexpectedly appears in the scene to prompt the reminder: tell us too.

The scripts are deliberately simple, grounded and dry, with the performances played straight and oblivious to the absurdity of the situation. Casting was central to the approach, with very British, authentic conversations designed to heighten the surreal nature of a DWP employee appearing mid-chat, while keeping the tone approachable rather than accusatory.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The "Tell DWP" campaign encourages Universal Credit claimants to proactively report changes in their circumstances to the Department for Work and Pensions to reduce payment errors and fraud.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Customers claiming Universal Credit, particularly those who might delay reporting changes due to everyday life priorities or those unaware of specific reporting requirements and penalties.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A multichannel campaign utilizing film as a primary medium, deployed across various platforms to reach the target audience.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reduce the annual cost of fraud and error in Universal Credit payments by increasing the number of customers who report changes in their circumstances, improving understanding of reporting requirements, and raising awareness of penalties.

content.description:
Following the pitch win late last year, AKQA Brussels is launching its first campaign for Mobile Vikings. In it, the well-known 'Play it Smart' promise gets an update. Less emphasis on just price, more on how people today really live with their internet. Thus, the telecom operator positions itself as the smart choice for those who live 100 per cent digitally and want to get the most out of their internet subscription.

Mobile Vikings has long been known as the smart choice for those who want a lot of data for little money. That logic remains. Only the story gets broader. Being smart is just as much about what you do with that data. About streaming without limits. About endlessly scrolling, gaming, testing and trying. About people not just being online, but living online.

The campaign shows this in a series of recognisable situations with an angle. Someone who watches everything at one-and-a-half speed because he is impatient and can just do it. Someone who uses A.I. to build a translation robot for his pet, purely out of curiosity. Or someone who tinkers a wifi-controlled scent dispenser to impress a date. Sometimes clever. Sometimes completely over the top. Always possible thanks to a subscription that lasts.

Humour remains a fixture in the brand DNA. The tone is light, self-deprecating and deliberately a tad exaggerated. No big technological statement, but recognisable digital gusto. Play it Smart thus becomes more than a price promise. It is an invitation to get everything out of your internet. And to have fun doing so.

For the production, AKQA Brussels worked with production house Hamlet and Canadian director Taso Alexander. The campaign will be rolled out over TV, online video and radio in the coming weeks and will herald a new chapter for Mobile Vikings.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign redefines Mobile Vikings' 'Play it Smart' promise by shifting focus from just competitive pricing to how modern users actively live their fully digital lives with their internet, enabling unlimited streaming, gaming, and exploration.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Digitally-native individuals and heavy internet users who actively engage in streaming, gaming, and extensive online activities, seeking to maximize their digital lifestyle rather than just finding cheap data.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Integrated campaign across various media channels to communicate the updated brand narrative and visual identity.
ai_enrichment.objective: To update and evolve Mobile Vikings' brand perception from solely being a provider of cheap data to the smart choice that truly understands and empowers a fully digital, data-rich lifestyle for its users.

content.description:
Teleflora®, the world’s leading floral delivery service, is proud to debut its new 2026 Valentine’s Day bouquet lineup alongside a brand-new campaign, “Tele-flora,” inspired by the belief that love often begins with the courage to speak from the heart.

“Tele-flora” turns unspoken words into unforgettable moments, transforming a floral phonebooth into an intimate and vulnerable space for people to share what’s in their hearts and “Love Out Loud.” These candid and unscripted messages of gratitude, admiration, and affection are reminders to leave nothing unsaid and share your love this Valentine’s Day.

“In a world filled with uncertainty, love is too important to leave unspoken,” said John Ludwig, vice president of marketing and e-commerce at Teleflora. “This Valentine’s Day, we wanted to give people a chance to speak from the heart – and flowers offer a beautiful way to share that love with someone special, whether they are near or far.”

Participants who shared their “Love Out Loud” through the public activation received a QR code with a recording of their message to share with their loved one, alongside a Teleflora bouquet.The ad will appear on Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok, and is supported by targeted in-stream media buys on YouTube. Content creators also took part in the activation, with sponsored content rolling out in the week leading up to the holiday. “Tele-flora” was developed by the Wonderful Agency, The Wonderful Company’s in-house creative team.

Say what matters most this Valentine’s Day by gifting your loved one a unique Teleflora arrangement. Teleflora’s best-selling Valentine’s Day floral bouquets are beautifully curated, made by hand and delivered to your doorstep by a local florist. Each bouquet comes complete with a festive keepsake container that can be enjoyed as part of home décor for years to come.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The "Tele-flora" campaign creates an intimate floral phone booth where people can "Love Out Loud" by sharing unscripted, heartfelt messages of affection, transforming unspoken words into unforgettable moments for Valentine's Day.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals celebrating Valentine's Day in 2026 who want to express their love, gratitude, or admiration and are looking for a meaningful way to communicate their feelings.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: An experiential floral phone booth activation provides a physical space for people to share candid messages, which are then captured and presumably shared through integrated media channels to inspire wider emotional expression and promote the new bouquet lineup.
ai_enrichment.objective: To encourage emotional expression and open communication of love this Valentine's Day, leveraging Teleflora's brand and its new 2026 bouquet lineup to turn unspoken feelings into shared moments.

content.description:
To celebrate the release of Diablo II: Resurrected - Reign of the Warlock, Diablo is teaming up with viral rapper and longtime Diablo II superfan bbno$ to create an original track titled “Save Me” and music video that doubles as a love letter to the community. Assets available here.

This collaboration, created in partnership with GUT LA, is rooted in authentic fandom – bbno$ has publicly celebrated Diablo for years, from incorporating Diablo-inspired design into his personal website to hosting D2 LAN birthday parties and showcasing the game in his own content.

“Diablo II is bar none my favorite game,” said rapper bbno$. “When I heard about the Warlock update, I basically screamed on the Zoom. Then they asked me to make a track? I immediately told my parents and they were just as excited as I was.”

The music video transforms bbno$ from player into character, starring him as the new Warlock class introduced in a recent game update, while pulling deep inspiration from Diablo’s history – including a nod to the iconic Deckard Cain rap that longtime fans will recognize.

The music video blends cinematic live action with game-inspired animation and dense lore references to blur the line between artist, player, and the Diablo universe itself. Designed as a fan-first cultural moment, the collaboration taps into bbno$’s massive digital reach and viral musical pedigree to celebrate Diablo II’s first major update in 26 years.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: To celebrate the release of Diablo II: Resurrected - Reign of the Warlock, Diablo II collaborated with longtime superfan and viral rapper bbno$ to create an original song and music video as a tribute to the game's community.
