ai_enrichment.target_audience: Existing Diablo II fans, particularly those deeply embedded in the game's community, and potentially younger gamers or fans of viral rap music interested in authentic pop culture collaborations.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Creation and release of an original music track titled 'Save Me' and an accompanying music video, distributed across digital platforms like YouTube and music streaming services, leveraging the viral rapper bbno$'s existing fanbase and online presence.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate excitement and engagement for the release of Diablo II: Resurrected - Reign of the Warlock, celebrate and reinforce loyalty within the game's community.

content.description:
MEET THE FINAL BOSS OF CHUPA CHUPS, THE HARDEST TO OPEN LOLLIPOP EVER

Chupa Chups launch new easier-to-open wrapper inside the “Final Boss”

In response to the legendary challenge of overcoming their infamously stubborn packaging, Chupa Chups is putting a playful spin on online chatter and has created a new easier-to-open wrapper. However, in an ironic twist, they’ve hidden it inside ‘their hardest to open lollipop yet.’ The new limited edition range is called Chupa Chups Impossible, and they’ve gone to extreme lengths to stop fans from getting inside.

The impenetrable lollipops, the ‘final boss’ of Chupa Chups, are encased in a carbon composite shell, wrapped in aramid fibres, coated in silicon carbide and finally, dipped in liquid rubber. The end-result is packaging that’s blade proof, flame proof and quite possibly, human proof. Fans of the iconic lollipop, however, will be relieved to know there’s a sweet surprise inside the wildly over-engineered treat – the new, easier-to-open wrapper.

Chupa Chups Impossibles have been sent to international influencers, challenging them to put the impenetrable lollipop to the ultimate test. Those influencers include wildly popular YouTube creators, The Hydraulic Press Channel, who shot to fame by crushing everyday objects in a hydraulic press, and Kenny Deuss, whose parenting prank videos have made him an internet sensation.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Chupa Chups playfully acknowledges its famously stubborn packaging by launching the 'Impossible' lollipop, an extremely difficult-to-open 'final boss' that ironically conceals a new, easier-to-open wrapper inside.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Existing Chupa Chups fans and a younger, internet-savvy audience (16-35) who are familiar with gaming culture and online meme humor, and have experienced the 'challenge' of opening their lollipops.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Launch of a limited-edition product range ('Chupa Chups Impossible') featuring an extremely challenging physical wrapper (carbon composite, aramid, silicon carbide, liquid rubber), designed to generate viral online chatter and PR by turning a consumer pain point into a playful 'challenge'. The product itself serves as the primary activation.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate significant brand engagement and positive buzz by playfully addressing a long-standing consumer pain point, while subtly introducing and highlighting a new, improved packaging feature.

content.description:
The Salvation Army is introducing its thrift brand to a new generation with the launch of “Thrift Score,” the world’s first digital thrift store on Roblox. Designed to engage Gen Z and Gen Alpha where they already shop, play, and express themselves, the experience launches Feb. 19 and invites players to explore thrift-inspired digital finds for their in-game avatars.Conceived and led by independent agency BarkleyOKRP and developed in partnership with immersive game studio The Gang, the experience reimagines a Salvation Army Thrift Store as a fully explorable and shoppable digital environment, introducing the brand and its thrift offering to an audience that might have never visited a store in the real world. Stocked with creator and brand collaborations, player donations, and digital replicas of real Salvation Army items, players can browse racks to uncover rare and limited edition items at accessible price points, ensuring broad participation across Roblox’s player base.“Thrifting has always been about more than price—it’s about creativity, individuality, and purpose,” said Lt. Colonel Mark Nelson, ARC Commander at The Salvation Army. “By bringing our thrift store into Roblox, we’re introducing a new generation to the spirit of thrifting at Salvation Army in a way that feels natural to how they explore, play, and express themselves today.”“Thrift Score” is integrated directly into several popular Roblox games— like Seaboard City and Daycare Party—placing the thrift store inside environments players already love and actively explore.The experience also features special, limited-edition UGC items donated by some of Roblox’s most influential creators, with select designs spotlighted throughout the store. Participating influencers include @PrestonPlayz, @BriannaPlayz and @RussoPlays, each contributing their own take on thrift-inspired digital fashion.“Gen Z doesn’t think in terms of storefronts and shopping carts,” said Tim McCracken, SVP, Creative and AI at BarkleyOKRP. “They discover through play, culture, and exploration. Roblox gave us the opportunity to rethink what a thrift store could be in a digital-first world—one built around interaction and discovery while staying true to The Salvation Army’s mission.”The Gang, an international studio known for building immersive brand experiences inside platforms like Roblox, led the development of the digital environment, bringing the look, feel, and flow of a Salvation Army Thrift Store into a dynamic digital space designed specifically for Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences.

“Personalisation is such a core part of the Roblox experience for its huge player base, which is why we loved the idea of bringing a thrift store to the platform with Salvation Army and BarkleyOKRP,” said Max Proctor, CEO at The Gang. “We hope to bring some of the surprise and delight that people get while thrifting IRL to Roblox's huge audience.”Just like in the real world, proceeds from “Thrift Score” support The Salvation Army’s mission to help people in need through rehabilitation, recovery, and community programs nationwide.The Salvation Army’s digital thrift store is now live on Roblox. Players can search for “Thrift Score” at  salvationarmythriftscore.com to explore the experience.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The Salvation Army is launching "Thrift Score," the world's first digital thrift store on Roblox, to introduce its thrift brand to a new generation by offering virtual thrift-inspired items for in-game avatars.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Gen Z and Gen Alpha, who shop, play, and express themselves on platforms like Roblox.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A fully explorable and shoppable digital thrift store experience on the Roblox platform, featuring creator and brand collaborations, and player donations of digital items.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand awareness and engage a new generation (Gen Z and Gen Alpha) with The Salvation Army's thrift offering, reaching those who might not have experienced a physical store.

content.description:
when ai action figures flooded our feeds, most people saw a trend.invisible animals saw an opportunity.

just another figure turns a viral ai format into a mirror — reflecting a reality most people never see: calves confined in cages from birth, unable to move, play, or live freely.

 .

For the Audience -Channeling Anna, For the Audience - Spo...

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Invisible Animals' "just another figure" subverts the viral AI action figure trend, transforming it into a poignant mirror. This campaign exposes the unseen reality of calves confined in cages from birth, unable to move or live freely.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Digitally-native social media users who engage with viral trends, particularly those unaware of industrial animal farming practices, and individuals receptive to animal welfare advocacy.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Creation and distribution of AI-generated content mirroring the viral action figure trend, deployed across social media platforms where such trends originate and spread to capture attention.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness about the hidden reality of calf confinement in industrial farming and spark public discourse on animal welfare.

content.description:
America doesn’t just have a wealth gap—we have a health gap. It’s why rich people live longer. They have healthcare that comes to them, specialists on call, and preventative care before they need it.

Why doesn’t everybody get this kind of care?

Through Hims & Hers, you can. With everything from diagnostic testing to help you get ahead of health concerns before symptoms appear, to early cancer detection testing, to customized treatments for weight loss, hair loss, menopause, low testosterone, and more.

The same science. The same access. All designed just for you.

Get the healthcare you deserve at hims.com

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign exposes the 'health gap' where wealth dictates health outcomes and positions Hims & Hers as the accessible solution to democratize comprehensive, personalized healthcare for everyone.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals aged 25-55 who are concerned about healthcare access and quality, feel underserved by traditional systems, and are actively seeking personalized solutions for preventative care, early detection, and specific conditions like weight loss, hair loss, menopause, or low testosterone.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film distributed across digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media, streaming services) driving traffic directly to hims.com for service enrollment and purchase.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive awareness and adoption of Hims & Hers' broad range of healthcare services by positioning them as an accessible alternative to premium care traditionally available only to the wealthy, ultimately increasing sales and customer acquisition.

content.description:
On Valentine’s Day, nothing gets Natural Light Beer fans motor’s running like some gas-powered grass-cutting machinery.

To prove that lawn care doesn’t have to clock out at dusk on the most romantic day of the year, Natty is dropping a frisky set of intimate apparel for its fans’ true love of lawn care…introducing Natural Light Lawngerie. Lawngerie is a lacy, silky protective cover to keep lawn care equipment looking extra sexy and ensure your equipment maintains its cutting edge. Check it out in this video HERE.To enter for a chance to win the only Lawngerie set in the world and a brand new lawn tractor, 21+ fans can follow Natural Light on Facebook, Instagram or X and comment on designated social posts using #NattyLawngerie and #sweepstakes from February 10-14.

As the favorite beer brand of grass whisperers and garage enthusiasts, this isn’t the first time Natty has celebrated its fans’ passion for lawn care. In January, the brand debuted the Treadmower - a first-of-its-kind turfed treadmill engineered to simulate the simple joy of mowing the lawn when the winter blues hit - and in November, Natural Light launched Garageflage with a ‘Lawn Warrior’ design for true soldiers of the sod. The Garageflage collection featured a head-to-toe camo pattern of the garage piled with push mowers so fans could hide out for the holidays with their favorite beer.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Natural Light launched 'Lawngerie,' a humorous line of intimate apparel designed as protective covers for lawn care equipment, allowing fans to celebrate their passion for lawn care on Valentine's Day.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Existing fans of Natural Light Beer aged 21+, particularly those who enjoy humorous, irreverent campaigns and have an affinity for lawn care.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Integrated campaign featuring a social media sweepstakes across Facebook, Instagram, and X, requiring followers to comment on designated posts with specific hashtags, supported by a promotional video showcasing the unique 'Lawngerie' product, with a grand prize of the Lawngerie set and a lawn tractor.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate brand engagement and awareness among Natural Light Beer's 21+ fan base through a humorous Valentine's Day-themed social media sweepstakes.

metadata.title: The Power To Quit Is In Your Hands
metadata.brand: Department for Health and Social Care

content.description:
AMV BBDO and the Department for Health and Social Care (DHSC) have today launched a new national campaign, The Power To Quit Is In Your Hands, to promote the NHS Quit Smoking app and Personal Quit Plan, highlighting that help to quit is never far away.Built on the insight that smokers are three times more likely to quit successfully when using proven tools and support, the campaign aims to give smokers a sense of hope, empowering them to quit successfully.Moving away from a traditional focus on the harms of smoking, the creative uses humour and hyperbole to bring the power of support to life, positioning the NHS digital quitting tools as catalysts for change.The hero film follows a man eating lunch in his work canteen who is invited out for a cigarette by colleagues. Tempted, he appears ready to join them, when his phone suddenly springs to life in his pocket. A powerful light beam bursts out of the phone, drawing him up and onto the table. He looks on in awe as he realises it’s his NHS Quit Smoking app, reminding him he is on day 11 of his quit journey — just enough to help him say no.The campaign will run across TV, online video, social media, radio, digital audio and out-of-home nationwide.

 

Agency: AMV BBDOCreative President: Owen LeeHead of Strategy: Laura PirkisCreative Director: Kris WheaterCreative: Simon Mannion/Clint HardingManaging Partner: Emily WhiteawayBusiness Director: Kirsten BarnesSenior Account Director: Marianne RobertsSenior Account Manager: Hugh O'SullivanSenior Producer: Vanessa HuntProduction Assistant: Josh OyediwuraBusiness Affairs: Nick RentonStrategy Director: Samuel JacksonDesign Director: Laura NunnInhouse editing: Charlie CoombsProduction Company: Magna StudioDirector: Arthur StudholmeExecutive Producer: Joel SpencerProducer: Drew O’NeillProduction Manager: Rhian GwenlanDirector of Photography: Simon RichardsProduction Designer: Sarah JennesonCasting: Beast CastingLocation Manager: Jamie Jamieson1st AD: Julian RichardsSound Recordist: Stephen HodgeGaffer: Marco Di GiulioStunt Coordinator: Matt SherrenCostume Designer; Florence JonesMair and Makeup: Eve ColesEditor: Nick Armstrong at TenThreeEdit Producer: Ed HoadleyPost-production: No8Post- production producer: Robson YeoVFX artists: Rob Peacock and Toby O’ConnellColorist: Matt TurnerAudio post-production: No8Audio producer: Karen NodenSound designer: Ben GulvinPhotographer: Gary SalterPhotographer Producer: Paul Cowen at Horton- StephensRetoucher: Karl HugillMedia planning/buying: Wavemaker/OMD

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses humour and hyperbole to demonstrate how NHS digital quitting tools empower smokers by positioning them as catalysts for successful change.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Smokers who are considering quitting or are open to using proven tools and support to overcome their habit.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film launched nationally on film/video platforms, likely including TV and digital channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To promote the NHS Quit Smoking app and Personal Quit Plan, empowering smokers with a sense of hope and highlighting accessible support for quitting.

content.description:
A pioneer in offering a global account with balances in U.S. dollars and euros, C6 Bank is launching its first advertising campaign of 2026 to highlight the benefits of its C6 Global Account for both individuals and businesses. Created by Tech & Soul and produced by O2 Filmes, the film “Portals” features Gisele Bündchen moving through portals that evoke iconic landscapes around the world, symbolizing the possibilities enabled by the international account, which allows dollar and euro transfers as easily as a Pix. The campaign reinforces the brand’s “For an Extraordinary Life” positioning and its commitment to democratizing a premium banking experience, while also spotlighting C6 Global Invest as part of its international solutions portfolio. The commercial will run across free-to-air and pay TV, radio, digital platforms, OOH, and websites.

 

Advertising Agency: Tech & SoulProduction Company: O2 FilmesChief Creative Officers: Flavio Waiteman, Angelica Pernau, Lusa SilvestreArt Direction: Angelica Pernau, Rodrigo Seixas, Sibeli SilveiraCopywriter: Lusa SilvestrePhotographer: Gui Paganini

Kaleidoscope, Satisfying, More than you think

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign features Gisele Bündchen moving through portals to symbolize the global financial possibilities and ease of international transactions offered by C6 Global Account, making transfers as simple as a Pix.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and businesses seeking easy-to-use global banking solutions with U.S. dollar and euro balances, aspiring to a premium banking experience for an extraordinary life.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film/commercial titled 'Portals' featuring Gisele Bündchen, produced by O2 Filmes, will run across free-to-air television and other channels consistent with a 360° medium approach.
ai_enrichment.objective: To highlight the benefits of the C6 Global Account and C6 Global Invest, reinforce C6 Bank's 'For an Extraordinary Life' positioning, and democratize a premium international banking experience for both individuals and businesses.

metadata.title: Ads are coming to AI. But not to Claude

content.description: This year’s Super Clio winner for the most creative commercial in the Big Game—bestowed by a jury of industry experts—mixed abs and ads to impressive effect. Mother delivered the flex for Anthropic’s Claude AI.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Claude AI distinguishes itself as an ad-free artificial intelligence through a creative film, aired during the Super Bowl, which cleverly blends themes of 'abs' and 'ads'.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: A broad general audience watching the Super Bowl, with a specific focus on users of AI or potential AI adopters who value a clean, ad-free user experience.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero commercial film broadcast on national television during the 'Big Game' (Super Bowl), recognized by industry experts with a Super Clio award for its creativity.
ai_enrichment.objective: To establish strong brand awareness for Claude AI and clearly differentiate it as a premium, ad-free AI service against competitors.

content.description:
In a scenario where images are posted, consumed, and forgotten within hours on social media, Canon presents the campaign “Stories expire, stories don’t”, created by Dentsu Creative. The communication invites reflection on the value of memories and the revival of printed photography as a way to preserve them — a context in which the brand introduces its new portable printers, the Selphy QX20 and Selphy CP1500.

 

Advertiser: Canon do BrasilProduct: Selphy QX20 and CP1500 PrintersAgency: Dentsu Creative, Sao Paulo, BrazilChief Creative Officer: Ricardo DollaCreative Director: Ricardo DinizArt Direction: Amanda SantosCopywriting: Camila RodriguesContent: Viviane Teves Frohlich / Gabriella Cunha / Natalia ChistiProduction: Juliana D’Antino de Carvalho / João Pedro Garcia Muniz / Luis Fernando de Carvalho / Ricardo LopezProject Management: Josi CartapattiBusiness: Bruno Panico / Denis VedanMedia: Viviana Maurman / Carol Nunes / Edgard TavaresApproved by: Leo Yamakawa / Daniela MayetaImage Production: Dentsu TeamSound Production: Canja

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Canon champions the lasting value of printed photographs over fleeting digital posts, inviting consumers to preserve personal stories using their new portable Selphy printers.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Social media users who value personal memories and seek tangible, easy-to-create keepsakes, particularly those interested in portable printing solutions.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 'Film' medium campaign designed to provoke reflection on the ephemerality of digital content versus the permanence of printed photos, distributed to introduce the new Selphy QX20 and Selphy CP1500 printers.
ai_enrichment.objective: To introduce and drive sales for Canon's new Selphy QX20 and Selphy CP1500 portable printers by reigniting appreciation for printed memories.

metadata.title: Chinese New Year - Glad I Met You

content.description:
A girl, a talking dog, and a meticulously thought out scheme make up this heartwarming tale about finding family. Apple joins forces with director Bai Xue for an imaginative blend of cinematography and stop-motion to usher in Chinese New Year.

 

Introducing iPhone 13 Pro, Introducing the all-new iPad m...

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign is a heartwarming Chinese New Year film by Apple, directed by Bai Xue, that uses a unique blend of cinematography and stop-motion to tell a tale of finding family through a girl and a talking dog. It subtly introduces the new iPhone 13 Pro and iPad products within the narrative.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Consumers celebrating Chinese New Year, including families and individuals, who are potential buyers of new technology like the iPhone 13 Pro and iPad, and appreciate culturally relevant, heartwarming stories.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film, blending live-action cinematography and stop-motion, disseminated digitally (e.g., YouTube, social media) and potentially broadcast television, integrating product showcases for iPhone 13 Pro and iPad.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand affinity and cultural relevance for Apple during Chinese New Year, while driving awareness and interest for the newly introduced iPhone 13 Pro and iPad.

content.description:
Toyota EVs are ready to take center stage, but there’s one key problem – Asian American consumers are still worried about long-term EV value and quality. We needed to get doubters to recognize that Toyota designs EVs with a human-first philosophy and are a great fit for their needs.

Asian cinema is not just a trend —it’s at the forefront of culture. Bold, imaginative, and resonant, it speaks universally while staying rooted in the unique and reflecting deeper cultural truths. So we tapped into the emotional power of Asian storytelling to show how even the biggest BEV critics are won over by the Toyota BEV experience.

Toyota launches a collection of short films, starring Toyota’s all-electric vehicle, the bZ. We partnered with the Asian film directors to create three 15-second films that tap into rising Asian cinema from melodramatic K-drama to intense Hong Kong action films to celebratory Indian Bollywood.

 

Advertising Agency: Intertrend CommunicationsProduction Company: Lawrence Fells ProductionsPR Agency: Meehan Business AdvisersIntertrend CommunicationExecutive Strategy & Creative Director: Matthew ChoySVP, Managing Director: Rita ChengVP, Account Management: Joe MinExecutive Account Director: Alyssa HuAccount Director: Mike ChouAccount Supervisor: Monique EsquilloAccount Executive: Violet HuynhCreative Director: Harim KimExecutive Producer: Jeanette KungStrategy Director: Pallavi ReddyAssociate Strategy Director: Esther LeeSr. Strategic Planner: Philip KimExecutive Media Director: Cheryl LeeMedia Director: Sophia HoMedia Manager: Tiffany KhuuSr. Media Planner: Jin ChoiMedia Planner: Lalain RizviToyota Motor North AmericaGroup Vice President, Marketing: Michael TrippVice President: Dedra DeLilliGeneral Manager: Owen PeacockManager, bZ: Chad MooreSr. Planner, bZ: Cody EmmertMedia Manager: Lorenzo HarrisSr. Planner, Media: Andrew George

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign leverages the emotional power of Asian storytelling through a collection of three 15-second short films, directed by Asian filmmakers, to demonstrate the human-first design and appeal of Toyota's bZ electric vehicle.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Asian American consumers who are skeptical or worried about the long-term value, quality, and suitability of Electric Vehicles (EVs).
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Production and distribution of three 15-second short films, starring the Toyota bZ, created in partnership with Asian film directors.
ai_enrichment.objective: To get skeptical Asian American consumers to recognize Toyota's EVs, specifically the bZ, as a great fit for their needs by highlighting their human-first design philosophy and addressing concerns about long-term value and quality.

content.description:
This Diwali, we celebrate those who bring light long before the diyas are lit.  The ones who work behind the scenes to make every celebration brighter, safer, and full of life.  Here’s to the hands that make every festival shine.✨

This Diwali, we celebrate those who bring light long before the diyas are lit.  The ones who work behind the scenes to make every celebration brighter, safer, and full of life.  Here’s to the hands that make every festival shine.✨

Mitron Ki Diwali is a heartwarming festive film by Torrent Electricals that shifts the spotlight from celebrations to the people who make them possible, especially electricians who work tirelessly behind the scenes. The film beautifully captures how these unsung heroes ensure homes are safely lit and ready for Diwali, even if they sacrifice their own time with loved ones. With an emotional and relatable narrative, it reinforces the idea that the true glow of Diwali comes not just from lights, but from the hands that power them.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A heartwarming Diwali film that shifts focus from the celebrations to the unsung heroes, particularly electricians, who work tirelessly behind the scenes to ensure safe and brightly lit festivities for everyone.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Indian consumers and families celebrating Diwali, who appreciate the efforts of essential service providers and value safety and reliability during festive seasons.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film distributed across digital platforms such as YouTube and social media, potentially complemented by television broadcast during the Diwali festive period.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand image and foster an emotional connection with consumers by celebrating the essential service providers who ensure safe and bright Diwali celebrations.

content.description:
Viktor Yushchenko’s honey brand “Dogoda” has unveiled a new packaging line crafted in Ukrainian ethnic style.

Ukraine’s third President personally put forward the inspiring idea of creating an authentic design imbued with Ukrainian history, traditions, and ethnic character.

The creative agency Brain Tank took on the challenge of bringing this unique project to life. Drawing inspiration from ancient ornaments and engravings – which became the foundation of the visual identity – they crafted a distinctive ethno-design that went on to define the packaging developed by the agency, spanning jars, boxes, and tubes all the way to stickers and gift bags.

One of the key artistic highlights, for example, was the incorporation of Petrykivka painting motifs, which formed the basis for a portion of the packaging designs. The characteristic floral and botanical patterns brought vitality to the visual range, complementing the restrained style of the engravings and underscoring the richness of Ukraine’s cultural heritage.

The process of modernizing and adapting ethnic style for projects like this is an extraordinarily painstaking undertaking in its own right. Preserving that very authenticity demands that designers engage in thoughtful, extensive study of traditional art – its logic, its rules, and its defining characteristics.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Dogoda's new packaging line features a unique Ukrainian ethno-design, inspired by former President Viktor Yushchenko, drawing from ancient ornaments and traditional motifs like Petrykivka painting to embody Ukrainian history and character.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Consumers who appreciate authentic Ukrainian culture, history, and traditions, likely seeking premium food products with a strong national identity.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Development and implementation of a distinctive ethno-design across product packaging including jars, boxes, tubes, stickers, and gift bags, leveraging the Print medium.
ai_enrichment.objective: To establish a strong brand identity and cultural connection for Dogoda honey through a unique and authentic packaging design.

metadata.title: Experience your day-to-day in Britânia mode

content.description:
Experience your day-to-day in Britânia mode

To stand out in a competitive Air Fryer market dominated by celebrity endorsements and technical specs, Britânia shifted the focus to the consumer's reality. Addressing the critical lack of free time in Brazilian routines, the concept "Experience your day-to-day in Britânia mode" repositioned the product from a simple appliance to a lifestyle facilitator.

Instead of celebrities, the 360° campaign featured real people in authentic family moments, humanizing the technology to build a genuine emotional connection. The strategy delivered massive results: sales grew 83% (vs. a 40% goal), market share increased by 4 p.p., and Britânia consolidated its position as the #2 Top of Mind brand in the segment.

 

Agency: Calia

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The core creative idea repositioned Britânia Air Fryers from a simple appliance to a lifestyle facilitator, emphasizing how it saves time and enhances consumers' daily routines.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Busy Brazilian consumers, particularly individuals and families, who lack free time and seek practical solutions to simplify their daily routines.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 360° campaign prominently featuring film (likely TV and digital video) that showcased real people in authentic family moments, eschewing celebrity endorsements.
ai_enrichment.objective: To significantly increase sales and market share for Britânia Air Fryers, and to elevate the brand's top-of-mind status in the competitive electronic devices market.

content.description:
Valentine’s Day is all about the celebration of love, but not only the candle-lit, hand-holding, couples-only kind; it’s also for groups of friends, too. Clash of Clans is “clashifying” familiar romantic comedy tropes into heartfelt Clash bromances.

Groups of friends who clan together can drift apart. Life happens, schedules misalign and those who once clashed together leave their clan hanging. The campaign film, created by DAVID New York, shows these players reuniting by reimagining famous romantic scenes. In one scene, a group of friends stands outside a friend's window with a phone playing iconic sounds from the game, calling out to their bro and pleading with him to come back to the clan. In another, two friends surprise a friend at his door, holding up signs inviting him to return to playing the game together. The next sequence shows in-game characters embodying that same spirit of bromance, by putting aside their rivalries to help each other compete in Clan Wars.

“Rom-coms have always shown us how powerful emotional storytelling can be. For this campaign, we reimagined those classic tropes to celebrate friendship and connection in a way that feels playful, heartfelt, and true to the world of Clash of Clans,” says Andre Toledo, Chief Creative Officer at DAVID New York.

The campaign airs on February 1, 2026.  Production was handled by Gravy Films, with Crobin Leo as the director.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Clash of Clans 'clashifies' familiar romantic comedy tropes to create heartfelt bromances, celebrating platonic friendships among players and encouraging them to reunite their inactive clans, especially for Valentine's Day.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Existing Clash of Clans players who have become inactive, and their active friends or clan members who wish to encourage their return, likely spanning teenagers to adults.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film created by DAVID New York, distributed via digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media) and potentially TV, reimagining classic romantic scenes with a gaming-themed 'bromance' twist.
ai_enrichment.objective: To re-engage inactive Clash of Clans players, foster community engagement, and strengthen the bonds within existing clans by celebrating the theme of friendship and reunion.

content.description: “The Honest Keys”: (“Ärliga Tangenter” in Swedish) functions as an add-on keyboard that users download via App Store and activate in their iOS settings – just like an emoji or language keyboard. It acts by detecting messages that signal poor mental health in the user, such as “I’m fine,” “It’s all good,” or “Everything’s okay”, which prompt the app’s autosuggest feature to react. It then suggests alternatives for more honest responses, such as “I’m actually not doing that well” or “It’s been a bit tough lately.” Created by suicide prevention charity Suicide Zero, the app makes it easier to take that first step of being honest and asking for help.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: An iOS keyboard app called "The Honest Keys" detects common phrases used to mask poor mental health, suggesting alternative, more honest responses to help users articulate their true feelings and seek support.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: iOS users who are experiencing poor mental health and struggle with expressing their feelings or asking for help.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Development and distribution of an integrated iOS add-on keyboard application, downloadable via the App Store.
ai_enrichment.objective: To encourage honest communication about mental health struggles and facilitate the initial step of seeking help, thereby contributing to suicide prevention.

content.description:
Many people associate cancer primarily with older age. But when it comes to testicular cancer, the reality is different. The group most affected is young men between the ages of 15 and 35. Ironically, it’s also the demographic that avoids preventative check-ups most often. This is where “Balls for Balls” comes in.“Testicular cancer is highly treatable when detected early, yet many young men avoid check-ups because of embarrassment or the belief that it won’t affect them.” comments Dr. med. Niklas Kreutzer, Deputy Clinic Director and Senior Consultant, Klinikum Dortmund. “Initiatives like this help remove psychological barriers and turn prevention into something accessible and normal.”Together with FAQ YOU - the youth-facing health brand by F/A/Q Health - and Borussia Dortmund, Saint Elmo’s developed a campaign that connects this sensitive topic with the reach, emotional impact, and cultural relevance of football – offering young men a clear incentive to get their testicles checked – footballs touched by their favourite team’s players.A literal ‘ball exchange’On matchday 22 of the Bundesliga, at Signal Iduna Park, fans attending BVB’s game against 1. FSV Mainz 05 were invited to undergo a free urological exam right at the stadium. Their reward: a real ball – an official PUMA football touched and autographed by BVB players. A literal ‘ball exchange’ that turned prevention into participation.Visually, Saint Elmo’s translated the campaign into a striking black-and-yellow world, bringing together BVB’s colours and FAQ YOU’s unique typography. Combining the ‘b’ and ‘a’, the ‘Balls for Balls’ logo itself was designed to mimic the right action of a testicular self-exam. The storytelling relied on minimalistic animations and graphic elements derived from the Balls for Balls logo.“We wanted to bring a topic no one talks about into a space where millions of men are listening. Football is the perfect lever for that,” adds Matthias Harbeck, CCO at Saint Elmo’s Group. “‘Balls for Balls’ works because the idea is simple, visually striking, and gets men to act.”“Health communication needs courage, honesty, and strong allies,” addsDaniel Nagel, CEO of F/A/Q Health. “With ‘Balls for Balls’, F/A/Q Health is proud to partner with Borussia Dortmund who have shown real commitment by lending their voices, faces, and time to encourage men to take their health seriously.”Launch on World Cancer Day, 4th FebruaryThe campaign launched publicly on World Cancer Day (February 4) with a short film and a centralized digital hub ballsforballs.de, which houses medically reviewed information, and FAQs tailored for young men. Watching an animated self-exam video right till the end unlocks a team-autographed certificate for fans on the website. This ensures that those who cannot be present at the stadium can still take part and get an incentive for checking themselves. 1000+ personalised certificates have been generated and downloaded so far.On social media, FAQ YOU (by F/A/Q Health) and Borussia Dortmund engaged fans in the lead-up and during the match, creators and influencers amplified the call to action with original content, behind-the-scenes footage, and teaser clips from the film.Matchday: Stadium Check-Ups and National AttentionDuring BVB’s home game against Mainz, the activation featured four check-up stations staffed by 8 urologists. Three tents were placed inside the stadium while one was placed outside for those who did not have a match ticket. Fans could get examined on-site, then pick up their signed PUMA ball at the BVB store after the game – making it one of the most accessible cancer preventive screening activations of its kind.Hundreds of fans had their testicles examined. Long queues formed in front of the tents. "At first, I wasn't sure how many men would actually come. Unfortunately, the topic is still too often associated with shame. I was surprised and delighted by how well the fans responded to the initiative," says Dr. Sebastian Homann, Senior Physician at the Urology Clinic of Dortmund Hospital.A BVB jersey belonging to Maximilian Beier, featuring special flocking, was also part of the campaign. The ‘B’ in Beier’s name was replaced with a hand that looks like a lowercase ‘b’. This made it look like a hand touching the word ‘EIER’ (meaning testicles in German). This jersey was autographed by Maxi Beier and was turned into a giveaway on social media.A giant, centre circle flag promoting the initiative was unfurled before kickoff for live television audience. The corner flags in the game also displayed the URL, ballsforballs.de. Balls for Balls took over LED Perimeter boards around the pitch and large screens in the stadium - making the message unmissable, even during gameplay. Urologist interviews on live TV further encouraged men watching at home to check themselves.Marius Happe, Head of Marketing, Borussia Dortmund, comments: “Borussia Dortmund has always stood for responsibility beyond football. With ‘Balls for Balls’, we are proud to support an initiative that uses the power of our club to encourage young men to take their health seriously. We value this partnership with FAQ YOU (by F/A/Q Health), because it turns awareness into real action.”

 

Agencies involved: Saint Elmo's Munich, Serviceplan PR & ContentFilm Production Company: Modern Minds, Tobias SchubertEditing Company: ASTRAL Kreativ GmbH, Johannes AuffermannRecording Studio: Hastings Audio Network, Oscar MeixnerSound Design / Music Company: HOOKED Music, Jan Finck Barboza

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The 'Balls for Balls' campaign leverages the influence of Borussia Dortmund to destigmatize testicular cancer and encourage preventative check-ups among young men, making health screening accessible and normal.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young men aged 15-35, particularly those who typically avoid preventative health check-ups due to embarrassment or perceived invulnerability.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: An integrated campaign, utilizing the brand power and reach of Borussia Dortmund in collaboration with youth-facing health brand FAQ YOU, to create accessible and normalized health messaging.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase early detection of testicular cancer by motivating young men aged 15-35 to overcome psychological barriers and engage in preventative check-ups.

content.description:
Fueled by the insight that most early-stage investors are paralyzed by the myth that investing requires perfection, the campaign pivots from traditional financial marketing focused on “saving” towards “momentum,” inviting investors to turn their hesitation into action.

The :60 hero, contrasts smart choices - like opening a GoSmart account and starting to trade in minutes, investing with as little as $10, and taking advantage of commission-free trading - with less-than-smart moments like getting a tattoo without checking the spelling or trying to impress your new GF’s dad with your hidden talent.

Developed in partnership with creative agency Battery, the launch marks a deliberate shift in how RBC shows up for a new generation, meeting them where they are, both culturally and financially.

It’s a statement that “smart” investing is just about getting started. Rather than treating investing as a complicated financial exercise, the campaign presents GoSmart as something Canadians can feel good about, backed by the trust and scale of Canada’s largest bank.

The campaign made its national debut during the Canadian broadcast of the Super Bowl before rolling out across TV, Connected TV, Digital, Social, and OOH.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign challenges the myth that investing requires perfection, encouraging early-stage investors to overcome hesitation by highlighting the ease and smartness of taking small, immediate steps with RBC Go Smart, contrasting it with relatable, less-than-smart everyday actions.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Early-stage investors from a new generation who are paralyzed by the perceived need for perfection in investing.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A :60 hero film developed in partnership with creative agency Battery, serving as the core creative medium for the campaign launch.
ai_enrichment.objective: To prompt early-stage investors to turn their hesitation into action by opening a GoSmart account and starting to trade.

content.description:
After teasing the identity of Sabrina Carpenter’s mystery lover, Pringles’ Super Bowl spot reveals he is both stackable and snackable. Enter ‘Pringleleo’, the man of her dreams, constructed from crisps after Sabrina declares she needs more than boys. Guided by Mr. P’s whispered advice to “build him”, she embarks on a whirlwind 30-second romance with the edible creation.

Set to Fred Buscaglione’s ‘Guarda che luna’, the honeymoon fantasy unfolds through road trips, duvet snuggles and playful intimacy, before ‘Pringleleo’ meets a crunchy end at the hands of Sabrina’s fans. The campaign marks Pringles’ ninth consecutive Super Bowl appearance and revives the iconic ‘Once You Pop’ slogan on the Big Game stage.

 

Mind Popping - You Were Made For Pringles

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Sabrina Carpenter creates her ideal man, 'Pringleleo', from Pringles crisps, leading to a romantic fantasy that ultimately sees him devoured by her fans in a Super Bowl ad.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General snack consumers, pop culture enthusiasts, and Super Bowl viewers, particularly fans of Sabrina Carpenter, likely aged 16-45.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film broadcast during the Super Bowl, supported by a preceding teasing campaign to build anticipation for Sabrina Carpenter's mystery lover.
ai_enrichment.objective: Enhance brand awareness and reignite engagement with Pringles' iconic 'Once You Pop' slogan among a broad audience during the Super Bowl.

content.description:
From simulation to real track. “El Gran Break” turned a break into a race, blending physical activation and TikTok participation to send two fans all the way to the F1 Grand Prix in Mexico.

From simulation to real track. “El Gran Break” turned a break into a race, blending physical activation and TikTok participation to send two fans all the way to the F1 Grand Prix in Mexico.

For its latest campaign, KitKat Ecuador, together with Publicis Groupe, launched “El Gran Break”, a phygital experience that merged BTL activation with a digital challenge inspired by the world of Formula 1.

The idea invited people to take a break like never before by stepping into the role of a racing driver. Through a branded physical setup that simulated the interior of a race car, participants recorded their own high-energy videos as if they were competing on track. The experience was designed to be mobile-first and seamlessly connected to TikTok, where users could easily upload their content using the campaign’s official audio.

That audio became a key asset: the narration was delivered by none other than the official voice of ESPN’s Formula 1 broadcast, bringing authenticity, intensity, and real racing atmosphere to every single video. This detail elevated the experience from a simple simulation to something that felt genuinely connected to the sport.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: KitKat's 'El Gran Break' transformed the idea of a break into an exhilarating Formula 1 race experience, blending physical race car simulations with a TikTok digital challenge to send fans to the F1 Grand Prix.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young adults and Formula 1 enthusiasts in Ecuador, likely Gen Z and Millennials, who enjoy interactive phygital experiences and social media challenges.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Physical BTL activations featuring branded race car simulators for immersive participation, complemented by a digital challenge leveraging TikTok for wider engagement and content creation.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive consumer engagement, enhance brand association with excitement and unique breaks, and reward participants with a premium experience (F1 Grand Prix trip).

metadata.title: “Broken Souls” The Invisible Wounds of the First World War.
metadata.brand: The Museum of the Great War – Pays de Meaux

content.description:
The Museum of the Great War – Pays de Meaux

“Broken Souls” The Invisible Wounds of the First World War.

Between 1914 and long after the guns fell silent, thousands of soldiers suffered from psychological trauma. In the absence of medical understanding, many were accused of cowardice, institutionalised, or subjected to brutal treatments. A century later, their invisible wounds finally have a name: post-traumatic stress disorder.

BROKEN SOULS, created by The museum of the Great War and BBDO Paris.Echoing the recently opened, life-size immersive trench at the Meaux site, this campaign invites audiences to grasp the intimate and psychological reality of soldiers’ experiences.By confronting past and present, it reminds us of a simple truth: when war ends on the battlefield, it does not end in the minds of those who fought it. To embody these “broken souls,” the campaign introduces a series of posters rooted in a deliberately slow, physical, and experimental process. These images are not generated.  They are altered, dissolved, weathered. Just like the men they portray.

“Just as the soldiers of the First World War endured their own ordeal, we wanted these posters to undergo a trial of their own. There was an element of unpredictability in the installations and in the final visual outcomes.”  Julien Beuvry, Art Director BBDO Paris.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The 'Broken Souls' campaign by The Museum of the Great War highlights the invisible psychological wounds of WWI soldiers, specifically PTSD, connecting historical suffering with contemporary understanding to reveal war's enduring impact beyond the battlefield.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals interested in military history (especially WWI), mental health awareness, museum visitors, and the general public seeking a deeper understanding of the human cost of war.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Integrated campaign, centered around a life-size immersive trench experience at the Museum of the Great War – Pays de Meaux, supported by broader communications to invite audiences to engage with the theme.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase public understanding and awareness of the psychological reality and lasting trauma suffered by WWI soldiers, fostering empathy and educating about the long-term human impact of conflict.

To celebrate the Year of the Horse, award-winning animator Nina Gantz created a film narrated by Dior ambassador 070 Shake.

 

Dior Forever - The Iconic Clean Foundation

XIN LIU and the 'Tribales Rose des Vents'
