content.description:
For the pan-European launch of the all-new Mitsubishi Grandis, Mitsubishi Motors Europe introduces a campaign rooted in real family life. No staged perfection. No exaggerated adventures. No picture-perfect clichés. Just everyday moments.

From a first school day to a spontaneous weekend or a chaotic morning rush, the campaign captures recognisable family stories where the Grandis plays a natural, supportive role. Designed to grow with you, this versatile SUV is built to handle whatever the day brings.

Created by Mad. Avenue in close collaboration with Mitsubishi Motors Europe and produced by Mayster, the modular campaign combines emotional storytelling with strategic flexibility. Seamlessly adaptable across more than 20 markets, it delivers one clear European message: the Mitsubishi Grandis is ready for all of life’s days, exactly as they are.

  Avenue.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign positions the all-new Mitsubishi Grandis as an integral and supportive part of real, everyday family life, shunning staged perfection for relatable moments. It highlights the SUV's versatility in handling the natural ups and downs of family routines.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Families, particularly parents, across Europe who value practicality, versatility, and reliability in their daily lives, seeking a vehicle that seamlessly integrates into their genuine family dynamic.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A modular film campaign developed for pan-European distribution across more than 20 markets, likely utilizing TV and various digital video platforms to deliver consistent emotional storytelling.
ai_enrichment.objective: To launch the all-new Mitsubishi Grandis across Europe, building awareness and establishing its identity as a versatile, family-integrated SUV.

content.description:
Today I come to tell all of you that being part of the team is not just about the starting 11, it's the attitude of an entire country that doesn't just watch from the sidelines.  The national team's jersey is worn by those who open their business before dawn, who cross the city from end to end because they know there are plays where you can't be late. And by those who know that the most important matches are often decided in extra time.  For what's coming, I want all Panamanians ready. I want the more than 4 million of you as part of the team. I want you to live it as starters, playing for the country.  You keep giving it your all on the field, because BAC takes care of the bench.  Let's go, let's go. BAC. Official bank of the national team. Let's go, let's go, Panama.

Today I come to tell all of you that being part of the team is not just about the starting 11, it's the attitude of an entire country that doesn't just watch from the sidelines.  The national team's jersey is worn by those who open their business before dawn, who cross the city from end to end because they know there are plays where you can't be late. And by those who know that the most important matches are often decided in extra time.  For what's coming, I want all Panamanians ready. I want the more than 4 million of you as part of the team. I want you to live it as starters, playing for the country.  You keep giving it your all on the field, because BAC takes care of the bench.  Let's go, let's go. BAC. Official bank of the national team. Let's go, let's go, Panama.

The 90 minutes that a match lasts, the protagonists are the 11 starters on the field. But for the rest of the time, there are other starters who sweat the jersey daily: all Panamanians.

For BAC, being the official bank of the national team meant going beyond sponsorship. For this, we created a 360 campaign that connects the bank's role in the cultural and emotional field with the effort of millions in their day-to-day lives.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign equates every hardworking Panamanian to a 'starter' on the national team, highlighting their daily contributions to the country's success, while positioning BAC as the essential supportive 'bench' that enables their efforts and ambitions.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Working adults, entrepreneurs, and small business owners across Panama, particularly those who identify with national pride and a strong work ethic, aged 25-60.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Given '360°' medium, likely includes a hero film across TV and digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media), radio spots, OOH advertising, and in-branch promotions, all leveraging BAC's official sponsorship of the national team.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build strong brand affinity and loyalty by emotionally connecting BAC with Panamanian national pride, hard work, and the spirit of individual contribution, thereby increasing consideration for BAC's financial services.

content.description:
This quirky heist film flips the script on the classic “first come, first serve” trope. Set in a chaotic, darkly comic universe, the story follows Prakash—a short, timid local thug often overlooked by other thugs. During a high-stakes mass robbery, a diverse crew of seasoned criminals, each more intimidating than the last, scrambles to steal a mysterious and powerful Book of Secrets. But Prakash arrives late to the scene! delayed not by fear, but by a craving—he stopped for a McSaver meal from McDonald’s. As the heist spirals into, betrayals, and backstabbing, it’s Prakash—the underdog—who ends up outsmarting them all and walking away with the prize. With a clever twist of fate (and fries), the film cheekily reminds us that sometimes, reaching late can be far more rewarding than rushing in.

 

Advertising Agency: DDB, Mumbai, IndiaChief Creative Officer: Rahul MathewGroup Creative Director: Sourabh Dubey

Production House: Ducktape Studios, Mumbai, IndiaDirector: Raylin VallesExecutive Producer: Apratim BhatnagarProducer: Ash ChawdaDA: Kunal Jadhav and Tiya Padhi

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A quirky heist film featuring an underdog thug who, delayed by a McDonald's McSaver meal craving, inadvertently outsmarts a crew of hardened criminals, humorously demonstrating that sometimes taking a moment for McDonald's can lead to unexpected rewards.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young adults and value-conscious consumers (18-35) who appreciate dark comedy, underdog narratives, and quick-service meals.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film launched across digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media) and broadcast television.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive awareness and consideration for McDonald's McSaver meals by linking them to moments of unexpected reward and a humorous, relatable delay.

content.description:
Australian comedians Lachy McIntyre and Edan McGovern have officially partnered with Australian Lamb to launch a new podcast titled The Roast.

Known for their popular podcast Nobody Asked, the duo's collaboration with the brand was teased in early 2026, building on their established reputation for humorous commentary on Australian culture and viral social media sketches.

The Roast Podcast: A new series hosted by the pair, launched in February 2026 in collaboration with the Australian Lamb Facebook page.

The campaign aligns with Australian Lamb’s long-standing tradition of using comedy to promote lamb as the "national meat," famously fronted by Sam Kekovich.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Australian Lamb partners with popular comedians Lachy McIntyre and Edan McGovern to launch 'The Roast' podcast, continuing its tradition of using humor to promote lamb as the national meat.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Australians who engage with digital comedy content, podcasts, and social media, typically younger to middle-aged adults interested in contemporary Australian humor.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Creation and launch of a new podcast series ('The Roast') hosted by popular comedians, pre-launch teasing, and promotion/collaboration via the Australian Lamb Facebook page.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reinforce Australian Lamb's brand positioning as the 'national meat' and enhance brand relevance and cultural connection through engaging comedic content.

content.description:
The TV commercial tells a simple human story about two neighbors living side by side, separated by a wall and by very different lifestyles. While their daily habits and choices seem worlds apart, their paths unexpectedly cross in the supermarket, where the wide variety of products mirrors the diversity of people themselves. In this shared space, differences become less important, revealing that the smartest choices are often the ones that bring very different people together.

 

Advertising Agency: Guilty Ogilvy, Riga, Latvia

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses a story of two diverse neighbors to show how a supermarket, with its wide variety, becomes a common ground where differences diminish and smart choices bring people together.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General public, particularly supermarket shoppers of all ages and diverse lifestyles who value community, variety, and making inclusive choices.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: TV commercial and potentially online film platforms showcasing the narrative.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand affinity for RIMI by positioning it as an inclusive community hub that celebrates diversity and facilitates connections through 'smart choices'.

content.description:
Apple unveils “Smart Group Selfies," a new campaign for iPhone 17 Pro that showcases the new Center Stage front camera that brings everyone into the frame. Group portraits of real communities united by shared passions, traditions, or identities from around the world are the protagonists of the campaign.

The Center Stage front camera on the new iPhone lineup features the first square sensor on iPhone, delivering a wider field of view and higher resolution — up to 18MP for photos — that automatically adjusts to fit everyone in the shot with greater detail. Users can shoot in portrait or landscape while holding their iPhone vertically, making it easier to capture group moments with a comfortable grip and centered gaze.

Among the groups featured in the campaign are:

Compton Cowboys reshaping urban cowboy cultureJeju Island's Haenyeo, the intergenerational sisterhood of women free diversKorea's Maltese dog owner communitySoutheast Asia's Y3K aesthetic movementIndonesia's long boat crew with viral sensation Rayyan Arkan DhikaJapan's cosplayers and rockabilliesBrooklyn nonnasThe iconic staff at Katz's Delicatessen

“Smart Group Selfies" launches with broadcast in the US followed by out-of-home, digital out-of-home, and social media across all markets starting the week of February 23. Market-specific OOH executions will spotlight local communities in each region.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Apple's "Smart Group Selfies" campaign showcases the iPhone 17 Pro's new Center Stage front camera through authentic group portraits of diverse communities, emphasizing its capability to automatically fit everyone into a wider, high-resolution frame with ease.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals who frequently capture group moments, such as members of communities, families, or social circles, and desire an effortless, high-quality, and inclusive front-camera experience, likely current or prospective iPhone users interested in premium mobile photography features.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film featuring real community group portraits captured with the iPhone 17 Pro, demonstrating the Center Stage camera's capabilities, distributed primarily via film (e.g., TV commercials, online video platforms) and potentially supported by social media content.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build awareness and generate desire for the new iPhone 17 Pro, specifically by demonstrating the innovative functionality and benefits of the Center Stage front camera for capturing superior group photos.

content.description:
Jumeirah and Soursop Launch a Global Cultural Platform Reframing Luxury Through Contemporary Arab CreativityA new global cultural platform brings together Beyoncé director Emmanuel Adjei, contemporary artist Farah Al Qasimi, Emirati poet Shamma Al Bastaki and London’s Young Poet Laureate Theresa Lola.Conceived and creatively led by Soursop for Jumeirah, the initiative takes shape through a major art book, Where Worlds Meet, and a cinematic work, Our Flame.The project draws on the majlis, the Arab tradition of convening poets, thinkers and guests, translating it into a contemporary framework for artistic collaboration at global scale.Within its pages, Lamya Gargash’s Majlis series (2008–2009) reframes domestic interiors as a cultural archive. Farah Al Qasimi, whose work has been exhibited at the Guggenheim and Tate Modern, represents a generation shaping Gulf visual language within the international canon.Arabic typography is reimagined through Mooni Studio and Egyptian designer Fares Waleed, while Iraqi master calligrapher Majid Al-Yousef contributes original calligraphic works to the book, grounding the project in a living tradition of Arabic script.The dialogue extends beyond the region. At Capri Palace Jumeirah, works by Giorgio de Chirico and Mario Schifano form part of Jumeirah’s permanent collection, positioning Arabian hospitality within a broader art historical continuum.If the book establishes the intellectual architecture, Our Flame translates it into motion. Directed by Emmanuel Adjei and written by Shamma Al Bastaki. The agency production was handled by Whale Amsterdam and the production company was 100%.  It centres on a single flame carried across Dubai, London, Capri, Bali, Mallorca and the open sea,  a symbol of warmth moving between cultures.Across book and film, contemporary Arab culture operates at institutional scale, shaping a new global identity for Jumeirah.For Soursop, the project signals a wider ambition: building brand universes that function as cultural platforms. For Jumeirah, it marks a decisive step into a future defined by artistic collaboration.

 

Agency: SoursopAgency Production: Whale AmsterdamProduction Company: 100%

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Jumeirah launched 'Our Flame', a global cultural platform collaboratively developed with Soursop, to redefine luxury through contemporary Arab creativity, inspired by the traditional majlis.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Culturally curious individuals, art and design enthusiasts, and consumers of luxury experiences interested in contemporary Arab culture and global artistic collaborations.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Development of a major art book titled 'Where Worlds Meet' and a cinematic work 'Our Flame', featuring collaborations with acclaimed directors, artists, and poets, presented as an integrated global cultural platform.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reframe Jumeirah's brand perception within the luxury market by positioning it as a patron of contemporary Arab creativity and fostering cultural dialogue.

content.description: Originals have one thing in common: a story worth telling. And the best are written in denim.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign positions Levi's denim as more than just clothing, but as a living canvas that collects and tells the authentic stories and experiences of individuals, making them 'originals'.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Fashion-conscious individuals, primarily young adults to mid-career professionals (e.g., 20-45), who value authenticity, self-expression, and appreciate the heritage and quality of iconic brands.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film deployed across digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media channels) and potentially traditional broadcast television, showcasing diverse individuals and their unique stories embedded in their Levi's denim.
ai_enrichment.objective: To strengthen brand identity and emotional connection by associating Levi's denim with authenticity, personal history, and the concept of timeless originality.

content.description:
To fight commoditization in the booming Air Fryer market, Britânia turned urban furniture into a multisensory experience. The "COOHking Air Fryer" campaign transformed street clocks into giant, animated appliances that offered more than just visuals: they opened and closed, played the iconic "DING" sound, and released the actual aroma of freshly cooked food.

Strategically placed in high-traffic areas during meal times, the activation engaged over 350,000 people directly. The stunt went viral, generating 1.2 million spontaneous social mentions and driving a 38% sales increase during the campaign.

 

Agency: Calia

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Britânia transformed urban street clocks into giant, animated air fryers that offered a multisensory experience, opening, closing, playing a 'DING' sound, and releasing the aroma of freshly cooked food.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General consumers and home cooks interested in modern kitchen appliances, particularly those in the market for an air fryer, who might perceive them as a commodity.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Multisensory OOH Outdoor activation transforming street clocks into giant, animated air fryers that dispensed aroma and sound, strategically placed in high-traffic urban areas during meal times.
ai_enrichment.objective: To fight commoditization in the booming air fryer market, differentiate the Britânia brand, drive sales, and generate widespread brand engagement and social virality.

content.description:
This powerful film builds on a viral video of a lone penguin trekking nearly 70 km away from its natural habitat, a moment that recently captured global attention. While the world rallies to save one penguin, the film asks a deeper, more unsettling question: are we doing enough to protect its Antarctic home?

Using the penguin’s journey as a trigger, the narrative shifts focus to the larger crisis unfolding in Antarctica. Rapid ice loss and habitat degradation driven by climate change are threatening entire ecosystems, not just individual species.

The film urges audiences to look beyond singular moments of sympathy and recognize habitat loss as the real emergency. By reframing concern from one penguin to its disappearing home, the campaign calls for collective responsibility and sustained action toward environmental preservation.

 .

Creative Concept & Script: Rohan AgarwalContent Writer & Copywriter: Adiba KashifResearch & Data: Illumineye Communications Research TeamEditor: Bhumika Saxena

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign leverages the viral story of a lone penguin to shift focus from individual animal sympathy to the larger climate crisis threatening Antarctica's entire ecosystem, urging recognition of habitat loss as the real emergency.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Globally aware individuals who were moved by the viral penguin story, and a broader audience concerned about environmental issues or open to understanding the deeper impacts of climate change.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A powerful digital film distributed across online platforms, leveraging the existing viral attention around the penguin story to maximize reach and impact.
ai_enrichment.objective: To educate and reframe public perception, moving from singular sympathy for an individual animal to recognizing habitat loss as the critical, systemic emergency demanding collective responsibility and sustained action.

content.description:
The term ‘facial’ is usually associated with words such as ‘relaxation’, ‘cleansing’, and ‘rejuvenation’. But dermal science skincare brand NIOD has flipped the script on facials in its latest campaign, creating a stomach-turning representation of the skin treatment that aims to illustrate the damaging effects of pollutants on the skin.

‘The New York Facial’, created by Uncommon, features a hero social film that takes inspiration from the popular beauty and skincare tutorials seen across our social feeds. But instead of slathering their face with luxurious creams and hydrating oils, the film captures attention by depicting a plethora of harmful toxins, all of which are in fact seeping into the skin of city dwellers as they go about their daily lives.

With content designed to truly stop the scroll, being a far cry from the light, bright beauty content and glossy product shots typically seen on socials, ‘The New York Facial’ has been created to promote NIOD’s Superoxide Dismutase 3 Enzyme Mist (SDEM3). The mist is designed to prevent damage against environmental irritants - with research revealing air pollutants found in urban areas can increase pigmentation by over 20%.

Alongside teasers of the hero film, the campaign includes extensive creator activity across London, New York, Los Angeles and Toronto, with kits containing a mock ‘hazardous’ New York Facial product - which recipients are unable to open for fear of its extensive toxic ingredients - and a bottle of SDEM3.

Ellie Daghlian and Elisa Czerwenka, Creative Directors at Uncommon Creative Studio, said: “The industry playbook to launching a premium skincare product isn’t to rub toxins on someone’s face, but that’s exactly why we did it. We wanted to hold up a mirror to city living and make the invisible impossible to ignore. The New York Facial is beauty content you can’t just scroll past - because it makes you feel something you probably didn’t want to. NIOD are pioneers in science-backed skincare, we wanted to disrupt the sea of picture-perfect skincare content to put the benefits of this innovative product front and centre.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign subverts traditional beauty tutorials by depicting a 'stomach-turning' facial using harmful urban pollutants to illustrate their damaging effects on city dwellers' skin.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Urban dwellers, particularly adults concerned about skin health and environmental pollutants.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero social film distributed on social media platforms, mimicking popular beauty and skincare tutorials.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness about the damaging effects of urban pollutants on the skin and capture audience attention.

content.description: the film follows a man searching all day for someone named Sara.We see him in different locations — streets, offices, cafés — always looking, always asking, but never finding her.As the story unfolds, the search becomes symbolic.Maybe you’ve been ghosted before. Maybe someone promised — and disappeared.But at Meydan Free Zone, we don’t disappear.We keep our promises.The search for Sara becomes a metaphor for reliability — because when we commit, we deliver.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A man's fruitless search for 'Sara' becomes a metaphor for broken promises and unreliability, contrasting with Meydan Free Zone's commitment to delivering on its word.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Entrepreneurs, businesses, and individuals seeking reliable financial or business services from a free zone, particularly those who value trust and promise-keeping.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across integrated channels, likely digital platforms (YouTube, social media), and potentially traditional media, showcasing the symbolic narrative.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand trust, enhance Meydan Free Zone's reputation for reliability, and differentiate it as a dependable partner in the finance industry.

content.description: In an industry obsessed with scale, celebrity, and spectacle, a new Richmond-based agency is debuting on advertising’s biggest night by doing the opposite.Two Birds, founded by advertising veterans Patrick Simkins and Jordan Rodericks, is making its Super Bowl debut through a regional Super Bowl buy for West Broad Honda, a dealership that has been part of the Richmond community since 1978 and chose to bet on a neighborhood partner it trusted.Rather than rely on traditional Tier 3 automotive playbooks or borrowed celebrity, Two Birds built the campaign around a simple belief: if a brand has real equity in its community, the work should reflect it. The resulting spot introduces “Tony from West Broad Honda,” a self-aware, character-driven idea designed to be as memorable as any celebrity cameo.For West Broad Honda, the Super Bowl represented a once-in-a-generation opportunity to do something special for the people who have supported them for decades. For Two Birds, it marked the chance to show what their collaborative, wall-less creative process can produce when trust replaces convention. Strategy, creative, and production worked together from day one, shaping a piece of work built for craft, confidence, and cultural fit—not just efficiency.The campaign extends beyond broadcast with a social-first activation that turns the Super Bowl moment into ongoing community participation. Viewers are invited to visit the dealership, meet Tony, and tag @westbroadhonda on Instagram while telling Tony where they want to go for a chance to win a fully paid 2026 road trip, complete with a West Broad Honda loaner vehicle and travel costs covered.For Two Birds, the debut isn’t about making noise for noise’s sake. It’s about showing that meaningful, distinctive work can still come from a small, trusted partnership. Built in Richmond, for its neighbors, and confident enough to air on the biggest night in advertising.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign introduces 'Tony from West Broad Honda,' a self-aware, character-driven idea designed to leverage the dealership's deep community roots and trusted neighborhood presence, counteracting industry trends of celebrity and spectacle.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Richmond-area residents in the market for automotive services or vehicle purchases, who value local trust and community connection over celebrity endorsements.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Regional Super Bowl television spot, part of an integrated campaign, featuring a relatable character to build brand equity.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand memorability and reinforce West Broad Honda's trusted standing within the Richmond community, making it as memorable as any high-budget celebrity campaign.

content.description:
The National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC), in partnership with the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT), has launched ‘Cyber Resilience’, a new campaign created by M&C Saatchi Group UK to increase awareness, consideration and uptake of Cyber Essentials certification among UK businesses.

The multichannel push responds to a growing gap between cyber threats and UK businesses defences. In 2025, over four in ten UK businesses experienced a cyber breach, yet many SME leaders continue to believe that their size or scale makes them less of a target. The work positions cyber security not as a purely IT issue, but as a core business imperative.

The Cyber Resilience campaign looks to encourage businesses to take practical action by signing up to and completing Cyber Essentials, the government-backed cyber security certification. The 2025–26 campaign focuses on increasing awareness and uptake of the scheme, driving more users to the website and boosting certifications over 24 months, with a particular focus on small and medium-sized enterprises and a secondary audience of larger organisations.

At the heart of the creative idea is the insight that cyber attacks can happen to any business, anywhere. The campaign brings this to life by placing everyday businesses in far-flung and fantastical locations, including on the moon, underwater and on the side of a cliff trying to evade the threat of cyber criminals. These dramatic, photo-real landscapes juxtapose the extraordinary with the familiar, reinforcing the message that nowhere is out of reach from cyber crime.

Each execution captures a moment in time, with subtle, eye-catching details that add richness to the scenes while quickly landing a simple story: no matter where your business operates, hackers can still reach you.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Positioning cyber security as a core business imperative, not just an IT issue, to encourage UK businesses to take practical action by adopting Cyber Essentials certification.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: UK businesses, particularly SME leaders who underestimate their vulnerability to cyber threats.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Multichannel push, including film.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase awareness, consideration, and uptake of Cyber Essentials certification among UK businesses.

metadata.title: It's a crime to eat just one!

content.description:
A getaway driver waits in the car for his accomplice to return, who soon appears running to the now-moving vehicle with the supermarket pasteizinhos in a bag.

 

Advertising Agency: CENTOE6Production Company: CENTOE6Creative Director: William MattosCopywriter: William MattosFilmmaker: William MattosEdition: William Mattos

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A humorous short video piece where the irresistible taste of Pistoni Supermercado's pasteizinhos is dramatized as something so desirable it's worth committing a 'crime' to obtain, playing on the tagline 'It's a crime to eat just one!'.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Shoppers of Pistoni Supermercado and potential new customers, particularly those who enjoy popular Brazilian snacks like pasteizinhos and appreciate humorous, engaging advertising.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A short, narrative-driven video content piece, likely distributed across social media platforms (e.g., Instagram, Facebook, YouTube) and potentially in-store digital screens, to entertain and highlight the product's appeal.
ai_enrichment.objective: To create desire and build awareness for Pistoni Supermercado's pasteizinhos by associating them with an irresistibly delicious and memorable experience.

content.description:
The wait is over: the next era of cola has arrived. Following a sold-out Black Friday online debut last fall, PEPSI® – the first legacy cola to introduce prebiotic fiber – officially launches Pepsi Prebiotic Cola nationwide.

Pepsi Prebiotic Cola delivers the “Unbelievably Pepsi” taste people love, now with functional ingredients that reflect today’s modern beverage preferences. Available in Original Cola and Cherry Vanilla, Pepsi Prebiotic Cola contains no artificial sweeteners, just 30 calories, only 5g of sugar, and 3g of prebiotic fiber – all without compromising the iconic Pepsi taste that consumers love.

Consumers can now find Pepsi Prebiotic Cola in 12 oz. single cans and 8-packs in the carbonated soft drink aisle at retailers nationwide and online.

Created for cola lovers and newcomers alike, Pepsi Prebiotic Cola brings a fresh take to the category – backed by decades of taste legacy.

Follow Pepsi on Instagram, Facebook, X, TikTok and YouTube (@Pepsi) for the latest on Pepsi Prebiotic Cola and brand news.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign introduces Pepsi Prebiotic Cola, a new line offering the iconic Pepsi taste combined with functional benefits like prebiotic fiber, low sugar, and no artificial sweeteners, targeting modern beverage preferences.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Existing cola lovers and health-conscious consumers seeking functional beverages, who are interested in low-sugar, low-calorie options without compromising taste.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Nationwide launch with product availability in 12 oz. single cans and 8-packs at retailers and online, following an initial online debut.
ai_enrichment.objective: To successfully launch and drive sales of the new Pepsi Prebiotic Cola nationwide, catering to evolving consumer preferences for functional and healthier beverage options.

content.description:
We ran a Big Game commercial to help solve a Big Problem: exploding heads from extreme energy drinks. Liquid Death Sparkling Energy is the easy-drinking energy drink. With just 5 calories, unextreme caffeine (100mg), zero sugar, and a clean refreshing taste, it’s an energy drink for days when you want more than one energy drink.

Watch our commercial and share it with a friend who you think might be at risk for an exploded head. Then head over to Amazon, Walmart, Target, Kroger, Albertsons, 7-Eleven, or any fine retailer near you to pick up as many cans of Liquid Death Sparkling Energy as your cart can hold.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Liquid Death humorously positions its Sparkling Energy as the 'easy-drinking' solution to the 'exploding heads' problem caused by extreme energy drinks, offering a moderate, clean, and refreshing alternative.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Current energy drink consumers, likely aged 18-40, who are looking for a less intense, healthier, and more palatable energy boost without the perceived negative side effects of traditional extreme energy drinks.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 'Big Game' commercial (TV/online film) with a strong call-to-action for social sharing, complemented by widespread retail distribution through major e-commerce platforms, supermarkets, and convenience stores (e.g., Amazon, Walmart, Target, 7-Eleven).
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive sales and market penetration for Liquid Death Sparkling Energy by establishing it as a distinct, healthier, and less extreme alternative within the energy drink category, supported by a memorable and shareable campaign.

content.description:
For its Back to School return, Hush Puppies came back the way it knows best: by owning an icon you can’t miss.We took one of the most familiar Back to School moments, the yearbook photo, and turned it on its head.Where students usually sit, there were different breads of cute dogs.Every single one wearing the iconic Basset Hound ears.The visual is instant. Clear. Simple.It needs no explanation because it works as a simple visual truth: everyone wants Hush Puppies.A fresh, playful and unmistakable piece that cuts through the clutter, brings the brand back into focus and proves that when an idea is this clear, it lands in seconds.

Cause face, what do you call a Basset Hound? A Hush Puppy.

 

Agency: The Juju PeruPresident, Partner, and CCO: Andrés BriceñoCreative Directors: Carlos Castillo, Muma.Copywriter: Andrés BriceñoBusiness Development Director: Valeria MaloneProduction Director: Lissette CervantesProject Manager: Rocío MartinezProject Manager: Brenda RuizExecutive Producer: Lucciana Proaño

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign reimagines the classic back-to-school yearbook photo by replacing students with various breeds of cute dogs, all wearing the iconic Basset Hound ears to represent Hush Puppies.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Students, parents, and young adults interested in comfortable casual footwear, particularly during the back-to-school shopping season.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Film advertisements distributed across relevant media channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: Re-establish brand relevance and increase awareness for Hush Puppies, particularly during the back-to-school period, by creating a memorable and playful visual campaign.

content.description:
One of Australia’s most decorated national sporting sides, the Australian Steelers wheelchair rugby team, has launched a groundbreaking public campaign – stripping back its iconic green and gold uniforms in a quest to find a long-term corporate partner.

Developed by Ogilvy, the ‘Officially Unsponsored’ campaign has been designed to put the Steelers in the shop window for brands looking for a point of difference and a way to cut-through, showcasing the team’s progressive mindset and boldly announcing themselves as an option for brands looking to engage with sport.  It will be seen across social, OOH and print.

In the campaign, athletes wear the minimalist all-white uniforms with QR codes positioned where a sponsor’s logo traditionally would be placed. Scanning the code will lead to a purpose-built microsite, aptly named the ‘SponsorSHOP’, an Australian-first digital marketplace where brands can explore a curated range of sponsorship opportunities for the Steelers, from naming rights and apparel partnerships to event and campaign collaborations and more.

 

Strategy, creative, PR: OgilvyProduction: WPP ProductionProduction Partner: TSE (Total Sport & Entertainment)Photographer: Sam Tolhurst

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The Australian Steelers wheelchair rugby team launched 'Officially Unsponsored,' a campaign featuring athletes in minimalist white uniforms with QR codes in place of sponsor logos. This bold approach aims to attract a long-term corporate partner by directly highlighting their unsponsored status.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Corporate brands and potential sponsors seeking unique, impactful, and purpose-driven sports partnerships to enhance their brand image and engage with a progressive sporting team.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Displaying athletes in all-white uniforms with QR codes in traditional sponsor logo placements across OOH outdoor advertising, social media, and print channels, directing scans to a dedicated pitch for potential corporate partners.
ai_enrichment.objective: Secure long-term corporate sponsorship for the Australian Steelers wheelchair rugby team by actively demonstrating their value proposition to potential brand partners.

content.description:
NHS England has launched ‘Talking Therapies’, a new UK-wide campaign created by M+C Saatchi Group UK, designed to increase awareness and use of NHS Talking Therapies services among people who may be living with anxiety conditions but have been quietly putting up with their symptoms.

The campaign supports the NHS’s long-term ambition to improve access to mental health services and patient outcomes, helping ensure that more people of all ages receive the treatment they need. It focuses on increasing awareness, knowledge and intention to use NHS Talking Therapies, particularly among people experiencing phobias, panic disorder, social anxiety disorder, obsessive compulsive disorder (OCD), post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) and body dysmorphic disorder (BDD).

Targeted at 30 to 50-year-olds who may recognise that they experience life differently but have not previously sought support, the work aims to encourage people to reappraise symptoms they may have normalised or felt reluctant to act on. The campaign reinforces that treatment is available, and that taking steps to access it can make a meaningful difference to day-to-day life.

At the heart of the creative idea is a simple but powerful truth: for someone living with these conditions, returning to everyday activities they once managed with ease can be genuinely worth celebrating. What may appear minor to others can represent a significant personal achievement.

The campaign brings this idea to life through subtle but joyful celebration cues, including balloons, confetti and even a single firework. These understated visual moments reflect the internal sense of progress felt by the protagonists, marking small but meaningful steps forward without overstating or dramatising them.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The 'Talking Therapies' campaign encourages 30-50 year olds quietly suffering from specific anxiety conditions to seek help and utilize NHS Talking Therapies services.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: 30-50 year olds in the UK experiencing phobias, panic disorder, social anxiety, OCD, PTSD, or BDD, who have been quietly putting up with their symptoms.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A UK-wide film campaign, created by M+C Saatchi Group UK, likely distributed via television and online video platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase awareness, knowledge, and intention to use NHS Talking Therapies services, thereby improving access to mental health support and patient outcomes.

content.description:
No clima de preparação para o Natal, a família Silva está em polvorosa com a possibilidade de receber uma “visita” indesejada: o Aedes aegypti. Enquanto todos discutem como a presença dele estragaria as festas, a vovó, figura prática e impaciente, dá a letra: é só não deixar a casa convidativa para o mosquito. A partir daí, ela assume o comando e coloca todo mundo pra eliminar água parada e organizar o quintal. No final, vemos que, sem bagunça e sem criadouros, o Aedes nem perde tempo: vai embora procurar outro endereço. Uma campanha leve, bem-humorada e direta, mostrando que combater o mosquito é simples quando todo mundo faz a sua parte.

Agência: Casa BrasilCliente: Prefeitura de GoiâniaDireção de Criação: Anna Clara DamascenoRedação: Anna Clara DamascenoDireção de Arte: Deivid PereiraAtendimento:  Lucas Valentim, Thays BatiolirMídia e Produção: Poliana Oliveira

 

Advertising Agency: Casa BrasilProduction Company: Black DogCreative Director: Anna Clara DamascenoCopywriter: Anna Clara DamascenoArt Director: Deivid PereiraAtendimento: Lucas Valentim, Thays BatiolirMídia e Produção: Poliana Oliveira

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign humorously depicts a family's preparations for Christmas being threatened by the Aedes aegypti mosquito, showing how a practical grandmother mobilizes everyone to eliminate breeding grounds. It highlights that preventing dengue is simple and effective when all household members do their part.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Residents of Goiânia, particularly families and household decision-makers responsible for home and yard maintenance, as well as the general public.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed via television and digital platforms (e.g., social media, YouTube) to broadly communicate the message of dengue prevention through simple, collective household actions.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise public awareness about the importance of dengue prevention and encourage active, collective participation from residents in eliminating Aedes aegypti breeding grounds within their homes and surroundings.

content.description:
There are many things that are awesome about E-470, the tollway that forms a 47-mile loop around the Denver metro area and provides access to Denver International Airport. But none of them are terribly entertaining to learn about. Who has time to spend getting up to speed on road maintenance or job creation? No one. But almost everyone can find time to dig into a good scandal. So we created a campaign that fuels existing rumors and conspiracy theories about the road, compelling people to investigate on their own. And what they ultimately found was all the great things E470 does, things they’d never see otherwise, served-up in highly entertaining fashion.

 

Agency: Standard PracticeCD/Art Director: Nate BruningCD/Copywriter: Steve DolanCD/Art Director: Matt AlexanderCD/Copywriter: Myles RiggAccount Director: Renae NewmanExecutive Producer: Andrew CampbellHead of Strategy: Dave SchiffDirector / Producer / Writer: Woody RoselandDirector / Producer / Writer: Chad Neidt

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign leveraged existing rumors and conspiracy theories about E-470 to pique public curiosity, compelling them to investigate and ultimately discover the tollway's positive contributions and benefits presented in an entertaining manner.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General public in the Denver metro area, especially residents and drivers who are familiar with or use E-470, and are intrigued by scandals, rumors, or conspiracy theories.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: OOH Outdoor advertising to tease rumors and generate interest, driving the audience to discover entertaining online content revealing E-470's positive attributes.
ai_enrichment.objective: To improve public perception and educate the community about the positive aspects and contributions of E-470.

content.description:
Feel-Good Brand Campaign Celebrating Togetherness

Developed with integrated marketing consultancy, Rival, Good Foods is launching its first brand campaign — and it’s less about introducing the brand than putting a feeling into words.The family-run, Wisconsin-based company has built a loyal following by making food that people don’t just eat, but pass around. Dips, spreads, guacamoles, and salsas that disappear faster than expected. The thing everyone ends up standing around in the kitchen talking about. The food that quietly becomes the reason people gather.

“Feels Good, Doesn’t It” is a 360 campaign spanning brand positioning, creative development, production, and media. The work leans into moments when flavor shifts the energy in the room and pulls people out of autopilot. No claims to decode. No permission required. Just food that brings people together.

“This campaign came from the heart of who we’ve always been,” says Vice President of Marketing Melanie Nemoy at Good Foods. “We’ve known for a long time that people don’t just eat Good Foods, they share it, talk about it, build moments around it. Feels Good, Doesn’t It is about finally bringing that truth to life. It’s less about telling people what to think and more about reflecting how our food already shows up in their lives.”

In a category dominated by functional language and beige restraint, Good Foods is leaning into something simpler and more human — food that makes you smile before you realize you’re smiling. Feels good, doesn’t it?

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign positions Good Foods products as the catalyst for spontaneous, joyful gatherings and the creation of shared, feel-good moments through food. It aims to put an emotional feeling into words rather than simply introducing the brand.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and families who value social gatherings, shared experiences, and convenient, delicious food that facilitates connection and conversation during informal social occasions.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 360 campaign leveraging film as the primary medium, distributed across various media channels, with creative content focused on showing moments where flavor shifts energy and encourages togetherness around food.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand equity by establishing an emotional connection with consumers, associating Good Foods with positive feelings of togetherness and shared joy, and solidifying its role as the facilitator of memorable social moments.

content.description: This film follows a woman who moves through the world guided by what she feels, not what she says. We stay close to her in quiet, intimate moments where emotion leads the frame. The jewelry isn’t just an accessory — it becomes part of her presence, subtle but strong, personal and real.“Let Your Heart Speak” is about expression without effort. Light, elegance, and honesty carry the story.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign centers on the idea of expressing genuine emotions and inner feelings without words, portraying a woman whose authentic presence is subtly enhanced by Pandora jewelry. It emphasizes effortless self-expression, light, elegance, and honesty through intimate, emotionally led moments.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Women, likely aged 25-55, who prioritize authentic self-expression, emotional depth, and understated elegance, seeking jewelry that resonates with their personal feelings and inner narrative.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across digital platforms such as YouTube, Instagram, and Facebook, potentially adapted for television commercials, and featured on Pandora's official website and in-store digital displays.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand perception by positioning Pandora jewelry as a medium for authentic, personal, and effortless emotional self-expression, fostering a deeper connection with the brand.

content.description:
Seven out of ten girls aged 10–18 say they wish they looked like a filtered version of themselves. At the same time, advanced skincare routines are becoming more common among teens and children. In response, Swedish pharmacy Apotek Hjärtat launches The New 7-Step Routine — an integrated campaign that shifts focus beyond products.

Only three steps in The New 7-Step Routine focus on skincare: cleansing, moisturising and sun protection, usually all young skin needs. The remaining four steps replace serums and toners with reflection exercises designed to strengthen self-esteem.

Across outdoor, digital and film, the brand borrows the polished language of beauty advertising but twists it to reveal what skincare can’t fix. The film begins like a classic skincare commercial before gradually revealing a more unsettling reality.The initiative aims to redirect young people’s focus to what truly matters and to spark a wider conversation about how beauty ideals shape their wellbeing.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Apotek Hjärtat redefines the popular 7-step beauty routine for young girls, replacing complex skincare steps with self-esteem building exercises to address the pressure of filtered beauty standards.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Girls aged 10-18 influenced by filtered beauty standards and complex skincare routines, and their parents.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Integrated campaign leveraging outdoor advertising, digital channels, and film (likely a hero film with a narrative twist), designed as an experiential routine.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reposition Apotek Hjärtat as a brand promoting holistic well-being and healthy self-esteem over superficial beauty standards, by shifting focus beyond product-centric solutions.

content.description:
Amazon leans into our collective unease about AI with a playful Super Bowl spot for Alexa+, starring Chris Hemsworth. When he finds his wife Elsa Pataky chatting to the upgraded assistant, a snake in hand, Chris instantly suspects Alexa+ is plotting to kill him.

Directed by Wayne McClammy, the 60-second film spirals into increasingly absurd fantasies, from bear fights to elaborate conspiracies, before Chris realises Alexa+ is not a threat after all. Airing in the third quarter, the ad reframes the next-gen assistant as surprisingly helpful rather than sinister.

 

Cooking made easy with Taste and Amazon Alexa

Aussies get Alexa. Because Alexa gets Aussies.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Amazon humorously addresses common anxieties about AI through Chris Hemsworth's escalating paranoia about Alexa+, ultimately reframing the upgraded assistant as a helpful tool rather than a threat.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General consumers interested in smart home technology, particularly those who might have anxieties or concerns about the advancements in AI and smart assistants.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 60-second hero film starring Chris Hemsworth and Elsa Pataky, directed by Wayne McClammy, airing during the third quarter of the Super Bowl (TV broadcast), likely supported by online and social media distribution.
ai_enrichment.objective: To shift public perception of Alexa+ from potentially sinister to surprisingly helpful, thereby building trust and driving consumer consideration for the next-gen assistant.

content.description:
During the Big Game, Alaska Airlines announced its newest localized Super Bowl spot: “Welcome to Alaska Airlines,” inviting travelers to explore the world with the 4th U.S. Global Airline.

Created in partnership with its agency of record, Mekanism, the work highlights Alaska Airlines’ new global routes and its lie-flat suites on the new Alaska 787 aircraft. The use of AI allows us to create more spots, so we can fill a very targeted media plan. We have specific spots for Seattle, Portland, San Diego all focused on the destinations that matter in those markets.

Set to the upbeat track “Walking on a Dream” by Empire of the Sun, the campaign transports viewers to different global dreamscapes, blending CG and AI to fuel imagination and the excitement of travel.  Kicking off with two :30 spots live at the center of the campaign, all an invitation to explore destinations in a personal and cinematic way.

Alaska Airlines and Mekanism will be updating the campaign throughout the year to feature top-of-mind travel moments. For quick, reactionary moments, they will leverage Mekanism’s AI Superstudio to update on a moment's notice. The first :30 spot follows a woman in the Alaska Airlines suite, transitioning into a dreamy version of Rome and London. She experiences a dream-like tea spread reminiscent of London before seeing oversized gelato and landmarks that remind her of Rome. The journey ends with her savoring an ice cream sundae in her lie-flat suite.

The second follows another young traveler departing San Diego nonstop to the destinations she has been dreaming of. The film moves through her dreams in the clouds, showing towering icons like the Statue of Liberty in New York City, the snowy slopes of Colorado, and the vibrant colors of Mexico, capturing the thrill of exploration.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign visually invites travelers to explore the world with Alaska Airlines' new global routes and lie-flat suites, using AI and CG to create dreamlike global travel experiences set to 'Walking on a Dream,' with localized spots for specific markets.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Travelers in key Alaska Airlines markets such as Seattle, Portland, and San Diego, who are interested in global travel and premium flight experiences including lie-flat suites.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film spots (:30s) deployed across a very targeted media plan, including specific localized versions for markets like Seattle, Portland, and San Diego, utilizing AI for efficient content creation.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness of Alaska Airlines' new global routes and premium lie-flat suites, thereby positioning it as a competitive global airline.

content.description: A story of finding joy in winter and reawakening your inner child.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign leverages the emotional themes of finding joy in winter and reawakening one's inner child to create a heartwarming narrative around the McDonald's Big Rösti product.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and families seeking comfort and warmth during winter, particularly those open to nostalgic experiences and rediscovering simple joys through food.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film deployed across broadcast television and digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media channels) to tell the emotional story and prominently feature the Big Rösti.
ai_enrichment.objective: To strengthen emotional brand affinity and drive awareness and consideration for the McDonald's Big Rösti product by associating it with positive winter experiences and nostalgia.

metadata.title: Helping Britain Invest Through It All

content.description:
Hargreaves Lansdown has appointed Wonderhood Studios as its new creative agency partner, with the launch of a new creative platform and campaign, “Helping Britain Invest Through It All”.After a significant year for the business, marked by a change in ownership and renewed marketing leadership, this campaign represents the beginning of a bold new chapter for the brand that has helped Britain invest for nearly half a century.With this new campaign, Hargreaves Lansdown reinforces its position as the nation’s trusted investment leader; confident, proud, and unshakably British. The campaign reminds Britons that confidence in investing isn’t about chasing fads; it’s about knowing your money is in the right hands. For 45 years, Hargreaves Lansdown has stood at the forefront of UK investing, trusted by more than two million clients and managing £190 billion in assets.The cinematic 60” launch film, “Through It All,” charts four decades of national change outside Number 10 Downing Street, as new leaders, shifting policies and defining moments of crisis and progress shape the country and investment landscape, all seen outside the same iconic black door. From the birth of the internet and the optimism of Cool Britannia, through the credit crunch, Brexit and the pandemic, the film captures the everchanging landscape of British politics. But through every speech, every swing, every shift, Hargreaves Lansdown has helped people stay steady and build wealth that lasts, reminding the audience that “We’ve seen a lot of change over the last 45 years. But one thing stays the same. We’ll be here, helping Britain invest through it all.”The campaign was created by James Rafter and Myles Vincent and directed by Tom Green through Stink UK.James Rafter & Myles Vincent, Creative Directors at Wonderhood Studios said: “We set out to make a film that no other investment company could. Being 45 years old and having invested through all the madness the country has faced meant we could craft a spot that brought to life politics, financial events, thick rim glasses, pin stripes, perms and so many moustaches. It’s been a showcase of wonderful human craft through the entire production, and probably the closest we’ll ever get to No.10.”Ciara Cox, Marketing Director at Hargreaves Lansdown, said “For 45 years, Hargreaves Lansdown has helped people invest through political change, economic uncertainty, and moments of real national optimism. “Helping Britain Invest Through It All” is a confident recommitment to that role. Created in partnership with Wonderhood, this campaign is about reminding people that successful investing isn’t about reacting to every headline. It’s about trust, perspective, and knowing your money is in safe hands with the UK’s #1 investment and savings platform. We’re incredibly proud of our heritage, our 2 million clients, and the role we continue to play in helping Britain build long-term financial confidence.”“Helping Britain Invest Through It All” will roll out across TV, cinema, social, radio, OOH and press. The campaign brings a new sense of pride to existing clients and reminds the nation why Hargreaves Lansdown is the benchmark in confident investing. Hargreaves Lansdown clients will receive their own personal 'through it all' timelines, in a highly personalised campaign delivered via direct mail and email, a ‘Spotify Wrapped for the tax year end’ that reminds clients how their investment habits over the years juxtapose with the stranger times in UK culture.Stitchy London led the social strategy, content and creator activation for the campaign.

 

Invest today and you'll thank yourself later

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign positions Hargreaves Lansdown as Britain's enduring and trustworthy investment leader, emphasizing that true financial confidence stems from entrusting one's money to experienced hands, rather than pursuing short-lived investment trends.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: British adults, including current and potential investors, who value trust, stability, and long-term security in their financial decisions, particularly those seeking a reputable investment partner.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film deployed across various channels, likely including television and online video platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media), serving as the primary medium for the creative platform launch.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reinforce Hargreaves Lansdown's brand positioning as the nation's trusted investment leader, build confidence among investors, and mark a new strategic chapter for the company.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign encourages consumers to break from conventional drink choices and embrace Ocean Spray as a distinctive and refreshing alternative. It aims to position Ocean Spray as a unique and appealing option for those seeking something different in their beverage selection.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Health-conscious individuals and younger adults (Millennials & Gen Z) aged 18-45 who are seeking unique, refreshing, and non-alcoholic beverage options that stand out from the ordinary.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Primarily OOH Outdoor placements including billboards, bus stop shelters, digital street furniture, and transit advertising to maximize visibility in urban and suburban areas.
ai_enrichment.objective: Increase brand awareness and shift consumer perception of Ocean Spray as a unique, modern, and desirable non-alcoholic beverage choice among the target demographic.

content.description:
Make-A-Wish Canada today announced a unique partnership with creator and singer/songwriter Stacey Ryan, supported by TikTok Canada and agency partner Zulu Alpha Kilo. Launched on February 20th, the original song, “Wish Song,” composed and sung by Montreal-born Ryan, is a one-of-a-kind Canadian collaboration.

“Wish Song” is inspired by and reflects upon the healing impact wishes have on children and families facing the unimaginable trials of navigating critical illness. Releasing on TikTok and major streaming platforms with a call for social engagement, the song is a symbol of the courage, strength, joy, and power in every wish kid. Fans, influencers, and creators are encouraged to use the track in their own content through an original Branded Effect karaoke feature on TikTok. Proceeds from the use of the song will benefit Make-A-Wish Canada, allowing the organization to grant even more wishes.

“Wishes give children the strength to fight their critical illness, said Meaghan Stovel McKnight, Chief Executive Officer, Make-A-Wish Canada. “We’re so proud to work with Stacey Ryan and TikTok Canada to create an original song that brings hope to life and as a feeling anyone can connect with. With ‘Wish Song,’ we are creating more awareness of the life-changing impact of a wish, and raising funds for future wish granting.”

"I’m so honoured and grateful for the opportunity I was given to write ‘Wish Song’ for Make-A-Wish Canada,” said Stacey Ryan. “We were moved and inspired by the real life wishes of Canadian kids and that drove us to write this song full of love, hope and healing. I want nothing more than for these kids' wishes to come true, and with this song we can truly make a difference.”

TikTok’s global social impact programming leverages TikTok’s tools and community to amplify important initiatives around the world. Through their collaboration with Make-A-Wish Canada, the platform’s Canadian team has donated ad support to bring “Wish Song” to market.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: An original song, "Wish Song," by Stacey Ryan, supported by TikTok Canada, reflecting the healing impact of wishes on critically ill children and families, with a call for social engagement and user-generated content.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Supporters of Make-A-Wish, individuals interested in critical illness causes, and a general audience of music fans, influencers, and creators, particularly on TikTok.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Release of an original song "Wish Song" by Stacey Ryan on TikTok and major streaming platforms, supported by a Branded Effect karaoke feature on TikTok encouraging user-generated content from fans, influencers, and creators.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness for Make-A-Wish Canada and the healing impact of wishes on critically ill children, while driving social engagement and generating donations through a unique music collaboration.

content.description:
Opendoor, the leading digital platform for residential real estate transactions, today launched "The Hardest Part," a national brand campaign that marks the most significant shift in the company's marketing strategy since its founding in 2014.

 

Agency: Ways & MeansFounder / Executive Producer: Lana KimFounder / Executive Producer: Jett SteigerCreative Director: Peter BrantAgency Producer: Gabrielle Majella PearsonArt Director: Melissa PloysophonCopywriter: Jon KornProduction Company: Ways & MeansFounder / Executive Producer: Lana KimFounder / Executive Producer: Jett SteigerDirector: Rosanna PengProducer: Jon LeeHead of Production: Danika CasasInterim Head of Production: Brianna LieblingDirector of Photography: Scotty UyedaProduction Designer: Mike BayerWardrobe Stylist: Emily AntonettiPost Production: Ways & MeansFounder / Executive Producer: Lana KimFounder / Executive Producer: Jett SteigerHead of Post Production: Grant KeinerPost Producer: Izzy ShillEditor: Grace Mcintee (House)Colorist: Didrik BraathernSound Design / Mix: Silver Aas

125 years of innovation, All-wheel drive, 125 years of in...

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A national brand film campaign by Opendoor titled 'The Hardest Part,' designed to highlight and simplify the challenging aspects of residential real estate transactions.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Homeowners and prospective buyers/sellers across the nation, especially those seeking or open to digital solutions for real estate transactions.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A national brand film campaign, likely leveraging television, online video platforms, and potentially connected TV for broad reach.
ai_enrichment.objective: To significantly boost national brand awareness for Opendoor and establish it as the go-to platform for easy residential real estate transactions.

metadata.title: Say Go and See the Cars Zoom!

content.description: In January, Netflix partnered with children’s creator Ms Rachel to bring her preschool content to the streaming platform, extending her reach beyond YouTube into a more traditional TV-style environment for families. The deal marked the start of Netflix’s effort to compete more directly with YouTube for family viewing time.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Netflix partnered with popular children's creator Ms Rachel to bring her preschool content to its streaming platform, aiming to extend her reach beyond YouTube. This strategic move is designed to attract families and directly challenge YouTube for family viewing market share.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Parents and guardians of preschool-aged children (approximately 2-5 years old), especially those already familiar with Ms Rachel's content on YouTube, seeking safe and engaging educational entertainment.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Content acquisition and licensing of popular preschool educational content (Ms Rachel's videos) for exclusive streaming on the Netflix platform. Promotion would likely leverage Netflix's internal channels and Ms Rachel's existing audience base.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase family viewing time and attract new subscribers to Netflix by offering popular children's content, directly competing with YouTube's dominance in the preschool entertainment space.

metadata.title: The Good, the Bad, and the Ouchy

content.description:
Amazon’s in-house team has launched a new campaign, ‘Save the Everyday’, with a hero film titled ‘The Good, the Bad, and the Ouchy’, drawing heavily on the visual language and tropes of classic Western cinema. Set against a dusty desert backdrop, the film follows a group of cowboys chasing down a moving train. Amid the action, one cowboy pauses mid-chase to order a pink silk scrunchie after losing his hat, then stops again to purchase anti-chafing balm before finally rejoining the pursuit. The story concludes with a package improbably delivered in the middle of nowhere, neatly resolving his mishaps.

The campaign leans into cinematic storytelling to dramatise everyday inconveniences, using humour and exaggeration to highlight Amazon’s speed and convenience. According to global chief creative officer Jo Shoesmith, the work was intentionally built around familiar film tropes, with the Oscars serving as a fitting stage for its debut. The stylised setting allows Amazon to elevate mundane problems into high-stakes moments, while still keeping its core product benefits clear.

Strategically, the campaign arrives at a time when value is a key concern for consumers in a challenging economic environment. Rather than focusing purely on price, Amazon uses a playful, narrative-driven approach to stand out, reinforcing both affordability and ease of access. ‘Save the Everyday’ positions the brand as a reliable solution for life’s small but frustrating disruptions, appealing to shoppers seeking both convenience and reassurance in their daily routines.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Amazon uses a humorous, exaggerated Western cinematic style to dramatize common everyday inconveniences, illustrating how its speed and convenience can resolve even the most unexpected needs, anytime, anywhere.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General consumers who experience everyday inconveniences and value quick, convenient solutions, appreciating humor in advertising.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film titled 'The Good, the Bad, and the Ouchy', distributed as a film (likely TV, online video platforms like YouTube).
ai_enrichment.objective: To highlight and strengthen the brand perception of Amazon's speed and convenience in resolving everyday needs.

content.description:
Texas toddler eyeing coveted milestone seeks H-E-B Bakery Butter Flour Tortilla.

For toddlers, they’re at a time in their lives when they reach several memorable milestones: getting a first tooth, taking their first steps, and uttering their first words. But none are as monumental for a Texas toddler than their first encounter with a soft, warm H-E-B Bakery Butter Flour Tortilla.

This year’s H-E-B Big Game spot centers around a young Texan with his eyes on the prize. A Texas favorite, H-E-B Bakery Butter Flour Tortillas ultimately become the toddler’s object of desire in this humorous skit that Texans, young and old, will relate to.

The adorable spot pays homage to one of H-E-B’s most identifiable and loved items. H-E-B Bakery Flour Tortillas are made fresh daily in store at H-E-B bakery locations across the Lone Star State. The soft, warm morsels are known to entice Texans to sneak a quick bite in the car after a grocery run. Eager travelers stuff packs of the floury delights inside their luggage to ensure they are stocked back home. And the enthusiasm around the H-E-B staple has even launched spin-off products such as butter tortilla scented candles and flour tortilla blankets.

“We always look forward to our Big Game commercials because they allow us to have a little fun with our fans and their favorite H-E-B products,” said Ashwin Nathan, H-E-B Group Vice President of Marketing. “There is something special about the bond between H-E-B and Texans, and these commercials are our way of celebrating and connecting with them during the biggest game of the year.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A humorous Big Game spot portrays a Texas toddler's desire for an H-E-B Bakery Butter Flour Tortilla as a monumental life milestone, making the beloved item the ultimate object of his affection.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Texan consumers, specifically families with young children and those who have a strong affinity for H-E-B and its iconic bakery products.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film broadcast as a commercial during the 'Big Game' (a major sporting event).
ai_enrichment.objective: To strengthen brand loyalty for H-E-B and drive preference for its Bakery Butter Flour Tortillas by connecting them to a relatable, humorous Texan cultural moment.

content.description:
What if, eight years from now, nothing made sense anymore?

Citroën and its agency BETC Paris unveil a satirical new film, “2034 ELECTION”. The 55-second spot imagines the world in 2034 where societal and technological upheavals have become the new normal, and even presidential elections have gone off the rails.

The film opens with a political news broadcast covering the presidential debate for 2034. The anchor presents the first two candidates in a seemingly normal fashion before arriving at the third candidate - a social media influencer - who also happens to be a dachshund in a tophat. While the crowd goes wild for the dog (who goes by Mr. Doodlesworth), we cut to a young man seated at home watching who can barely believe what he’s seeing.

As the absurdity unfolds, a calm voice reassures: “Don’t freak out. In 8 years, your Citroën will still be under warranty.” The hero glances out the window at his Citroën, parked safely in front of his house, a moment of relief in a wildly unpredictable world.

Produced by STINK / VERY CONTENT and directed by Traktor, the film blends cinematic craft with bold surrealism, amplified by the unmistakable energy of Village People’s “Macho Man”. This universal music adds a new touch of absurdity to this film, staying very much in line with the latest Citroën comedies.With “2034 ELECTION,” the brand turns reassurance into a statement. When the future looks unpredictable, Citroën chooses humor and delivers certainty.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Citroën's satirical film "2034 ELECTION" imagines a near-future where societal and technological chaos has normalized absurdities, including a dachshund as a presidential candidate, to comment on current trends and provoke thought.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General audience, particularly those aged 25-55, interested in current events, social commentary, future trends, and satire, who are potential car buyers.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 55-second satirical film spot, likely distributed on digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media) and potentially television, as a hero film.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand awareness and relevance, positioning Citroën as a thoughtful and concerned brand amidst potential future societal and technological upheavals, aligning with the campaign name 'We Care'.

content.description:
Myopia is an invisible epidemic set to hit 740 million children by 2050. Parents often ignore it, mistaking vision problems for lack of attention or laziness. The real issue? Parents can’t see what their kids see.

To make the invisible visible, we hacked the most visual place for kids during winter break: the Cinema, with an animated movie trailer. 'The Dream Thief' features a villain who steals clarity from children's eyes—and from the entire theater

In between normal cinema trailers, our trailer suddenly went out of focus, leaving the audience uncomfortable, cleaning their glasses, and squinting. We forced adults to physically experience the struggle of childhood myopia, turning a passive movie session into a collective diagnosis event.

 

Brand: ZEISSAgency: REF+

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: To make the invisible epidemic of childhood myopia visible, the campaign created an animated movie trailer, 'The Dream Thief,' which intentionally went out of focus in cinemas, forcing adults to physically experience the struggle of blurred vision.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Parents of children, particularly those unaware or neglecting the early signs of myopia, who attend cinemas.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Experiential cinema activation using a deliberately out-of-focus animated movie trailer inserted between regular trailers during winter break.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness about childhood myopia by making its symptoms tangible for parents, thereby prompting them to consider diagnosis and intervention.

content.description:
Naked Smoothie's Chief Smoothie Officer, Coco Gauff, is back to introduce the world to the New Naked with a series of lighthearted, tennis-world moments. Co-starring is her ever-enthusiastic––but often misguided––sidekick Smoothie Boy. Set on the court, the spots use playful miscommunication to spotlight New Naked’s refreshed packaging and boosted benefits, bringing the brand’s cheeky personality to life.

Across the spots, Smoothie Boy tries his hardest to keep up with the news––but keeps getting it hilariously wrong. Each mix-up becomes a playful way of reinforcing what the campaign sets out to do: make the new look and boosted benefits of New Naked unmistakably clear to everyone (even Smoothie Boy).

Developed in partnership with Dentsu Creative and dentsu X, the campaign is running through the end of March, with assets expected to stay in paid rotation for most of the year. The work spans Meta, TikTok, and YouTube.

Having collaborated with Coco previously, the team leaned into what resonates most authentically on set: her ability to laugh, play, and poke fun at moments from the perspective of a tennis athlete.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses lighthearted, tennis-themed film spots featuring Coco Gauff and her comedic sidekick, Smoothie Boy, to playfully reinforce and make unmistakably clear the refreshed packaging and boosted benefits of Naked Smoothies' 'New Naked' product.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Consumers of non-alcoholic beverages, particularly those interested in healthy lifestyles or sports, who are receptive to humorous and engaging content.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A series of comedic film spots featuring Coco Gauff and Smoothie Boy set on a tennis court, distributed across digital video platforms and potentially television to reach a broad audience.
ai_enrichment.objective: To introduce and build awareness for Naked Smoothies' 'New Naked' product, clearly communicating its refreshed packaging and enhanced benefits to consumers.

content.description:
Claritev unveils their latest brand campaign directed by Voyager’s Grayson Whitehurst via agency Brunswick, highlighting the cost of guesswork in healthcare decision making and the clarity that Claritev offers to the process.

https://www.claritev.com/more-clarity-less-guesswork/

The campaign rolls out across three spots, each built around a familiar workplace or scenario where random chance leads to poor outcomes. The first spot features a healthcare worker using a makeshift “price picker” out of ping pong balls to determine the price of a tonsillectomy. In the second, a man blindly throws darts around his office to optimize his healthcare plan the same way he chooses lunch, entirely at random. The third and final spot shifts to a golf course, where PGA Tour Pro Neal Shipley relies on a picker wheel to select a club for a crucial shot.

Grayson brings his signature ability to find humor in unexpected places, treating each scenario with subtle absurdity that underscores the risks of guesswork while keeping the tone grounded. Each spot closes with a clear alternative: using Claritev to make smarter, data-driven healthcare decisions with more clarity and less guesswork.

“We approached these spots with restraint,” shares Grayson. “Locked-off frames, wider compositions, neutral light - everything grounded in real spaces and played completely straight. The tone is observational and deadpan, letting awkward beats linger just long enough for the absurdity to surface on its own. That contrast is the point. When you treat randomness seriously, it stops being broad and starts feeling unsettling. The more sincerely we framed it, the more it revealed itself.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses three humorous film spots to illustrate the absurdity and negative consequences of making healthcare decisions based on random chance, positioning Claritev as the solution that brings much-needed clarity to the process.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adults involved in personal or organizational healthcare decision-making, including employees, individuals managing their healthcare plans, and potentially healthcare workers, who are frustrated by complexity and lack of transparency.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A series of three film spots, featuring relatable scenarios, are rolled out across various channels (likely TV and digital platforms) to deliver the campaign's core message.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise brand awareness for Claritev and establish its value proposition as the definitive provider of clarity in healthcare decision-making, highlighting the high cost of guesswork.
