metadata.title: Is your door in the draw?

content.description:
This Sunday, People's Postcode Lottery launches its Big Spring Win campaign, fronted by its celebrity team and promoting a £25.7 million prize pot in their April draws. Alongside the chance to win, the campaign reinforces the wider impact of participation, with players contributing to charitable projects across Britain and beyond.

Created in-house and directed by Vince Peone through The Gate+, the hero 60-second TV spot sees Davina McCall, Emma Willis, Mike Wedderburn and Fred Sirieix back on Britain’s doorsteps asking the question: “Is your door in the draw?”

The work continues to centre on real people and real locations, from city apartment blocks to rural cottages - reinforcing the message that any postcode can win, whether you own or rent. In one scene, Sirieix is comically chased from a working farm by a charging bull, a reminder that the team can arrive at any door, anywhere, and that entries close at midnight on 31 March.

This spring sees a new evolution in format. Alongside contextual ad reads on Sky Sports by Mike Wedderburn, the celebrity talent will also appear directly within broadcaster continuity segments across ITV and Channel 4, creating more seamless integration between the core creative and channel executions.

TikTok Postcode Millions event takeoverNew to the spring media plan is a TikTok activation designed to capture the live energy of prize day. During the April’s draws Postcode Millions event, influencers will accompany the Postcode Lottery team on selected door knocks, filming real-time winner reactions as big cheques are presented.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Celebrity ambassadors visit real doorsteps across Britain, asking 'Is your door in the draw?' to emphasize that any postcode can win, while also highlighting the charitable impact of participation.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: The general adult population in Britain, especially those interested in lottery games and contributing to charitable causes.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero 60-second TV spot (film medium) featuring celebrities Davina McCall, Emma Willis, Mike Wedderburn, and Fred Sirieix visiting diverse real-world locations.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive participation in the April draws by promoting a £25.7 million prize pot and reinforcing the brand's charitable contributions.

content.description:
Accelerator Active Energy, in partnership with AI-first creative agency Dolsten & Co., is tapping into Valentine’s Day dating chaos with two social-first campaigns that introduce the brand’s newest flavor, Citrus Freeze — and land a simple takeaway: even the “walking red flags” still choose clean energy.

Blind Date Taste Test: A first-date setup with strangers turns into a blind taste test challenge. As the chemistry unfolds on camera, participants sample Citrus Freeze and react in real time, naturally tying back to what Accelerator is built on: great taste, transparent ingredients, and functional energy.

Toxic Taste Test: Classic “red flag” archetypes, the ghoster, gaslighter, and cheater, go through a blind taste test of Citrus Freeze. Twist: despite the questionable dating habits, they all gravitate toward the cleaner option, reinforcing Accelerator’s better-for-you performance positioning.

 

Agency Credits: Dolsten & Co.CEO/CCO: Simon DolstenExecutive Director AI & Digital: Isaac RodriguezBusiness Lead: Sydney SadlerSenior Copywriter: Henry Plumridge

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Accelerator Active Energy is launching its new Citrus Freeze flavor with two social-first campaigns that use Valentine's Day dating chaos and 'red flag' archetypes to showcase the product's appeal and clean energy attributes.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young adults, 18-35, familiar with modern dating culture and 'red flag' humor, interested in energy drinks and clean energy options.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Social media video content featuring blind taste tests in two distinct scenarios: genuine first dates and with actors embodying classic 'red flag' dating archetypes (ghoster, gaslighter, cheater).
ai_enrichment.objective: To introduce the new Citrus Freeze flavor, drive product awareness, and reinforce Accelerator's core brand values of great taste, transparent ingredients, and functional energy.

content.description:
KIND Snacks, the brand known for its delicious and nutritious snacks, is bringing a little more kindness to mornings everywhere by partnering with reality TV star Rob Rausch to launch the RISE & KIND Breakfast Box, a limited-edition giveaway inspired by one of TV’s most-talked-about breakfast rituals.

Fans know Rob for his strategic gameplay, magnetic personality, and commitment to starting each day with a protein-packed breakfast—often featuring the now-iconic smoked Scottish salmon that quickly became a breakfast-room staple. Now, Rob and KIND are bringing that moment to fans everywhere, showing how a protein-forward breakfast can fuel both your body and a kinder mindset—all while making sure there’s plenty of the beloved salmon to go around.

KIND and Rob Rausch have teamed up to prove that even the most strategic competitors can CHOOSE KIND. Rob’s love of breakfast inspired the RISE & KIND Breakfast Box giveaway, which pairs KIND’s all-new Protein MAX Granola with Scottish salmon, delivering satisfying, protein essentials straight to your kitchen.

“This partnership with KIND is about starting the day on a positive note,” said Rob Rausch. "The RISE & KIND Breakfast Box gives people something simple they can enjoy and share in the morning, like we shared the salmon. If kindness is the first thing you lead with, it can set the tone for everything that comes after.”

WHAT’S IN THE BOX & HOW TO CLAIM ONETwo bags of the NEW KIND Protein MAX Granola, Peanut Butter and Sweet & Salty Caramel, packed with 15 grams of protein per serving and 0g of sugar to fuel your mornings and power you through the day.Scottish Salmon, the now-iconic breakfast star that sparked endless internet chatter is now available for fans to enjoy at home.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: KIND partners with reality TV star Rob Rausch to launch the limited-edition 'RISE & KIND Breakfast Box' giveaway, allowing fans to recreate his iconic protein-packed breakfast ritual and promote a kinder mindset.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Fans of reality TV, specifically followers of Rob Rausch, and individuals interested in protein-forward, nutritious breakfast solutions.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Limited-edition product giveaway (RISE & KIND Breakfast Box), celebrity endorsement and partnership with Rob Rausch, and integrated promotion likely across digital, social media, and PR channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase brand engagement and awareness for KIND within the breakfast category, leveraging celebrity influence to associate KIND with positive, protein-rich morning routines and a 'kinder mindset'.

content.description:
Ralph introduces SlopBowl: the not-so-useful AI tool for advertising’s annual moment in the spotlight

Ralph, the entertainment company  on a mission to make stuff people love and share, has today launched SlopBowl, a real-time AI roast machine built for Super Bowl Sunday — the one day of the year when 120 million Americans watch live broadcast TV and instantly become self-appointed ad critics.

Not everyone is funny. SlopBowl is. Built to relieve the pressure of dissecting, digesting and meme-ing the hell out of Meal Diamond, Will Shat and whatever gem Doritos pull out this year.

Point your phone at any Big Game ad and SlopBowl roasts it in real time - like having a jaded ad connoisseur on your couch who's seen every trick but still respects the craft. Powered by GPT Vision, SlopBowl watches alongside you, delivering punchy one-liners that land before the logo even fades to black.

As the hot takes roll in, users can say them aloud to wow/baffle their friends, or sling them into the group chat and wait for the verdict. Users can share their favorite roasts individually or drop a full highlight reel of every ad they've watched into a single post - ready-made content for the post-game group chat or Monday morning Slack chaos.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Ralph introduces SlopBowl, an AI-powered real-time 'roast machine' built for Super Bowl Sunday that allows users to point their phone at a Big Game ad and receive an instant, humorous critique.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Super Bowl viewers in the US who enjoy critiquing and discussing ads, especially those looking for a humorous and interactive way to engage with the commercials in real-time.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A mobile-first, experiential AI tool utilizing GPT Vision, accessible via phone, that provides real-time ad roasts during the Super Bowl broadcast.
ai_enrichment.objective: Generate brand awareness and engagement for Ralph by providing a unique, entertaining, and shareable real-time interactive experience during the Super Bowl.

content.description:
NIGERIAN ARMED FORCES - Take Your Flowers

NIGERIAN ARMED FORCES - Take Your Flowers

The Nigerian Armed Forces Remembrance Day, observed annually on January 15th, commemorates the end of the Civil War and serves as our nation's moment to honour fallen military heroes and celebrate serving personnel.Currently, the day is marked by key activities including wreath-laying ceremonies, military parades, and the launch of the remembrance emblem. However, a significant gap has emerged between the rituals of the day and the emotional connection of the public. Our core challenge is that these activities are perceived as formal routines, and the profound essence of sacrifice, honour, and national unity is not deeply felt by the average Nigerian. This body of work seeks to bridge that gap.

 

Agency: JT Agency, AbujaProduction Company: JT Agency, AbujaCreative Director: Olalekan AkinyeleCopy Writer: Olalekan Akinyele / Seyi AjagbeArt Director: Olalekan Akinyele / Ife Olulade / Timothy IgbokweDirector: Uduak EtukudoLead Photographer: Oluwasegun OgunleyePhotographer: Samuel Alabi / Ifeoluwa Agboola / Muhammad UmarVideographers:  Temitope Olanrewaju / Segun jana / Muhammad AbubakarEditor: Favour OjovwoVoice Over: Seyi AjagbeProject Manager : Blessing Adata-Jaja

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign aims to bridge the emotional gap between formal Remembrance Day rituals and the average Nigerian, fostering a deeper public connection and appreciation for the sacrifices of fallen heroes and serving military personnel.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: All Nigerians, particularly the general public who currently perceive Armed Forces Remembrance Day as a mere formal routine.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film as the primary medium, distributed widely to reach the general Nigerian public.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase public emotional engagement and appreciation for the Nigerian Armed Forces Remembrance Day, shifting perception from routine to profound national unity and honour.

content.description:
Director Kevin O’Brien directs the latest campaign for ThreatLocker, a Zero Trust cybersecurity solutions platform delivering enterprise security software to organizations worldwide. From initial ideation through final delivery, O’Brien remained creatively involved throughout, crafting a campaign that imagines an alternate universe where physical security works like most cybersecurity today -- responding only after the damage is done.

Turning digital risks into real-world scenarios, the hero spot opens in a money vault set to a classic blues tune, where a single security guard calmly allows people to file in and collect their money. The campaign unfolds across three scenarios: a man livestreaming from his phone in the middle of an operating room as surgeons work, a robber stuffing cash into a duffle bag inside the campaign’s opening vault, and a woman deliberately contaminating a fermenting liquid marked “Do Not Modify.” As each situation escalates, and the characters flee when authorities respond, the voiceover reminds viewers that reacting quickly isn’t enough when the breach has already happened. The spot concludes with the campaign’s central message, “Because a fast response isn’t fast enough. Stop data breaches before they start with Zero Trust from ThreatLocker.”

“This project started as an open canvas, and the idea that stuck was an absurd alternate universe where physical security works like most cybersecurity today, detect and respond,” shares Kevin. “Collaboration was central from the start. Everyone involved wanted this to be as strong as possible, which created a healthy creative push and pull throughout the process. Success isn’t just the final product. It’s how we get there: early decisions, trust across the team, and kindness from beginning to end, all while making something truly great together. Everyone was extremely happy with the end result.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign visualizes an alternate universe where physical security operates reactively, like most current cybersecurity, allowing damage before intervention. It translates digital risks into absurd, real-world scenarios to highlight the critical need for proactive security.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: IT security professionals, CIOs, CISOs, and other decision-makers within organizations worldwide responsible for implementing enterprise cybersecurity solutions.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film (medium: Film) depicting three distinct scenarios (money vault, operating room, robber) that illustrate reactive security failures, likely distributed via digital platforms relevant to B2B technology audiences.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness about the inherent flaws of traditional reactive cybersecurity and to drive consideration for ThreatLocker's proactive Zero Trust cybersecurity solutions among enterprise organizations.

content.description:
'Unbelievably Cool' has picked up the baton from 'Unbelievably Long Lasting', a creative milestone that boosted sales in the European market and was recognised with 9 medals at El Ojo de Iberoamérica, a Clio Health and 3 Suns at the Malaga Festival.

Directed by Maxi Blanco for Landia, the spot highlights the unique freshness of Old Spice’s Cold Spice line of antiperspirants and shower gels.

Created by the independent Spanish agency Burns, 'Unbelievably Cool' represents the second chapter of the brand's creative platform 'Unbelievably.' While the first campaign focused on product durability, this instalment focuses on the sensation of freshness and sensory benefits.

The campaign, developed entirely in Spain and filmed in Mexico, launches in 28 European markets through television, digital, outdoor and point of sale, and its centrepiece is a 60 second film which shows how Cold Spice can keep you cool even in the most extreme situations, like being trapped on a deserted island in sweltering heat.

The star of the show is an antiperspirant that promises freshness, a cooling effect, and protection against sweat. The best way to showcase this, Old Spice-style? A delightfully absurd twist: a remote island, surfing penguins, and a pleasantly perfumed castaway.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign, a sequel to 'Unbelievably Long Lasting', focuses on the unique, sensory freshness provided by Old Spice's Cold Spice line of antiperspirants and shower gels.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Men in 28 European markets interested in personal hygiene products, specifically those seeking effective antiperspirants and shower gels with a strong emphasis on freshness and sensory benefits.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film directed by Maxi Blanco for Landia, distributed across television and digital platforms in 28 European markets.
ai_enrichment.objective: Drive sales and market penetration for Old Spice's Cold Spice antiperspirants and shower gels by emphasizing their unique freshness and sensory benefits, building upon the successful 'Unbelievably' creative platform.

content.description:
The film playfully reframes our modern obsession with excess and reminds us that perfection often lies in balance. “Just Enough Extra” taps into a cultural truth. In a media landscape overflowing with spectacle, outrage and relentless volume, many of us have asked: Why are you so extra?

Chicken Licken’s answer is simple: be extra where it counts. And nowhere does it count more than in the Soulman Load Up.

Not too much. Not too little.Just enough extra.

ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Primary execution is a hero film for television and potentially online video platforms, leveraging a playful narrative to communicate the core message.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive preference and sales for Chicken Licken's Soulman Load Up product by associating it with a relatable cultural truth about balanced indulgence ('Just Enough Extra').

content.description:
Nature’s Heart redefined cravings, turning them into a healthier way to take care of yourself

Nature’s Heart redefined cravings, turning them into a healthier way to take care of yourself

For its new campaign, Nature’s Heart Ecuador, together with Publicis Groupe, launched “Come mejor, siéntete mejor”, a creative approach that redefines the role of cravings in everyday eating habits. Instead of portraying "cravings" as something to avoid or feel guilty about, the campaign turns them into a positive trigger for better choices.

The idea is simple but powerful: cravings don’t disappear they follow you. And when they do, Nature’s Heart shows that you don’t have to run from them or give up flavor to start taking care of yourself. By placing antojos at the center of the story, the campaign connects with real moments and favorite recipes, proving that eating better doesn’t mean sacrificing taste, enjoyment, or the foods people love.Through a playful, relatable visual language, cravings come to life as constant companionspresent throughout the day until they are satisfied with delicious, better-for-you options.

This shift positions Nature’s Heart as a brand that understands real eating behaviors and invites people to eat better in a way that feels natural, accessible, and emotionally close.Come mejor, siéntete mejor.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign redefines cravings from something to avoid into positive triggers for healthier choices, showing that one can indulge healthily with Nature's Heart products without sacrificing flavor.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Health-conscious individuals and those seeking healthier eating habits who struggle with managing cravings, particularly in Ecuador.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A core campaign film (medium: Film) focused on a narrative redefining cravings, supported by a creative approach across various unspecified media channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To redefine the perception of cravings as positive triggers for healthier eating choices and position Nature's Heart as the go-to brand for healthy indulgence.

content.description:
20 seconds. Short for an ad creative. An eternity for a Kencko consumer.“The fastest smoothie ever” is the title of a TV campaign made up of four films, built around a simple idea:Showing just how quickly a Kencko smoothie is prepared. So fast that, even in a 20-second ad, there’s still time left.

 

Advertising Agency: Clube RecriativoCreative Directors: Kiko Pedreira, Vasco GasparArt Director: Carlos GasparCopywriter: Kiko PedreiraDirector: Duarte LemosDOP: Tomás Paiva Raposo

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign visually demonstrates the unparalleled speed of Kencko smoothie preparation, showcasing that even within a short 20-second ad, there's significant time remaining after making the smoothie.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Health-conscious individuals and busy professionals who prioritize convenience and quick, nutritious meal or snack options, typically those short on time but unwilling to compromise on healthy eating.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A series of four 20-second TV commercials (films) broadcast as a dedicated TV campaign.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand awareness and differentiate Kencko by vividly highlighting the core product benefit of incredibly fast and easy smoothie preparation.

metadata.title: Ramadan Offer 2026
tags: Ads of the World Highlighted Ramadan Campaigns

content.description:
In a bold and unconventional Ramadan campaign, Zain Iraq presented its seasonal offers through a vibrant musical-comedy spectacle that transformed a simple sales inquiry into an unforgettable entertainment experience. Executed by Khumra Group and creatively directed by Muhanned Abu Khumra, the commercial was the result of a dynamic collaboration between Zain’s team and Ogilvy, merging strategic marketing with high-end cinematic production.

The film begins with a relatable everyday moment: a customer walks into a modest mobile phone shop and asks the familiar question — which network offers the strongest and best deals? The salesman confidently presents the “Kafu” line from Zain. The instant the customer asks about its offers, reality shifts. Music erupts, lighting transforms, and the ordinary shop explodes into a theatrical musical performance.

The salesman morphs into a charismatic stage performer, singing and dancing in a playful, exaggerated style reminiscent of classic theatrical comedy. Walls open dramatically, transporting both the customer and the audience into a colorful carnival-like universe — a fully immersive “World of Zain.” Each bundle, from internet packages to call offers and 4.5G services, is represented as a dynamic performance zone with choreography, live music, bold lighting, and imaginative set design.

Khumra Group delivered a large-scale production built on:

Multi-level theatrical set construction symbolizing each offer as a distinct visual environment.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Zain Iraq's Ramadan campaign transforms a typical customer sales inquiry into an elaborate, vibrant musical-comedy spectacle to creatively present its seasonal offers and the 'Kafu' mobile line.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Mobile phone users in Iraq seeking new network offers, particularly during the Ramadan period, who appreciate engaging and entertaining advertising.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A central musical-comedy commercial/film distributed across TV and streaming platforms, forming the core of a broader 360° campaign during Ramadan.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand engagement, recall, and interest in Zain Iraq's 'Kafu' line offers during Ramadan by providing an unforgettable entertainment experience.

metadata.title: Kicks Off Official Partnership with LAFC

content.description:
Following the news in October 2025 around the three-season partnership with the Los Angeles Football Club (LAFC), Liquid I.V. today released never before seen content celebrating the collaboration ahead of the 2026 season opener set to take place this Saturday Feb. 21 at 6:30pm PST at the Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum against Inter Miami CF.

The social-first content features players like Aaron Long, Nathan Ordaz, Denis Bouanga, Nkosi Tafari alongside a celebrity part owner of LAFC to highlight how LAFC leverages performance science in player training and how Liquid I.V. provides support where performance matters most, delivering hydration faster and more effectively than water alone.

As the Official Functional Hydration Partner, Liquid I.V. is bringing LAFC’s hydration game to the next level. To kick-off the season, Liquid I.V. will have digital signage in-stadium and the brand’s custom built electric Hydratrucks, featuring HOPE Hydration refillable water systems, will be on-site at the FanFest celebration, starting at 2PM, where guests can snag free product samples to hydrate prior to kick-off. Additionally, this season back at LAFC’s home turf, BMO Stadium, Liquid I.V. will be sold in the concession stands across the stadium.

 

Liquid I.V.® and its robots are taking over Times Square

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Liquid I.V. is celebrating its official three-season partnership with LAFC through social-first content and OOH advertising, showcasing how its functional hydration product enhances player performance and recovery.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: LAFC fans, sports enthusiasts, individuals interested in athletic performance and hydration, and broader consumers seeking effective hydration solutions.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: OOH Outdoor advertising, social-first content featuring LAFC players (Aaron Long, Nathan Ordaz, Denis Bouanga, Nkosi Tafari) and a celebrity part-owner, launched ahead of the 2026 season opener.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand awareness for Liquid I.V. as the official functional hydration partner of LAFC, highlight its product's role in elite sports performance, and celebrate the new collaboration.

metadata.title: New Interactive Ways to Shop Modular Sofas

content.description:
New Interactive Ways to Shop Modular Sofas

On most e-commerce furniture sites, people start by browsing collection pages and comparing looks; here, the brand’s new shopping flow puts interactive tools upfront.  The Sofa Expert is a one-on-one appointment—available in-person or virtually—that collapses the entire process into a single session. It’s designed for busy people who want a free, concierge-style way to shorten the entire research cycle: view fabrics and finishes live, compare favorites side-by-side, review configurations and sizing, and get questions answered before momentum fades.

Instead of bouncing between tabs, screenshots, and half-decisions, a one-on-one session turns the “bookmarked sofa” into a clear, guided collaboration—one appointment, one expert, and a clean path. The new age furniture store flips the order into something closer to a modern buying standard: preference-led and interactive by default.

The brand’s interactive flow is built for that reality—so the decision starts with how you live, stays clear at every step, and settles exactly where you do. Workdays that stretch longer than planned. Movie nights that turn into sleep. Hosting that starts as drinks and ends with everyone posted up, shoes off, leaning in.  Mario Capasa is betting on a simple truth: people aren’t shopping for “a sectional,” they’re shopping for the version of their life that happens on it.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign launches 'The Sofa Expert,' a one-on-one interactive appointment service (in-person or virtual) designed to condense the modular sofa shopping process into a single, guided session.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Busy individuals who are looking to purchase modular sofas and are seeking an efficient, personalized, concierge-style shopping experience to avoid research fatigue and simplify decision-making.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Offering one-on-one interactive appointments ('The Sofa Expert') available both virtually and in-person, integrated as a primary feature on digital e-commerce platforms to guide customers through the shopping flow.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase customer engagement and conversion rates for modular sofas by offering a streamlined, personalized shopping experience that reduces friction and decision fatigue.

metadata.title: 10 Year Anniversary - All for the Crown

content.description:
Clash Royale celebrates its 10-year anniversary with a film created by DAVID New York. The new campaign for Clash Royale is an ode to the game’s passionate community, featuring a combination of fan-created content, heroic live action, and animation.

The film features a mix of fan-generated content and heroic live-action elements inspired by players who have gone to the extreme, from dressing up as the game’s Goblin characters in parks to students who have taken the game’s iconic emotes into classrooms.

The film also includes cameos from a variety of notable influencers, content creators, and e-sports players who have served as a cornerstone of the community over the past 10 years.

The campaign is just the start of Clash Royale’s 10-year anniversary celebrations, which will continue throughout the month with in-game events.

"Over the past 10 years, there’s been so much passion for the game that has manifested as this unique kind of content on social media. We wanted to celebrate all the absurd fandom, and the community that has helped make the game what it is today,” says André Toledo, Chief Creative Officer at DAVID New York.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A 10-year anniversary film by Clash Royale that celebrates its dedicated community through a dynamic blend of fan-generated content, heroic live-action, animation, and appearances by key influencers.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Existing passionate Clash Royale players, loyal fans, and followers of gaming influencers and esports.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film combining fan-generated content, live-action, animation, and influencer cameos, serving as the kickoff for ongoing 10-year anniversary celebrations.
ai_enrichment.objective: To celebrate Clash Royale's 10-year anniversary, engage its passionate community, and reinforce brand loyalty.

content.description:
when Burgasko launched its new brand film, something unexpected happened: people didn’t skip the ad — they searched for the song. Assuming it was a famous track, thousands tried to find it on Shazam and social media, only to discover it was an original composition. Instead of simply answering the question, Burgasko officially released the song as a single across streaming platforms, turning curiosity into culture. What started as an ad became the unofficial anthem of Summer 2025 — and the commercial people chose to watch.

 .

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign leveraged unexpected audience engagement with an original song from a brand film by officially releasing it as a single on streaming platforms, transforming an ad into a cultural anthem.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General consumers, particularly younger audiences aged 18-35, who are active on social media, use music streaming services, and are receptive to trending cultural content.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Launch of a brand film featuring an original song, followed by an official release of the song as a single across major music streaming platforms to capitalize on audience curiosity and search behavior on platforms like Shazam and social media.
ai_enrichment.objective: To significantly boost brand engagement, cultural relevance, and create an emotional connection with the audience by turning an advertisement into a sought-after piece of popular culture.

content.description:
Building on the success of its McDo+ app which was launched in 2025, McDonald’s France is pushing its mobile strategy further with a new gaming experience.

‘Course Givree’ - meaning Frosty Race - is a fun, fast paced gaming experience which transports players into McDonaldland, the brand’s iconic imaginary universe created in the 1970s, and brought to life by legendary mascots including Grimace, Hamburglar, Birdie, and the Fry Kids.

The game was created by Razorfish and designed and developed by makemeplay, the gaming arm of creative tech studio, makemepulse, and puts players in the role of Grimace - the lovable purple character and true pop culture icon - as he hurtles down the snowy slopes of McDonaldland on a snowmobile.

The objective is simple: achieve the highest score by collecting as many blueberries as possible, the signature ingredient of Grimace’s milkshake. The more berries players collect, the more points they earn - and the more valuable seconds they unlock for the spectacular ‘Mega Jump’ bonus phase, allowing them to scoop up an avalanche of milkshakes.

But the descent is anything but smooth. To stay on track, players must demonstrate sharp reflexes and precision to dodge giant ice balls, frozen holes, signs, and slippery caramel streams scattered along the way.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: McDonald's France launched 'Course Givree', a mobile gaming experience within its McDo+ app where players control Grimace on a snowmobile in McDonaldland, aiming for high scores. This fun, fast-paced game leverages iconic brand mascots to further develop their mobile strategy.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Users of the McDo+ app, mobile gamers, and fans of McDonald's iconic characters, particularly those aged 13-35 interested in interactive brand experiences and nostalgic elements.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Launch of a new, engaging mobile gaming experience called 'Course Givree', directly integrated into McDonald's France's existing McDo+ app. The game was developed by Razorfish and makemeplay, featuring Grimace as the playable character.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance user engagement with the McDo+ app and further advance McDonald's France's mobile strategy by offering a unique and interactive gaming experience.

metadata.title: New Menu Launch Stars Wonho Chung

content.description:
KFC Arabia has launched its new Korean BBQ Twister with a campaign that asks: what happens when you give a bilingual comedian full creative control over your menu launch? Chaos. Delicious, culturally-aware chaos.The brand partnered with Wonho Chung, the world's only Arabic-speaking Korean comedian, to introduce Korean flavors to the region. But instead of playing it safe with a standard endorsement, they let Wonho become the overconfident "expert" who meddles with the recipe, rewrites the messaging, and even attempts to rebrand KFC as "Korean Fried Chicken."Spoiler: the kitchen staff lock him out.The campaign, created with TBWA\RAAD, tackles a real paradox: how do you localize a global food trend without losing authenticity, or your brand identity? The answer: lean into the tension with humor and self-awareness."Korean flavors are dominating global food culture, but we didn't want to just borrow the trend, we wanted to make it ours," said Ahmed Arafa, Chief Marketing Officer at KFC Arabia. "Wonho gave us the perfect way to bridge cultures while staying unmistakably KFC."Derek Green, Chief Creative Officer at TBWA\RAAD, added: "Korean BBQ is everywhere, which means every brand will try to own it. We could've played it safe, but KFC doesn't do safe. We handed creative control to a comedian who speaks both languages, literally and culturally, and let him go too far. That's where the magic lives."The campaign rolls out across digital, social, and in-store, targeting young, trend-driven audiences and loyal Twister fans looking for their next go-to order.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: KFC Arabia launched its new Korean BBQ Twister by giving bilingual comedian Wonho Chung full creative control, leading to humorous chaos as he attempts to overly 'Koreanize' the brand and its new menu item.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Younger adults and food enthusiasts in the MENA region interested in trying new international flavors, specifically Korean cuisine, who appreciate humor and culturally relevant content.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film and potentially a series of short-form video content featuring Wonho Chung, distributed across digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media) and potentially traditional TV.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate awareness, excitement, and drive trial for KFC Arabia's new Korean BBQ Twister by engaging the audience with a humorous and culturally resonant campaign.

metadata.title: Dip Into A World of Flavor

content.description:
Domino’s, the UK's favorite pizza takeaway, has today unveiled ‘Dip Into A World of Flavor’, a playful new campaign created by UK creative agency of record VCCP, promoting its new chicken concept, CHICK ’N’ DIP.

Domino’s is synonymous with takeaway pizza, and CHICK ‘N’ DIP now sits alongside it as part of the brands’ core offer. It features boneless bites, wings, tenders, and nine globally inspired dips. The recent rollout to nearly 1,400 stores aims to showcase the brand’s versatility and reinforce its reputation for delivering high-quality food at an unbeatable speed (a delivery time of 25 minutes on average). CHICK ‘N’ DIP meets the growing consumer demand for chicken and gives customers more reasons to choose Domino’s.

CHICK ’N’ DIP promises to bring big flavor to the night-in experience by offering customers chicken alongside a wealth of dips inspired by tastes from around the world. From the fire of Buffalo Hot Dip to the zest of Mexican Mayo and the richness of Katsu Curry Dip, Domino’s new concept transforms a simple meal into an immersive experience. To capture the bold energy of CHICK ’N’ DIP, Domino’s has introduced a unique cast of characters who represent each dip: a world flavour adventure, delivered.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Domino's 'Dip Into A World of Flavor' campaign introduces CHICK 'N' DIP, a new chicken concept featuring boneless bites, wings, tenders, and nine globally inspired dips, designed to expand Domino's menu and enhance the 'night-in' takeaway experience.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: UK consumers who enjoy takeaway food for their 'night-in' experience, particularly those seeking high-quality and fast-delivered chicken options from a trusted brand.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Film-based advertising, likely including TV commercials and online video content, developed by VCCP to promote the new CHICK 'N' DIP product across nearly 1,400 stores.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive awareness and sales of the new CHICK 'N' DIP product, showcase Domino's versatility beyond pizza, and reinforce its reputation for delivering high-quality, fast food to meet growing consumer demand for chicken.

content.description:
“Breakthroughs... almost”, co-created by Perk and agency AnalogFolk and produced by Untold Fable, promotes Perk’s new global brand platform – which unites travel and spend management into a single experience – by taking a satirical swipe at the annoying admin work that saps business productivity.The campaign imagines how history’s greatest innovators, from Isaac Newton to Marie Curie, might have fared if their breakthroughs had been derailed by booking trips, filing expenses, coding invoices or chasing approvals.Perk, the AI-native platform for travel and spend management, calls these tasks “shadow work” – invisible, non-core work employees do outside their main job. New research by Forrester Consulting, commissioned by Perk, found that shadow work costs businesses in the US, UK, France, Germany, Spain and the Netherlands $1.7 trillion annually, with employees losing around seven hours a week and 11 minutes of focus each time they switch back to core tasks.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses satire to imagine how historical innovators would be hindered by business admin, promoting Perk's new platform that unifies travel and spend management by spotlighting the productivity cost of 'shadow work'.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Businesses and employees experiencing productivity loss due to administrative tasks related to travel and expense management, who are seeking integrated, AI-native solutions.

ai_enrichment.objective: To promote Perk’s new global brand platform that unifies travel and spend management, while raising awareness of the impact of 'shadow work' on business productivity.

content.description:
“I Can’t Imagine” Isn’t Good EnoughChildhood Cancer Canada launches “Let’s Face the Unimaginable,” a new national awareness platform by The Garden

Childhood Cancer Canada (CCC) is launching a new national brand platform and awareness campaign, “Let’s Face the Unimaginable,” developed by independent agency The Garden, part of Humanise Collective. The work challenges the reflexive response many people have when confronted with childhood cancer — “I can’t imagine” — and seeks to reframe public understanding of the lifelong, family-wide realities that follow.

Right now, more than 10,000 families in Canada are caring for a child with cancer, and an average of six children are diagnosed every day. CCC is the leading national organization in Canada focused exclusively on children’s cancer, supporting families through awareness, research funding, and long-term emotional and financial care.

At the centre of the campaign is a hero film that asks viewers to pause and imagine the unimaginable, the cumulative impact of childhood cancer beyond the moment of diagnosis, from job loss and financial strain to relocation, isolation, and the emotional toll carried by parents and siblings alike. Defined by raw, unguarded moments, the film captures how childhood cancer continues to shape family life long after diagnosis.

Directed by Jason van Bruggen of Suneeva, the film features real CCC families at various stages of treatment and recovery. Shot with a deliberately small, intimate crew, the production prioritized sensitivity and trust, drawing directly from families’ lived experiences to shape the visual narrative.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign challenges the common 'I can’t imagine' response to childhood cancer, reframing public understanding to highlight the profound, lifelong, and family-wide realities of the disease.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: The general Canadian public, specifically those who may have a limited understanding of the long-term impacts of childhood cancer.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A national awareness platform centered around film content, developed by The Garden, suggesting distribution via broadcast and/or digital video channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise national awareness about childhood cancer and reframe public understanding of its lifelong impacts on families.

content.description:
Telstra is launching an ambitious sequel to its ‘wherever we go’ brand campaign, building on the cultural response to the now iconic Telstra walk, often affectionately referred to as the ‘Telstrut’.

Directed by Smith & Foulkes from Riff Raff Films, the campaign sees the return of ‘Mick’ and his partner continuing their whistling duet, but this time inspiring a wide collection of fun characters to join in. The core idea of the ‘duet’ is a metaphor for the partnership and possibility that sits at the heart of Telstra’s promise to all Australians.

Launching as a 90 second film to coincide with the start of the footy season, the campaign is also supported by a series of 15s spots featuring standalone duets.

In out-of-home, an array of walking legs have been brought to life by hand – with intricate layers of folded and crafted paper created by artist Jeff Nishinaka. Shot by Carl Kleiner, the range of artworks is unique to each media site.

Alita McMenamin, Telstra Head of Brand and Marketing Communications, said: “We were surprised to see more than 27 million views on TikTok, with people across the world creating their own versions of the Telstra walk, last time. This new iteration of the campaign is inspired by the fact it’s not just about the journey, it’s about those you share it with. It’s been a joy to work on. The team has put so much care and craft into the campaign execution.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign is a sequel to the popular 'Telstrut' walk, featuring 'Mick' and his partner's whistling duet inspiring diverse characters, symbolizing partnership and possibility for all Australians.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: All Australians, with a broad appeal leveraging mainstream cultural moments such as the footy season.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 90-second hero film launching with the footy season, complemented by a series of 15-second spots and out-of-home (OOH) advertising featuring hand-drawn walking legs.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build on the established brand recognition and cultural connection of the 'Telstrut' campaign, reinforcing Telstra's brand promise of partnership and possibility.

content.description:
In February, speed dating is on fire. Test drives? Not so much. This year, Renault decided to flip the script. We leaned into a simple yet powerful insight: everyone can talk a big game about who they are, but you only truly know someone when they’re behind the wheel.

That’s how "Drive Me Crazy" was born an experience that hijacked the traditional speed dating format and transformed it. We swapped the bar stool for the driver’s seat, turning the first date into a real-world test, free from filters or rehearsed speeches.

As they navigated through traffic and curves, participants forged genuine connections while discovering the new Renault E-Tech range. The result was an experience where technology became the stage for authentic human interaction, proving that a perfect match isn't just made between two people it’s also made with a Renault.

 

Chief Creative Officer: Andrés RedrovánCreative Director: Xavier PradoHead of Art: Luis YacelgaCopywriter: Verónica CastroAccount Director: Andrés NavasAccount Executive: Dayana OramasGraphic Designer: Gabriela LarreaMotion Graphics Designer: Alex ManosalvasMultimedia Support: María Gabriela QuinteroCommunity Manager: María José Bastidas

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Renault's "Drive Me Crazy" campaign transformed traditional speed dating into a test drive experience, allowing participants to forge genuine connections while discovering the new E-Tech range behind the wheel.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Singles interested in authentic connections and experiencing new automotive technology, likely urban-dwelling potential car buyers.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: An experiential marketing activation that repurposed the speed dating format, replacing conventional venues with the driver's seat of new Renault E-Tech vehicles for real-world test drives.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase product discovery and engagement with the new Renault E-Tech range by creating a memorable, authentic human interaction experience.

content.description:
NYF now has a new Triple Cheese Poutine. Something your taste buds can sing about—thank God!

Oso Audio directed and produced an original gospel-inspired track to ring in the occasion.

 

Music/Audio: Oso AudioCreative Director: Harry KnazanComposers: Adrian Underhill, Lisa YafalianProducer: Kristine LippettSingers: Kyla Charter, Alex Samaras

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: New York Fries introduced its Triple Cheese Poutine with a gospel-inspired original track, humorously elevating the indulgent new menu item to a praise-worthy, almost divine experience.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Fast-food consumers, particularly young adults aged 18-35, who enjoy indulgent comfort food and appreciate humorous, culturally resonant marketing.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Production and distribution of an original gospel-inspired audio-visual content piece (song/music video) across digital and social media channels to announce and promote the new product.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate awareness and create buzz for the new Triple Cheese Poutine, encouraging product trial and engagement.

content.description:
JustFoodForDogs has a bone to pick with dog dinners, and they’re getting loud about it in the #1 vet-recommended fresh dog food brand’s first national campaign with full-service agency Giant Spoon.

Pets deserve better than stale dog food standards. The pioneers in science backed, vet-formulated fresh dog nutrition tapped a dog-loving (and people not-so-loving) human spokesperson to berate on dogs’ behalf the state of fresh food: “fresh” by name, just because it’s stored in the fridge. That’s not good enough for JustFoodForDogs and their menu of real, 100% human grade ingredients. In the campaign, the irate owner may not be home for callers, but he’s always here to stand up for dog diets.The ad will air across Meta and Amazon for CTV & YouTube, with local linear TV spanning news, entertainment and sports content, as well as placements during Olympics broadcasts in Chicago and San Diego.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: JustFoodForDogs' first national campaign features an "irate owner" spokesperson who passionately challenges misleading "fresh" claims in the dog food market, advocating for their genuinely human-grade, science-backed dog nutrition.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Dog owners who prioritize their pets' health and nutrition, seeking high-quality, human-grade fresh food alternatives and are skeptical of existing 'fresh by name' products.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Ads featuring a human spokesperson will be broadcast across digital platforms including Meta, Amazon (for CTV and YouTube), and on local linear TV spanning news and entertainment channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build national brand awareness for JustFoodForDogs and differentiate its truly human-grade fresh dog food from competitors that offer only refrigerated products.

content.description:
FDJ (French leader in lottery games, creator of the bi-weekly lottery brand LOTO) and BETC Paris are reviving France's most addictive investigation with "Grandma's Secret," a mockumentary-style true crime show that transforms a simple Sunday lunch into a national scandal.

Since 2023, the LOTO platform "It Doesn't Only Happen to Others" has been based on an insight as true as it is captivating: winners don't usually share the good news to their loved ones. Not out of malice, but to avoid disrupting their daily lives. They prefer to improve their lives little by little, almost discreetly, until suspicion creeps in.

This is how "Grandma" was born: a mysterious grandmother, an ordinary Sunday lunch, and an entire family that begins to notice something was looking funny. Surimi that looks a little too much like lobster. Gifts that are a little too generous. Trips... And a question that obsesses everyone: Did Grandma win, yes or no?

GRANDMA'S SECRET: A TRUE CRIME TRAILER, LIKE ON NETFLIXTo launch this new chapter, FDJ and BETC Paris unveil “Grandma’s Secret”: the highly stylized trailer for a Netflix-style documentary, suspenseful and narrative-driven, inspired by the major investigations that captivate French households. Interviews, surveillance videos, dubious family photos, absurd clues… All the ingredients of true crimes are there. Except here, the mystery isn’t a murder, but a lottery ticket.

A 2-minute trailer, and its official poster, like a real series release.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses a mockumentary-style true crime show, 'Grandma's Secret,' to depict a family's humorous 'investigation' into their grandmother's subtle lifestyle changes, fueled by the suspicion that she won the LOTO and is keeping it a secret.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adults who play or might consider playing LOTO, who appreciate relatable humor and the discreet fantasy of winning the lottery, likely within France.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A mockumentary-style true crime show (likely a hero film or a series of spots), distributed as an integrated campaign across TV, digital, and social media platforms to generate a 'national scandal' narrative.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase engagement and consideration for the LOTO brand by humorously illustrating a relatable post-win scenario and reinforcing the brand platform that 'It Doesn't Only Happen to Others'.

At the heart of the campaign from Greenhonchos Solutions is a cinematic brand film crafted entirely using advanced AI models, blending hyper-real visuals with poetic storytelling to shape a new era of fashion content creation.

metadata.title: Tomás Pola — Turning curiosity into quality

content.description:
Tomás Pola — Turning curiosity into quality

Bavaria wanted an educational platform to talk about the quality of its ingredients.But instead of explaining processes, formulas or technical data, we started from something much more human: questions.People don’t learn about beer in classrooms.They learn in conversations.Among friends, couples and families asking simple but powerful questions:Why is the bottle green?Why is dark beer dark?Does taste really change?So we created Tomás Pola, a character who doesn’t know about beer because he studied it, but because he loves it.A former psychologist turned beer enthusiast, who answers everyday questions with passion, humor and cultural insight.Through short-form entertainment content, Tomás Pola became the voice that connects curiosity with craftsmanship — showing that every beer made by Bavaria is quality beer.Because quality isn’t something you explain.It’s something you understand when the right question is asked.

 

Advertising Agency: Zelva AgencyGrowth Director: Pedro SantosChief Creative Officer: Hamilton PeñaCreative Directors: Felipe Miranda, Wilmar DazaArt Directors: Wilmar Daza, Jenny Saenz, Sofía Naranjo De ZulategiCopywriters: Felipe Miranda, Luis Felipe HernándezMotion Designers: Juan José Ocampo, Brayan JaraProduction: Dirty Kitchen

Stylish since 1719, Fashionable since 1719, The independe...

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Bavaria created Tomás Pola, a relatable beer enthusiast character, to answer common beer quality questions with humor and cultural insight. This approach transforms everyday curiosity into an engaging educational platform, fostering deeper understanding without technical jargon.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adult beer drinkers who are curious about beer quality and facts, preferring relatable, conversational explanations over technical data, including friends, couples, and families.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Development and distribution of short-form entertainment content featuring the Tomás Pola character, deployed across a 360° media strategy.
ai_enrichment.objective: To educate consumers about the quality of Bavaria's ingredients and beer through an accessible, engaging platform, fostering brand connection and understanding of craftsmanship.

content.description:
Sepenuhnya Indonesia (Wholeheartedly Indonesia)

Starring celebrated Indonesian actor, Ario Bayu, ‘The Boat’ launches McDonald’s Sepenuhnya Indonesia (Wholeheartedly Indonesia) year-long 35th anniversary campaign.

Starring celebrated Indonesian actor, Ario Bayu, ‘The Boat’ launches McDonald’s Sepenuhnya Indonesia (Wholeheartedly Indonesia) year-long 35th anniversary campaign.

It’s been 35 years, but it feels like yesterday since McDonald’s first opened its doors in Indonesia. For decades, McDonald’s Indonesia has proudly called Indonesia home, and embraced local flavours, tradition, culture, community – and above all, the nation’s deeply-rooted values: unity in diversity. Sincerity. Generosity. Inclusivity. Positivity. Gotong royong (communal cooperation). The very things that make Indonesia uniquely Indonesian but are often overlooked and taken for granted.

Starring celebrated Indonesian actor, Ario Bayu, the film titled ‘The Boat’ launches McDonald’s Sepenuhnya Indonesia (Wholeheartedly Indonesia) year-long 35th anniversary campaign.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: McDonald's Indonesia celebrates its 35th anniversary with a year-long campaign, 'Sepenuhnya Indonesia (Wholeheartedly Indonesia)', launched by a film featuring actor Ario Bayu, highlighting its integration with Indonesian culture, values, and community.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: The general Indonesian public, especially those who value local culture, tradition, community, and national values like unity and communal cooperation, including existing and potential McDonald's customers.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Launch of a hero film titled 'The Boat' featuring celebrated Indonesian actor Ario Bayu, serving as the kick-off for a year-long anniversary campaign.
ai_enrichment.objective: To celebrate McDonald's Indonesia's 35th anniversary, reinforce its deep-rooted connection and commitment to Indonesian culture and values, and strengthen brand affinity and loyalty among the Indonesian population.

content.description:
New project makes period underwear part of the school uniform, guaranteeing access and menstrual dignity for students.

As the new school year begins, a special initiative is challenging the menstrual inequity that prevents many young girls from attending school regularly. Supported by the Vice Minister of Education, Somos Martina, a Colombian leader in sustainable intimate care, has integrated period care into the official school uniform.The matter is urgent. Across Latin America, 1 in 4 girls regularly misses school during their period because they don't have access to period products. This is linked to a deeprooted menstrual stigma: Periods are often not even discussed at home, which makes it difficult for girls to ask for support. Without protection, many of them stay home to avoid leaks and the shame that comes with them. Repeated absence affects education and future opportunities.“It's a big problem for us that girls stay home because they fear a leak or cannot afford pads. Our responsibility is to ensure no student is left behind. By adding period care to the uniform, we fulfill our promise of an equal education,” says Jhoana Rincón M., Teacher at Institución Educativa Mayor de Mosquera – the first school to officially adopt The Period Uniform, in Bogotá.

A Fundamentally Different ApproachSchool uniforms, which are mandatory in Colombia, have long been used to promote inclusion and equality in public education. The Period Uniform extends this principle to period care. Instead of distributing period products through temporary, local programs that require separate logistics, the Period Uniform model builds on the existing school uniform system, making period care accessible by design.Launched at Institución Educativa Mayor de Mosquera in January, the initiative is now expanding to additional schools, supported by Lucy Maritza Molina Acosta, Vice Minister of Education in Colombia. “It is fundamental to talk about menstrual health in education in Colombia. And I believe that menstrual care should be discussed in all schools," she says. The principle of the initiative is straightforward, add period underwear to the list of required school items.The Period Uniform achieves four main goals: Normalize Period Protection by turning period protection from an extra into the standard; Avoids stigma by lifting the burden of initiating the conversation from girls, as schools communicate the requirement to parents; Works at Scale by building with existing school uniform infrastructure and processes which, once established, works for every school, without additional effort; and Reduces Cost, as period underwear is already more affordable than single-use period products over time. As part of the initiative, Somos Martina provides period underwear to participating schools at a price comparable to regular underwear, along with access to information and resources for period care.To inspire public discussion and encourage the government to adopt the model and inspire a worldwide conversation, Somos Martina has launched a global awareness campaign – not only in Colombia, but in different countries. It includes a dedicated website with more information accessible in different languages, and an editorial by New York Times contributing journalist María F. Fitzgerald, as well as a short film by award-winning director Claudia Barral, which illustrates the reality faced by students during their period. The initiative was created in partnership with the agency Serviceplan Innovation.

Why Period Underwear?Experts agree that period underwear is the best choice for young girls. It's noninvasive and functions just like the daily underwear they already know, and is more comfortable while eliminating the skin irritation and health risks associated with pads or tampons. Unlike disposables, it provides up to 12 hours of protection, suitable for long school days. With a lifespan of up to 3 years, period underwear is significantly more affordable and environmentally sustainable than single-use alternatives."This initiative is rooted in our mission to fight for menstrual justice. Supporting girls during their first period allows us to build trust and lifelong relationships. We believe period underwear is the best option to reconnect with our bodies and take care of the environment. I look forward to it becoming a standard for all women," says Patricia Franco Reyes, CMO at Somos Martina.

A Global IssueThe lack of access to menstrual products and education is a global issue. According to UN Women, 1 in 4 teens in the United States struggle to afford period products, especially teens of colour and lower-income households. In the United Kingdom, the same thing happens for 3 in 10 girls – and more than half of them used toilet paper instead. The World Bank estimates that 500 million girls and women lack access to menstrual products and adequate facilities for menstrual hygiene management (MHM) – around 35% of all people menstruating.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The Period Uniform is an initiative by Somos Martina, supported by the Vice Minister of Education, integrating period underwear into official school uniforms to guarantee access to menstrual products and uphold students' menstrual dignity.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young girls and female students in Latin America who lack access to menstrual products and face menstrual stigma, leading to school absenteeism.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Policy integration of period underwear into official school uniforms, facilitated by collaboration with the Vice Minister of Education, and direct distribution to students.
ai_enrichment.objective: To guarantee access to period products and uphold menstrual dignity for students, thereby reducing school absenteeism caused by menstrual inequity and stigma.

metadata.title: A Bit Of A Pill
metadata.brand: SC BHDD Office of Substance Use Services

content.description: It just takes one fake pill to kill. And because the fentanyl in counterfeit pills is unevenly distributed, there's no sure way to know whether that pill in your hand is going to be your last.To raise awareness about this lethal "chocolate chip cookie effect," the effort brings a pill to life to save lives - and open up conversation between adults and kids around a devastating reality.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: To highlight the lethal 'chocolate chip cookie effect' of fentanyl in counterfeit pills, the campaign personifies a pill to visually demonstrate this deadly risk and initiate vital conversations between adults and kids about substance use.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Parents, guardians, and their children (primarily adolescents and teens) who need to be educated about the dangers of counterfeit fentanyl pills and how to discuss substance use.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Educational content featuring an animated or personified pill (likely video/digital content), distributed via public service announcements, social media, and educational platforms to spark dialogue.
ai_enrichment.objective: Raise awareness about the lethal danger of fentanyl in counterfeit pills and facilitate crucial conversations between adults and children to prevent substance use fatalities.

metadata.title: Defy Being Silenced By Flipping The Script

content.description:
Defy Being Silenced By Flipping The Script

The North Carolina LGBT+ Chamber’s lingering reputation as a social club, overshadowed their business-related services, making it difficult to attract new, younger members. As visibility and impact waned, we set out to renew interest in the chamber’s mission of inspiring, supporting and engaging underserved businesspeople and their allies. Starting with a new, inclusive brand identity - HARMONY - we created a series of eye-catching messages revealing core values everyone could embrace.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign revitalizes the North Carolina LGBT+ Chamber, now Harmony, by establishing a new inclusive brand identity and creating eye-catching messages to shift its perception from a social club to a vital business support organization.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Younger, underserved LGBT+ businesspeople and their allies in North Carolina who are seeking professional support and community engagement.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Development of a new, inclusive brand identity (HARMONY) and creation of a series of eye-catching messages designed to reveal and communicate core values.
ai_enrichment.objective: To renew interest in the chamber's mission, attract new and younger members, and increase its overall visibility and impact by redefining its brand identity.
