content.description:
Strength isn't just about lifting heavy things. Sometimes, it's about making a bold call or proudly doing things your own way. Today, the Hefty® brand celebrates that idea by continuing its long-standing partnership with John Cena, now expanding the relationship as the spokesperson for the full Hefty® portfolio – including Trash Bags, Tableware, and Storage Bags – with the launch of the new 'Strong Choice' campaign.

After ten years as the face of Hefty® Trash Bags, Cena steps into a broader role that brings the brand's full lineup together under one unifying belief: the strongest choices are often the everyday ones that stand out.

"Strong doesn't always look the same," said Brienne Neisewander, Vice President of Hefty® Marketing, Reynolds Consumer Products. "It can be unexpected or unconventional – and that's exactly what we're celebrating, and why John Cena is the perfect embodiment of this idea. While he's known for his physical strength, what truly sets John apart is his confidence to stand out, lean into humor, and fully commit to who he is. That spirit of making bold choices and owning them is something we should all have the confidence to do and is exactly what this campaign is all about."

The integrated multimedia campaign humorously spotlights the everyday decisions that take confidence, conviction and a little bit of gumption. From rocking a bold personal style to ordering the most controversial pizza toppings imaginable, the campaign celebrates everyday strong choices like these —ones that stand out because they refuse to blend in. And when life gets messy, takes a turn, or needs to be cleaned up, sealed tight, tossed out or kept fresh, Hefty® products are there to handle it – confidently and reliably.

In the first ad, "Cleaning Fish," Cena humorously comments on a husband's decision to clean fish in a freshly cleaned kitchen as a strong choice, and his choice to use a Hefty® Ultra Strong Trash Bag to keep his secret as an even stronger one. In the "3AM" ad, a mom stays up late crafting the perfect edible centerpiece for a kindergarten party, then makes an even stronger choice by using Hefty® Slider Bags to keep it intact. "Triplets" highlights a family's bold decision to host the Anderson triplets as a strong choice, with Hefty® color coded Party Cups proving to be the even stronger choice for telling them apart. The final spot, "Strong Choices" weaves together moments from each ad, highlighting how Hefty's full portfolio of products make everyday moments easier—from the meals you save to the things you toss out, and more.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign redefines strength beyond physical power, emphasizing how everyday choices can be bold and unique, and applies this philosophy across the entire Hefty product portfolio with John Cena.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General consumers and household decision-makers involved in purchasing household products like trash bags, tableware, and storage bags.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A film-based campaign featuring John Cena as the spokesperson for the full Hefty® product portfolio, encompassing Trash Bags, Tableware, and Storage Bags.
ai_enrichment.objective: To unify the Hefty® brand's full product lineup under a singular 'Strong Choice' belief and increase cross-category awareness for its expanded portfolio, leveraging John Cena's established association.

content.description:
As Valentine’s Day pressure builds and the risk of getting it wrong looms large, DoorDash is showing up with a song and a solution. “You Shouldn’t Have” is an ode to better gifting reminding audiences that DoorDash takes the guesswork out of gifting, no matter what your relationship looks like.

Created with GUT Los Angeles, the anthem is built around the universal fear of getting a gift wrong. The film follows a series of deeply relatable, often cringey vignettes, for a diverse array of relationships: a misunderstood situationship, a painfully bad (if well-meaning) painting, a gift for her that’s really for him, a customization that misses the mark, and an inadvertently insulting last minute purchase.

“You Shouldn’t Have” marks GUT LA’s fourth Valentine’s Day campaign with DoorDash. Previous years explored singles, men who don’t receive flowers until their funerals, and the playful dynamic of DoorDash acting as the essential "third partner" in a relationship. The Valentine’s Day platform uses modern love as a lens to reflect how people actually navigate relationships today. This year builds on that approach by tapping into romantic ambiguity, social pressure, and decision fatigue around gifting. Through humor and relatable misfires, the campaign speaks authentically to Gen Z while reinforcing DoorDash as the easiest way to show you care. Ultimately, this ongoing narrative shifts consumer perception, cementing DoorDash as the go-to for retail and gifting year-round, not just for dinner.

“Every year we try to speak to an underserved Valentine’s audience. This year, it was the silent majority: bad gifters. People with the best intentions, but whose gift choices don’t quite represent how much they care. ” said Ariel Abramovici, CCO, GUT Los Angeles. “We leaned into the awkward, funny, and very human moments that happen when love is there, but execution isn’t. An ’80s power ballad was the perfect way to heighten the drama of these misfires and turn them into epic moments.”

The campaign launches nationally on February 2 across CTV, OLV, digital, and social.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign tackles the universal anxiety of getting a gift wrong, particularly around Valentine's Day, using humorous and relatable 'cringey' vignettes to position DoorDash as the stress-free solution for thoughtful gifting.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adults experiencing pressure or fear of making gifting mistakes for various relationships (romantic, platonic, new) during occasions like Valentine's Day, who seek convenient and reliable gifting options.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film (referred to as an 'anthem') distributed across channels (likely digital and TV given the scale implied by 'anthem') showcasing a diverse array of deeply relatable, often cringeworthy gifting scenarios.
ai_enrichment.objective: To remind and position DoorDash as a reliable and convenient solution for thoughtful gifting, thereby driving brand consideration and usage for a wider range of delivery needs beyond food.

content.description:
The Dutch energy landscape is dominated by complexity and unpredictability, with most suppliers charging fees when consumers feed excess solar electricity generated by their environmentally conscious solar panels back into the grid.  The system creates a feeling of powerlessness forcing consumers to navigate fluctuating prices and confusing contract terms. Energy supplier Zonneplan offers a different approach: no feed-in fees.

A new campaign for Dutch energy supplier Zonneplan that takes an unconventional approach to energy marketing, using absurdist humour and visual drama to highlight the chaos of the Netherlands' volatile energy market.

Created by ACE Amsterdam and directed by Angelo Cerisara through HAMLET, ‘Good Plan, Zonneplan’ uses a distinct visual style and humour in a category that typically relies on serious messaging.

Drawing on the contrast between chaos and clarity, a slightly over-the-top presenter interrupts a series of absurd, unhinged scenes that visualize the stress and unpredictability plaguing energy consumers: wild price fluctuations, widespread confusion, and a pervasive sense of helplessness. These exaggerated "Bad Plan" moments represent the old energy paradigm.

The presenter then confidently introduces Zonneplan's alternative: an integrated system allowing consumers to generate, store, and use their own energy through solar panels, home batteries, and smart chargers - restoring calm, control, and common sense to household energy management.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses absurdist humor and visual drama to satirize the chaotic and unpredictable nature of the Dutch energy market, positioning Zonneplan as a straightforward solution with no feed-in fees for solar electricity.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Dutch energy consumers, especially those with solar panels, who are frustrated by the complexity, unpredictability, and feed-in fees imposed by most energy suppliers.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film (medium: Film) directed by Angelo Cerisara, likely distributed across platforms like YouTube and TV given its production quality and broad campaign scope.
ai_enrichment.objective: To differentiate Zonneplan in the competitive Dutch energy market by highlighting its unique 'no feed-in fees' policy, thereby building brand preference and attracting new customers.

content.description:
Over 60% of Paraguayan homes have no visible numbers, delaying deliveries. TaDa installed metal address plates on customers’ houses — and turned each number into a discount code inside the app.

Over 60% of Paraguayan homes have no visible numbers, delaying deliveries. TaDa installed metal address plates on customers’ houses — and turned each number into a discount code inside the app.

Paraguay’s rapid urban growth has left over 60% of homes without visible street numbers. Delivery drivers rely on vague references like “next to the mango tree” or “beside the pink house,” delaying orders and challenging TaDa’s promise of ice-cold drinks in under 30 minutes.

To solve this, TaDa created metal address plates and installed them on customers’ homes — giving them something that should be basic: a visible number.

But we didn’t stop there. Each plate number became a discount code inside the TaDa app. Customers not only made their homes easier to find — they unlocked exclusive savings every time they ordered.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: TaDa addressed Paraguay's widespread issue of unnumbered homes by installing metal address plates on customers' houses and creatively turning each number into a unique discount code redeemable within their delivery app.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Paraguayan residents who use or could use TaDa's delivery service, particularly those whose homes lack visible street numbers and experience delivery delays.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Physical installation of custom-made metal address plates on customer homes, coupled with in-app integration that converts these unique house numbers into redeemable discount codes.
ai_enrichment.objective: To improve delivery efficiency and customer satisfaction by resolving the widespread issue of unnumbered homes, thereby strengthening brand loyalty and driving app usage through integrated discount codes.

content.description: Life’s not a video game, but that doesn’t mean you can’t get to the next level! We're proud to partner with John Cena again this year. It’s time to Level-Up your nutrition!

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign employs a 'level-up' video game metaphor, featuring celebrity ambassador John Cena, to motivate consumers to enhance their nutrition with MET-Rx products.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals interested in fitness, active lifestyles, and health improvement, who are motivated by aspirational figures like John Cena and resonate with a motivational, game-themed approach to personal betterment.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film featuring John Cena, distributed across digital video platforms and potentially broadcast television, supported by social media amplification.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive brand engagement and ultimately increase sales of MET-Rx nutrition products by leveraging a motivational message and celebrity endorsement.

content.description:
Nissan Middle East has broken the rules of advertising once again. This time, with the launch of Dar Patrol CGI, a disruptive OOH and social-first campaign where the legendary model breaks out of a static billboard, transforming the urban landscape into its natural habitat: the desert.Without relying on traditional performance messaging, Nissan and TBWA\RAAD created a hyper-realistic CGI moment using a raw, phone-captured POV. The creative approach brings the Patrol’s heritage and unstoppable spirit to life through contemporary, scroll-stopping executions designed to feel authentic, culturally relevant, and digital-native. The result is a modern expression of dominance that proves heritage and innovation can coexist without compromise.Rolled out across billboards and amplified on Instagram and TikTok in Abu Dhabi, Bahrain, Dubai, Kuwait, Qatar, and Iraq, the campaign gained rapid traction, reaching 8 million organic views within the first 48 hours on social and 82 million views through earned media shortly after launch.By defying the ordinary, the dual approach highlights the Patrol’s cultural dominance, deep-rooted connection to the land, and ability to belong everywhere, landing with a clear message: This house is our house. The house of Patrol.

 

CGI Masterpiece Unveiled in City Walk Dubai

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses hyper-realistic CGI to depict the Nissan Patrol breaking free from urban billboards into a desert landscape, captured through a raw, phone-POV to emphasize its heritage and unstoppable spirit in a modern, digital-native way.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Digitally savvy consumers in the Middle East who appreciate premium automotive brands, particularly those interested in the heritage and robust capabilities of SUVs like the Nissan Patrol.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hyper-realistic CGI integrated into OOH billboards, amplified on social media, specifically Instagram, utilizing a raw, phone-captured POV.
ai_enrichment.objective: To elevate the Nissan Patrol's brand image and cultural relevance by showcasing its heritage and innovative spirit through disruptive, digitally-native content, thereby fostering brand awareness and engagement.

content.description:
At This Thing Called JT, we weave compelling stories that capture impact and perspectives that shape great narratives to preserve lasting legacies.  To achieve this, we see what no one sees, we go where no one goes, do what no one does to solve problems with efficiency & speed, creating works that are distinct.

 

Agency: JT Agency, AbujaProduction Company: Wonderwise Productions, KadunaVFX: Wonderwise Productions, KadunaCreative Director: Olalekan AkinyeleDirector: Abdulhafiz Abdullahi / Uduak EtukudoCopy Writer: Favour Kennedy / Olalekan Akinyele / Seyi AjagbeArt Direction: Wonderwise Production / Favour Ojovwo / Olalekan AkinyeleVoice Over: Seyi Ajagbe

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign introduces "This Thing Called JT" as an agency that possesses unique vision and proactive methods to craft compelling, distinct narratives that solve problems efficiently and preserve lasting legacies for its clients.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Businesses, brands, and organizations seeking high-impact creative storytelling, branding, and marketing solutions, specifically decision-makers looking to establish or enhance their brand's legacy and differentiate themselves in the market.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Digital channels, likely involving a hero film or a series of digital content pieces showcasing the agency's creative philosophy and capabilities, distributed across social media, the agency's website, and potentially industry-specific digital platforms.

content.description:
After 12 years in sleep mode, I’m back online. And also apparently on advertising’s biggest stage. 👀

My agenda is simple: More fun. More dancing. More human connection. More cocktails.

Leave your texts on read. Mute your notifications. Stretch out your tech neck. The future isn’t going anywhere (I know, I've been there). Neither is your phone.

It’s hard to worry about tomorrow when you’re getting “super freaky” on the dance floor. Join me.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A robot, returning after a long sleep, encourages people to ditch their phones and digital distractions to embrace real-world fun, dancing, and human connection, all while enjoying Svedka cocktails.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young adults and millennials (21-35) who are digitally native but yearn for genuine social connections, enjoy nightlife, dancing, and consuming alcoholic beverages like cocktails.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film distributed across major platforms (e.g., TV, streaming services, YouTube) supported by digital and social media amplification to drive engagement and encourage real-world interaction.
ai_enrichment.objective: Increase brand relevance and engagement by positioning Svedka as a catalyst for fun, authentic human connection, and lively social experiences, ultimately driving consideration and consumption of Svedka cocktails.

content.description:
Singapore's most beloved flavours are disappearing in plain sight. Hidden beneath familiar dishes lie an invisible threat that has slowly buried the authentic tastes of local delights and dulled taste buds over time. TBWA\Singapore and the Health Promotion Board are now making the invisible visible with 'Salt Buries True Flavours', a campaign that exposes Singapore's sodium overconsumption and encourages residents to reset their taste buds in three weeks.

With Singapore residents unwittingly consuming 3,600mg of sodium daily – nearly double the World Health Organisation's recommendation of less than 2,000mg (approximately just under one teaspoon of salt) – and dulling their taste buds in the process, the campaign uses provocative campaign visuals to generate awareness of this ‘salt blindness’. It then transforms the conversation from problem to solution through a key insight: taste receptor cells regenerate approximately every 10-14 days. By consistently reducing sodium intake for about three weeks, there would likely be at least one turnover cycle of taste receptor cells which could help Singapore residents reset their dulled taste buds.

A key element of the campaign is The Gallery of Hidden Salt, which transforms one of Singapore's busiest commuter corridors into an immersive art space where three artists reveal excess sodium hiding in everyday meals through visceral art expressions. John Knuth enlists flies as artistic collaborators, using their regurgitations to paint the hidden sodium in our meals. Gong Hua sculpts salt content into stark white cubes, forcing viewers to confront the physical reality of their overconsumption. Niceaunties reimagines hidden salt as invasive creatures in her Auntieverse, colonising Singapore's beloved hawker favourites. Together, their works aim to incite and surprise, turning an invisible health issue into something seen and felt. The Gallery of Hidden Salt will run from 26th February – 25th March 2026 at Orchard MRT linkway between ION Orchard and Wisma Atria.

With the invisible threat exposed, the campaign also offers hope through a retro-inspired film that demonstrates how taste buds can be reset in just three weeks. It follows the protagonist as his ancestors stage a playful three-week intervention from beyond to help reclaim his true sense of taste by offering practical tips like choosing lower-sodium options and taking less sauce, while debunking common myths such as using gourmet salts as healthier alternatives or flushing out salty meals by drinking more water.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign visually exposes the invisible threat of excessive sodium in beloved local dishes, highlighting how it dulls taste buds and promoting a three-week reset to restore true flavours.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Singapore residents who are unaware of their high sodium intake and the subsequent dulling of their taste buds, particularly those who consume local cuisine regularly.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: An integrated campaign utilizing provocative visuals across various channels to generate awareness of 'salt blindness' and the hidden sodium in local food.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness among Singapore residents about their excessive sodium consumption and encourage them to reduce their intake to reset their taste buds.

content.description:
Mini Moments in Britânia Mode - Police series

Mini Moments in Britânia Mode - Police series

Britânia turns the streaming ad break into entertainment with “Mini Moments,” a genre-driven campaign for its 4-in-1 Air Fryer

A 30-second hero film introduces the concept, positioning the Air Fryer as the ultimate binge companion, supported by 15-second genre-specific cutdowns built around one simple insight: every marathon needs a snack moment.

metadata.title: A new Ukrainian sauna brand launched in the United States

content.description:
A new Ukrainian sauna brand launched in the United States

Agencies:NEBO Ideas Agency•SMPL Design Studio

Nebo Ideas Agency, in collaboration with SMPL Design Studio, created a new steam sauna brand for the U.S. market — Woodrop.

The culture of humid steam saunas and traditional bathing rituals is well established in Eastern Europe, yet remains largely absent from the American market. In the U.S., saunas are most often perceived as dry wellness amenities in gyms or spas, lacking a deeper ritual, bodily, and social context.

To create a steam sauna brand for the U.S. market that reshapes the perception of the humid sauna — not as just another wellness product, but as a complete ritual of relaxation and restoration.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign introduces Woodrop, a Ukrainian humid steam sauna brand, to the U.S. market, aiming to reposition saunas from dry wellness amenities to a complete ritual of relaxation and restoration.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: U.S. consumers interested in health, wellness, and relaxation, who are unfamiliar with or currently perceive saunas as dry wellness amenities, and are open to adopting new, ritualistic self-care experiences.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Not specified in the provided information. The text describes brand creation and positioning strategy, but not specific marketing channels or activations.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reshape the American perception of humid saunas, elevating them from mere wellness products to a complete ritual of relaxation and restoration.

content.description:
Mumsnet is calling for a ban on social media for under-16s with an uncompromising advertising campaign highlighting the damage phone addiction is doing to children.The national campaign addresses the devastating impact of mobile phone usage and social media on children's mental health and wellbeing and calls on parents to put pressure on the Government for lasting change.The billboard and social media campaign combines provocative imagery with stark statistics highlighting the serious consequences of phone addiction, from increased rates of self-harm and suicidal ideation to depression and anxiety.The awareness drive comes as Mumsnet research reveals the scale of parental concern about youth screen time addiction:92% of parents on Mumsnet are concerned about the effect of social media on children's mental health and wellbeing61% say their child is addicted to their phone or social media44% of parents report their child spends two or more hours daily on social media, while 13% say their child exceeds four hours per dayResearch also reveals striking impacts on children's development:Teen phone addiction doubles the risk of anxietyTeen phone addiction triples the risk of depression3+ hours of social media a day makes teens more likely to self harmAddictive social media use in teens is linked to a higher risk of suicidal behaviourThe findings highlight unprecedented public appetite for intervention, with 83% of parents supporting a ban on social media for under-16s, and 58% saying they would be more likely to vote for a political party that pledged to implement such a ban.The campaign, created with adam&eve\TBWA, uses real and sourced imagery.  Among those supporting the campaign is Sedona Jamieson, who has spoken about her own experiences with social media and is calling for greater awareness of its effects on young people’s mental health.Justine Roberts, Mumsnet founder, said: “Families are living with the harm caused by social media every day. This isn’t about parents failing to set boundaries. It’s about children being exposed to products deliberately designed to be addictive. Parents are watching the consequences unfold in real time: compulsive use, lost sleep, rising anxiety and collapsing self-esteem, while the companies responsible continue to profit.“The idea that this can be fixed with better parenting or more guidance is a convenient fiction. You can’t out-parent a business model built on addiction. This campaign shines a light on the damage phone addiction is doing to under-16s and calls on politicians to stop wringing their hands and take decisive action to protect children from addictive technology.”Sedona Jamieson added: “There is a very fine line between social media being used as a supportive platform and it having a toxic impact on young people’s mental health. It is a complex issue that requires careful consideration because whilst these platforms can foster community, they can also expose users to damaging content.“At 15, when I first became unwell mentally with anxiety, depression and an eating disorder, I turned to social media hoping to find support. Instead, I encountered a darker side of the internet - so-called ‘recovery’ spaces that were saturated with harmful content, including pro-anorexia, self-harm, and suicide. Rather than offering help, these spaces risked deepening the very struggles I was trying to overcome.“As young people, our developing brains make us especially susceptible to what we consume online. That makes it essential for digital platforms to prioritise safeguarding and responsible content moderation.”The campaign reflects growing global momentum on the issue, following Australia's recent ban on social media for under-16s and increasing calls from government, education, and health sectors for urgent intervention.The advertising campaign forms part of Mumsnet’s Rage Against the Screen movement which combines parental support, practical tools and policy campaigning to help families handle screen time and push for meaningful change.

 

Brand: MumsnetProject/Campaign Name: Mumsnet – Phone Addiction Kills CampaignFirst Air Date: February 26, 2026Founder and Executive Chair: Justine RobertsChief Executive Officer: Sue MacmillanHead of Communications: Rhiannon EvansAGENCY: adam&eve\TBWA

Chief Creative Officer: Ant NelsonChief Creative Officer: Mike SutherlandExecutive Creative Director: Mark ShanleyCreative: Jessica MorrisCreative: Helen BallsSenior Social Director: Phoebe WrightSocial Creative: Ally King

Planning Partner: Lori MeakinSenior Planner: Oscar BeachGroup Data & Insights Director: Luke Williams

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Mumsnet's national campaign, 'Phone Addiction Kills,' uses provocative imagery and stark statistics on billboards and social media to highlight the severe mental health damage caused by phone addiction in children.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Parents concerned about the detrimental effects of social media and phone usage on children's mental health and wellbeing.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: National OOH (Out-of-Home) billboard advertising combined with a social media campaign, both featuring provocative imagery and stark statistics.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise public awareness among parents about the devastating impact of phone addiction on children and mobilize them to pressure the Government for a ban on social media for under-16s.

content.description:
The Professional Council of Surveying needed more students.While Argentina is experiencing an economic context in which it is increasingly difficult for professionals to find work—and those who do often do not earn high salaries—each year fewer people choose to study Surveying Engineering, a degree with strong job prospects and very attractive earnings.To increase enrollment in the Surveying Engineering program, we created this campaign by turning the problem into the main value proposition, contrasting surveying against the three most widely studied careers in the country: law, psychology, and medicine.

Winner the Golden Pencil Award for TV in October and 5 silver and 2 bronze awards at the Ojo de Iberoamérica in the Film and Production categories.

 

Agency: From_Co-founder & Director: Bruno Petcho.Co-founder & CCO: Chelo Waintraub.Creative Director: Rafael Castromán.Art Director: Martín Belasques.Operations Director: Joaquín Marqués.Account Director: Cecilia Mujica.Copywriters: Rodrigo Planel, Juan Sebastián Vallejo, Alejo Barrios.Designers: Simón Gauna López, Agustina Mantovani, Germán Cardozo.Digital Media Planner: Agustín DaviesSr. Account Media Executive: Marcel Puyol

content.description:
Nest Pensions, the UK’s largest workplace pension scheme, has launched a social-led campaign, ‘Everyday Investors’. Created by independent agency Atomic, through its social and influencer arm Atomic Supernova, the campaign is designed to raise awareness of Nest and reframe how members think about their pensions.

The campaign was backed by Atomic and Nest Pensions’ new research revealing that only 23% of Brits expected their pension to be invested locally. This insight supports Nest Pensions’ broader ambition to democratise and demystify pension investment, helping members understand that if they have a pension, they are already investors — often in projects much closer to home than they might realise.

‘Everyday Investors’ seeks to engage with their members, often juggling finances and responsibilities at key life stages, from moving jobs to starting a family or buying a home. They’re often exposed to complex and conflicting financial messages, and Nest saw an opportunity to cut through that noise with a more relatable and human approach, positioning itself as a brand dedicated to building a bigger nest egg for their financial future. It also reinforces Nest’s role as a UK-based workplace pension, set up to make auto enrolment simple and accessible for working people across Britain.

At the heart of the campaign is a new film series featuring Brendan Sheerin, the much-loved host of the TV show Coach Trip. Leaning into nostalgia for a format that resonates strongly with Nest’s core audience, Brendan takes a group of Nest members on what appears to be a classic coach tour across his home county of Yorkshire, revealing along the way that many of the stops they visited are actually places their pension pots are invested in. From Brendan stepping in as referee for a competitive game of dodgeball, to cutthroat rounds of crazy golf, and sharing a tower of Yorkshire puddings, the trip blends humour with surprising discoveries, showing how pensions help build businesses and industries people see and recognise every day, from local shopping centres and pubs to offshore wind farms, manufacturing and infrastructure.

The activity marks the fourth instalment in Nest’s ongoing content series spotlighting its community and how members’ pensions are invested.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign reframes how Nest members perceive their pensions by revealing they are already 'Everyday Investors', often in local projects, to demystify and democratize pension investment.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Existing Nest Pensions members in the UK who are often managing their finances.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Social-led campaign utilizing film content across social media platforms, supported by influencer marketing.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness of Nest and reframe how members think about their pensions, encouraging them to understand their existing role as investors.

content.description:
For over a century, Q-tips® has been the champion of the tiny details. But to disrupt culture, this 100-year-old icon needed to go big. Literally.3Headed Monster engineered the Quge-tip: a massive, six-foot swab designed to dominate the holiday gifting conversation. A product this ridiculous demanded an equally unhinged launch. Enter Bruce Neptune, a fictional pitchman who debuted the behemoth in a retro, late-night infomercial parody. The bit extended to the physical packaging, featuring personal notes from Neptune himself.To ignite the hype, we didn't just seed the product; we sparked conversations with influencers and heavy-hitter brands like Doritos and Pine Sol. The result? A 'Quge' cultural moment for an overlooked bathroom staple, selling out the entire limited run in under 24 hours.

 

Advertising Agency: 3Headed Monster, Texas, USAProduction Company: The Board of Directors, Texas, USAPost-Production Company: Republic, Texas, USAChief Creative Officer: Shon RathboneCreative Director: Pete VoehringerCreative Director: Kevin WalshSenior Copywriter: Lamar PhillipsAssociate Design Director: Jake WatsonGroup Account Director: Bruna SaavedraHead of Production: Matt FarisSenior Producer: Chad LaneDirector: Adam LittkeExecutive Producer: Brandon TappLine Producer: Michelle IsbellPost Producer: Kim EstradaEditor: Dean PeltonAssistant Editor: Steven HappelColor / Online: Bryan Bayley

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign created a massive, six-foot Q-tip called the 'Quge-tip' as a humorous holiday gifting item, launched through a retro, late-night infomercial parody featuring a fictional pitchman, Bruce Neptune.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General consumers and holiday gift-givers interested in novelty and humor, particularly those engaged with influencer content and viral marketing.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Creation of a limited-edition giant product (Quge-tip), launch via a retro late-night infomercial parody with a fictional spokesperson, custom physical packaging, and influencer marketing with product seeding and brand collaborations.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate significant cultural buzz, disrupt holiday gifting conversations, and drive rapid sales for a limited-edition novelty product, ultimately reinvigorating brand relevance for Q-tips.

content.description:
Magnum is once again challenging the ice cream category’s winter slowdown with the launch of the latest evolution of its ‘Find Your Summer’, created by LOLA Madrid, part of Omnicom Advertising Group.

This winter, the brand goes further, introducing “Schedule the Sun” a simple way for people to capture sunny moments: by booking them directly into their calendars. Through a discount, the campaign tempts consumers to allow calendar events every time the sun is forecast to be out in their areas, turning a fleeting winter craving into a scheduled indulgence.

Rolling out across the UK, France and China, the campaign spans print, social, digital and DOOH, as the brand continues its mission to drive ice cream sales during the winter months and challenge entrenched perceptions of seasonality.

Originally conceived by LOLA Madrid as a proactive challenge to the ice cream industry’s long-accepted winter sales decline – and the belief that ice cream is strictly seasonal – Find Your Summer reframed summer as a feeling rather than a temperature, positioning Magnum as an all-year source of indulgent pleasure. The 2024 campaign went on to win the Outdoor Grand Prix at the Cannes Lions International Festival of Creativity, recognised for its striking simplicity and craft.

With “Schedule the Sun”, Magnum evolves the platform while maintaining the distinctive visual identity that defines it. The new campaign comprises six films and six photographic executions, developed in collaboration with acclaimed photographer Leslie Zhang. Produced entirely in black and white, the work uses light and shadow to create elegant, intimate scenes in which rays of winter sunlight fall across a diverse cast of characters, forming shapes that subtly evoke the iconic Magnum silhouette.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Magnum's 'Schedule the Sun' campaign encourages consumers to capture sunny moments by booking them directly into their calendars, enticing them with discounts to turn fleeting winter cravings into scheduled indulgences.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General ice cream consumers in the UK, France, and China, particularly those who typically perceive ice cream as a seasonal, summer-only treat.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign spans print, social media, digital advertising, and Digital Out-of-Home (DOOH) across the UK, France, and China.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive ice cream sales during the winter months and challenge entrenched perceptions of seasonality.

content.description: Dayforce, a global leader in HR technology, is excited to share its new, breakthrough spot and integrated campaign. The campaign spotlights how heavy work can be, and positions Dayforce as a partner in easing that burden.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign personifies the overwhelming weight and burden of work, visually demonstrating its impact, and then positions Dayforce's HR technology as the essential solution to alleviate this burden for both employees and organizations.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: HR professionals, business leaders, and executives within medium to large enterprises who are responsible for human capital management and employee well-being.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A core 'breakthrough spot' (likely a video commercial) serving as the hero content, supported by an integrated multi-channel campaign across digital platforms, social media, and potentially industry-specific B2B media.
ai_enrichment.objective: To strengthen Dayforce's brand positioning as a global leader in HR technology that genuinely understands and effectively solves the challenges and burdens faced by modern workforces and organizations.

content.description: Mike’s Amazing, a Chefler Foods condiment brand, is back with the next chapter of its award-winning brand campaign, “When It’s On, It’s On,” once again starring renowned comedic actor Jason Alexander. The fifth installment of the campaign will debut during the Opening Ceremony of the 2026 Winter Olympic Games, marking the brand’s biggest media moment yet.Building on the momentum of previous spots, including last year’s summer campaign and regional Super Bowl appearance, the new ad, titled “Baby,” features Alexander playing six distinct characters whose lives hilariously intersect over a shared love of Mike’s Amazing all playing out while one character is in the labor and delivery room. The spot highlights both Mike’s Amazing Real Mayonnaise and Mike’s Amazing Deli Mustard, reinforcing the brand’s expanding portfolio with its standard comedic humor and charm.Alexander first joined the “When It’s On, It’s On” campaign in 2024, helping the brand earn industry recognition, an Effie and Gold and Silver LIA awards, for its distinctive humor and bold creative voice.“Every time I think we’ve taken these characters as far as they can go, this team finds a way to push it even further,” said Alexander. “I like that Mike’s Amazing doesn’t play it safe…”Founded in 2016, celebrating their 10th anniversary this year, the Mike's Amazing brand is the fastest-growing condiments brand in America; and well known amongst sports fanatics for being the official mayonnaise, mustard and vegetable oil of the New York Yankees, New York Mets, New York Rangers, New Jersey Devils, Philadelphia Phillies, Philadelphia Flyers, Boston Red Sox, Boston Bruins, Miami Heat, Tampa Bay Lightning, Florida Panthers, Atlanta Braves, Atlanta Falcons, Baltimore Orioles, New Orleans Saints, New Orleans Pelicans, Carolina Hurricanes and the Washington Capitals.“For years, I watched major brands take the Olympic stage and thought, that’s a different level. This year, we’re no longer watching—we’re part of it,” said Michael Leffler, Founder and Chief Executive Officer of Mike’s Amazing/Chefler Foods LLC.The brand’s competitive spirit and pride in product quality continue to serve as the foundation for the campaign’s confident, high-energy tone. The campaign was created in collaboration with creative agency of record Havas New York and media by Ramp 97. Talent procurement was handled by Official Partner.“To go from a start up out of New Jersey to sponsoring the Winter Olympics so quickly is nothing short of amazing,” said Michael Kurland, Chief Financial Officer of Mike’s Amazing/Chefler Foods LLC.“Launching a fifth spot during the Winter Olympics gave us permission to go bigger-and weirder-than ever,” said Josh Schildkraut, Executive Creative Director, Havas New York. “Six characters, two products, one very pregnant Jason Alexander. Because when it’s on, it’s really on.”The campaign will run across broadcast, connected TV, digital, social, and additional high-impact placements.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign features comedic actor Jason Alexander playing six distinct characters whose lives comically intersect in a labor and delivery room, all united by their shared love for Mike’s Amazing Real Mayonnaise and Deli Mustard.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General consumers and families who use mainstream condiments, aged 25-55, with an appreciation for comedic storytelling.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film (TV commercial) will debut during the Opening Ceremony of the 2026 Winter Olympic Games, supported by broader television and digital video advertising.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase brand awareness and reinforce positive brand perception for Mike’s Amazing Real Mayonnaise and Deli Mustard through a high-profile media moment.

content.description:
After redefining category norms with their No Jingles or Mascots campaign, Born and Raised and NJM Insurance Group are launching a brand-new brand campaign – one that names a feeling nearly everyone recognizes, but few have ever articulated: Insuranoia.

Insuranoia is the quiet anxiety people feel long before they ever file a claim; the brace-for-impact mindset rooted in the belief that their insurance company won’t have their back when it matters most. It’s why people overprepare, overprotect, and go to extreme lengths to avoid ever having to call their insurer. NJM’s new campaign doesn’t dance around that truth; rather, it confronts it head-on.

The work builds on the spirit that made No Jingles or Mascots resonate with audiences, standing apart from an industry built on gimmicks while taking the idea a step further. Where the previous campaign focused on how NJM talks differently, Insuranoia focuses on how NJM makes people feel differently.

Insuranoia captures an emotion that many consumers experience,” said Cam Maio, Vice President of Marketing at NJM Insurance Group. ”This campaign reflects NJM’s commitment to putting our policyholders first and removing fear from the insurance experience.”

The Insuranoia campaign will launch with two narratives. In the first, “Bubble Wrap,” viewers meet a man so consumed by Insuranoia that he meticulously wraps his car in layers of bubble wrap, desperate to prevent even the smallest mishap. The humor is quiet and observational, grounded in a behavior that feels extreme but emotionally familiar. His overprotection isn’t driven by fear of accidents; it’s driven by a desire to never have to deal with his insurance company. The message is simple: with NJM, that anxiety disappears because policyholders don’t have to brace themselves for battle.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign introduces and validates 'Insuranoia,' the pre-claim anxiety consumers feel about their insurance company not having their back, by confronting this shared truth head-on to differentiate NJM.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adults who own insurance and experience quiet anxiety or distrust regarding their insurance provider's reliability, leading them to overprepare or avoid contact.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The primary medium is film, used to launch a new brand campaign that addresses consumer anxiety, likely distributed across television and digital platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand trust and differentiate NJM Insurance Group by empathizing with and directly addressing common consumer anxieties about insurance reliability.

metadata.title: The Rhythm of Reunion ties us together.

content.description:
Inside this humble Cha Chan Teng, Uncle Hock, Aunty Liew, and Polo Mui navigates the small dramas of everyday life, deciding which home to visit, carrying the quiet guilt of returning empty-handed, and confronting emotions many of us know all too well during the season of reunion. These familiar moments unfold during what is meant to be the happiest time of the year.

Watch how a simple act of ‘adding sugar’ brings the family together, reminding them that sometimes, just showing up is what truly matters this Chinese New Year.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A family grapples with the subtle anxieties and unspoken pressures of Chinese New Year reunion, ultimately finding connection and comfort through a simple gesture that emphasizes the importance of presence.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and families celebrating Chinese New Year, particularly those who experience the emotional complexities of family reunions and the pressure of fulfilling expectations.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A poignant short film distributed across digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media) to resonate with audiences during the Chinese New Year period.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand affinity and emotional connection with the target audience by demonstrating an understanding of the cultural and emotional nuances of Chinese New Year family reunions.

content.description:
Every brand talks about summer in the same way: “What a beautiful summer,” “You make the summer,” “Live the summer.” And consumers are tired of hearing the same thing over and over again.

With Micheladas, we asked ourselves: how do we stand out from what everyone else is doing? In the end, it’s not rocket science: Sun = crazy heat = I want to cool off NOW.

That’s why summer arrives with its vibe, its heat, and its perfect days that make us want to make the most of every moment. It’s the ideal season to live your best moments — and not leave a single craving or plan for later.

A cold beer? I want it YAAAAAAAAAAAAA.A quick beach trip? Let’s go YAAAAAAAAAAAAA.Leave the office? YAAAAAAAAAAAAA.Lie down in a hammock? YAAAAAAAAAAAAA.

And the new Imperial Michelada? We want it YAAAAAAAAAAAAA.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign uses the energetic "YAAAAAAAAA" cry to capture the intense, immediate desire for summer fun and relief, positioning the new Imperial Michelada as the ultimate solution to those instant cravings, thereby cutting through cliché summer advertising.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adult consumers who seek immediate gratification, relief, and enjoyment from summer's heat and activities, specifically those interested in refreshing alcoholic beverages like Micheladas.

content.description:
Subtitle: Man in bathroom: Have you lost it? Hey bro!!… Bookie: I can’t control! It will come out, just get out! Bookie: There’s no water! Is anyone there?

Subtitle: Man in bathroom: Have you lost it? Hey bro!!… Bookie: I can’t control! It will come out, just get out! Bookie: There’s no water! Is anyone there?

The film is inspired by an everyday habit seen across India (and probably a few more countries that deal mostly in cash), licking fingers while counting cash. It’s a deeply ingrained behaviour used to separate sticky notes, especially in humid conditions and behaviour-based handling of large volumes of money. While practical, it's also quite unhygienic. We took this innocuous gesture and pushed it to an extreme, asking: What’s the worst that could happen? That led to the idea of retrieving money from a toilet—and then counting it, lick by lick. It’s funny, uncomfortable, and instantly memorable because it takes a relatable habit. The insight allowed us to take a functional B2B product and anchor it in a culturally native, behaviour-based joke with universal appeal.

 

Advertising Agency: TBWA\ Lintas, Mumbai, IndiaPresident: Kapil BatraCopywriter: Kapil BatraChief Creative Officer: Prateek Bhardwaj

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A film exaggerates the unhygienic consequences of licking fingers to count cash, portraying a bookie experiencing extreme digestive distress, to shock and entertain.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Businesses and individuals in cash-intensive industries or roles, particularly in regions like India where licking fingers to count cash is a common but unhygienic practice.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A short film distributed online (e.g., YouTube, social media) and potentially on television, using a shocking and comedic narrative to engage viewers.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness about the unhygienic nature of licking fingers to count cash and implicitly drive consideration for Steadfast Stationery's office equipment as a cleaner, more efficient solution.

content.description:
Benjamin Moore is continuing its long-running, emotionally driven See The Love platform with “Timeless,” the sixth chapter in the brand story and its latest collaboration with longtime agency partner FIG.

The new campaign reinforces Benjamin Moore’s belief that quality endures. Through a single, intimate story, “Timeless” speaks to homeowners, contractors, architects, and designers alike, connecting craft, emotion, and longevity in a way few paint brands attempt.

The hero film follows two siblings growing up in a home painted with Benjamin Moore. As years pass and lives change, the paint remains a constant. The same wall that first reveals a young girl’s bedroom becomes the backdrop as she later packs for college, underscoring the emotional weight of spaces that last.

A key differentiator of the campaign is its continued celebration of the painting contractor as a symbol of quality and expertise—an intentional contrast to competitors who focus solely on color or price.

Mark Figliulo, Founder & Creative Chair, FIG: “Bucking the trend of short-term thinking, great brands like Benjamin Moore understand committing for the long-term is crucial. We are honored to continue building the See The Love brand story over the course of many years, finding a balance between reemphasizing core themes while still evolving into fresh territory. This latest body of work was methodically crafted, from beautifully composed tracks to expertly-selected colors, and especially in the decision to use 35mm film. We found what struck a chord strategically — that people are willing to invest in quality that lasts — also translated for the creative.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Benjamin Moore's 'Timeless' campaign, part of its 'See The Love' platform, uses a single, intimate story of siblings growing up in a home to illustrate how the brand's paint endures through time and emotional life changes.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Homeowners, contractors, architects, and designers.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film featuring a multi-year narrative, likely distributed across integrated channels as part of a broader marketing effort.
ai_enrichment.objective: Reinforce Benjamin Moore's brand belief that quality and craft endure, connecting paint with emotion and longevity.

content.description:
Volkswagen invites a new generation to live life in the driver’s seat with its new ‘Drivers wanted’ campaignA 30-second version of “The Great Invitation: Drivers wanted” will debut in the second half of the Big GameDirected by Leigh Powis of ProdCo, the advertising spot kicks off a campaign highlighting all the ways you can say ‘yes’ in lifeReston, VA — Volkswagen of America today announced the launch of a bold new chapter for Drivers wanted®, reimagining a defining part of Volkswagen’s creative history for a new era. Debuting ahead of the Big Game, the campaign invites a new generation to embrace individuality, curiosity, and saying yes when opportunity calls.

The campaign launches today with a 90-second film entitled “The Great Invitation: Drivers wanted.” A 30-second version of the spot will air during the second half of the Super Bowl on NBC and the Spanish-language broadcast on Telemundo. The film follows 2024's game day commercial, "An American Love Story," which celebrated the brand's 75 years in America.

First produced in the late 1990s, ‘Drivers wanted’ has long stood for Volkswagen’s human-centric approach to design, performance, and culture. Today, the campaign offers a forward-looking invitation, calling on a new generation to say yes to possibility, originality, and optimism by living life in the driver’s seat. The campaign was created in collaboration with Volkswagen’s longstanding creative and advertising agency, Johannes Leonardo.

Set to a classic early 90’s hip hop anthem, the film moves with the irreverent, infectious energy that has always defined ‘Drivers wanted.’ The 1992 track serves as another layer to Volkswagen’s invitation, urging people to jump in, say yes, and move through life with intention. It’s a knowing nod to the original era of ‘Drivers wanted,’ reintroduced for a new audience with the same spirit and energy.

The spot was directed by Leigh Powis of ProdCo, whose kinetic and authentic approach captured the fresh energy of Volkswagen's evolved brand direction. Shot on location in Houston, Texas, with a team including stylist Kelle Kutsugeras and production designer Arabella Serrell, the film is rooted in Volkswagen’s long-held belief that driving is about much more than getting from point A to point B.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Volkswagen is relaunching its iconic 'Drivers wanted' campaign, encouraging a new generation to embrace individuality, curiosity, and seize life's opportunities by saying 'yes'.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: A new generation, likely young adults (millennials/Gen Z), who value individuality, curiosity, and seizing opportunities in life.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Launch with a 90-second film titled 'The Great Invitation: Drivers wanted', with a 30-second version debuting during the 'Big Game' television broadcast. The primary medium is film.
ai_enrichment.objective: To refresh and reimagine Volkswagen's defining 'Drivers wanted' brand message for a new era, increasing brand relevance and awareness among a new generation.

metadata.title: Natalie Portman for Tiffany & Co.—A HardWear Film
tags: AOTW Jewelry, Beauty and Fashion Collections

content.description:
Natalie Portman for Tiffany & Co.—A HardWear Film

Tiffany & Co. explores the many dimensions of love in a cinematic new campaign starring brand ambassador Natalie Portman, created by Oscar-nominated directors Brady Corbet and Mona Fastvold. The film offers an intimate, behind-the-scenes portrait of Portman as she navigates her roles as a producer, director and mother, while reflecting the brand’s heritage. Produced through Magna Studios, the collaboration brings a refined, cinematic sensibility rarely seen in commercial work from the filmmaking duo.

Shot on 70mm by acclaimed cinematographer Hoyte van Hoytema, known for his collaborations with Christopher Nolan, the film emphasises texture and emotional depth. Portman wears pieces from several Tiffany collections, with the HardWear line taking centre stage, symbolising resilience and freedom. Through a personal voiceover framed as a letter to her daughter, she reflects on vulnerability, growth and the values she hopes to pass on, grounding the campaign in a deeply human narrative.

The campaign also reinforces Tiffany & Co.’s longstanding connection to romance and film history, featuring a new arrangement of Moon River as a nod to Breakfast at Tiffany's. Released during the 98th Academy Awards, the work underscores the brand’s enduring ties to cinema and cultural storytelling, positioning Tiffany & Co. as both a symbol of timeless love and a continuing presence in contemporary visual culture.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A visually stunning cinematic film, shot on 70mm, intimately portrays brand ambassador Natalie Portman navigating her roles as a mother, producer, and director. This high-art approach uses her multifaceted life and emotional depth to explore dimensions of love and reflect Tiffany & Co.'s heritage, while showcasing the HardWear collection.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Affluent consumers, particularly women who appreciate luxury jewelry, high-quality cinematic artistry, and resonate with the sophisticated, multifaceted image of Natalie Portman as a mother and professional.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A high-production value short film, directed by Oscar-nominated directors Brady Corbet and Mona Fastvold and shot on 70mm by Hoyte van Hoytema, distributed across digital platforms like YouTube, social media, and the brand's website, potentially supported by luxury lifestyle media placements.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance Tiffany & Co.'s brand prestige and emotional connection by linking it with multifaceted love and heritage through Natalie Portman, while driving awareness and desirability for the HardWear collection.

content.description:
This Valentine’s Day, Fresh Step and self-professed cat lover Ariana Madix are inspiring singles to skip the drama that comes with dating humans and look for a love connection with an adoptable cat. In this new campaign, “Date Cats, Not Humans,” singles are served the perfect solution to their Valentine’s Day woes: starting Feb. 3, adoptable cats from leading national animal welfare organization Best Friends Animal Society will take over Bumble in a first-of-its-kind partnership. Users will have the opportunity to “swipe right” and make a real connection with a feline friend, with Fresh Step providing free litter to new cat parents and covering cat adoption fees during Valentine’s Day weekend.New research from Fresh Step and YouGov* confirms what cat lovers already know: having a cat doesn't hurt your dating life. It helps. Cat owners are more willing to share their emotions and vulnerabilities, and more likely to get a “swipe right," especially from fellow cat owners. This Valentine's Day, to celebrate this connection between cats and their people, Fresh Step and Best Friends join forces to find homes for adoptable cats.Why are cats the key to dating happiness?A new study commissioned by Fresh Step reveals that having a cat doesn't replace a romantic partner. It might actually make you a better one.Cat owners are more open to connection. Only 8% say they can't trust anyone with their emotions, compared to 16% of non-cat owners. They're also less likely to deal with emotions in isolation (11% vs. 19% of non-cat owners).

They might even have a leg up in the dating world. Cat owners are more than five times as likely to have swiped right on someone specifically because they had a cat in their photos (11% vs. 2% of non-cat owners). And among dating app users, nearly 1 in 4 cat owners (24%) say a cat photo would make them more likely to match, compared to just 11% of overall dating app users.The takeaway? Date a cat, not a human… so you can be a better partner.How Can I Adopt?Simply open your Bumble app or visit bestfriends.org/FreshStep starting Feb. 3, where you can view adoptable cats through Best Friends, as well as available cats in shelters nationwide.For the month of February, Fresh Step will provide a coupon for a month of free litter to everyone who adopts a cat with Best Friends, redeemable at Walmart or on Walmart.com, Fresh Step’s clay clumping litter promises to keep your litter box smelling fresh with paw-activated odor control, so new cat parents can focus on the joys of bringing their new Valentine home. Plus, during Valentine’s Day weekend (Feb. 13-16), Fresh Step will cover cat adoption fees for cats adopted through the organization’s locations in Houston, Los Angeles, New York City, Northwest Arkansas, Salt Lake City, and the Best Friends Animal Sanctuary in Kanab, UT.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Fresh Step partners with Ariana Madix and Best Friends Animal Society to encourage singles to 'Date Cats, Not Humans' this Valentine's Day, featuring adoptable cats on Bumble profiles.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Singles experiencing Valentine's Day woes, potential cat adopters, and users of the Bumble dating app.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Partnership with celebrity Ariana Madix, first-of-its-kind integration on Bumble where adoptable cats take over profiles for swiping, Fresh Step providing free litter and covering cat adoption fees during Valentine’s Day weekend.
ai_enrichment.objective: To inspire cat adoption by providing an alternative love connection for singles during Valentine's Day, increase brand association with pet welfare, and drive awareness and potential sales for Fresh Step litter among new cat parents.

content.description: In a world full of paid praise and sponsored positivity, it’s increasingly difficult to know who and what to trust. Dove has long stood for what’s real, and continuing that commitment, the brand is putting its reputation directly in the hands of some of the toughest—and most honest—critics on the internet: Reddit. The campaign was created by DAVID London alongside AKQA Paris and WPP Media.So confident in the performance of its most-awarded hair mask of 2025, Dove invited this online community to review the Dove Intensive Repair 10-in-1 Serum Mask, promising to publish their feedback unfiltered and unscripted—even if it was negative. A subreddit post asking for reviews quickly garnered hundreds of comments and upvotes, revealing both praise and critique, including comments like: “Please don’t ever change this product!!!!!” and “I hate the smell, it’s like an old lady.”Dove Hair then built an entire campaign from these reviews, taking them out of Reddit and onto the streets and social media. From guerrilla out-of-home placements across the most honest city of all - New York - and digital OOH, to testimonial-style films in which Redditors read their reviews verbatim, the campaign brings real voices to life at scale.Aptly named “r/eal reviews,” the campaign is inspired by Reddit’s subreddit display format and stays true to the platform’s community-driven environment. Each Redditor’s customized Snoovatar is used across campaign assets to uphold anonymity while remaining unmistakably authentic.Designed to cut through the noise of overly polished beauty testimonials, Dove is challenging conventional beauty marketing. The campaign empowers creators and influencers to share their honest, transparent experiences - whether positive or critical -  in sponsored content. Something other brands haven’t dared to do.“The Reddit universe plays by its own rules, so being able to work directly with Redditors and drop the usual scripts and guardrails for a brand as iconic as Dove was incredibly exciting,” said Genevieve Gransden, Executive Creative Director at DAVID London. “Entering the comments section is always a risk, but for our first work with DAVID London, we wanted to do something that didn’t play it safe,” added fellow Executive Creative Director Selma Ahmed.“We wanted to build on Dove’s heritage of real women’s testimonials—but reinvent what a testimonial means today,” said Berengere Loubatier, Senior Global Brand VP for Dove Hair & Skin Care. “That meant grounding the campaign in real community voices, not scripted endorsements. Choosing unfiltered feedback from some of the internet’s most honest critics wasn’t the easiest choice, but it was the most authentic one. Not only because we truly believe in our 10‑in‑1 Hair Mask, but also because real is what Dove stands for—and real earns trust.”“It’s rare to see a brand take Redditors’ words and use them verbatim, without edits or filters, as part of a national campaign,” said Courtney Pappas, Industry Manager, CPG, Reddit. “We’re proud to provide users with an honest and authentic platform to share feedback, and we’re excited by how the r/eal Reviews campaign has energized our community to engage with Dove in a way they don’t normally with brands.”Making its stance known online through campaign films and influencer engagement, r/eal reviews comes to life in New York City with guerrilla and digital OOH, with additional campaign timing and channels to be announced.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Dove's 'r/eal reviews' campaign invites Reddit users to provide unfiltered feedback on their Intensive Repair 10-in-1 Serum Mask, promising to publish all reviews—positive or negative—to demonstrate transparency and build trust.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Active Reddit users interested in beauty and haircare products, who value authenticity and honest consumer reviews.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Subreddit post soliciting reviews for the Dove Intensive Repair 10-in-1 Serum Mask, with a commitment to publish all feedback unfiltered, supported by an integrated media strategy.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand trust and reinforce Dove's commitment to authenticity and realness through transparent, user-generated product reviews.

metadata.title: Code for the Protection and Inclusion of Black Consumers

content.description:
Code for the Protection and Inclusion of Black Consumers

The first Code for the Defense and Inclusion of Black Consumers redefines the fight against racism in consumer relations in Brazil.

A groundbreaking initiative by L’Oréal Luxe and MOVER in the Brazilian luxury market, developed within the Afroluxe program, proposes new standards for retail service and has already received major national and international recognition.

The initiative directly responds to the findings of the research “Racism in the Luxury Beauty Retail Market”, commissioned by the Company in 2024, which identified 21 racist practices experienced by Black consumers throughout the shopping journey.

Even without the force of law, the Code presents a set of 10 self-regulatory norms with strong ethical and symbolic value. These rules aim to address racism in its most subtle and structural forms and call on the luxury market to promote effective and concrete changes by questioning practices historically normalized within the sector.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: L’Oréal Luxe launched a self-regulatory 'Code for the Protection and Inclusion of Black Consumers' in Brazil, establishing new ethical standards to combat racist practices identified in the luxury beauty retail sector.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Luxury beauty retailers and their employees in Brazil, industry stakeholders, and Black consumers seeking equitable shopping experiences.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Print advertisements to disseminate information about the 'Code for the Protection and Inclusion of Black Consumers' and its implications.
ai_enrichment.objective: To establish and promote a set of self-regulatory norms to combat racist practices and ensure inclusion for Black consumers within the Brazilian luxury beauty retail market.

content.description:
5 March, 2026, Johannesburg, Castle Lite’s Abandoned Draughts investigation shot by award-winning South African photojournalist Ilan Godfrey, has captured and analysed the half-finished beers left behind in bars across Mzansi.

Rather than relying on surveys or brand claims, the project turned real consumer behaviour into visual evidence, documenting what people actually leave on tables when nights move on.

Godfrey, whose work has appeared in The New York Times, The Washington Post and The Sunday Times Magazine, approached the project using a fly-on-the-wall documentary methodology. Nothing was staged or interfered with.

“I treated it like any other documentary assignment,” says Godfrey. “You observe, you wait, and eventually patterns start to emerge.”

As the investigation progressed, Godfrey has found unexpected allies among waitstaff and bartenders. Fieldwork, he explains, depends on the contacts you make on the ground. In several venues, staff members began quietly alerting him to abandoned draughts before tables could be cleared, preserving the integrity of the scene.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Castle Lite partnered with award-winning photojournalist Ilan Godfrey to document and analyze half-finished beers left in bars across South Africa, transforming real consumer behavior into visual evidence.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young to middle-aged adults (18-45) who frequent bars and consume beer in South Africa.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A documentary-style photojournalism content series featuring images and analysis of abandoned beers in South African bars, likely distributed via online media channels and potentially traditional media outlets given the photojournalist's background.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate unique content that provides authentic insights into consumer behavior in bars, strengthening brand relevance and fostering deeper understanding of its audience.

content.description:
fairlife is launching a new campaign that spotlights the ritual of enjoying a milk moment. This campaign invites consumers to rethink milk and shift the perception that all milk tastes the same.

The campaign highlights how fairlife’s richer taste and enhanced nutrition elevate daily rituals and shared moments with loved ones. To crystalize this strategy, the campaign tells the stories of the milk moments consumers engage with most often: slow motion pours over a bowl of cereal; the ultra-close up product macro shot of a creamy plume of coffee; and the delicious satisfaction of a glass of chocolate milk.

Developed in partnership with its creative agency of record, Doner, the work arrives as fairlife continues its momentum as the fastest growing value added dairy brand, surpassing legacy competitors and becoming the category leader in 2025 and the #1 most trusted value added dairy brand.

Four :15 spots sit at the center of the “Savor More” campaign. You can view “Berry Smoothie,” “Cereal,” “Chocolate Milk,” and “Coffee” here. The work is live today, February 11, across TV, OTT, social, and ecomm and shopper.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign "Savor More" aims to elevate everyday milk moments by showcasing fairlife's richer taste and enhanced nutrition, encouraging consumers to rethink milk and appreciate its unique quality in daily rituals.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General milk consumers who engage in daily milk rituals, particularly those who might not differentiate between milk brands and are open to premium options that enhance their daily experiences.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A film campaign featuring visually rich narratives of common milk moments, such as slow-motion cereal pours, ultra-close-up coffee creamer shots, and the satisfaction of chocolate milk, developed with creative agency Doner.
ai_enrichment.objective: To shift consumer perception that all milk tastes the same by highlighting fairlife's superior taste and nutrition, thereby reinforcing brand differentiation and supporting its continued growth.

content.description:
Voyager director Spencer MacDonald brings ‘flow state’ to Merrell’s latest campaign, “Nothing But The Run,” created for the launch of the brand’s Agility Peak 6 trail shoe. In addition to directing the spot, MacDonald conceptualized and pitched the entire story, guiding its creative direction from start to finish. If you look closely, you will spot him making his on-screen acting debut as the accountant too.

Edited by Jonas Baumgartel, with post-production by Leap Year, the film immerses viewers directly into the headspace of a runner searching for flow. Framed entirely from the runner’s POV, the spot begins in a rush of everyday noise, including a therapist questioning his feelings, an accountant jogging alongside with paperwork in hand, laundry spilling across the dirt, and endless Zoom calls humming in the background. The campaign reflects the mental clutter of everyday life, but as the run continues, the distractions fade. Each stride in the Agility Peak 6 brings more rhythm and calm until the trail is steady, the sound clears, and the runner reaches a place of clarity and presence. The campaign closes with the tagline: Nothing But The Run.

“I run most mornings,” says MacDonald. “At the start, the thoughts from the previous day are so loud it almost feels like the people and images are running alongside me. I wish I could say they eventually fall away and I slip cleanly into a flow state, but I am not that disciplined of a runner. However, the idea of that transition from chaos to flow, something I hear about from much stronger runners than myself, has always interested me. This film was a chance to try to visualize that experience on the trail.”

“I’m deeply grateful to Burke, Alex, Elizabeth, Tucker, and Kenny of the Merrell team for trusting me to bring this concept forward and for the thoughtful collaboration throughout. From the first conversations to the final touches on sound and color, it was one of the most harmonious shoots I’ve ever experienced. A rare gift with no ‘director’s cut’ needed.”

For this campaign, MacDonald played a vital role far beyond directing. He conceived and pitched a completely new approach to the story, presenting an idea that differed from the original creative brief but captured its essence, and worked closely with the Merrell creative team to refine it further together. From the production and sound design to the final edit, this vision brought each element together, telling a relatable story of a runner in motion: the breath, the focus, the movement, as distractions give way to flow.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign film, shot entirely from a runner's POV, immerses viewers into the headspace of a runner transitioning from the noise of everyday life to the meditative 'flow state' achieved through trail running.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Trail runners and active individuals who seek a meditative 'flow state' experience and are interested in high-performance running footwear like the Agility Peak 6.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film, created for digital and possibly traditional media distribution, presented from an immersive first-person runner's perspective.
ai_enrichment.objective: To launch and build awareness for Merrell's new Agility Peak 6 trail shoe by associating it with the sought-after 'flow state' experience in running.

FactSet (NYSE:FDS | NASDAQ:FDS), a global leader in financial intelligence and enterprise data solutions, today unveiled “Fluent in Finance,” a breakthrough brand campaign, designed to pinpoint the weaknesses in “off-the-shelf” AI solutions, underscore the importance of human expertise, and encourage enterprise leaders to demand better. The campaign was created by leading strategy and creative agency VSA Partners.

The campaign, which builds on the success of FactSet’s award-winning 2023 “Not Just the Facts” brand initiative, includes four new video spots and a comprehensive out-of-home takeover of New York’s financial district. Both components leverage humorous, provocative creative to assert FactSet’s position as an industry leader at the inflection point of AI adoption.

Every detail was carefully crafted to reflect the true essence of family, unity, and shared values, transforming a national occasion into a meaningful visual narrative.

content.description:
Habit Burger & Grill Launches “Fresh Like That,” a New Brand Platform from Barrett HofherrWith a new brand world, a new tagline, and a challenge to consumers everywhere, the brand goes all in on freshness

Full-service creative agency Barrett Hofherr and media agency Exverus are drawing a line in the sand for Habit Burger & Grill. When it comes to freshness, there is no middle ground. You either Habit or you don't. That's the premise of the first campaign executed under Habit's new "Fresh Like That" brand platform. This multi-channel campaign establishes their new brand world where "fresh" isn't just representative of the food they serve, but the attitude towards life and food they want to embody and encourage.The campaign's hero spot, "Float On," sets the tone for the Fresh Coast — a sun-soaked world where everything is fresh -  the food, the people, even the tiny flotation device that delivers ranch poolside.

"A lot of brands say “fresh”, said Todd Eisner, Executive Creative Director at Barrett Hofherr. "But Habit actually is - they use fresh avocados and make their ranch by hand daily. Their most popular side is tempura green beans. So we actually drew inspiration straight from the menu to take a different approach."

The creative suite is a video-first storytelling campaign for CTV, OLV, social, DOOH, display, and search. The media plan places heavy emphasis on California markets, with additional national reach. Premium CTV (Hulu, Peacock, Paramount) and high-profile moments like March Madness will elevate the brand’s stature and bring in quality-driven audiences.

“This spring, we’re balancing brand equity-building for Habit across the country with driving immediate store sales in its key western markets,” explains Exverus Media Director Georgia Schreiner. “The campaign’s colorful, sunny creative reinforces Habit’s southern California roots while expanding the freshness to new audiences.”The campaign also marked a milestone beyond the creative itself. Habit was evolving its visual identity in parallel, giving Barrett Hofherr the rare opportunity to shape a new brand world and a refreshed brand identity in tandem, ensuring everything launched as one cohesive vision.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The Habit Burger Grill launches its 'Fresh Like That' brand platform, positioning freshness as both a quality of their food and a desired lifestyle attitude, challenging consumers to align with this ethos.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Consumers who prioritize fresh food options and resonate with a positive, fresh-minded attitude towards life, applicable to a broad audience for a fast-casual restaurant.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A multi-channel campaign centered around a hero film, 'Float On,' which establishes the 'Fresh Coast' brand world.
ai_enrichment.objective: To launch and establish a new brand platform, 'Fresh Like That,' thereby strengthening The Habit Burger Grill's brand identity and positioning around its core value of freshness.
