content.description:
Comfort, Unilever’s leading fabric conditioner brand, presents the main film of its biggest bet of the year in Brazil: the Comfort Segredos Bridgerton line, which seeks to translate the Bridgerton universe into an unmistakable fragrance. Starring Hugh Sachs, the iconic Brimsley from the Netflix series, the campaign dives into this setting to unravel the mystery behind the disappearance of the castle’s flowers.Created by Dark Kitchen Creatives, co-created by Netflix’s Brand Partnership team, and produced by MyMama, the film uses investigative storytelling. Sachs, in his persona as the queen’s loyal squire, searches for the whereabouts of the court’s missing flowers, only to discover that their essence has been preserved in the fragrance of Comfort Segredos Bridgerton.The fragrance was created to be the “essence of the romances and secrets” that surround the characters. It brings together the mystery of red fruits combined with the nobility of roses and the delicacy of lily of the valley, with a sweet and enveloping vanilla base.“Bringing Hugh Sachs to the center of the narrative was a strategic decision to connect Bridgerton’s aspirational universe to Comfort’s most fragrant royal secret. With his discretion, dedication, and as the queen’s main confidant, Brimsley is the perfect link to lead this story. The campaign combines high fragrance performance with a sensory experience worthy of royalty, transforming the laundry routine into a moment of indulgence,” says Larissa Rezek, Fabric Conditioners Marketing Manager at Unilever Brazil.The production, filmed on a sustainable set, mobilized 120 professionals and used 26 meters of natural flowers which, after filming, were distributed to the crew and suppliers, avoiding waste.The communication strategy for the campaign is robust and 360°, focusing on digital channels, social media, strong influencer presence, and sensory activations during Carnaval to bring the “fragrance of royalty” to the Brazilian public. The campaign reinforces Comfort’s social-first vision, seeking to be where culture and conversations happen, connecting with consumers in a natural and genuine way.The Comfort Segredos Bridgerton line, which includes the concentrated fabric conditioner and Comfort Boom in-wash scent booster crystals, is now available at major retail outlets across Brazil.

 

The National Anthem of Pakistan As Sung Through Face Masks

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign features Hugh Sachs (Brimsley) investigating the disappearance of flowers in a Bridgerton-esque setting, ultimately revealing their essence is captured in the new Comfort Segredos Bridgerton fabric conditioner.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Consumers of fabric conditioner in Brazil, particularly fans of the Netflix series Bridgerton, who are interested in premium, themed fragrances.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: An integrated campaign centered around a hero film, co-created with Netflix's Brand Partnership team and starring Hugh Sachs, likely distributed across television, digital, and social media channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To launch and generate awareness for the new Comfort Segredos Bridgerton fabric conditioner line by distinctly associating its fragrance with the popular Netflix series.

metadata.title: Ink Is More Than You Think

content.description:
Today, Custom Ink launches its first major brand refresh in 15 years, shifting beyond a custom t-shirt provider to a comprehensive team success engine. Created in partnership with Mekanism and titled “Ink Is More Than You Think,” the campaign taps into The Office-style humor, introducing a new brand character, Janine, who makes the inherently complex swag process look simple by treating Custom Ink as her cheat code for solving organizational challenges with absurd confidence.

The hero :30 film introduces us to a corporate team as their boss breaks the news that investors have pulled their funding. One employee says that she just bought a jet ski, while another states she will start a cash burn analysis in reaction. Meanwhile, Janine decides it’s the perfect moment to order Stanley tumblers for the team. She makes the point that the more water they have, the more productive the team will be, which will ultimately solve the funding problem. The film closes with the team cheering, holding their matching water bottles with custom t-shirts that say “Let’s Funding Go!” as a voiceover delivers the tagline: "With over 10,000 swag options... Ink is More Than You Think."

To further bring the campaign to life, Custom Ink is a participating partner at the Winter House - a community hub for the US Skating Association at the 2026 Winter Olympics in Milan. Throughout the duration of the Winter Olympics, the Custom Ink booth in the hotel lobby is selling U.S. Skating branded merch. “Ink Is More Than You Think” includes updates to the visual identity, messaging, architecture, and digital presence, reflecting the company’s evolution from its iconic “Thanks, Custom Ink” jingle era to a sophisticated end-to-end platform.

The work marks the first brand campaign from Mekanism since Custom Ink named the agency its agency of record in 2025. As the beginning of a new creative chapter for the brand, “Ink Is More Than You Think” establishes Mekanism’s role in leading Custom Ink’s strategic and creative evolution as it redefines what swag can do for teams of all sizes.

The campaign is live today across linear TV, OTV/OLV, Meta, email, and digital display. Please see below for quotes and team credits and let me know if you’re interested in covering. Thanks for your consideration!

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses "The Office"-style humor and a new brand character, Janine, to reposition Custom Ink as a comprehensive "team success engine." It demonstrates how Janine simplifies complex swag processes, using Custom Ink as her cheat code for solving organizational challenges with absurd confidence.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Businesses, organizations, and corporate teams looking for custom products to enhance team cohesion, improve branding, or solve organizational challenges.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: An integrated campaign, prominently featuring a hero :30 film introducing a new brand character and humorous narrative style.
ai_enrichment.objective: To refresh Custom Ink's brand perception, shifting it beyond a custom t-shirt provider to a comprehensive "team success engine" that solves organizational challenges.

content.description:
When the FIFA World Cup comes to Seattle in 2026, millions of visitors will descend on the city.

Visit Seattle wanted to make sure the entire community—from transit systems to corner cafés—could participate in welcoming the world with one unified voice. They turned to Seattle independent agency Copacino Fujikado to make it happen.

The result is Let’s Play SEA '26: a bold, ownable visual identity that any business, organization, or resident in Seattle can use at zero cost to celebrate this once-in-a-lifetime moment.

Copacino pulled inspiration from what makes Seattle distinctive—the lush forests, sparkling waterfront, and iconic skyline—to create vibrant colors, bold patterns, and a standout "SEA" mark that feels unmistakably Seattle.

"We saw an opportunity to create something that could benefit the entire city," said Vince Soliven, Executive Creative Director at Copacino Fujikado. "SEA '26 feels specific to Seattle while being flexible enough for anyone to use."

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Let’s Play SEA '26 is a free, distinct visual identity system provided by Visit Seattle for the entire community to adopt, fostering a unified welcoming voice for the city during the FIFA World Cup 2026.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Millions of FIFA World Cup visitors to Seattle in 2026, and the broader Seattle community including local businesses, organizations, and residents.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Creation and free distribution of a distinct visual identity kit, including vibrant colors, bold patterns, and an iconic 'SEA' mark, for widespread adoption by Seattle businesses, organizations, and residents.
ai_enrichment.objective: To foster community engagement and pride in Seattle, creating a unified and welcoming experience for the millions of visitors during the FIFA World Cup 2026.

content.description:
In Chile, we have a bad habit: we sign a lot of things without really paying attention to what we’re signing — and often, without understanding them. The problem comes later, when we realize that among everything we signed… there was a health plan that wasn’t the right fit for us.

Consalud is a Chilean health insurance brand that supports people until they truly understand their needs. Every plan is personalized, designed to fit each person — not the other way around.

So to illustrate this habit of signing without knowing what we’re signing, we launched our campaign with the people who sign the most without looking: famous artists. And among them, Kramer, one of Chile’s most beloved comedians.

 

Agency: Puer.toECD: Ingrid LiraCreative director: Gus GilCopywriter: Franck MarcianoHead of art: Leono

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign highlights the common Chilean habit of signing documents without understanding, especially health plans, by featuring famous artists like comedian Kramer who frequently sign things, to underscore Consalud's commitment to personalized plans and informed choices.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Chilean adults who are current or potential health insurance customers, particularly those prone to signing documents without full comprehension.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film featuring famous Chilean artists, specifically comedian Kramer, illustrating the act of signing without understanding.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand preference and trust for Consalud by educating consumers on the importance of understanding health plans before signing, and positioning Consalud as the brand that facilitates this understanding.

metadata.title: Rethink Healthy
tags: Clio Health - Celebrating Mental Health Awareness

content.description:
Asia’s leading life and health insurance company, AIA Group, is encouraging people to “Rethink Healthy” in its latest campaign from BBH Singapore. The new work challenges long established definitions of what it means to be healthy, promoting a more relevant, inclusive and compassionate approach to health and wellbeing that invites people to reflect on their habits and redefine health in ways that truly work for them.

AIA and BBH Singapore’s campaign follows the stories of three individuals, each experiencing a moment that inspires them to think again about what good health really looks like. The films will be released at monthly intervals, with each one highlighting a different trigger that leads someone to “Rethink Healthy” by showing that stereotypes and established norms of health can sometimes do more harm than good.

The stories are crafted to open people’s eyes, encouraging viewers to pause and reflect on the image of health that society perpetuates and understand how it impacts their own wellbeing and the wellbeing of those they love.

AIA’s first spot focuses on a father’s pride in his high-achieving son, Ben, celebrating his promotions and relentless drive. At the same time, Ben navigates the demands of work and family. As his stresses accumulate with no outlet, he is forced to confront the unrealistic standards that shape not only his own wellbeing but also the life he shares with the people he loves most.

Stuart A. Spencer, Group Chief Marketing Officer, said: “At AIA, we are taking a stand for the health of Asia and taking Rethink Healthy further than ever. Our goal is to inspire one billion people to live Healthier, Longer, Better Lives. To shift behaviours and drive real change, we must confront the outdated ways we define ‘healthy’, and recognise how our daily habits shape not just our own lives, but the lives of our families and communities. Our campaign taps into the emotions that unite us all, including the desire to make our parents proud, the hope for our children’s happiness, and the need to feel fully like ourselves. Through these stories, we aim to spark real moments of realisation that challenge people to rethink what health truly means. This campaign holds up a mirror to those habits and challenges everyone to take control, make meaningful choices, and set out on a journey toward Healthier, Longer, Better Lives for themselves and those they love.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign challenges conventional definitions of health, encouraging individuals to adopt a more personalized, inclusive, and compassionate approach to their wellbeing.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General public in Asian markets, existing and potential customers of AIA Group interested in life and health insurance.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A series of narrative films released at monthly intervals, likely distributed across digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media) and possibly television.
ai_enrichment.objective: To shift public perception of health, redefine what it means to be healthy, and position AIA Group as a thought leader in holistic wellbeing.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A cinematic film designed to articulate and celebrate the core tenets, vision, or achievements of Scientology, acting as an inspirational and foundational message for the organization.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Existing members of Scientology for reinforcement and inspiration, as well as individuals curious about or potentially open to joining the religion, seeking to understand its principles and community.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Screenings at Scientology churches, community centers, and special events; distribution via Scientology's owned media platforms (e.g., Scientology.tv) and potentially mainstream digital video channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To strengthen allegiance and morale among current adherents, attract new members by communicating core values, and enhance public understanding and perception of Scientology.

content.description:
Design consultancy Synoptic Office today announces the launch of a new website for VoteRiders, the nation’s leading nonpartisan nonprofit focused exclusively on eliminating voter ID barriers.

Launching ahead of the fall voting season, the new platform transforms one of the most complex and confusing aspects of civic participation – voter ID laws – into a clear, accessible and action-driven digital experience.

At its core, the project embodies the simple yet essential idea that creativity can spark action. VoteRiders’ mission is to eliminate ID barriers to the ballot box so every eligible voter can cast a ballot that counts.

The organization envisions a thriving American democracy with an empowered, civically engaged, diverse and representative electorate, and a future in which ID requirements do not prevent eligible Americans from having their voices heard.

Inspired by the bold visual language of 1960s and 70s public service announcements, the new VoteRiders site – with brand identity by Morgan Searcy – uses high-contrast color, confident typography and clear structural hierarchy to bring clarity to a fragmented legal landscape.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Transforming complex voter ID laws into a clear, accessible, and action-driven digital experience to empower eligible voters.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Eligible voters who may face voter ID barriers, with a focus on fostering a diverse and representative electorate.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Launch of a new digital website platform, leveraging digital channels to provide clear information and actionable steps.
ai_enrichment.objective: To eliminate voter ID barriers and ensure every eligible voter can cast a ballot that counts, thereby increasing civic engagement and voter participation.

content.description: Through every era, every shared laugh, and every unexpected turn of fate, love has a way of guiding us home. This Chinese New Year, Joseph Lee and Dior take us on a journey across time and seasons, reminding us that while life may change, the people we love remain our true destination. As years pass and paths shift, love continues to lead us back to where our hearts belong. Because with every new beginning, every reunion, and every celebration, love is the greatest blessing of all.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A heartwarming Chinese New Year film by Spritzer showcasing love's enduring power to bring families together across time and seasons, highlighting reunion and celebration.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and families celebrating Chinese New Year who cherish themes of enduring love, family connection, and tradition, likely aged 18-55 in Asian markets.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film featuring celebrities Joseph Lee and Dior, distributed across digital platforms like YouTube and social media, potentially with broadcast TV placements.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand affinity and emotional resonance during the Chinese New Year festive season by associating Spritzer with heartfelt celebrations and family togetherness.

content.description:
Agency Repositions 100% Organic Fruit Snack With Packaging & Visual ID RedesignFortnight Collective, the independent advertising and branding agency, recently launched a rebranding campaign for Solely, including a brand repositioning and redesigned packaging.

Solely launched a major packaging rebrand for its Organic Fruit Jerky and snacks, designed to emphasize ingredient simplicity and improve shelf impact. The redesign, which has contributed to a 296% sales boost and earned a 2025 Designalytics Effectiveness Award, streamlines communication by focusing on "nothing but the fruit" and removing, in some cases, previous "one whole fruit" messaging and specific, redundant or excessive, badges.

“We saw that Soley had many strong advantages in the marketplace, but its messaging and the packing and visual ID were not consistent or clear,” said Andy Nathan, founder and CEO, Fortnight. “They needed a new, compelling and crisp articulation of the brand idea/platform—Nothing But the Fruit—that would unify the company’s marketing strategy, package design and creative execution.”

Fast Company ranked Soley its #2 Most Innovative Food Company in 2025 and both agency and client saw the enormous growth opportunity for a brand with such a disruptive and innovative position in the market – especially when consumers raved about the brand’s taste experience.

“We needed to establish Solely as a distinct, revolutionary force driving the food industry forward,” Nathan added. “Our mission was to increase brand awareness and relevance and ramp up consumer trial and adoption. The first and most important line of attack was the product packaging.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign involved a rebranding and visual identity redesign for Solely's organic fruit jerky, emphasizing ingredient simplicity and 'nothing but the fruit' messaging through updated packaging.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Health-conscious consumers and snack buyers who seek organic products with simple ingredients, influenced by clear and impactful packaging in retail environments.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A major packaging rebrand and visual identity redesign for Solely's Organic Fruit Jerky and snacks.
ai_enrichment.objective: To boost sales and improve shelf impact for Solely's organic fruit jerky by streamlining communication and emphasizing ingredient simplicity through updated packaging.

content.description: Shaping your ideas into apps that work your way.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Base44 helps individuals and businesses transform their unique concepts into functional, personalized applications, emphasizing user-centric design and custom solutions.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Entrepreneurs, startups, and small to medium-sized businesses in the electronics and technology sectors seeking bespoke application development.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across digital platforms like YouTube, Vimeo, and potentially industry-specific tech media, possibly accompanied by shorter social media video cut-downs.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate leads for custom app development services and position Base44 as an expert in transforming unique ideas into personalized, functional applications.

content.description:
The Vienna Tourist Board invited the entire Scottish village of Dull to visit Vienna and use their expert knowledge to judge whether the city is dull in January or not.

“Is Vienna Dull?”, a concept born from a collaborative creative workshop between the Vienna Tourist Board and Jung von Matt DONAU, combines humour, timing, and cultural storytelling to invite visitors to rethink the traditionally quiet month of January.

The campaign spotlights the Austrian capital as a vibrant, surprising city. After all, January is when the Viennese ball season reaches its peak, with the city hosting more than 450 balls every year.

Using a charming, self-ironic narrative, the PR stunt turns the real Scottish village of Dull into the ultimate authority tasked with judging Vienna.

Invitations to take on this important role were handed over to the residents of Dull in November 2025, setting the stage for what has unfolded in the new year.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The Vienna Tourist Board invited the entire Scottish village of Dull to visit and judge if Vienna is dull in January, using self-irony and humor to promote the city's vibrant ball season.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Potential tourists and visitors who might perceive Vienna as quiet or unappealing in January, encouraging them to rethink winter travel to the city.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: An integrated PR stunt involving a formal invitation to the village of Dull, followed by their visit and expert judgment, likely amplified through media outreach and content showcasing the experience.
ai_enrichment.objective: To challenge the perception of Vienna being dull in January and reposition it as a vibrant, surprising destination during the winter, thereby boosting tourism.

content.description:
This "new" colleague has a photographic memory and never forgets a detail. She seems perfect, yet there’s something "different" about her. 🧐 When an emergency strikes and panic sets in, she reacts in a way nobody expected...

As we step into the New Year, times may evolve, but our heart for protecting your health remains unchanged. Wishing everyone a Happy Chinese New Year, filled with health and prosperity!

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A mysterious new colleague with extraordinary memory reveals unexpected capabilities during an emergency, symbolizing BIG Pharmacy's unwavering commitment to health protection during the Chinese New Year.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Malaysian Chinese community and the broader Malaysian public, particularly those celebrating or observing the Chinese New Year, who prioritize health and well-being for themselves and their families.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film deployed across digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media channels) and potentially broadcast television, strategically released to coincide with the Chinese New Year festive period.
ai_enrichment.objective: To strengthen brand association with health protection and community care, while fostering goodwill and connection with the audience during the Chinese New Year celebrations.

metadata.title: What Having It All Tastes Like

content.description:
Marie Callender’s•Healthy Choice•Conagra Brands

Consumers are seeking value in food purchases, but are constantly having to compromise.

Rising food prices have outpaced overall inflation rates in the last year and consumers are feeling it, both at the grocery store and at restaurants. Cooking at home may offer a cheaper/healthier option, but can’t stack up to the convenience of dining out. On the other hand, takeout and delivery come with an ever-growing list of fees and charges. Consumers are yearning for a nutritious, delicious, and convenient option that fits their budget, but it feels impossible to find.

INSIGHTOutdated perceptions have kept consumers from choosing a meal that checks all of their boxes.Certain brands/products have distorted consumer views of the frozen category, but a second look at Marie Callender’s and Healthy Choice might surprise many. Quality protein, made-from scratch sauces + pastas, and crisp vegetables - all at a fraction of the cost of dining out.

CREATIVE SOLUTIONHaving it all can feel like a superpower - and you may want to see how far you can take it.In our “Waiter” and “Sven” spots, our characters are surprised by how easily Marie Callender’s and Healthy Choice can deliver on everything they want in a meal. Packed with protein and quality ingredients at an affordable price, it almost feels like a cheat code that breaks the rules they’ve come to expect when buying food. This breakthrough sparks their imagination and begs the question - what else could I ask for if I’m getting everything I want in a meal?

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Marie Callender's challenges the notion that consumers must compromise on taste, nutrition, convenience, or budget, positioning its frozen meals as the ideal solution that truly offers 'having it all'.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Budget-conscious consumers who are frustrated with current food options (expensive dining out, inconvenient home cooking, costly takeout) and seek convenient, nutritious, and delicious meals without compromising on cost or quality.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across relevant digital platforms and potentially traditional broadcast channels to tell the brand's story.
ai_enrichment.objective: To shift outdated consumer perceptions of the frozen food category and establish Marie Callender's as the premium, convenient, and affordable solution for consumers seeking uncompromised meals.

content.description: Rocket and Redfin’s Super Bowl LIX Spot Features Lady Gaga's "Won't You Be My Neighbor?" Cover and a Million-Dollar Home GiveawayOn game day, Gaga's reimagined track becomes the heartbeat of Rocket and Redfin’s 60-second Super Bowl spot—directed by Henry Alex-Rubin and created by MIRIMAR.The commercial centers on two girls from different backgrounds moving into the same neighborhood and struggling to find a sense of home until they find each other.We see a series of vignettes: two families going through different challenges – one girl moving away from friends while the other is moving due to her parents divorce – but later finding community in their fated friendship. These are familial topics other brands rarely touch, especially during Super Bowl, but feel all the more real for it. Another neighbor reaches out only to get ignored – and later becomes closer through a neighborly act. These moments mimic the connection that we yearn for as neighbors and feel awkward at first, but then essential. The narrative follows neighbors resisting community, then realizing that letting people in isn't weakness — it's exactly what this country needs right now.Layered over Gaga's voice singing to the tune of “Will You Be My Neighbor?” these scenes tell a story about what it means to choose belonging in a highly divided time. It's Rocket's message distilled: that homeownership isn't just about property — it's about planting yourself somewhere and deciding the people next door matter. That home isn’t just about you and yours, it’s about everyone around you.But the spot doesn't end when the screen fades to black.In another Super Bowl first, Rocket, Redfin, and MIRIMAR are using the world’s biggest stage to pull off much more than an ad—one homeseeker will become a new homeowner, making the American Dream feel tangible and joyous again while creating new neighbors for everyone to welcome.By hiding a million-dollar home in plain sight and asking America to find it first in the Redfin app, Rocket and Redfin are creating more homeowners and neighbors across the country—and on Super Bowl Sunday, they’ll create one more in the most epic way possible.Chapter Two of a Cultural MovementLast year, Rocket extended its Super Bowl moment beyond advertising by transforming Allegiant Stadium into a global singalong of "Take Me Home, Country Roads." What started as a 60-second spot became one of those rare Super Bowl moments that transcended marketing and entered shared culture—pushing a decades-old John Denver song back up the Billboard charts and reminding millions that sometimes the most powerful thing a brand can do is bring people together through something as simple as a song.It was the debut of Rocket's Own the Dream platform, created in partnership with independent agency MIRIMAR. And it proved that the Super Bowl could be more than a commercial break—it could be a cultural rallying cry.At its heart, this work is about reviving belief—belief in America, in neighborliness, in each other, and in the enduring power of showing up for whoever lives next door. It reflects a conviction that finding a home and community to call your own is still possible, even when it feels further away than ever.From Lady Gaga breathing new life into Mr. Rogers' timeless invitation to be more neighborly, to a 60-second spot that invites millions into the story, to a million-dollar home giveaway that proves the dream is still within reach—Own the Dream Season Two is more than mere advertising. It's a multi-week cultural moment built on the belief that home is where community begins, and community is what America needs most right now.MIRIMAR QUOTES - John“Super Bowl is the world's biggest stage; we need a cultural message, big and worthy enough to match. Having Lady Gaga and Mr. Rogers remind us all of the power of neighborliness in such an iconic way is a timely and timeless message that America is better when we get to know each other and look out for the people next door.We are very proud to work with the Rocket Team to create this work that honors the original message of Fred Rogers, and that’s worthy of this moment in time.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: An emotional Super Bowl LIX spot featuring Lady Gaga's cover of "Won't You Be My Neighbor?" telling the story of two girls finding home and community through friendship, coupled with a million-dollar home giveaway.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Potential homebuyers and sellers, particularly families undergoing life transitions such as relocation or divorce, who value community and emotional connection in their housing journey.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 60-second hero film broadcast during Super Bowl LIX, featuring a Lady Gaga song cover, complemented by a prominent million-dollar home giveaway promotion.
ai_enrichment.objective: To significantly increase brand awareness and emotional affinity for Rocket and Redfin among potential homebuyers and sellers.

metadata.title: Plins. The sparkle of Christmas in Britânia mode.

content.description:
Plins. The sparkle of Christmas in Britânia mode.

To break through the noise of a saturated Christmas market, Britânia turned a functional alert into an emotional symphony. Recognizing the Air Fryer's "DING" as a potential musical note, the brand created the world’s first Air Fryer choir.

Renowned Maestro João Carlos Martins conducted this unusual orchestra at Teatro Renault, harmonizing the appliances' sounds with a classical orchestra in a live-streamed concert. The campaign humanized the technology, delivering a 3% engagement rate (3x the benchmark) and driving market share from 13% to 18% during the holidays.

 

Agency: Calia

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Britânia transformed the utilitarian 'DING' of an Air Fryer into a musical note, creating the world's first Air Fryer choir to perform a live-streamed concert with a classical orchestra.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General consumers and holiday shoppers, particularly those interested in home appliances, cooking, and innovative brand experiences.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A live-streamed concert event at Teatro Renault, featuring a renowned Maestro conducting an Air Fryer choir alongside a classical orchestra.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase brand engagement and drive market share growth for Britânia Air Fryers during the competitive holiday season.

content.description:
The film presents a shredded depiction of a potentially perilous love murder that looms in possibility. Set within the intimate confines of a romantic bathroom, a husband and wife are ensnared amidst alcohol-fueled passion and desire. With a myriad of twists, the plot unravels into a fragmented narrative, rendering it challenging for viewers to reconstruct the story's shattered pieces.

 

Production House: Ducktape Studios, Mumbai, IndiaAgency: DDB Mudra, Mumbai, IndiaDirector - Raylin VallesExecutive Producer - Apratim BhatnagarCCO - Rahul MathewGCD - Sourabh DubeyGCD - Gagan BindraCreative Director - FatemaProducer - Ashwarya CCostume - PratishrutiDA - Kunal Jadhav

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A psychological thriller film that depicts an alcohol-fueled, fragmented narrative of a couple's passionate yet perilous relationship, hinting at a potential love murder and challenging viewers to piece together the story.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Mature film enthusiasts, particularly those who appreciate psychological thrillers, non-linear storytelling, and art-house cinema.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Distribution through film festivals, streaming platforms, and potentially limited theatrical release, supported by trailers and critical reviews.
ai_enrichment.objective: To engage audiences with a unique and challenging cinematic experience, fostering critical acclaim and discussion around its innovative narrative structure.

content.description:
Auchan and TBWA\Paris are launching “Colorfood”, an innovative and ambitious campaign that places health and play at the heart of children’s nutrition. A first-of-its-kind initiative in the retail sector, the program is designed to help children eat better and is supported by a large-scale public awareness campaign.A real emergency: today, 1 in 5 children in France is overweight or obese

According to a study published in February 2025 on the French National Health Insurance website, childhood overweight and obesity have reached alarming levels: 20% of children aged 6 to 17 are overweight, including 5.4% who are obese. The study sounds the alarm—without immediate and effective action from public authorities, an unprecedented epidemic could affect one in three children in France by 2030.

“Beige Food”: monochrome foods taking over children’s platesBut what explains this rise in obesity?The primary reason is the increase in the energy density of meals—foods are higher in fat, and portion sizes have grown. Nutritional quality is also a major concern: monochrome foods are now widely available, alongside increased exposure to fast food and sugary drinks.

These overly processed, high-fat, monochrome foods are commonly referred to as “beige food” in English-speaking countries. Unfortunately, they have become omnipresent on children’s plates, despite parents’ ongoing efforts to diversify their children’s diets.A balanced meal is a colorful meal

This is where mass retail has a role to play. Auchan decided to act by promoting a simple idea of its own: a balanced meal is a colorful meal.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The "Colorfood" campaign is an innovative initiative by Auchan and TBWA\Paris that aims to improve children's nutrition by making healthy eating fun and engaging, placing health and play at its core.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Parents of children aged 6-17 in France, aiming to help their children develop healthier eating habits and combat childhood overweight and obesity.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A large-scale integrated public awareness campaign, alongside first-of-its-kind initiatives within the retail sector (Auchan stores), designed to engage children and educate families.
ai_enrichment.objective: To significantly improve children's nutritional habits and reduce the prevalence of childhood overweight and obesity in France.

content.description:
The Jeep® brand released its new spot for the all-new 2026 Jeep Cherokee hybrid as the modern mid-sized SUV makes its highly-anticipated arrival into dealerships across the U.S.

The online spot, called “Billy Goes to the River,” casts its fishing line into the social media waters during the advertising industry’s biggest week, looking to catch the prize for the “best Big Game commercial that’s not in the Big Game!”

Fish! Bears! Eagles! Oh my! “Billy Goes to the River” takes viewers on what starts as a sweet father-son drive when the young boy innocently seeks to fulfill Billy’s request to “take (him) to the river,” and ends with far (far) less than expected results. See the video here.

“While we’re not airing a commercial in the Big Game telecast this year, we still wanted to show up with something to say,” said Olivier Francois, Global Chief Marketing Officer, Stellantis. “This spot was built very intentionally around the 140 driver-assistance features of the all-new Jeep Cherokee Hybrid —because safety matters, but it has to be expressed in a way that’s true to Jeep. ‘Billy Goes to the River’ delivers humor and a bit of chaos, but always in service of a concrete message. If there were a prize for “the best Big Game commercial that’s not in the Big Game”, this is the ad we’d submit. Jim Jenkins, who directed our award-winning Groundhog Day spot, is back with us and once again strikes the right balance between entertainment and meaning.”“From crossing rivers to crossing generations, Jeep has spent 85 years driving freedom and adventure—and ‘Billy Goes to the River’ reconnects the brand with that enduring sense of fun,” said Bob Broderdorf, Jeep CEO. “The all-new 2026 Jeep Cherokee 4x4 hybrid, now arriving at U.S dealerships, brings drivers over 500 miles of range on a single tank so adventure is always within reach. Across our entire Jeep lineup, Jeep now offers better SUVs with better prices, more standard features, greater value, and a renewed focus on the technology and content our owners care about most.”

The commercial infuses AI-forward methodology through Parliament, an AI-forward post-production company - marrying practical effects & CG elements to create the most realistic sequences with real footage. All of the animals featured are digital generations - no real animals were used in the spot.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses a humorous online film about a father and son's unexpected journey in the new Jeep Cherokee hybrid to fulfill a request from a character named Billy, aiming for viral social media engagement.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Families and individuals in the U.S. aged 30-55 who are seeking a modern, mid-sized SUV, are open to hybrid technology, and appreciate humor and family-centric storytelling.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero online film, 'Billy Goes to the River,' released strategically across social media platforms during the advertising industry's biggest week to generate significant digital buzz and recognition.
ai_enrichment.objective: To create widespread buzz and awareness for the highly anticipated arrival of the all-new 2026 Jeep Cherokee hybrid into dealerships across the U.S., while also aiming for industry recognition as a top-performing non-Super Bowl commercial.

content.description:
Zip the digital financial services company offering innovative, people-centered products for everyday Americans, today announced the launch of its new brand platform, “In You We Trust”, which articulates Zip’s belief in customers’ financial choices and guides how the company shows up through its products, policies, and partnerships. The platform launches with “You Trust Me?,” a national campaign, developed in partnership with Majority Agency and Noble People, running from February through June 2026.As some traditional financial institutions continue to rely on established, institution-led approaches to flexibility, Zip is taking a different stance. “We’ve heard it again and again from our customers. They trust themselves more than institutions, and we agree,” said Jinal Shah, Chief Marketing and Customer Officer. “Through this brand platform, we put that belief into action by backing customers with tools designed for real life, offering access, transparency, and flexibility that reflect how they actually manage their money.”

The brand platform was born from real conversations with customers who have often been overlooked by traditional financial systems. In addition to the campaign, Zip’s product team has made thoughtful evolutions to the app experience and introduced new payment constructs and features that deepen the trust experience beyond the campaign.The launch campaign recognizes customers as considered and capable financial decision-makers. The creative builds from everyday moments into playfully exaggerated scenarios where customers ask Zip “You Trust Me?” The response is always the same: Zip trusts you with your money. The campaign highlights product constructs (from flexible payment dates to clear, upfront fees) designed to enable this trust and features one of Zip’s customers, Destiny Modeste, in the campaign’s anthem video, grounding the platform in real customer experiences.“Being known as trustworthy is our north star. To earn trust, you have to give trust,” Shah added. “This platform reflects a simple belief at the heart of Zip: people deserve financial tools that work with them, not against them.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Zip's new brand platform, 'In You We Trust,' articulates the company's belief in customers' financial choices, contrasting with traditional institution-led approaches. It is launched with a national campaign titled 'You Trust Me?'.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Everyday Americans who value self-trust in financial decisions over traditional institution-led approaches, seeking innovative, people-centered financial products.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A national campaign, 'You Trust Me?,' running from February through June 2026, primarily executed via film (implying TV and digital video distribution).
ai_enrichment.objective: To launch and establish Zip's new brand platform 'In You We Trust,' building brand awareness and positioning Zip as a financial services company that trusts its customers' choices.

metadata.title: The Pub That Refused To Die
tags: AOTW Beer, Wine and Spirits Collection

content.description:
A powerful new documentary capturing the true story of one village’s stand to save its last remaining local pub premiered on Saturday, 28 February 2026 at the Dublin International Film Festival.

Making its official global big-screen debut as part of the festival’s programme, The Pub That Refused To Die is directed by award-winning Irish filmmaker Gar O’Rourke, whose film Sanatorium was selected as Ireland’s entry for Best International Feature Film at the 2026 Academy Awards.

The documentary tells the story of 26 residents of Kilteely who united to save the heart of their community before it was lost forever. When long-time publican Noreen Ahern was no longer able to keep Aherns open, the village faced losing its last remaining shared social space and, with it, a vital hub of connection and community life. Rather than accept its loss, 26 locals from across the village came together to buy, reopen and relaunch the newly named Street Bar, despite having no prior hospitality experience.

When Heineken learned of their efforts, it stepped in to support the group, providing business advice, bar staff training through the Heineken Ahhh-cademy, and ensuring they were set up with the right tools and equipment to help them not only reopen, but begin rebuilding the business for the long term.

Today, the renewed pub has become a thriving community space once again, a symbol of pride and what people can achieve together.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Heineken is presenting a documentary that tells the true story of 26 residents of Kilteely, Ireland, who united to save their last remaining local pub, preserving their community's vital social hub.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Film festival attendees, documentary enthusiasts, individuals interested in community stories, Irish culture, and the preservation of local social institutions.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Premiere and screening at the Dublin International Film Festival, leveraging the 'official global big-screen debut' for PR and media attention and potentially future wider distribution of the documentary.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance Heineken's brand image and cultural relevance by associating the brand with authentic community stories, local heritage, and the importance of shared social spaces, through the medium of a critically acclaimed documentary.

content.description:
Casey House releases Big Fucking Deal, a short film examining the compounding effects of stigma for people living with HIV

Study reveals only 44% of Canadians are confident that people living with HIV receive stigma-free health care when they need it – a gap the film recognizes through storytelling

Casey House, a hospital unlike any other providing care for people living with and at risk of HIV, today announces the release of Big Fucking Deal, directed by Academy Award–nominated filmmaker Hubert Davis, as part of the hospital’s ongoing Smash Stigma campaign created in partnership with creative agency Bensimon Byrne and its sister shop, NarrativeXPR.

Thanks to decades of scientific innovation, HIV is medically manageable for many people. But the ability to access and sustain consistent HIV care is not equally shared, especially when barriers compound and reinforce one another. A study commissioned by Casey House found that more than half of Canadians (54%) don’t feel they understand what it means to live with HIV today – a gap that leaves stigma unchallenged and barriers unseen. Big Fucking Deal responds by demonstrating how stigma intensifies and outcomes diverge when an HIV diagnosis intersects with challenges such as housing insecurity, substance use dependency, mental health challenges, and discrimination tied to identity.

“We’ve made extraordinary progress in HIV treatment, but equity has not kept pace,” said Joanne Simons, CEO, Casey House. “This film, the sixth edition of our longstanding Smash Stigma initiative, is a call to see the full humanity of people living with HIV. It asks Canadians to replace assumption with understanding and empathy, especially when other challenges are present in people’s lives.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Casey House's 'Big Fucking Deal' is a powerful short film directed by Hubert Davis, created to expose and challenge the profound, compounding effects of stigma faced by people living with HIV and the resulting barriers to stigma-free healthcare. It uses storytelling to highlight this critical, often overlooked societal issue.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Canadians, including the general public, healthcare professionals, and policymakers, who may hold misconceptions about HIV or are unaware of the ongoing stigma and healthcare disparities faced by people living with HIV.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The primary tactic is the release of 'Big Fucking Deal,' a short film directed by Academy Award–nominated filmmaker Hubert Davis, as a central piece of Casey House’s ongoing 'Smash Stigma' public awareness campaign.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise public awareness about the persistent and harmful stigma faced by people living with HIV and to advocate for equitable, stigma-free healthcare.

metadata.title: KFC Doritos Zinger — Better with Pepsi

content.description:
KFC was already good, but now? It’s next level! Step 1: Grab a KFC Sandwich. Epic flavor, right? Step 2: Make it crunch with Doritos. Did you ever imagine something like this? Step 3: Add a Pepsi. The combo you didn’t see coming. Your favorites, all in one. UUUFFFF – CRUNCHHH – AAHHHH Better with Pepsi.

 

Executive Creative Directors: Daniel Ávila / Francisco Villa  .  Creative Directors: Juan David Pérez  .  Copywriter: Alejandro Muñoz / Geraldine Rodríguez  .  Senior Art Director: Juan David Castelblanco  .  Art Director: Valentina Delgado  .  Content Director: Paula DANIELA Gil  .  Content Creator: Ana Ospina  .  Production: Macarena

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign positions Pepsi as the ultimate enhancer for the new KFC Doritos Zinger sandwich, creating an unexpected and elevated flavor and crunch experience by combining three popular brands into one extraordinary meal. It highlights the synergistic combination of three popular brands to deliver an extraordinary meal.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young adults and teenagers (16-35) who enjoy fast food, snack innovations, and are looking for exciting, indulgent, and unexpected flavor combinations.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film, likely deployed across digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media) and potentially traditional TV, showcasing the multi-sensory experience of the KFC Doritos Zinger with Pepsi.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive brand association and preference for Pepsi by positioning it as the indispensable beverage that completes and enhances the new, exciting KFC Doritos Zinger meal, ultimately boosting Pepsi sales.

content.description:
The new HONOR X9d is more than a smartphone with a durable body and long-lasting battery - it mirrors the character of those who rely on it: strong, dependable, and ready for anything.

To express this idea, we told the story of a gamekeeper - a man whose life unfolds in the untamed landscapes of Kazakhstan. Side by side with his HONOR X9d, he navigates harsh terrain, captures rare moments, and proves that true reliability is built for the real world.

HONOR Innovative Packaging: VIP Box Design Concepts

metadata.title: El Conejo que Soñó en Grande

content.description:
Hicimos un homenaje a Benito, un artista que se ha convertido en símbolo de lo que significa para los latinos creer en sí mismos y atreverse a soñar en grande. Cada paso, cada trayecto y cada guiño representan el esfuerzo detrás de los sueños que parecen imposibles. Esta pieza no solo celebra el éxito, sino el proceso de creer, intentarlo y avanzar de un latino. Porque nuestra campaña busca inspirar a todos a confiar en su talento, a romper límites y a alcanzar aquello que se proponen. A todos soñemos en grande.-----We paid tribute to Benito, an artist who has become a symbol of what it means for Latinos to believe in themselves and dare to dream big. Every step, every journey, and every nod represents the effort behind dreams that seem impossible. This piece doesn’t just celebrate success, but the process of believing, trying, and moving forward as a Latino. Because our campaign seeks to inspire everyone to trust their talent, break boundaries, and achieve what they set out to accomplish.

 

Advertising Agency: Norlop VML, Quito, EcuadorCEO Norlop VML: Cecilia Solá | Francisco SoláGeneral Manager: Rafael OrdóñezGeneral Account Director: Valeria IzquierdoChief Creative Officer: Danilo MolinaCreative Director: Andrés SánchezLead Copywriter: Karina RonArt Directors: Juan Ordóñez | Roberto GuevaraGraphic Design: Madelein GálvezAccount Executive: Cristina Freire | Paullette ArceCEO, Corporación Maresa: Santiago CastroChery Ecuador Manager: Andrés CadenaChery Ecuador Marketing Manager: Christian CazarChery Ecuador Marketing Coordinator: Camila Ron

ai_enrichment.target_audience: Aspirational individuals within the Latino community who identify with cultural symbols of success and are striving to achieve their dreams, likely young to middle-aged adults interested in automotive mobility.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A narrative-driven content piece, likely a hero film or a series of inspiring short-form videos, distributed across digital platforms to maximize reach within the target community.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand affinity and emotional connection with the Latino community by aligning Chery with their values of perseverance, dreaming big, and achieving personal success.

content.description:
BURGER KING® France is launching a new outdoor campaign in the Paris metro to celebrate its most loyal friends of all: its Baby Burgers.

Available in boxes of 3 or 9, Baby Burgers are perfect for every occasion. Binge-watching nights. The big game. A slump in energy. End of the month blues. They always show up. And above all… they never leave dirty dishes in the sink.

You might not wear them around your neck, but they’re never far away: delivered to your door, in-restaurant, or at the drive-thru. And unlike many others, they never make excuses. Simple friends. Highly recommended. Always in. Always made to share.

Created by Buzzman, orchestrated by Wavemaker, with the support of Mediatransports, the campaign is now live in France throughout the Paris metro.

Any resemblance to an existing or past campaign is purely coincidental. Obviously.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Burger King's "SORRY MY FRIEND" campaign personifies its Baby Burgers as the ultimate loyal and hassle-free friends, highlighting their convenience and shareability as superior companions for any occasion.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young adults and millennials (approx. 18-35) who enjoy casual dining, entertainment, and social sharing, seeking convenient, affordable, and comforting food options for various everyday occasions.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Print-based outdoor advertising prominently displayed throughout the Paris metro system in France.
ai_enrichment.objective: To celebrate and promote Burger King's Baby Burgers, highlighting their convenience and shareability, thereby encouraging consumption and driving sales.

content.description:
Morgan & Morgan’s latest national campaigns bring cinematic craft and spirited comedy to a category rarely known for either. The firm is rolling out new installments of its national campaigns, “You Wouldn’t” and “Power Move.” One plays in the world of social logic and everyday misfires. The other leans into unapologetic confidence. Together, they make a simple point: when the stakes are high, you don’t gamble.

The newest “You Wouldn’t” spot, titled “Laid Off,” opens inside a corporate office where an employee is called in to see his boss and instead is met by a full-blown barbershop quartet. The singers harmonize their way through the worst news of his career. It’s awkward. It’s unnecessary. It’s exactly the kind of thing you wouldn’t do.

“I’ve never been afraid to break the mold or crack a smile with our ads,” said Morgan & Morgan founder John Morgan. “Humor is a hallmark of our brand, but results are our legacy. Our newest campaign bridges that gap, striking a balance between creativity and delivering an important message: when it matters most, you want the firm that has the resources to win against corporate defendants.”

The “You Wouldn’t” campaign previously spotlighted social self-sabotage including hiring your ex to officiate your wedding.

Meanwhile, “Power Move” launched with a crime-drama inspired interrogation spot starring an owl as the enforcer of smart decisions.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Morgan & Morgan leverages spirited comedy and cinematic storytelling to create memorable, national film spots that satirize awkward 'you wouldn't' situations, contrasting them with the serious need for competent legal help in high-stakes personal matters.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals facing high-stakes life events requiring professional legal services (e.g., job loss, personal injury) who appreciate a fresh, confident, and slightly humorous approach to a typically serious industry.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: National film spots broadcast across television and digital video platforms, specifically showcasing 'You Wouldn't' campaign installments like the 'Laid Off' spot.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand awareness and consideration for Morgan & Morgan, positioning them as the trustworthy and indispensable legal partner when stakes are high, by differentiating through unique and memorable creative.

Go F****** Yourself." — reads as provocation until you count the asterisks. The URL pays it off: FisforFlorida.com. Three displays, one idea, zero headshots.

content.description:
For the world, Costa Rica is pura vida, nature… and that rolled “ERRE” (R) that gives us away anywhere.A letter that, while part of our identity, has become a subject of mockery in other countries.

Imperial — the beer, or rather the "biRRA" of Costa Rica — believes there’s no room to be ashamed of what makes us unique. That’s why we decided to transform our label, one of our most iconic elements and a source of pride for Costa Ricans, to remind them that there’s something else to be even prouder of: the rolled “R.”

 

Production Company: RINO FILMSExecutive Producer: Jaime E. Forero S.Director: Juan Gordon .Director of Photography: Andres Morales (ADFC).

​​​​​​​Advertising Agency: Garnier BBDODGC: Sergio Chinchilla / DGA: Nani AstúaCreative Director Agency:  Camilo Valencia - Julián RobertArt Direction Agency: Juan Aguilar - Camilo ValenciaCopywriter Agency: Jimena Sagot - Julián RobertBusiness Director Agency: Ana ChinchillaAccount Executive Agency: Natalia Pereira

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Imperial Beer rebrands itself as 'biRRA' by altering its iconic label to celebrate and instill pride in the distinctive Costa Rican rolled 'R,' turning a potential point of mockery into a unique national identifier.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Costa Ricans, particularly adult beer drinkers who identify with national symbols and may feel sensitive about the distinct rolled 'R' pronunciation.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A film campaign (likely for TV and digital platforms) accompanied by a special edition product label redesign for Imperial Beer.
ai_enrichment.objective: To foster national pride among Costa Ricans by celebrating their unique rolled 'R' pronunciation, thereby strengthening Imperial Beer's emotional connection and relevance as a brand deeply rooted in Costa Rican identity.

Under the tagline “No Matter What Happens, Youse,” the humorous campaign promotes the brand’s two-year fixed-price auto insurance, reinforcing its key differentiator of price stability, digital convenience, and customer flexibility across multiple purchase channels.

metadata.title: The RelationChip
metadata.brand: The Portuguese Association for Victim Support

content.description:
The Portuguese Association for Victim Support

BBDO SHOCKS PORTUGAL WITH BIOCHIPS FOR COUPLESThe Portuguese Association for Victim Support (APAV), a national non-profit organization, was worried because Gen-Z couples use their smartphones for abusive control.With that in mind, BBDO Portugal created a fake brand and a fake product: The RelationChip, two microchips for couples to implant in one another and monitor each other through a phone app.One week before Valentine’s Day, influencers introduced the "RelationChip" to their communities and were the first to criticize it and amplify this negative feeling. Things blew off. People were shocked.Three days later came the revelation and APAV stated its true intentions: to fuel the discussion on abuse and confront young couples with their own controlling behavior. Many were outraged over a physical chip — yet, in reality, they exert the exact same control over others through their smartphones.The goal was overachieved: the campaign opened an urgent discussion, established digital control as a form of violence, and left a powerful statement: that young couples need to change, or, as we say, need to "change the chip".

 

Agency – BBDOChief Creative Officer – Rui SilvaExecutive Creative Director – Pedro PinhoCreative Directors – João Guimarães | Julliano Bertoldi | Raoul Van Harten | Pedro GonçalvesCreatives:Copywriter – Margarida TorresArt Director – João FredericoArt Director – Beatriz GonçalvesCopywriter – Joana HenriquesAccount Director – Joana MargalhaAccount Supervisor – Dina CamachoAccount Executives – Matilde Moura | Beatriz RamosHead of Digital & Innovation – David QuintaSocial Media Manager – Mara CostaWeb Designer – Leonor MontenegroMotion Designer – Adriana LourençoWeb Developers – João Camacho | Carlos SalvadoStrategic Planning Director – Lettícia MarconStrategic Planner – Carmem EstevesFinal Artworker – Cristina SantosProduction Company – LOOKS:GOODExecutive Producer – Inês FerreiraHead of Production – Manuel BarbosaProduction Coordinator – Leonor NobreDirector – António Amaral1st Assistant Director (1st AD) – Nelson SantosDirector of Photography – João Castela1st Camera Assistant – Francisco Borges2nd Camera Assistant – Daniel FerreiraVídeo Assist – Sara SousaGimbal Operator – Rodrigo AlbuquerqueGaffer – Ricardo GiglioElectrician Assistant – Carlos ZneidaElectrician Assistant – Dan IlcoSound – Ricardo BarrileiroWardrobe – Marta LoboMake-up / Hair – Beatriz TexugoArt Director – Margarida DiasArt Assistant – Silvia DiasArt Assistant – Vitor SerranoArt Assistant – Luis ÁguaImage Post-Production – Tribo FXSound Studio – Som de LisboaVoice Over – Diana NicolauMusic – Pedro Gomes aka kamaraumEditor – César SantosPhotography – Diana NetoEquipment – ContraCampo & HHA DigitalLocations – Hotel RomaOmnicom PR GroupOMDAccount Director – Rui MenaiaAccount Executives – João Fernandes | Sara Martinho

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: BBDO Portugal created a fake brand and product, 'The RelationChip' – microchips for couples to monitor each other via a phone app – which was introduced by influencers and then criticized, leading to a public revelation by APAV to spark discussion about abusive control in relationships.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Gen-Z couples in Portugal who may be unknowingly engaging in or affected by abusive control through smartphones, and the broader Portuguese public.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Influencer marketing (introducing and then criticizing the fake product one week before Valentine's Day), social media amplification of negative sentiment, and a public revelation and statement from APAV three days later.
ai_enrichment.objective: To fuel public discussion on relationship abuse, confront young couples with their own co-dependent and potentially abusive behaviors, and raise awareness for The Portuguese Association for Victim Support (APAV).

content.description:
Run Club Abuja (RCA) is a dynamic FCT fitness community with a mission to promote health & fitness, and drive measurable social impact across education, health, and community development.  It's primary philanthropic vehicle is the annual Charity Half Marathon, consistently hosted since 2021. The club effectively converts community passion into social capital, positioning running as a means for tangible change in the Federal Capital Territory, Nigeria.In 2025, Government Secondary School Gwagwa, Abuja was adopted and the challenge  the students had was limited access to modern learning resources and basic digital tools which hindered their ability to compete in an increasingly digital world.With zero budget, Run For Tomorrow was a 3 months campaign that was beyond running miles but running to support the kids with digital literacy.

 

Agency: JT Agency, AbujaProduction Company: JT Agency, AbujaCreative Director: Olalekan AkinyeleCopy Writer: Olalekan Akinyele / Seyi AjagbeArt Director: Timothy Igbokwe / Pelumi Aina-Badejo / Ife Olulade / Favour KennedyAnimation: Favour OjovwoPhotographer: Samuel Alabi / Ifeoluwa Agboola / Muhammad UmarVideographers: Chimobi Onwualu / Temitope Olanrewaju / Segun JanaEditor: Segun Jana / Favour OjovwoVoice Over: Seyi Ajagbe

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The 'Run for Tomorrow' campaign mobilizes Run Club Abuja's community to leverage their passion for running as a vehicle for social change, aiming to bridge the digital divide for students at Government Secondary School Gwagwa through resource acquisition.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Fitness enthusiasts, community-minded individuals, and potential donors in the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Nigeria, aged 20-60, interested in health, fitness, and educational philanthropy.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A film (likely documentary or promotional) distributed through organic digital channels (e.g., social media, club website, community screenings) and integrated with the annual Charity Half Marathon to engage participants and solicit support, all executed with a zero budget.
ai_enrichment.objective: To mobilize community support and resources to provide modern learning tools and basic digital access for students at Government Secondary School Gwagwa, driving measurable social impact in education.

content.description:
In a market where, according to data from the National Center of Cinematography, only 11 out of 108 cinemas in Romania are art-house cinemas, Apollo111 Cinema sets out to establish itself as a clear point of reference for author-driven cinema in Bucharest.The campaign marks the launch and positioning of Apollo111 Cinema within the art-house circuit, addressing audiences who actively seek festival films, independent productions, classics, and films that prioritize artistic, cultural, and innovative value over mass-market commercial success.The creative concept is built around a cultural contrast between mainstream cinema and art-house cinema. Through visual reinterpretations and copy lines that play with well-known pop culture titles, the campaign clearly differentiates Apollo111 Cinema from multiplex offerings—without dismissing or mocking them. Instead, it uses subtle humor, cultural references, and self-awareness as a shared language with a cinephile audience.The tagline “Art films, mostly” functions as a statement of identity: honest, relaxed, and deliberately unpretentious. Apollo111 Cinema does not promise rigid exclusivity, but a carefully curated selection where cinema is treated as an experience, a conversation, and a form of artistic expression.

 

Advertising Agency: Rusu+Bortun / Bucharest / RomâniaAgency website: www.rusubortun.ro

Creative Director: Catalin RusuCopywriter: Catalin Rusu, Anita KevorchianArt Director: Anita Kevorchian, Lucia Parapiru, Catalin Rusu

Published: January 2026

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The core creative idea differentiates Apollo111 Cinema as an art-house venue by contrasting it with mainstream cinema through visual reinterpretations and wordplay on well-known pop culture titles.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Art-house cinema enthusiasts in Bucharest who actively seek festival films, independent productions, classics, and films prioritizing artistic, cultural, and innovative value.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Print advertisements featuring visual reinterpretations and witty copy lines that play with well-known pop culture titles.
ai_enrichment.objective: To launch and position Apollo111 Cinema as a clear point of reference for author-driven cinema within Bucharest's art-house circuit.

content.description:
Developed in partnership with creative Agency of Record, Bakery, Tree Hut debuted its first-ever Super Bowl spot, "Uncontain Yourself” this morning on TikTok and Instagram.

The 30-second film, which stars several of the social media Creators who have fueled the brand’s fervent social media fandom, follows the brand’s December rebrand and first linear advertising push.

This new spot pushes against increasingly bland conventions for the beauty category, encouraging people to break out of the mold and embrace color, texture, scent, and unapologetic joy.  In “Uncontain Yourself,” a group of people in bathrobes methodically follow a rigid “clean girl” routine, guided by a voiceover reciting a polished script.  The uniformity is abruptly disrupted when one woman grabs a bright pink scrub and hurls it at the speaker. The room erupts into action, bathrobes drop, color floods the space, and participants cover themselves in bold, vibrant scrubs. What begins as restraint transforms into release, as everyone breaks free from sameness and discovers joy that refuses to be bottled up.

Instead of relying on celebrity endorsements, Tree Hut flipped the traditional Super Bowl formula by casting real fans, creators, and longtime community members in the spot.  The result is a community-powered spectacle that brings the energy of TikTok to broadcast television, celebrating the people who helped build the brand from the start.Featured creators include:Lanie Kristin (200.9k followers)Olivia Dejarnett (443.7k followers)Valeria Stephanie (192.3k followers)Paul Fino (2.5M followers)Emily Lula May (1.9M followers)Courtney Quinn (340k followers)Lisi German (3.4M followers)

Tree Hut decided to air the spot during the big game nationally on Peacock because the vast majority of the brand’s target audience will tune in via-streaming.  Media was handled by the brands media company’s sister company, KEN Media (https://www.ken.media/). The work will also air via regional broadcast buys in Tree Hut’s top selling core markets—DFW, NYC, LA, Chicago and Miami.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign challenges conventional, rigid 'clean girl' beauty routines by showcasing Tree Hut's vibrant products, encouraging people to break out of the mold and embrace color, texture, scent, and unapologetic joy. It depicts a shift from uniformity to lively self-expression.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals, likely younger demographics and beauty enthusiasts, who feel constrained by traditional or 'bland' beauty norms and seek to express their individuality, embrace color, texture, and joy in their self-care routines.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 30-second hero film launched as a Super Bowl spot, amplified simultaneously on TikTok and Instagram, featuring social media creators to engage and leverage existing brand fandom.
ai_enrichment.objective: To significantly increase brand awareness and engagement for Tree Hut following its recent rebrand, positioning it as a brand that champions joyful, expressive beauty.
