content.description:
Developed in partnership with Anomaly, the Super Bowl campaign stems from the growing frustration consumers feel with excessive food delivery fees, positioning Grubhub as the brand willing to “eat the fees” on orders over $50.

As the original food delivery website, Grubhub has always centered its business around food. However, in recent years, the barrage of fees across the category has grown increasingly out of control, often overshadowing the experience itself. In the :30 hero film, the story unfolds during a bizarre and dramatic dinner party, where a theatrical cast of guests dine together in an opulent setting.

Lending his signature visual style and tone, Lanthimos transforms the formal meal into an unsettling and darkly humorous experience, building tension with a track from his frequent musical collaborator, and Oscar-nominated composer, Jerskin Fendrix. As the meal reaches its conclusion, the waiter serves the final course, the fees. One guest asks, “Who will eat the fees?” The camera pans to reveal George Clooney, who calmly and heroically declares that Grubhub will eat the fees. The tension breaks as the guests erupt in celebration and shock, chanting Clooney’s line and reinforcing the brand’s promise.

The campaign airs nationally during the Super Bowl on February 8, 2026 on NBC.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Grubhub addresses widespread consumer frustration with high food delivery fees by positioning itself as the brand willing to 'eat the fees' on orders over $50, showcased through a darkly humorous and unsettling Super Bowl film.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Food delivery users who are frustrated by excessive delivery fees, especially those who frequently place larger orders (over $50).
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A :30 hero film aired during the Super Bowl, developed in partnership with Anomaly and directed with a signature visual style and tone.
ai_enrichment.objective: Position Grubhub as a consumer-centric brand that actively solves a major pain point (excessive fees) in the food delivery industry, aiming to attract and retain customers by differentiating itself from competitors.

content.description:
The fight for freedom never truly ends. That's why we've created The Bunker Kit - an embroidered blanket with a book full of stories about war heroes who fought for our freedom during WWII.We've used the fact that the blanket forts built by kids are called the same as uprising fighter hideouts - “bunkers”. This way, the bunker’s meaning had shifted from wartime protection to a child’s safe hideout, creating a parent-led entry point for anti-fascist learning.

To support The Bunker Kit, we've created this short film, in which a young girl's imagination transports her into one of these war stories. She follows a resistance fighter of the Slovak National Uprising during WWII and experiences the fear every hero had to face - and overcome - so we can live free today.

 

Creative Agency: Free AndyProduction Company: RE:SET PRODUCTIONPR Agency: Wordin

Creative Director: Juraj BartošAssociate Creative Director: Tomáš SlobodníkCopywriter: Adriana Takáčová, Eva Myjavcová, Juraj Bartoš, Marek Surovec, Tomáš SlobodníkHead of Art: Karol KrištofovičArt Director: Viktória Babjaková, Martina ValíčkováGraphic Designer: Marek Čemez, Lucia HorinekováPhotorapher: Tereza KopeckáAccount manager: Ivana Šramatá, Veronika ZacharováStrategist: Emil MyjavecDirector: Dano DekanDOP: Majo ŽilinanCameraman: Rafael SkurkaExecutive Producer: Martin RepkaLocation Producer: Jana RepkováProduction Manager: Adriana IngeliEditor: Marek KráľovskýColor Grading: Lucia KovaľováSound Design: Vladimír Bajnoci, Bajko StudioIllustrator: Ivana ŠátekováPR director: Adam Bačkor

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign leverages the dual meaning of 'bunker' – a child's blanket fort and a WWII resistance hideout – to create an educational kit (embroidered blanket + storybook) that helps parents introduce anti-fascist learning through heroic war stories, supported by a short film.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Parents of young children interested in teaching their kids about history, freedom, and anti-fascist values, specifically related to WWII.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A short film depicting a young girl's imaginative journey into WWII resistance stories, used to promote and contextualize 'The Bunker Kit' (an embroidered blanket and storybook).
ai_enrichment.objective: To educate families and promote anti-fascist values by sharing stories of WWII heroes and encouraging historical learning.

content.description:
New Zealand's 'Bureau of Nicknames' (ANZBON)

Product design and development platform Figma has launched Australia and New Zealand's 'Bureau of Nicknames' (ANZBON), with director Angie Bird from TRUCE and Hellions.

Acting as an entirely self-appointed authority, the Bureau was established to shine a light on the Aussie and Kiwi art of nicknaming. Born from Figma’s mission to help teams turn ideas into real products, the project functions as a playful department for the region’s most informal ideas, celebrating the everyday spirit of local creators.

Developed in collaboration with director, Angie “Birdie” Bird, Truce Production Co., and indie shop Hellions, the world of ANZBON unfolds through a series of narrative films, socials, and a microsite dedicated to ensuring every Australian and New Zealander can have their unofficial name officially recognised.

The ambitious product demo showcases Figma’s ability to take a thought from concept to reality by designing, prototyping and bringing an entire fictional organisation to life.Yosub Kim, director of brand marketing at Figma, said the project was designed to showcase how effortlessly ideas can be built and shared.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Figma's 'Bureau of Nicknames' (ANZBON) is a self-appointed authority celebrating the unique Australian and New Zealand art of nicknaming, linking it playfully to the creative process of turning informal ideas into real products.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and creative teams in Australia and New Zealand who appreciate local culture, humor, and the informal genesis of creative ideas, likely those interested in design and technology.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A series of narrative films, social media content, and a dedicated microsite.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand awareness and engagement for Figma in Australia and New Zealand by playfully connecting its mission of turning ideas into products with the local culture of nicknaming and everyday creativity.

metadata.title: The Network That Works For You

content.description:
LinkedIn Teams Up with McCann to Launch New Brand Campaign - “The Network That Works For You”

The new campaign marks McCann’s first creative work for the professional network

LinkedIn knows the world of work. They know that although people face a lot of challenges at work today, there are a lot of funny moments, too. Created in collaboration with McCann New York, LinkedIn’s new campaign – The Network that Works For You – uses humor to depict those hilarious and sometimes awkward work moments so many people have experienced. The campaign acknowledges that LinkedIn can't fix every work challenge, but it does work when it matters most to keep you growing – in your career or business.

Across film and social, sharp lines like “LinkedIn doesn’t make your boss easier to deal with” land with a confident payoff: “but it does connect you to your next one.” The same smart contrast shows up when “LinkedIn Hiring Pro can help you find the ideal candidate,” followed by a painfully awkward welcome lunch that proves the network “cannot help you find the ideal restaurant for their welcome lunch.” The work playfully positions LinkedIn as the partner that helps people and companies move forward — turning real effort into tangible career and business growth.

This campaign, which includes two focus areas – Grow in Your Career and Grow in Your Business - marks a shift in the brand’s marketing strategy to tell a more unified story about the value that LinkedIn delivers, reinforcing how the network works for people and companies.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses humor to depict relatable, often awkward work moments, acknowledging that while LinkedIn can't solve every work challenge, it is crucial for career and business growth.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Professionals and business owners experiencing the challenges and humorous moments of modern work life, seeking growth and networking opportunities.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign will be executed across film and social media platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reinforce LinkedIn's value as a professional network that supports career and business growth, while increasing brand affinity through relatable humor.

content.description:
Today, Hawaiian Airlines launches “We Fly for Hawai‘i,” celebrating the spirit, people and culture of Hawai‘i, airing before and after the Big Game across the state of Hawai‘i. Developed in partnership with Mekanism, the airline’s agency of record, the campaign is inspired by the Hawaiian word mo‘olelo, meaning storytelling, and leans into the notion that the brand’s story continues to unfold with new and exciting ways for people to experience Hawaiian Airlines.

The campaign positions Hawaiian Airlines as more than a way to get from point A to point B — it shares the spirit of Hawai‘i around the world. From the naming of its aircraft to its onboard hoʻokipa (hospitality) and cabin design, every detail reflects the airline’s island home. Guided by aloha and kuleana (responsibility), Hawaiian Airlines proudly serves its communities and welcomes its guests with warmth and a deep respect for the culture that defines it.

The work incorporates employee-generated content, giving the campaign an authentic, made-by-its-own-people perspective. It will also spotlight key brand initiatives, including Atmos Rewards and Huaka‘i by Hawaiian, further connecting guests to the airline’s offerings and Hawaiian culture.

The campaign will run in the Super Bowl on February 8, 2026 during pregame coverage.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Hawaiian Airlines' 'We Fly for Hawai‘i' campaign positions the airline as a living story of Hawai‘i, embodying and sharing the islands' spirit, culture, and hospitality globally rather than just being a mode of transport.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Travelers seeking an authentic Hawaiian experience and residents of Hawai‘i, as well as individuals globally who appreciate Hawaiian culture and hospitality.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film airing on television before and after the 'Big Game' across the state of Hawai‘i.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand perception by positioning Hawaiian Airlines as the authentic embodiment and global ambassador of Hawai‘i's spirit, culture, and hospitality.

content.description:
Across languages, accents, and cultures, a simple act — speaking — can carry weight. Words feel heavier when you’re afraid of being misunderstood. When your accent might be judged. When clarity matters most.

This film introduces Verkada’s Intercom Live Translation through a deeply human lens. Real voices, in multiple languages, share the same vulnerability: the fear of not being heard as intended.

 

Director: Bo DengProducer: Barbara VekaricCinematographer: Konstantin LyubimovEditor: Patrick Dear

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign introduces Verkada’s Intercom Live Translation through a deeply human lens, showcasing the universal vulnerability and fear of misunderstanding due to language barriers and accents, and positioning the product as the solution for clear, empathetic communication.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Businesses, organizations, and institutions with diverse workforces, international operations, or customer bases that require reliable real-time multilingual communication solutions; decision-makers in IT, operations, or security.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film, professionally produced (evident from listed director, cinematographer, editor), distributed across digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media, company website) to introduce the product.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build awareness and educate potential clients about Verkada’s new Intercom Live Translation feature and its ability to overcome communication barriers.

content.description:
Patak’s has launched a partnership with Mob as part of a new content-led campaign designed to demonstrate how easy it is to ‘cook like a curry house’ at home using Patak’s Spice Pastes.

The collaboration was negotiated by independent media agency the7stars, and brings Patak’s together with Mob’s highly engaged, digital-first audience to inspire a new generation of home cooks.

Mob has produced a series of videos featuring their talented chefs rustling up a selection of tasty recipes to showcase the versatility of Patak’s range of products and inspire the nation to bring the craft of a curry house into their homes using Patak’s Spice Paste.

The first video, live today, sees chef Adam Bush creating a creamy chicken korma with cumin-spiced crispy naan croutons made with Patak’s Korma Paste Pot. This is followed by Sophie Wyburd cooking up a zingy veggie curry combining squash and halloumi with caramelised limes using Patak’s Tikka Masala Paste Pot.

The activity will run across Mob’s website and social platforms, with recipe content appearing on Instagram, Facebook and TikTok. The partnership is part of a broader integrated campaign, including an AV spot created by Ourselves, and spanning TV, VOD and YouTube, which launched on 19th January. VOD ad placement is handled through the7stars’ programmatic platform Prospero.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Patak’s has partnered with Mob to launch a content-led campaign featuring video recipes that demonstrate the ease of creating authentic curry house dishes at home using Patak's Spice Pastes.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Digital-first home cooks, particularly younger adults, who are interested in preparing authentic and convenient international cuisine, specifically Indian food.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Partnership with Mob to produce a series of instructional cooking videos featuring Mob chefs showcasing Patak's Spice Pastes, distributed to Mob's highly engaged digital audience.
ai_enrichment.objective: To inspire and educate a new generation of home cooks on the ease and versatility of creating authentic curry house meals using Patak's Spice Pastes, aiming to drive product usage and engagement.

content.description:
This Chinese New Year, we celebrate the journeys that bring us back to what matters most.

Follow one woman’s heartfelt journey home, reminding us that distance is never just about miles travelled, but about the moments, memories, and reunions that make every return meaningful.

Wherever your journey begins, may it lead you closer to the people and moments that matter most.

Wishing you and your loved ones a warm andmeaningful Chinese New Year.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A heartfelt narrative centered on the emotional significance of journeys home for Chinese New Year, emphasizing that true value lies in family reunions and cherished memories, not just the distance traveled.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals of Chinese heritage or those celebrating Chinese New Year, who travel or plan to travel to reunite with family, and value emotional connections and meaningful experiences.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film depicting a woman's journey home, primarily distributed across digital platforms like YouTube and social media, potentially complemented by television broadcast for broader reach.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand affinity and establish Journify as the facilitator of meaningful journeys and family reunions, particularly during the Chinese New Year period.

metadata.title: Black Mirror realized with autonomous AI Selves

Black Mirror realized with autonomous AI Selves

This is the new launch film from AI startup Pika for their latest product: AI Selves—AI you birth, raise, and set loose to be a living extension of yourself. The insanity of this concept needed an equally startling marketing plan, one that felt like Black Mirror coming to life.

content.description:
A silent “epidemic” affecting an increasing number of young people, self-harm, is brought into the light through the new film for the Greek Ministry of Health in collaboration with UNICEF, as part of the National Initiative for the Promotion of Child and Family Health. Teenagers and young adults often resort to razors, knives, compasses or even fire to hurt themselves. The film draws inspiration from these real incidents, but subverts them; the marks we see don’t cut into skin, but into everyday surfaces around us. Through this inversion, the film manages to create a powerful impact without showing graphic imagery and highlights its urgent message: 1 in 6 teenagers and young people in Greece self-harm.

ai_enrichment.target_audience: Teenagers and young adults in Greece who may be at risk or experiencing self-harm, along with their families, educators, and the broader Greek public, as part of a national health initiative.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across relevant media channels (e.g., TV, online video platforms) to maximize reach and impact.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise public awareness about the prevalence of self-harm among teenagers and young people in Greece and to support the National Initiative for the Promotion of Child and Family Health.

content.description:
Deel, the rapidly growing all-in-one payroll, HR, and IT services platform has spent the last year ramping up its presence across brand partnerships and category-first crypto integrations. This month it’s debuting a new, 100% AI-generated creative campaign from Superbloom House (SBH).

Called “Dreaming of DEELING,” the campaign expands on the hybrid AI-powered visuals from last year’s “Feeling of Deeling” work, visualizing more expansive, dreamlike worlds where Deel customers are transported to a space where their emotional needs are met. “Dreaming of DEELING” integrated AI from idea to execution, with the only human-developed element being the creative copywriting. This push to a fully-AI generated workflow is an intentional evolution from the brand's first campaign, which used hybrid AI and real actors and real production set design with transportative AI world building.

The concept was taken to new heights thanks to Creative Collective member, Si Parmeggiani (the AI creator behind Neptunian Glitterball, a viral IG followed by creative tastemakers like Darren Aronofsky, James Gunn and Will.i.Am). Si worked with the agency creative team from start to finish, helping not only create a new visual brand identity and virtual world inspired by real world customers, told through a series of 30 second spots, rolling out globally from February into May. The worlds created by Si and produced by SuperBloom will include an entire package of creative assets that the brand can customize and leverage across its lower-funnel executions, giving the campaign continued life far beyond launch.

The campaign includes the launch spot, as well as two other :30 films (coming in March), a host of digital ads, and a series of massive OOH work that will be used to ambush dream locations like SXSW, host cities for the 2026 World Cup, and conferences and key events in Vegas.

For the campaign, SBH tapped Parmiggiani to leverage his deep knowledge of creative technology and expertise in AI. Si acted as not only a technologist and practitioner of AI worlds, but also the Co-Creative Director, leading art direction and design across all creative, including still imagery and video.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign employs 100% AI-generated film visuals to transport Deel customers into expansive, dreamlike worlds where their emotional needs concerning HR, payroll, and IT services are met.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Businesses and their decision-makers (HR and finance professionals) seeking an all-in-one payroll, HR, and IT services platform, who value innovation and emotional reassurance.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 100% AI-generated creative film campaign, developed by Superbloom House.
ai_enrichment.objective: To strengthen brand perception, build deeper emotional connections with customers, and highlight Deel's innovative use of AI in its marketing and service offering.

metadata.title: Hay algo hermoso que nos une

content.description:
Deprati celebra la belleza real en su Semana de la Belleza

En un mundo donde la belleza ya no se define por estándares únicos, Deprati presenta Semana de la Belleza con un mensaje claro y cercano: “Hay algo hermoso que nos une”. Una campaña que entiende la belleza como un acto cotidiano, espontáneo y profundamente humano.

Creada bajo la dirección creativa de la agencia independiente BBA, en colaboración con el equipo de Deprati, la campaña pone en el centro una verdad simple pero poderosa: la belleza sucede en los momentos más reales, no en los más perfectos.

Una belleza que se vive, no que se impone

El insight que da vida a la campaña nace de una escena universal: el auto como espacio de complicidad, ese lugar donde nos miramos en el espejo retrovisor, nos retocamos el maquillaje, arreglamos el cabello y, sobre todo, compartimos risas, confidencias y tiempo juntas.Porque antes de llegar al evento, al trabajo o a una cita, la belleza ya ocurrió: ocurrió en el camino, entre amigas, siendo una misma.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Deprati's "Semana de la Belleza" campaign celebrates the universal, spontaneous, and everyday acts of beauty, emphasizing that "something beautiful unites us" in real, imperfect moments.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals who appreciate authentic, relatable beauty and self-care in their daily lives, recognizing that beauty is a personal and shared human experience; likely current and potential beauty product consumers of Deprati.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 360° integrated campaign, likely utilizing video content across various channels (TV, digital, social media) and potentially print or out-of-home advertising, to depict real, everyday moments of beauty.
ai_enrichment.objective: To redefine beauty standards for Deprati, celebrating real and everyday beauty to enhance brand relevance and connection with consumers during "Semana de la Belleza".

content.description:
Alexis Gomez, a gifted visual artist on the roster of Little Minx, directed “Big Steps,” the stunning Lincoln Nautilus campaign that broke this month. The campaign tells the story of a beautiful wedding reception and features the bride and her father settling back in a 2026 Nautilus Hybrid, sharing a quiet acknowledgement of their unspoken bond. The serene and heartfelt moment captures the ultra-comfort and tranquility inherent in the luxurious two-row SUV as well as the striking panoramic display that wraps around the front of the cabin. The interior of the Nautilus envelops their moment together, and time itself seems to pause.

Gomez is known for creating enigmatic worlds through the beauty and power of the seemingly ordinary, subtle and simple, melding the vitality of raw, energetic human moments with the quietly intimate to portray our duality and spontaneity. Gomez’s unique vision is also on display in sumptuous campaigns for Nike, Fiat, Gatorade, and Alfa Beer, to name a few. His early work in music videos earned recognition at the Latin Grammys, Ciclope, UKMVA among others.

Alexis Gomez and Little Minx’s entire roster of world class directors are represented on the East Coast by Simpatico, on the West Coast by RED and in the Midwest by Nikki Weiss-Goldstein. Little Minx has been honored by Advertising Age as a production company to watch on their annual A-List in 2022 and 2024.

 

AGENCY - HUDSON ROUGEJon Pearce - CCOTodd Houlette - Creative DirectorHoward Collinge - Creative DirectorMina Cruz - Senior Art DirectorJaclyn Ling - Senior CopywriterMary Ellen Verrusio- Head of ProductionTom MIshra - Senior ProducerTamara Taylor - Group Account DirectorAGENCY - ZUBIFrancisco Losada - ACDSatiago Revillas - CopywriterMichelle Hernandez - Account SupervisorDiego Colombo - ProducerPRODUCTION COMPANY - LITTLE MINXAlexis Gomez - DirectorRodrigo Prieto - Director of PhotographyFounder/President - Rhea ScottExecutive Producer: Nicole BarnetteWords and Creative: Ximena PrietoHead of Production - Mini BhogalCreative Executive - Dave RandolphLine Producer - Suzie Greene TedescoProductiond Designer- Shane ValentinoProduction Supervisor - Nikki MullerEDITORIAL - CHURCH EDITArmen Harootun - EditorMusic and Sound - BDSColor - Mikey RossiterVFX - PREYMAKER

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign showcases the Lincoln Nautilus Hybrid's luxurious comfort and tranquil interior by depicting a serene, heartfelt moment between a bride and her father, emphasizing their unspoken bond and the vehicle's ability to create an intimate, timeless experience.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Affluent individuals and families who appreciate luxury, comfort, emotional connection, and advanced design in a high-end SUV, likely considering a premium vehicle purchase.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film distributed across relevant channels (e.g., TV, online platforms like YouTube) to convey the cinematic narrative and emotional experience.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand perception for the Lincoln Nautilus Hybrid by highlighting its luxurious comfort, advanced features, and emotional connection, driving consideration for the 2026 model.

metadata.title: Christian Vieri Wakes to Wash Dishes

content.description:
Svelto's playful campaign with Christian Vieri and Costanza Caracciolo

Svelto's playful campaign with Christian Vieri and Costanza Caracciolo

Unilever’s iconic dishwashing brand Svelto has launched a new campaign in Italy that humorously presents dishwashing as an unexpectedly enjoyable experience. Starring former Italian footballer Christian Vieri and his wife, actress and model Costanza Caracciolo, the film follows a light-hearted and relatable storyline in which a husband, hooked on the feel and performance of Svelto, sneaks into the kitchen at night to wash dishes — only to be caught by his knowing wife. Built around the idea “It feels better, because it is better”, the campaign transforms dishwashing from a routine chore into an experience that encourages consumers to slow down and enjoy the moment.

 

Omnicom Advertising GroupAndrew Ho - Creative DirectorNatasha Benedicta - Senior Art DirectorSofia Benchimol - Art DirectorEmilio Carrozzino - CopywriterXin Ying Peh - Sr. CopywriterJulie Chua - Go To Market DirectorJewel Ng - Sr. Account ExecutiveHwee Ling Koh – Account DirectorAlycia Lee - Sr. Account DirectorSubarna Prabhakar - Global Business DirectorUnilever ItalyLuana Rossolini – Home care Italy LeadMatteo Iegri – Home Care Italy H&H Marketing lead & Head of CSPSilvia Finicelli – Home Care Italy Marketing Specialist

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A humorous film depicts Christian Vieri secretly enjoying dishwashing with Svelto, positioning the chore as an unexpectedly pleasurable experience rather than a mundane task.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Italian adults and couples, particularly those managing household responsibilities, seeking to make daily chores more enjoyable and relatable.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film featuring celebrity endorsers (Christian Vieri and Costanza Caracciolo) distributed via film as a primary medium.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance Svelto's brand perception by reframing dishwashing as an enjoyable experience, driving increased brand engagement and preference.

content.description:
Ginsters, Britain’s Biggest Pastry Brand is set to win the hearts and minds of even more British shoppers in 2026, with a further £4m investment that welcomes back the brand’s award-winning ‘Taste The Effort’ campaign and their beloved farmer character ‘Merryn’.  As category leader, every £1 spent on Ginsters media has a halo impact on the savoury pastry category - generating £2.75 of total category salesThe new campaign will reach 94% of UK adults more than 9 times, across TV, Video on Demand (VOD), YouTube, Radio, Social and Shopper marketing.  Taste and quality messages are supercharged in even more entertaining moments throughout the creative, where Ginsters delicious Original Cornish Pasty is enjoyed.Building on the success of the first ‘Taste the Effort’ creative, the new TV ad continues to highlight the dedication and care that Ginsters puts into making delicious high-quality products, using only the tastiest British ingredients, including 100% British Beef and locally sourced vegetables.The new creative, developed by TBWA\London, uses the tongue-in-cheek humour of Ginsters’ much loved farmer character Merryn. We see her back on the farm, proudly demonstrating the changes she’s made to continue producing the highest quality ingredients for Ginsters, and, like the brand, going the extra mile in everything she does to ensure it’s the best. The spot is directed by Florence Poppy Deary and produced by Biscuit Filmworks UK.Since launching ‘Taste the Effort’ in 2023, Ginsters has driven increased brand relevance and is now even more strongly associated with ‘Is a Brand for Me and My Family’, which is significantly ahead of competitor brands in the category. In H1 2025, Ginsters recruited over 123k more households YoY over the three months while on air.  In addition, executional branded memorability has increased by +10 points (+9 points above norms), Remembered Reach has increased +15 points (+24 points above norms), and Persuasion, Enjoyment and Involvement have also increased YoY, each year since launch. Key message takeout is also increasing, with more people believing that Ginsters products are tasty and use only the best quality ingredients.Alongside the NEW TVC, a suite of social first assets have been created to drive engagement across digital channels. The social campaign will kick off from 9th February, with the new TVC launching on 13th February and running until the end of May.Sarah Babb, Ginsters Marketing Director, commented: “Our Taste the Effort brand platform has done a fundamental job to drive reappraisal for Ginsters, putting the brand firmly in the hearts of the nation. Like Merryn, we’re always building on our successes, and this new creative allows us to continue to tell the nation how great our Cornish Pasties are in an engaging and humorous way with more exciting stories from the farm. We hope everyone loves this next instalment as much as we do!”Lauren Coates, Creative Director, TBWA\London added: “All the great grafters of our time have one thing in common - they never stop tinkering. Merryn's no different. This year, we’re back on her farm to discover what she’s changed, upgraded and improved in the name of even tastier pasties. Think scarier scarecrows, pampered cows, a new crew of farm hands and plenty of effort poured into flavour.”

ai_enrichment.target_audience: UK adults, specifically existing and potential British shoppers of savoury pastries.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign will be executed across multiple channels including TV, Video on Demand (VOD), YouTube, Radio, Social media, and Shopper marketing.

content.description:
Tesco’s ‘Every Little Helps’ is the UK’s most memorable brand slogan* and is synonymous with the way it serves customers and communities every day. Now, Tesco is launching a new brand platform designed to give renewed relevance to the iconic slogan through an ubiquitous question; “Need Anything From Tesco?”.

The campaign transforms the everyday colloquialism into a vehicle for the brand’s mission, and positions it as an ongoing conversation with colleagues, customers and society to show how Tesco is there to help the nation get the groceries they need, and so much more.There is no doubt that UK’s biggest supermarket plays a critical role in feeding the nation, but customers not only need to shop for household essentials; they expect great quality, excellent value and convenience. And, in the current climate, they need even more from Tesco, whether that is free nappies for premature babies, the biggest own brand Free From range of any supermarket, free fruit and veg for hundreds of schools and ways to make money go further with Clubcard Reward partners on everything from meals out, holidays abroad to £2.50 cinema tickets.That is why Tesco is transforming a familiar question into a nationwide invitation. The campaign is designed as more than a slogan; it’s a conversation to help bring Every Little Helps to life.Ashwin Prasad, Tesco’s UK CEO said:“At Tesco, ’Every Little Helps’ has always been rooted in the real actions our colleagues take every day to support customers, whether that’s going the extra mile in store to support a customer who needs it or supporting hundreds of schools to access free fruit and veg for their pupils. The new ‘Need anything from Tesco?’ platform is intended to build on that as we continually work towards new, thoughtful ways to do more for our customers and local communities whilst still providing great quality food at the right price. I am excited to see where the campaign takes us as we start to actively ask the question: Need anything from Tesco?”The hero film, created by BBH and directed by Tore Frandsen, through new-land, uses classic British humour and centres on a relatable family to show how the answer to the question "Need anything from Tesco?" can be a lot more than bananas and milk. It’s a fun, high energy reminder that for Tesco, Every Little Helps is about being there for the moments that matter.Set to the track Blue Monday by New Order the films will be delivered in various formats; the hero 60 second, a 30-seconds, three 20 seconds, three 10 seconds and idents to support Tesco’s Britain’s Got Talent sponsorship.For the print and OOH executions, BBH showed that it’s more than just groceries when you shop at Tesco.Felipe Serradourada Guimaraes, Executive Creative Director of BBH said: “It's a privilege to put renewed meaning into Every Little Helps with this platform. The team is pumped for what's to come. Watch this space.”The launch campaign is running across TVC, social, print, OOH and radio around the country. EssenceMediaCom UK is the media agency.

 

Campaign title: Need anything from Tesco?

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Tesco revitalizes its iconic 'Every Little Helps' slogan by transforming the common question 'Need Anything From Tesco?' into a brand platform, positioning itself as an ongoing helpful partner for the nation's grocery and wider needs.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: The general UK public, particularly existing and potential Tesco customers who value quality, convenience, and value in their daily shopping, and seek a helpful partner for their household needs.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign is described as an 'Integrated' medium, but specific channels like TV, digital, social media, or in-store activations are not detailed in the provided information.
ai_enrichment.objective: To renew the relevance of Tesco's 'Every Little Helps' slogan and reinforce its brand perception as a comprehensive and helpful partner for the nation's diverse needs.

content.description:
Survivor-led nonprofit The SHERO Foundation and its agency partner Duncan Channon have launched “Girlhood,” a pro bono campaign that reframes how sex trafficking is portrayed. The work blatantly omits showing the crime itself and instead focuses on what trafficking takes away from girlhood. The work was created as part of Purpose, Produced, a partnership between Kevin Bacon’s SixDegrees.org and Advertising Week that pairs top agencies with deserving nonprofits to create pro bono campaigns.

The campaign includes a two-minute hero film and three 30-second spots that capture a diverse group of girls simply being girls: taking selfies, making TikToks, and going to school. Viewers hear the real stories of women who were trafficked as girls, describing what was stolen from them long before anyone noticed something was wrong.

The contrast is deliberate. Rather than relying on familiar imagery of violence or exploitation, “Girlhood” exposes a reality that is often misunderstood: many victims are hiding in plain sight. Domestic sex trafficking most often affects girls and young women who are U.S. citizens, frequently meeting traffickers through social media or gaming platforms, while continuing to attend school, live at home, and post online.

According to the National Center for Biotechnology Information, the average age a girl is first trafficked is between 11 and 13, a statistic that shaped the emotional core of the campaign. By pairing survivor testimony with moments of everyday joy, “Girlhood” highlights what is truly at stake when a sense of safety, normalcy, and possibility is taken.

The campaign was produced pro bono, and came to life through an outpouring of support from the creative community and the town of Fort Walton Beach, Florida, where much of the work was filmed. Photographer Sean Murphy and the team at Gannet Creative House led production alongside cinematographer Tyler Trant and line producer Skye Bailey, with local residents, businesses, and young performers volunteering their time, venues and resources.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign reframes the portrayal of sex trafficking by deliberately omitting the crime itself and instead focusing on the profound loss of normal girlhood experiences, using real survivor stories to highlight what was stolen.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: The general public and potential advocates for anti-trafficking efforts, specifically those whose perceptions of sex trafficking may need to be shifted away from graphic depictions towards understanding its impact on victims' lives.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A two-minute hero film and three 30-second spots, likely distributed across digital video platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media like TikTok) and potentially broadcast television.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness and reframe public perception of sex trafficking by highlighting the stolen aspects of a normal girlhood, thereby fostering empathy and support for survivor-led organizations like The SHERO Foundation.

metadata.title: Doner and Colle McVoy join forces to form DonerColle Partners

content.description:
Doner and Colle McVoy join forces to form DonerColle Partners

Today, leading Stagwell (STGW) agencies Doner and Colle McVoy have joined forces to form DonerColle Partners, a new entity created to serve the brands that move America.

As the Midwest emerges as the demographic and cultural center of today’s consumer landscape, DonerColle Partners is strategically positioned to help brands connect where it matters most. Unifying operations across more than 500 employees in Detroit, Minneapolis and Chicago, the partnership is built on a foundation of modern creativity and is grounded in a genuine understanding of the people and places that drive American business. Geographically and fundamentally, DonerColle Partners is poised to more effectively and authentically relate to and embrace the sensibilities of everyday Americans who are often overlooked or disregarded by coastal agencies.

“These agencies have a proven ability to combine mainstream marketing with modern techniques and strong client relationships that drive real results,” Stagwell chairman and CEO Mark Penn said. “Together, we’re creating a combined offering that honors that legacy while embracing the future, leveraging AI, modern marketing and an informed perspective of the communities and consumers that shape America.”

Doner and Colle McVoy will maintain their unique identities and client relationships under DonerColle Partners. Structured as a strategic alliance rather than a merger, the arrangement preserves the specialized expertise and culture that have made each successful while enabling deeper collaboration, broadened creative capabilities, scaled media offerings and state-of-the-art production studios. The enhanced offering will be led by Jessica Henrichs as CEO and David DeMuth as executive chairman.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Stagwell agencies Doner and Colle McVoy have merged to form DonerColle Partners, a new entity created to leverage their combined expertise and Midwest presence to better serve brands and connect with American consumers.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Prospective client brands, particularly large and mid-sized businesses seeking an integrated advertising partner with a strong understanding of the American consumer landscape, especially within the Midwest.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Integrated campaign likely including official press release distribution, comprehensive updates across agency websites and social media platforms, and internal communications to employees.
ai_enrichment.objective: To announce the formation of DonerColle Partners, establish its new brand identity, and strategically position the merged agency as a powerful and relevant partner for brands looking to connect with consumers across America.

Bringing 100% real mashed potatoes to families across the country for more than 65 years, Idahoan Foods is ditching the manicured, filtered lifestyle to lean into the messiness of real-life moments

Idahoan Foods, a leading U.S. producer of 100% real mashed potato products, recently announced the launch of its new 'Get Real' creative campaign.

Created by The Brandon Agency, 'Get Real' highlights Idahoan's Real Potatoes, Real Easy™ promise and positions Idahoan mashed potatoes as a fast, affordable, and wholesome side dish or snack for busy families. With products ready in just minutes, Idahoan helps home cooks serve 100% real mashed potatoes with homemade taste, without spending extra time in the kitchen.

What Is the Idahoan 'Get Real' Campaign?For over 65 years, Idahoan Foods has delivered Real Potatoes, Real Easy™. This campaign encourages families to embrace authentic mealtime moments instead of curated, time-consuming meals often seen on social media. Idahoan mashed potatoes are ready in minutes, making them the perfect solution for time-strapped home cooks who want homemade taste without the hassle. The new campaign will run across Meta, TikTok, retail, email communications, digital display and streaming.

Key Insights That Inspired the 'Get Real' Campaign*

content.description:
Seattle independent agency Copacino Fujikado has launched a playful new campaign for ORCA (One Regional Card for All), the unified transit payment system serving the greater Puget Sound region.

The campaign introduces its brand new feature: tap-to-pay. Now, commuters across King, Kitsap, Pierce, and Snohomish counties can board Community Transit, Everett Transit, Kitsap Transit, King County Metro, Pierce Transit, and Sound Transit using any credit or debit card, with no ORCA card required.

The strategy was simple: reach riders in the moments when they might choose transit but don't have an ORCA card on them, or time to find a ticket kiosk. Now, they can just tap and go!

To bring this concept to life, Copacino partnered with 3D animation studio Laundry. The hero spot shows an animated group of payment cards coming together to ride the bus, with a cameo from Boop, ORCA's orca whale mascot, as the bus driver. “Boop” is a playful nod to the sound the card reader makes when riders tap.

Copacino’s in-house media plan runs across OOH, transit placements, YouTube, and Meta from February through June. The campaign’s elements are available in Spanish, Vietnamese, and Chinese, with OOH executions including “Every Card is Welcome Aboard,” “Invited to Tap On,” and “Tap to Ride.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign introduces ORCA's new tap-to-pay feature, allowing Puget Sound commuters to board transit using any credit or debit card without an ORCA card, emphasizing convenience through a playful, animated hero film.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Commuters and potential transit users across King, Kitsap, Pierce, and Snohomish counties, specifically those who might choose transit but lack an ORCA card or time to purchase a ticket.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film featuring playful 3D animation, designed to reach riders during moments of transit consideration.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive awareness and adoption of ORCA's new tap-to-pay feature, removing barriers to entry and encouraging more convenient transit use.

content.description:
McDonald’s Japan challenged itself to transform seasonal product advertisements into "stannable entertainment" rather than transient promotions.

Her name is Miss Eatie, a name derived from being the ultimate "Beauty" born to "Eat." While this blunt and literal naming initially provoked laughter online, the creative execution was entirely serious. By recruiting top creators at the forefront of Japan’s "Kawaii" culture to produce a high-quality idol music video, the project turned her from a mere joke into a figure that fans could genuinely support.

As a result, she transcended the boundaries of traditional advertising, sparking countless pieces of fan art, derivative works, and theory videos to become a complete entertainment phenomenon.

content.description: Super Bowl halftime is widely known as the nation’s biggest “Super Flush” — the largest simultaneous toilet flush in the U.S. This year, Rich Earth Institute is asking people to pause and consider a simple, radical alternative.Pee On A Plant.Launching ahead of the February 8, 2026 championship game, PeeOnAPlant.com is a new public-awareness campaign created by independent creative agency, LERMA/ in collaboration with Rich Earth Institute, that reframes urine not as waste, but as a valuable resource — one that can help reduce water waste, ease pressure on treatment systems, and protect rivers and lakes from nutrient pollution.“Every time we flush, we’re using clean drinking water to move nutrients into systems that are already overwhelmed,” said Jamina Shupack, Executive Director of Rich Earth Institute. “At a time when our waterways are experiencing historic lows and harmful algae blooms, we need smarter, more sustainable habits, even the small ones.”In the U.S., toilets account for nearly 30% of indoor household water use. Flushing sends nitrogen and phosphorus — the same nutrients plants need to grow — into wastewater treatment plants and waterways, where excess nutrients fuel algae blooms, create “dead zones,” and harm aquatic life.Urine, by contrast, is rich in plant-friendly nutrients like nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium – the same elements found in synthetic fertilizers, which are costly to produce and environmentally damaging to mine.“Urine is already made, every day,” said Shupack. “The question is whether we waste it — or use it wisely.”Timed to the Super Bowl halftime moment, when over 120 million viewers are watching and millions step away, the campaign uses culture and humor to spark conversation around water scarcity and nutrient pollution.The initiative was conceived and developed in partnership with LERMA/, the independent creative agency known for culture-shaping ideas that turn complex global issues into accessible, conversation-driving moments.The campaign’s homepage message is direct: “This Halftime, Go With Purpose.” Visitors to PeeOnAPlant.com or the Instagram page @peeonaplant can learn:How flushing contributes to water waste and harmful algae bloomsWhy urine is a natural fertilizer when properly dilutedSimple, science-backed ways to reduce household water useFor those without a plant handy, the site also offers practical alternatives, from fixing leaks and upgrading to low-flow fixtures, to supporting the Rich Earth Institute’s efforts through donations.As part of the campaign, an extremely limited run of “Pee on a Plant” pots will be available for purchase at PeeOnAPlant.com.To expand reach beyond traditional environmental audiences, creative agency LERMA/ created a 60-second launch film [watch it here] and will be shared with creators, athletes, entertainers, and media personalities, inviting them to engage with the idea, spark debate, and help normalize conversations around water use.“This campaign is designed to be talked about,” said Paco Conde, Chief Creative Officer at creative agency, LERMA/. “It’s surprising, funny, and grounded in real science – the kind of idea that breaks through culture while still delivering a serious message.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign challenges the traditional Super Bowl halftime 'Super Flush' by encouraging people to 'Pee On A Plant,' reframing urine as a valuable resource to conserve water and prevent nutrient pollution.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Environmentally conscious general public, particularly Super Bowl viewers, open to simple behavioral changes that reduce water waste and protect ecosystems.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A public awareness campaign centered around a dedicated website, PeeOnAPlant.com, supported by a film medium strategically launched ahead of the Super Bowl championship game.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise public awareness about urine's potential as a resource and encourage a simple, radical behavioral change to reduce water waste and nutrient pollution.

content.description:
Sleep Number Corporation debuted a campaign to highlight its newest product, marking a major shift in how the brand connects with consumers by moving beyond traditional mattress specs to focus on real-life moments. The ad is inspired by the insight that what you do today changes what you need tonight, and every moment deserves a great night’s sleep. Created in partnership with Fallon, the spot showcases ComfortMode, the company’s most comfortable mattress at a sub-$1,600 price point, creatively highlighting the ways the bed adapts to people’s changing lives to bring comfort.

“In creating this ad, we were inspired by a simple insight: people’s sleep needs change as their lives change,” said Amber Minson, Sleep Number’s EVP and Chief Marketing Officer. “By telling stories that mirror people’s real, evolving lives, the campaign brings the value of ComfortMode to life in a more authentic way. We shifted our creative approach to show how our newest bed delivers personalized comfort night after night at an approachable price, allowing us to meet customers where they are and making the Sleep Number brand relevant for people at every stage of life.”

This ad launch marks a key step in Sleep Number’s brand evolution as the company expands its relevance to more customers and shifts from a traditional retailer toward a more customer-centric sleep wellness brand. As part of this work, the brand is reshaping its marketing and creative to better connect with today’s consumer and drive engagement with a focus on better ROI. And, the new spot signals a move away from the mattress industry’s crowded sameness of tech specs and plain foam imagery, and toward a real-life story that underscores what makes Sleep Number beds unique: the ability to adjust to each sleeper night after night.

"Sleep Number is a wellness icon, and this partnership is about joining forces to unlock the brand's next level of growth," said Nikki Baker, Fallon CEO. "Our ‘outsmart versus outspend’ philosophy is the perfect match for Sleep Number’s spirit of innovation—it’s about moving past the technical noise of the category to reconnect with the human side of sleep. Together, we’re not just launching a campaign; we’re modernizing the brand identity to ensure Sleep Number is the obvious choice for a much broader audience."

The new 30-second ad is available on YouTube and will be distributed across linear TV, online video, audio platforms and Sleep Number’s social platforms.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign introduces Sleep Number's new 'ComfortMode' mattress by showcasing how it dynamically adapts to people's changing daily lives and sleep needs, emphasizing real-life comfort and an accessible sub-$1,600 price point over traditional technical specifications.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Consumers whose sleep needs fluctuate with their daily activities and life changes, seeking an adaptable and comfortable mattress solution, particularly those interested in a quality product at an accessible price point (under $1,600).
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A film spot, developed in partnership with Fallon, that creatively highlights the mattress's adaptability through vignettes of real-life moments, likely distributed via television and online video platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To launch the new ComfortMode mattress, shift Sleep Number's brand connection with consumers by focusing on real-life adaptable comfort rather than mattress specifications, and drive consumer awareness and interest in the new product.

content.description:
Disney x SportClub | TRON: Ares Immersive Activation

To promote the release of TRON: Ares, 5SEIS developed a co-branded experiential activation with Disney and SportClub at SportClub DOT. A SportCycle room was fully transformed into the TRON universe through neon lighting, LED elements and graphic takeovers, creating an immersive, high-energy experience for invited influencers. The activation blended fitness, cinema and pop culture, extending the film’s world into a physical, shareable environment designed for engagement and content creation.

content.description:
iQIYI Thailand brought Running Man to Thailand. In a city where billboards dominate every road and skyline, another static ad would simply blend into the background. So instead of placing the message in the city, it was sent through the city.Introducing The Running Billboard, a mobile installation that travelled across Bangkok’s most iconic landmarks including Wat Phra Kaew, Giant Swing and Democracy Monument. Moving like the show itself, fast, playful and impossible to ignore, the billboard turned traffic into spectators and daily commutes into live teasers.What started as outdoor media became a citywide performance. In a landscape full of ads, the one that ran was the one people chased.

content.description:
Who needs a Michelin star when you have a Grandma’s secret? 🏆 In Brazil, "Grandma’s cooking" is the ultimate gold standard. To launch our reformulated recipe, we didn't just run ads—nosy neighbors and best friends did the talking for us. We sparked a social media "soap opera" that proved our new sauce is so good, even the pros (Grandmas!) are claiming it as their own.

Who needs a Michelin star when you have a Grandma’s secret? 🏆 In Brazil, "Grandma’s cooking" is the ultimate gold standard. To launch our reformulated recipe, we didn't just run ads—nosy neighbors and best friends did the talking for us. We sparked a social media "soap opera" that proved our new sauce is so good, even the pros (Grandmas!) are claiming it as their own.

In Brazil, the ultimate gold standard for flavor isn’t a Michelin star—it’s "Grandma’s cooking." And every Grandma has a "secret ingredient" she refuses to share.

Instead of a traditional "New & Improved" campaign, we created a social soap opera. We launched "The Feud of the Year" (A Treta do Ano), featuring three real-life elderly best friends. The plot? One of them, Neide, was gaining fame for a "secret sauce" that was, in reality, the new Gomes da Costa sardine.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign leverages the Brazilian cultural reverence for 'Grandma's cooking' by creating a social media 'soap opera' where even professional Grandmas claim the reformulated sauce as their own due to its superior taste.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Brazilian consumers who appreciate traditional homemade flavors and are active participants in social media conversations.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A digital-first social media 'soap opera' designed to spark organic word-of-mouth, encouraging 'nosy neighbors and best friends' to discuss and endorse the product.
ai_enrichment.objective: To successfully launch a reformulated recipe by generating widespread social buzz and establishing its quality and authenticity through cultural validation.

content.description:
What is generosity? For us, generosity doesn’t ask permission.. It doesn’t hesitate.. It comes straight from the heart.. A habit. A natural instinct.. This is generosity.. And this is generosity.. And this.. is generosity too! Generosity is rooted in who we are. And in Ramadan… our generosity is eXtra.

What is generosity? For us, generosity doesn’t ask permission.. It doesn’t hesitate.. It comes straight from the heart.. A habit. A natural instinct.. This is generosity.. And this is generosity.. And this.. is generosity too! Generosity is rooted in who we are. And in Ramadan… our generosity is eXtra.

Brand: eXtra (United Electronics Company)

Synopsis: In the Middle East, generosity isn’t just a behavior; it’s an identity. As the Holy Month of Ramadan approaches, this spirit of giving reaches its peak. "Our Generosity is eXtra" explores the multifaceted nature of "Karam" (generosity)—from the grand gestures of welcoming family home and sharing massive feasts to the quiet, everyday acts of kindness like holding an elevator or surprised gift-giving.

The campaign bridges the gap between traditional values and modern living, showing how eXtra, Saudi Arabia’s leading electronics retailer, plays a role in these moments. Whether it’s gifting a much-wanted coffee machine to a friend or preparing a meal for a stranger, the ad highlights that while generosity is always in our DNA, during Ramadan, it becomes "eXtra."

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign defines generosity as an inherent, natural instinct that comes straight from the heart, deeply rooted in identity, and positions eXtra as embodying this spirit, especially during Ramadan.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Consumers in the Middle East, particularly those who observe Ramadan and value the cultural significance of generosity, and are potential buyers of electronics and technology.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Integrated campaign, likely featuring narrative storytelling content across various media channels to elaborate on the definition and expression of generosity and link it to the eXtra brand.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand affinity and relevance for eXtra by emotionally connecting the brand with the deeply cherished cultural value of generosity during the Holy Month of Ramadan.

metadata.title: The Man Who Changed the Rhythm of the World
tags: AOTW Beer, Wine and Spirits Collection

content.description:
The Man Who Changed the Rhythm of the World

What do “Thriller,” by Michael Jackson, “La Isla Bonita,” by Madonna, “All Night Long,” by Lionel Ritchie, “We are the World,” by USA for Africa, and many hits by Whitney Houston and Tina Turner have in common? Brazilian musician Paulinho da Costa on percussion.A legend in contemporary percussion and one of the most influential instrumentalists in the world, Paulinho produced work that transcended borders, influenced generations, and brought Afro-Brazilian rhythms to the heart of the global music industry, helping shape the musical landscape we know today. Over the course of his career, Paulinho played on over 6,000 tracks, recorded with 972 different artists, and participated in projects that led to 161 Grammy nods and 59 wins, with 180 gold and platinum singles and albums. Even so, most Brazilians have never heard his name.“Some Brazilian artists have gotten more recognition abroad than at home, including some big names in bossa nova,” says Guilherme Martins, CMO of Diageo Brasil. “Today, Brazilian culture is being celebrated more than ever, and it’s become a real source of pride, especially when that brilliance translates into recognition at major awards. When we look at Paulinho’s trajectory and everything he’s done, it just doesn’t make sense: he’s on thousands of songs, the biggest albums, the most iconic soundtracks in history, and even so, too few Brazilians know who he is. Paulinho da Costa’s story is made up of steps that changed music and echo to this day – and that’s why we want to celebrate him.”Paulinho is the star of a new campaign by Johnnie Walker, which uses a manifesto film to celebrate his career and cultural impact. Set to “All Night Long,” by Lionel Richie, the film connects iconic rhythms to the journey of an artist who built up his career step by step, crossed borders and helped shape the sound of global classics. By telling this story, the brand reinforces its connection to stories of progress and movements that shaped pop culture as we know it.The initiative is part of the brand’s cultural movement of highlighting trajectories that transformed music, in step with its broader global platform. As part of the campaign, the brand also financed a documentary about Paulinho da Costa, directed by Oscar Rodrigues Alves and produced by Alan Terpins. The film, to be launched on streaming in the first semester of 2026, will depict his life and work, with interviews with some of the biggest artists and producers in music history, from Quincy Jones to Will.i.am.“Music is one of the clearest expressions of progress you can find. It crosses generations, connects people, and helps transform society – and that’s exactly what Johnnie Walker has always supported,” says Paloma di Santo, director of Scotch Marketing for Diageo. “In Brazil, we’ve entered the territory through our ‘Steps that Change the Rhythm of the World’ platform, which connects music, culture, and immersive experiences to bring iconic stories and journeys to an even larger audience.”Born in Rio de Janeiro, Paulinho began playing percussion instruments at the tender age of five, in the early 1950s, and embarked on a remarkable career in international music. Not only did he work with artists such as Michael Jackson, Madonna, Whitney Houston, Diana Ross, Stevie Wonder, Elton John, Quincy Jones, Lionel Richie, Tina Turner, Céline Dion, and Earth, Wind & Fire, Paulinho also released six albums of his own, in which he blends jazz, funk, Latin rhythms, and Afro-Brazilian influences.As if that weren’t enough, he also contributed to over 350 films, composing or playing on iconic soundtracks including The Color Purple, Saturday Night Fever, Dirty Dancing, Purple Rain, Jurassic Park, Transformers, Mission: Impossible, Hairspray, and Indiana Jones, to name just a few – with tracks that racked up 12 Oscars. A multi-instrumentalist, Paulinho plays over 200 percussion instruments from a variety of cultures, and has garnered global recognition as an authority in his field.This celebration is poised to peak in a historic landmark: in 2026, Paulinho da Costa will become the first person born in Brazil to receive a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame.The campaign, created by AlmapBBDO, is a part of the music platform “Steps that Change the Rhythm of the World,” launched by Johnnie Walker in 2025 as a natural evolution of its historical aim of inspiring progress. “It was embarrassing to realize we didn’t have the faintest idea of who he was, but we all knew the songs he gave his rhythm to,” say Edu Lima, Pedro Corbett, and Rodrigo Adam, executive creative directors at Almap.

“This project couldn’t mean more to me. Music has always been my path, and to see this story being told and celebrated in such a beautiful way fills me with pride and joy,” says Paulinho.Globally, Johnnie Walker’s musical initiatives have included partnerships with Sabrina Carpenter, the brand’s ambassador. In Brazil, apart from supporting artists, festivals, and cultural projects, the brand received a Grand Prix at Cannes for “Errata 88,” the campaign that finally gave Alaíde Costa, one of the founding mothers of bossa nova, her dues – correcting a historical wrong.The campaign reaffirms Johnnie Walker’s stance as a brand present in the music world as a cultural agent, helping to ignite stories, connect generations, and inspire the world to keep on walking.

 

Agency: AlmapBBDO

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign celebrates the unsung legend Paulinho da Costa, a Brazilian percussionist whose pervasive influence on global music, through countless iconic tracks, shaped the rhythm of the world and transcended cultural boundaries.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adults aged 25-55 who appreciate music, cultural pioneers, and stories of enduring impact and craftsmanship, likely affluent and globally aware consumers of premium alcoholic beverages.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: As an Integrated campaign, it likely involves documentary-style video content (hero film), social media storytelling, PR outreach, digital content marketing highlighting Paulinho da Costa's legacy, and potentially experiential music events or partnerships.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand equity and reinforce Johnnie Walker's association with themes of lasting influence, mastery, cultural impact, and the celebration of inspiring pioneering spirits.

Because when technology meets talent, attitude and the streets, transformation isn’t just something you see. It’s something you feel.

content.description:
To relaunch Lupita Flores, the 80-year-old iconic character of Salvadoran liquor brand La Trenzuda, we turned a product update into a cultural event. In a country where folklore and supernatural legends are deeply rooted in everyday life, we made Lupita “appear” in real life. A woman with long braids was seen walking alone at night along highways from San Miguel to the capital, sparking theories, fear, and massive organic conversation across social media.

Days later, at the country’s largest content creator event, we revealed the truth: the mysterious figure was Lupita Flores, reborn through AI. With no paid media and a minimal budget, the campaign transformed a traditional brand character into a contemporary cultural phenomenon — proving that when a brand understands its culture, it doesn’t launch campaigns. It makes appearances.

 

Advertising Agency: FCB CREA El SalvadorCreative General Director: Javier UrbanejaCreative Director: Alejandro AlasCreative: Carlos GallegosGraphic Design: María José García / Friné AbarcaAccount Director: Cecilia RodríguezAccount Executives: Michelle Fajardo, Michelle ValenciaSocial Media Manager: Merary NoyolaProducer: Beneration MediaProduction: Valería Pérez / Lourdes Miranda / Katherine Aguirre

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign resurrected an 80-year-old brand character, Lupita Flores, by staging her mysterious real-life 'apparition' as a woman with long braids, sparking widespread organic social media conversation rooted in Salvadoran folklore, before revealing her rebirth via AI at a content creator event.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Salvadoran adults who are active on social media and appreciate local folklore and supernatural legends.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A guerilla marketing stunt involving a woman embodying the character walking along highways to generate buzz, followed by organic amplification and discussion across social media, culminating in a reveal at a major content creator event.
ai_enrichment.objective: To relaunch and revitalize the iconic brand character, Lupita Flores, generating significant cultural relevance, organic engagement, and buzz for the La Trenzuda liquor brand.

content.description:
ContextIn Panama, Carnival isn't just a party  it’s a national tradition so important that no one wants to miss it. Some people go as far as selling or pawning their belongings just to be able to attend. It’s no longer a luxury  it’s a cultural priority. And as the official beer of Carnival, Atlas Golden had to live up to that level of passion.

IdeaWe created the first-ever pawn shop where people didn’t hand over objects, but their wildest, most absurd, and unforgettable Carnival stories.

ExecutionWe built a real pawn shop complete with glass displays, signs, counters, and all the traditional aesthetics — but fully reimagined with Atlas Golden’s identity. Instead of trading in objects, people shared their stories at the counter, where a team evaluated each one. The most memorable were rewarded with brand merchandise or a trip to experience Carnival like never before.

The activation was amplified through influencers and media, becoming the most talked-about pawn shop in Panama  and a living tribute to the stories that make Carnival unforgettable.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Atlas Golden created an experiential pawn shop where people exchanged their wildest and most unforgettable Carnival stories instead of physical objects.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Panamanian Carnival-goers and beer enthusiasts, particularly those deeply passionate about the cultural tradition of Carnival.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A physical, branded experiential 'pawn shop' activation, complete with traditional aesthetics reimagined with Atlas Golden's identity, where participants directly shared personal Carnival stories at a counter for evaluation.
ai_enrichment.objective: To deepen Atlas Golden's cultural relevance and engagement with consumers during Panama's Carnival by connecting with their passion for the tradition.

content.description:
When you shave off unwanted hair, you probably don’t miss it. But in MANSCAPED’s first-ever Super Bowl campaign, your hair definitely misses you.

Airing on NBC just before kickoff on February 8, the brand’s Big Game debut reimagines unwanted hair as a chorus of melodramatic, puppet-like monsters belting out an emotional power ballad as they mourn their untimely demise at the hands of MANSCAPED. Think The Little Mermaid meets Meat Loaf, if both were somehow made entirely of chest hair.

Developed with creative agency Quality Meats, directed by The Perlorian Brothers and produced by MJZ, the spot brings a big, cinematic sensibility to a category that usually plays it safe, bringing to life what most men wash down the drain as tiny hand-crafted hair puppets with big voices and big feelings.

While MANSCAPED’s monsters make their national debut during the Super Bowl, the Big Game is only the beginning. The characters will continue to appear across additional brand moments and content later this year, extending the campaign well-beyond game day.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign reimagines unwanted body hair as melodramatic, puppet-like monsters singing an emotional power ballad to mourn their removal by Manscaped, bringing a cinematic musical sensibility to men's grooming.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Men who engage in or are considering body hair grooming/shaving, likely aged 18-45, interested in personal care and potentially a humorous, edgy brand approach.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero 30-second TV commercial aired on NBC just before Super Bowl kickoff, developed as a 'Big Game debut' with a cinematic production quality, part of a broader 360° campaign.
ai_enrichment.objective: To significantly increase brand awareness for Manscaped, leveraging the high-reach Super Bowl platform with a distinctive and memorable creative concept.

content.description:
Few brands are as synonymous with football food as Frank’s RedHot, America’s #1 Hot Sauce. For decades, its unmistakable blend of flavor and heat has been the finishing touch on wings, dips, and game-day spreads nationwide.

Now, Frank’s RedHot is officially claiming what fans already know with a new Super Bowl-timed campaign from creative AOR, Colle McVoy, called “Eat the GOAT.”

Starring hip hop legend Ludacris, alongside a cameo from his Disturbing the Peace Posse: Chingy, Shawnna, I-20 and DJ Infamous and, yes, an actual rapping goat, Eat the GOAT declares Frank’s RedHot the greatest hot sauce of all time. It’s the one ingredient that transforms ordinary football food into game-day legends.

“Timeless flavor, timeless flow," said hip-hop legend, Ludacris. "When something’s been the GOAT this long, you don’t just taste it – you feel it. I’m excited to partner with Frank’s RedHot to revel in GOAT status.”

Targeting heat-seeking Millennials and Gen Z, the :45 hero spot speaks to fans who chase flavor, variety, and excitement, especially during Super Bowl gatherings. Whether mixed into Buffalo chicken dip or poured straight onto wings, Frank’s RedHot isn’t just part of the spread. It is the spread.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Frank's RedHot's "Eat the GOAT" campaign uses hip-hop legend Ludacris, his posse, and a rapping goat to declare itself the Greatest Of All Time hot sauce, positioning it as the essential ingredient for game-day food.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Football fans and general consumers nationwide who enjoy game-day food, hot sauce, and pop culture references, likely aged 25-55.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A Super Bowl-timed integrated campaign featuring a hero commercial/film starring Ludacris, his posse, and a rapping goat, likely broadcast on TV and across digital platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reinforce and officially claim Frank's RedHot's position as the greatest hot sauce of all time, particularly for game-day food, and enhance brand affinity.

metadata.title: The one with the bicycle - Choose the right tool

content.description:
The one with the bicycle - Choose the right tool

A high-octane visual metaphor for the modern workplace. Set on a professional racetrack, a rider in full racing leathers sits among a pack of revving motorcycles. The twist? He’s sitting on a simple bicycle.

Power is useless if it’s the wrong tool for the task. CAKE.com positions its suite—Clockify, Pumble, and Plaky—as the streamlined alternative to bloated, overcomplicated software or underpowered tools that simply can't keep up. Don’t bring a bike to a Superbike race—and don’t let the wrong tools stall your business.

 com Creative HUB.
