metadata.title: A Minecraft Movie Avant Premiere + FOOH Airport Activation

content.description:
A Minecraft Movie Avant Premiere + FOOH Airport Activation

Warner Bros. | A Minecraft Movie – Bringing the Game into the Real World

To celebrate the release of A Minecraft Movie, we partnered with Warner Bros. to bring the blocky universe of the game into the physical world through two complementary activations that combined immersion, play, and surprise.

The campaign kicked off with an exclusive IMAX avant premiere in Buenos Aires. The theater lobby was transformed into a fully pixelated Minecraft environment, where guests competed in a live Fan Battle trivia game centered on Minecraft lore, winning themed prizes along the way. A large LED-lit portal created the illusion of stepping into another dimension, while custom papercraft cubes were gifted as souvenirs, allowing attendees to take a piece of the Minecraft world home.

The experience continued with a FOOH video activation at Aeroparque Jorge Newbery Airport, one of the busiest airports in Argentina. Without dialogue, iconic Minecraft characters unexpectedly appeared throughout the terminal—emerging from luggage carousels, peeking behind digital screens, and interacting with travelers through gestures, sound effects, and music. The activation highlighted Warner Bros.’ airport branding assets, including screen takeovers and a custom-wrapped baggage belt.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign aimed to bring the blocky universe of the Minecraft game into the physical world through immersive, playful, and surprising activations to celebrate the release of A Minecraft Movie.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Fans of the Minecraft game, moviegoers, and families interested in immersive entertainment experiences and the upcoming film.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: An exclusive IMAX avant premiere event featuring a fully pixelated Minecraft-themed theater lobby, a live Fan Battle trivia game with themed prizes, a large LED-lit portal experience, and custom papercraft cube souvenirs. (The campaign also includes a FOOH Airport Activation, though details are not provided in this text.)
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate excitement, engagement, and a sense of immersive participation around the release of A Minecraft Movie by transforming physical spaces into the game's world.

content.description:
With Shadow Runners, Distance has decided to shine a light on those who run in the shadows of others - Strava Jockeys - to finally give them the compensation and recognition they deserve.

Since 2025, a phenomenon has been gaining traction on running apps: Strava Jockeys. Behind this stylish name lies a real scandal. Young runners, often from disadvantaged backgrounds, are recruited and paid a few euros to run on behalf of wealthier users, hungry for likes and digital fame.

These are real performances, achieved by real athletes, but attributed to fake runners. It's a symptom of a society obsessed with appearances, which is now contaminating the world of running.

Nike has its major stars. Distance has Oscar, Clara, Ousmane, Howard, and Eva.With Shadow Runners, Distance has made five Strava Jockeys the faces of its new ad campaign. A real contract, real pay, a real photo and video shoot, equivalent with the standards of the biggest sports brands.

« From the beginning, our mission has been to highlight real athletes. Those who run for real. Not those who run after likes... », explains Guillaume Pontier, co-founder of Distance.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Distance's 'Shadow Runners' campaign exposes the hidden world of 'Strava Jockeys' – disadvantaged runners paid to perform for wealthier users – to give overdue recognition and compensation to these legitimate athletes, challenging the sport's obsession with digital appearances.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Runners and sports enthusiasts active on running apps like Strava, individuals concerned with ethical issues in sports and digital culture, and those who value authenticity in athletic achievement.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Integrated campaign featuring profiles and storytelling of five specific 'Strava Jockeys' (Oscar, Clara, Ousmane, Howard, Eva) to highlight their real performances and spark public conversation, likely across digital platforms and potentially through direct compensation/support initiatives.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness about a significant ethical issue within the running community, position Distance as a brand that champions authentic talent and fair play, and drive engagement by challenging superficiality in sports.

content.description:
Molly-Mae Hague has partnered with Unilever to launch the Pure Heaven Scent range for Persil and Comfort, promoting a new, sensitive-skin-friendly laundry line. The seven-figure, 360-degree campaign features Molly-Mae and her daughter, Bambi, highlighting the products' blend of gentle ingredients with long-lasting fragrance.

The Range: The Pure Heaven Scent collection includes Persil Non-Bio capsules, Persil Wonder Wash Sensitive, Comfort Fabric Conditioner, and Comfort Scent Boosters.

The campaign was launched in May 2025, featuring TV ads and social media content. The products are designed to be gentle on sensitive skin while providing a luxurious, long-lasting scent (white musk, patchouli, and apple blossom).

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Influencer Molly-Mae Hague and her daughter Bambi front a 360-degree campaign for Unilever's Pure Heaven Scent range (Persil and Comfort), emphasizing a new laundry line that is gentle on sensitive skin while delivering a luxurious, long-lasting fragrance.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Mothers and caregivers, particularly those with babies or family members with sensitive skin, who seek laundry products that offer both gentle care and a luxurious, long-lasting fragrance experience.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 360-degree campaign leveraging influencer Molly-Mae Hague and her daughter Bambi, featuring TV ads and social media content to showcase the new Pure Heaven Scent range.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive awareness and consideration for Unilever's new Pure Heaven Scent range for Persil and Comfort, highlighting its unique blend of gentle care for sensitive skin and luxurious, long-lasting fragrance.

content.description:
HIDDEN VOICESA visceral new short film by Meena Ayittey turns London’s everyday journeys into a powerful meditation on silence, survival and the unseen realities of FGM/C.

Moving through buses, train carriages and fleeting moments between strangers, Hidden Voices is a visually arresting short film that transforms public transport into a living tapestry of unspoken stories.Written and directed by Meena Ayittey, the film blends poetic realism with social urgency, inviting audiences to witness what is often hidden in plain sight.

Rather than presenting FGM/C through traditional narrative exposition, Hidden Voices unfolds through fragmented glances, gestures, overheard conversations and internal worlds. A pregnant woman’s journey intersects with commuters, young girls and quiet observers, creating a mosaic of perspectives that challenge viewers to reconsider how trauma, culture and community exist side by side in everyday spaces.

Ayittey’s direction draws from contemporary European art-house cinema and documentary naturalism, using intimate cinematography, raw performances and immersive sound design to blur the line between fiction and lived experience. Cinematographer Pablo Rojo captures London not as a backdrop but as an emotional landscape with steel, glass and motion reflecting the resilience and vulnerability of the characters who move through it.

“When I was briefed by Evie Wright at Media Trust and Natalie Roberts at Orchid Project Charity, I decided that I wanted the audience to feel like witnesses rather than spectators,” says Ayittey. “The film isn’t about explaining it’s about feeling the weight of silence and recognising the humanity behind it.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A visually arresting short film that transforms London's public transport into a living tapestry of unspoken stories, using fragmented glances and overheard conversations to powerfully explore the hidden realities of FGM/C and its profound impact.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General public, particularly urban commuters and individuals interested in social justice and women's rights, to raise awareness and foster empathy regarding FGM/C.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero short film titled 'Hidden Voices' distributed online (e.g., YouTube, Vimeo, social media platforms) and potentially through film festivals or community screenings.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase public awareness and understanding of the hidden realities and social urgency surrounding FGM/C, fostering empathy among the audience.

content.description:
Today, Kinder Bueno introduces its first-ever Super Bowl campaign, “Yes Bueno,” featuring style expert, Daphne founder, and co-host of Giggly Squad Paige DeSorbo and acclaimed Hollywood actor William Fichtner.

Created in partnership with Anomaly and directed by Björn Rühmann, the campaign marks Kinder Bueno’s Big Game debut, flipping everyday “No Bueno” moments into “Yes Bueno” ones through the brand’s crispy wafer, smooth chocolate, and creamy hazelnut filling.

The :30 spot unfolds as a high-stakes, space-themed adventure, following two astronauts navigating a dangerous journey through a starry galaxy while space communicators back on Earth, played by Fichtner and DeSorbo, track their progress.

As the expedition encounters meteor showers, aliens, and a trip through a black hole, moments that would typically be “No Bueno” are instead met with enjoyment of Kinder Bueno and reframed as “Yes Bueno.”

The astronauts travel through a series of transformations, shifting through comic, gnome, porcelain, and clown forms before ultimately becoming infant versions of themselves in spacesuits. The film concludes with Kinder Bueno orbiting the screen as the narrator delivers the line, “Irresistible chocolate, wafer, hazelnut. Yes Bueno.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses a high-stakes, space-themed adventure featuring celebrities to transform everyday 'No Bueno' moments into 'Yes Bueno' experiences with Kinder Bueno's unique taste.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Broad audience of adults and young adults (18-45) who enjoy entertainment, pop culture, and confectionery snacks, particularly those watching major sporting events like the Super Bowl.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 30-second Super Bowl TV commercial, part of an integrated campaign, featuring celebrity endorsements (Paige DeSorbo and William Fichtner) within a space-themed narrative.
ai_enrichment.objective: To significantly increase brand awareness for Kinder Bueno through its Super Bowl debut and establish its brand messaging around transforming negative moments with its product.

content.description:
True wealth isn't measured by what we have, but by being able to journey through life with those who matter. No matter what this Chinese New Year brings, we’re committed to being by your side through it all. Gong Xi Fa Cai!

 

Left brain vs right brain - Bike, Left brain vs right bra...

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A Chinese New Year campaign leveraging a film to convey that true wealth is found in cherished relationships and shared life journeys, positioning Prudential as a steadfast partner through all of life's challenges.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and families celebrating Chinese New Year who prioritize family bonds and long-term security, likely those interested in life insurance and financial planning.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across digital platforms such as YouTube and social media, potentially supplemented by television or OTT placement during the Chinese New Year period.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand affinity and trust by emotionally resonating with the audience during the festive Chinese New Year period, highlighting Prudential's commitment as a reliable life partner.

content.description:
Mina®, a trusted halal brand from Maple Leaf Foods, has launched A Mina Welcome, a first-of-its-kind Canadian program designed to support Muslim newcomers during their first months of settling in Canada. The initiative, which invited Canadians to nominate Muslim newcomers for a warm welcome, culminates in the release of a hero video capturing the surprise moment a winning family received a fridge stocked with months worth of Mina halal essentials.

The program responds to a specific and under-addressed challenge Muslim newcomers face upon arrival: finding halal food they can trust, conveniently and consistently, as they begin building daily routines in a new country. While many newcomers default to distant ethnic grocers or butchers, Mina’s research revealed a widespread assumption that big-box grocery stores either don’t carry halal products or can’t be trusted to do so.

For Mina, the initiative reflects a deliberate decision to speak to Muslim newcomers not as a monolithic audience, but as a distinct and growing community with unique life experiences.

Mina’s approach to the “A Mina Welcome” campaign was grounded in first- and third-party research, including qualitative focus groups with Muslim newcomers. These conversations revealed how critical the first grocery shop is in shaping feelings of belonging, comfort, and confidence. Many participants reported traveling up to 90 minutes to access trusted halal food, unaware that major Canadian retailers already carry HMA-certified halal options in their local neighbourhoods.

The timing of the hero film’s release ahead of Ramadan was intentional. Ramadan is a period when food, faith, and community are deeply interconnected, and when meaningful brand actions resonate more strongly with Muslim Canadians, whether newly arrived or second generation.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Mina launched 'A Mina Welcome,' a program inviting Canadians to nominate Muslim newcomers to receive a fridge stocked with Mina halal essentials, addressing the challenge of finding trusted halal food upon arrival in Canada.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Muslim newcomers settling in Canada and Canadians who can nominate them.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Digital medium, nomination program, and a hero video capturing the surprise delivery moment.
ai_enrichment.objective: To support Muslim newcomers during their initial months in Canada by providing trusted halal food, thereby building brand loyalty and awareness for Mina within this community.

content.description: Brazilian law guarantees CLT workers the right to one day off every twelve months for voluntary blood donation, without loss of pay, according to Article 473, item IV. With this in mind, Publicis Brasil developed the Save the Day campaign for Grupo Pulsa, the largest blood therapy group in Latin America. The main argument is to have an irresistible appeal to the gaming community: one less day of work to save lives in the game and in real life.The mechanics of the action promote blood donation drives aligned with the main releases on the gaming calendar, dates eagerly awaited by this audience segment. Anyone between the ages of 16 and 69, weighing more than 50 kg and in good health can donate blood. Those under 18 only need authorization from their parents or guardians. Due to the shortage of donations during the holiday season at the beginning of the year and during Carnival, blood banks across the country have critical stock levels.“We need to connect with new communities to reinforce the importance of blood donation, and that means understanding their habits, passions, and motivations. Save the Day helps us bring the cause closer to the gaming community, showing that a simple gesture can have a huge impact on the lives of many people,” says Paulo Pontes, Donor Recruitment Coordinator for the Pulsa Group. Present in 18 Brazilian states, the group allocates 30% of all blood collected to the Public Health Network.On the campaign calendar, the first activation takes place this Friday, February 27, when one of the most anticipated games of the year will be released. Following the schedule of new games, the next actions will take place on March 4, March 27, April 24, and April 28. By encouraging donation on release dates, the gesture of saving real lives adds to the experience of living a memorable and desired moment in gamer culture.The campaign features a film created by Publicis Brasil, which conveys the Save the Day concept in a direct way that connects with its audience's universe. The film features characters inspired by classic gaming archetypes, such as zombies and samurai, who inform the audience and encourage them to visit blood banks.“The idea stems from a real and urgent problem: blood donation. By turning a little-known right into an incentive, we have created a double benefit: gamers donate blood and also get a whole day to play the launch of their favorite game. In this way, we show that it is possible to save lives both inside and outside the game,” says Mauro Ramalho, Chief Creative Officer at Publicis Brasil.Complete information about blood donation: https://pulsandovidas.com.br/

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The 'Save the Day' campaign appeals to the gaming community by reframing blood donation as a chance to take a paid day off work, leveraging Brazilian law to save lives in both virtual and real worlds.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Brazilian gamers aged 16-69 who are eligible to donate blood (weighing over 50 kg and in good health).
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A film campaign promoting blood donation drives that are strategically aligned with major gaming calendar release dates.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase blood donations by appealing to the gaming community and encouraging them to utilize their legal right to a paid day off for this purpose.

content.description: Brand: Prime NowAgency: COdesignProduction Company: Indie ReelsDirector: Abrar AtharDirector of Photography: Abdul MamunExecutive Producer: Didarul Islam ShishirEditor: Mahaday Hasan Khan SamratMusic: Dhawal TandonColourist: Christian LeivaArt: Razim AhmedCostume: Bijaya

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign emphasizes the urgency and importance of making timely financial decisions, positioning 'Prime Now' as the solution for seizing current opportunities. It encourages individuals to act promptly on their financial goals.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and potentially small businesses in the finance sector who are contemplating financial decisions or seeking opportunities and are motivated to take immediate action.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film, titled 'Because your time is now.', intended for distribution across various visual channels such as television, digital platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media), and potentially streaming services.

content.description:
Milka, the iconic chocolate brand belonging to Mondelēz International, and official sponsor of LALIGA, is launching a campaign that connects with the emotion of football in a very special way. The brand's objective was to find out if there was room for tenderness, its most essential value, in the king of sports. And the affirmative answer was found in its maximum exponent outside the field, in families, and, specifically, in the figure of GRANDPARENTS.

Recognizing the fundamental role they play, Milka is launching an initiative -conceived by Ogilvy- that celebrates the role of grandparents as unconditional support for their grandchildren's dreams; as that haven where ambition finds support and fear dissipates with a hug. While the world rushes and demands results, grandparents typically offer their grandchildren the time and patience needed to nurture their earliest hopes and aspirations, becoming a great emotional support that allows a young person to leave home to pursue their destiny.

This bond transcends any material value, as it is based on simple values and unconditional love that celebrates every step of the way. There is no purer pride than that of a grandmother who sees her grandson succeed, demonstrating that behind every great achievement there is always a family history that made the flight possible.

In the words of Sofía Brás, Senior Brand Manager Milka Iberia, "With this campaign we celebrate the figure of grandparents, who play a fundamental role in supporting the Spanish family; they are that inspiration of effort and dedication that connects directly with the tenderness of a brand like Milka."

To illustrate this concept, Milka has sought a pair within the football world that represents this connection, this mutual dedication and devotion between a player and their grandparent, and has opted for the story of Fermín López and his grandmother Loli. They are the protagonists of the brand's new campaign, created by the Ogilvy agency, which launches today.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Milka connects its core value of tenderness with the emotion of football by celebrating the fundamental role of grandparents as unconditional supporters of their grandchildren's dreams.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Families, especially grandparents and their grandchildren who are football enthusiasts, and the broader audience that values family bonds and emotional support.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film as the primary medium, distributed across various platforms to reach the target audience, coupled with an initiative celebrating grandparents.
ai_enrichment.objective: To integrate Milka's essential value of tenderness into the world of football, demonstrating that emotional connection and support have a place in the sport.

content.description:
Partners for six years, Renault and the French national rugby team celebrate their shared vision in an intense sports film. Because in rugby, just as in the French manufacturer's vision, you must look back to move forward.Rugby is the only sport in which backward passes are mandatory to advance and gain ground. This unique feature is part of what makes the game so beautiful.Today, the French national team is a solid, mature one. They have learned from their difficulties, failures, and victories to improve their game and keep moving forward.For its part, Renault is continuing on its path by reinventing models that have made it successful in the past—the Renault 5, Renault 4, and now the Twingo—to affirm its commitment to reducing its carbon footprint. The brand continues to reinvent the spirit of “cars for living” and pave the way for more sustainable, more human, and resolutely modern mobility.A NEW ADVERTISING FILMA sports film featuring Damian Penaud, star player and record try scorer for France's national rugby team, Mickaël Guillard, promising talent in the team, and Fabien Galthié, their manager.In the middle of a match, when Damian Penaud seems to be blocked, an inner voice calls out to him and tells him to look behind him. Time stands still, and he sees all the moments, training sessions, achievements, and trials that led him to this moment.At the end of the film, we discover that this voice belongs to Fabien Galthié, his manager. Damian Penaud looks behind him and sees his teammate Mickaël Guillard at the other end of the field. He makes a long pass to him and Guillard scores a try. The film signed “look back, to move forward,” then reveals the old Renault 5, Renault 4 and Twingo, which are giving way to their new electric versions.It is the orchestral trip-hop track “Clubbed To Death” (Kurayamino variation) by Australian artist Rob Dougan, a song made popular by the movie The Matrix, which provides the campaign with musical support that is both impactful and introspectively profound.

A CROSS-MEDIA EUROPEAN CAMPAIGNIn France, the film is broadcast on television, digital media, and social networks since January 29, ahead of the Six Nations tournament, which kicks off on February 5 with a match between France and Ireland at the Stade de France. It will be accompanied by press inserts in sports and rugby newspapers and magazine.The campaign will be broadcast during all the major rugby events throughout the year, in France and Europe.“When you're a rugby fan, you know that looking back is how you gain ground. It's an approach that seems counterintuitive at first glance, but it resonates with me and makes perfect sense for Renault today. We are proud to be a premium partner of the French Rugby Federation and their promising teams.”Arnaud Belloni, Global Chief Marketing Officer – Renault

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Renault partners with the French national rugby team to illustrate a shared philosophy of learning from the past to drive future progress, mirroring rugby's unique backward pass strategy to advance. The campaign connects the team's evolution through past experiences with Renault's strategy of reinventing iconic models to embrace a sustainable future.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Automotive consumers, particularly in France, who appreciate sports (especially rugby), heritage, innovation, and a brand's commitment to sustainability.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across digital platforms and broadcast television.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reinforce Renault's brand identity as a forward-thinking innovator that respects its heritage and is committed to sustainability, leveraging its partnership with the French national rugby team.

content.description:
Comcast’s Xfinity makes its Super Bowl debut with a playful reimagining of *Jurassic Park*, reuniting Sam Neill, Laura Dern and Jeff Goldblum 33 years on. Titled *Jurassic Park… Works* and directed by Taika Waititi, the spot imagines how the 1993 classic might have unfolded if the park had been powered by Xfinity and WiFi.

 

Housewives, Birthday, Nun, Child, Birthday

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Xfinity playfully reimagines the classic film *Jurassic Park*, reuniting its original stars, to demonstrate how the park's infamous failures could have been averted with reliable Xfinity internet and WiFi.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: US households and internet users aged 25-65+, especially those familiar with the *Jurassic Park* franchise and who appreciate humor and reliable technology, given its Super Bowl context for mass appeal.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero commercial film aired during the Super Bowl, leveraging celebrity talent and a renowned director, supported by digital distribution across platforms like YouTube and social media.
ai_enrichment.objective: To significantly boost Xfinity's brand awareness and reinforce its image as a reliable and essential internet provider, particularly to a mass audience during a high-visibility event like the Super Bowl.

content.description:
In their first campaign for the Irish Defence Forces, The Public House shift focus from armament to empowerment.

The Public House lives by the mantra that ‘Boring Doesn’t Sell’...  but what does ‘boring’ look like when you’re advertising a client like the Defence Forces?

In this case it’s the usual things seen in campaigns all over the world for clients of this nature: action, armament, and adrenaline.

In their new campaign, the first collaboration between the independent creative agency and The Irish Defence Forces, those three things take a back seat to an emotional story that will be all-too-familiar to women all over Ireland.

The campaign, which will run across TV, VOD, OOH, Social, Radio, and Press, encourages people to ignore the little voice inside their heads which sews the seeds of self-doubt.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign shifts the focus from traditional military action and armament to individual empowerment, telling an emotional story that encourages people, particularly women, to overcome self-doubt and believe in their potential.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals in Ireland, specifically women, who may be hesitant or doubt their ability to join the Defence Forces.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Multi-channel campaign across TV, VOD, Out-of-Home (OOH), Social Media, Radio, and Press.
ai_enrichment.objective: To encourage potential recruits, especially women, to overcome internal self-doubt and consider a career with the Irish Defence Forces by shifting brand perception towards empowerment.

content.description:
Publicis London has launched “Love You Cans”, its debut work with Foster’s, part of HEINEKEN UK’s lager portfolio.

The work sees the iconic Foster’s can lose its wordmark to make way for the unconventional and authentic love language of British men: rude nicknames. The campaign aims for mates to use the cans to reach out to one another.

The work draws on new research which found that as they get older, British men are increasingly losing touch with their friends, with 61% of men surveyed admitting that reaching out to reconnect can feel awkward or uncomfortable. The research also found that many men find it less awkward to express affection through light-hearted mock insults - 59% of British millennial men say calling their mates a crude nickname is how they show that they care, while almost half (48%) say these nicknames help them to break the ice with friends they have lost touch with.*

‘Love You Cans’ invites men to break the ice with their mates by sending them a tinnie, emboldened with the words “b*llend, d**khead, kn*bhead, t*sser or w*nker”, as a gesture of authentic and light-hearted bonding. People can gift one of these cans of Foster’s to a friend, branded with their chosen nickname, by visiting www.loveyoucans.com.

Teased across OOH fly-posters and social from 23rd February, the campaign launched nationwide on 24th February, rolling out across bold digital out-of-home, social and influencer activity through to 9th March. The bold DOOH and OOH fly-posters spotlight the playful nicknames of the campaign, with tactical placements in Cardiff, London, Manchester and Newcastle. Media planning and buying was handled by Dentsu.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Foster's replaces its wordmark on cans with common 'rude nicknames' British men use for their friends, encouraging them to use the cans as an unconventional and authentic way to show affection and reconnect.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: British men, particularly millennials, who find it awkward to express affection or reconnect with friends directly and often use light-hearted mock insults or crude nicknames as a sign of care.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Special edition Foster's cans featuring various rude nicknames, promoted and distributed via OOH Outdoor advertising.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive brand engagement and relevance by facilitating authentic emotional expression and reconnection among British male friends through the product.

content.description:
Every year, the New Year holidays gift everyone anxiety and an existential crisis. People try to chop all the salads in time while also reflecting on the past and thinking about the future. But the meaning of existence is needed not only by those who cook, but also by those who are being cooked (especially them). So we gave voice to the ingredients and showed that LOKO delivery helps resolve all issues before it’s too late.The campaign lasted 42 days during the holiday season. Within that time, it managed to exceed expectations. CTR was 19% above the target. The number of clicks on ad links rose by 22%. Long-term ad recall showed an absolute increase of +4.1%.

 734.

BTS and 2nd Unit: Anastasia BazanovaLocation: One LocationPost Production Producer: Victoria KhmelnytskaEdit&VFX: Tykhonchuk Dmytro, Dima SuhodolAI: Yevhen ChernyshovColorist: Kostiantyn LishchynaSound: Dima SnizhkoMusic: Maksym Bondarenko

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign animated food ingredients, giving them a voice to express their existential holiday anxieties, and positioned LOKO delivery as the solution to alleviate the stress of festive meal preparations for everyone.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals experiencing stress and time pressure related to holiday meal preparation, particularly during the New Year season, who are seeking convenient delivery solutions.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Visually rich digital advertising campaigns utilizing creative design to anthropomorphize ingredients, distributed across online platforms to drive engagement and clicks.
ai_enrichment.objective: To boost engagement and improve long-term brand recall for LOKO by positioning its delivery service as a stress-relieving solution during the busy holiday season.

content.description:
Novartis, a leading global innovative medicines company and the official pharmaceutical partner of the NFL, today announced the launch of “Relax, it’s a blood test,” a new national prostate cancer awareness initiative that kicks off with a commercial airing during Super Bowl LX on February 8, 2026.

Prostate cancer is the most commonly diagnosed cancer and second leading cause of cancer death among men in the United States. Early detection leads to a greater than 99% relative 5-year survival rate, yet many men avoid prostate cancer screening, and 60% mistakenly believe that it requires an invasive digital rectal exam. The reality is the first step is a simple prostate-specific antigen (PSA) blood test.

“Relax, it’s a blood test” cuts through stigma and addresses the anxieties and misperceptions that can cause men to avoid prostate cancer screening. Novartis has partnered with leading patient advocacy organizations, including the American Cancer Society, Malecare, National Alliance of State Prostate Cancer Coalitions, Prostate Cancer Foundation, Prostate Health Education Network, ZERO Prostate Cancer, and others to help shape the campaign and drive increased PSA screenings. The initiative aims to demystify the process, empowering men to replace hesitation with action and making early detection easy, accessible, and potentially lifesaving.

“Novartis has a long history of tackling the toughest challenges in healthcare, including prostate cancer, the second leading cause of cancer death for men,” said Victor Bultó, President, US, Novartis. “Early detection of prostate cancer can make a meaningful difference in outcomes, but the fear of a potentially uncomfortable exam causes many of us to avoid making an appointment. By teaming up with the NFL and these celebrated players, we’re empowering men nationwide to take the first step: a simple blood test.”

For the “Relax, it’s a blood test” campaign, Novartis and a roster of eight influential NFL leaders and icons, including Super Bowl-winning coach Bruce Arians, have teamed up to champion prostate cancer screening and early detection. The campaign features current tight ends George Kittle (San Francisco 49ers) and Colby Parkinson (Los Angeles Rams), along with NFL greats Rob Gronkowski (Tampa Bay Buccaneers), Greg Olsen (Carolina Panthers), Tony Gonzalez (Atlanta Falcons), Delanie Walker (Tennessee Titans), and Vernon Davis (San Francisco 49ers). Each brings a personal connection to prostate cancer and a shared commitment to breaking down stigma, normalizing men’s health conversations, and motivating men to discuss PSA blood tests with their doctors.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign aims to de-stigmatize prostate cancer screening by highlighting that the initial step is a simple blood test, not an invasive digital rectal exam, thereby addressing anxieties and misconceptions.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Men in the United States who are at risk for prostate cancer and avoid screening due to misconceptions about the process.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A national TV commercial airing during Super Bowl LX, leveraging the 'Film' medium.
ai_enrichment.objective: Increase awareness of the ease of prostate cancer screening to encourage early detection and improve survival rates.

metadata.title: Casa da Esfiha, It's All Good!

content.description:
Every year, our greetings reach for the promise of fortune and wealth. Yet the truest blessings are often right in front of us, quietly waiting to be seen. This Chinese New Year, may we find them, cherish them, and hold them close.

Happy Chinese New Year from all of us at Maybank.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign redefines Chinese New Year blessings, shifting focus from traditional fortune and wealth to the often-overlooked, intangible blessings of loved ones and cherished moments.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals of Chinese descent celebrating Chinese New Year, particularly Maybank's customers and potential customers, who appreciate reflective and emotionally resonant messages.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A short film released on digital platforms such as YouTube and social media, likely complemented by traditional TV broadcasts, to deliver the festive message during Chinese New Year.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand affinity and goodwill by associating Maybank with a heartfelt message of cherishing true blessings during the Chinese New Year celebration.

metadata.title: The BS Meter Calls BS on Competitors’ Razors

content.description:
The BS Meter Calls BS on Competitors’ Razors

Dollar Shave Club is doubling down on its no-holds-barred, truth-telling roots and taking aim at Big Razors with a hilariously bold new campaign, dubbed the “Bullshit Meter.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Dollar Shave Club leverages a 'Bullshit Meter' in a hilariously bold film campaign to humorously expose and call out perceived misleading claims made by larger, traditional razor competitors.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Men and women who are skeptical of traditional 'Big Razor' brands, value transparency and authenticity in their grooming products, and appreciate bold, humorous advertising.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film distributed across digital video platforms (e.g., YouTube, social media) and potentially linear television, featuring the 'Bullshit Meter' concept.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reinforce Dollar Shave Club's honest, no-nonsense brand identity, differentiate from competitors by satirizing their marketing, and attract new customers by highlighting value and truth.

content.description:
From “Field of Dreams” to “Bull Durham” to “For the Love of the Game,” for nearly four decades actor Kevin Costner has been synonymous with America’s pastime.  To help launch Topps’ Series 1 Baseball set – and to help celebrate 75 years of Topps Baseball – Costner has narrated a nostalgic look at the history of cards while also introducing this year’s flagship offering in a 60-second creative spot from the brand titled “Every Card Tells a Story”:

On its diamond year, the video’s POV takes the fan and collector through a diamond-like dream world, weaving through a virtual museum of the top cards of the last three quarters of a century.  A quick cut takes you to this year’s Series 1 cards, “the next stories to be written,” says Costner, “...new faces…new dreams…preserved as a single frame in time…where the next chapter begins.”  The spot was directed and designed by yU+co, who developed the opening credits for HBO’s “House of the Dragon” as well as 100 feature films, including working with Steven Spielberg, Ang Lee and Ridley Scott.

 

AGENCY: Kamp GrizzlyExecutive Creative Director: Dan PortraitCreative Director: Jeff HardingAccount Director: Madi SmukallaArt Producer: Camille Westerberg

CLIENT: Fanatics CollectiblesChief Marketing Officer: Ken TurnerVice President, Creative and Content: Andrew GoodmanVice President, Content: Brian Terwilliger

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Kevin Costner narrates a 60-second film spot for Topps' 75th anniversary and Series 1 Baseball set, taking viewers on a nostalgic journey through the history of baseball cards and introducing the new collection as future stories.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Baseball fans and collectors, particularly those with a nostalgic appreciation for the sport's history and card collecting.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 60-second hero film spot featuring Kevin Costner, distributed across digital platforms and potentially broadcast television.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build awareness and drive excitement for the launch of Topps Series 1 Baseball set while celebrating the brand's 75th anniversary.

content.description:

metadata.title: With BRICO DEPÔT, you no longer have to give up on your improvement projects

content.description:
With BRICO DEPÔT, you no longer have to give up on your improvement projects

Brico Dépôt is reaffirming its low-cost promise with a new TV and print campaign created by AUSTRALIE agency. More than ever, the leading low-cost brand in the French DIY market offers quality products in bulk quantities at low prices, so that everyone—homeowners and renters, occasional or experienced DIYers—can get their projects off the ground without delay.In an economic climate marked by inflation, declining purchase volumes, and increasingly tight budgetary constraints, construction projects are often postponed or even suspended. Consumers are hunting for bargains and prioritizing their spending. DIY is no exception to this reality.In response, Brico Dépôt is changing its message. Yesterday, the focus was on “accessing more” for your projects. Today, the challenge is more fundamental: enabling access to the possibility of carrying out your projects.This ambition comes to life through a new campaign made up of four films and four print ads, which will be released throughout the year. Directed by English director Scott Lyon, the creative approach is resolutely dynamic: immersive action sequences, carefully crafted material effects, and ultra-dynamic camera effects. The energetic and spectacular staging is accompanied by a voiceover from a coach who conveys a simple yet powerful message: with Brico Dépôt, thanks to its low prices and constantly available stock, DIYers no longer give up—they get motivated right away, roll up their sleeves, and get startedThe campaign is driven by a direct and engaging tagline: “Come on, get to work.”

 

Agency: AUSTRALIECreative Direction: Thomas Martin-Lalande, Sydney MonjauzeCreatives: Thomas Martin-Lalande, Sydney MonjauzeSales: Vincent Leclabart, Anaïs Courbez, Chloé Oizillon, Mathilde Lacour, Jérémie VéjuxTV Production: Solène L’HelguenAdvertiser: Brico DépôtMarketing, Digital and Client Director: François ChardonStrategic Marketing & Image Director: Andreea BalanescuStrategic Marketing & Branding Group Manager: Alexandra CerveauProduction: La PacDirector: Scott LyonProducer: Esha WoottumProduction Director: Chrystelle MalletSound Production: La Cuisine

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Brico Dépôt reassures consumers that they can still undertake their home improvement projects, even amidst inflation and tight budgets, by offering quality products at low prices.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: French homeowners and renters, from occasional to experienced DIYers, who are facing budgetary constraints due to inflation and are seeking affordable solutions for their improvement projects.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: TV campaign and print advertisements.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reaffirm Brico Dépôt's low-cost promise and position the brand as an accessible solution for home improvement projects despite current economic challenges.

metadata.title: Flowers: How Naptural Inspired a Community of Natural Hair Creators

content.description:
Flowers: How Naptural Inspired a Community of Natural Hair Creators

Your favorite natural hair blogger Naptural85 set the standard for what natural hair content creation could be, helping the community flourish into what it is today. Now she gets the flowers she deserves.

Online video trends have evolved to become the driving force of pop culture. But do you know who’s creating them? From the moment of upload, YouTube’s award-winning series Flowers reveals the untold, true stories of the Black creators and culture behind your favorite trends.

 

Youtube Music. It’s not just what we listen to. It’s who we are.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The YouTube "Flowers" series highlights influential Black creators who shaped online trends and culture, with this film specifically honoring Naptural85 for her pioneering role in the natural hair content community and inspiring its growth.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Fans of natural hair content and its creators, particularly Black women aged 18-45, as well as aspiring content creators and those interested in the cultural impact of online trends and influencer stories on YouTube.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film released on the YouTube platform as part of their "Flowers" documentary series, featuring influencer Naptural85, likely promoted across YouTube's own channels and related social media platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance YouTube's brand image as a platform that recognizes and empowers diverse creators, particularly Black creators, by celebrating their cultural impact and contribution to online trends, thereby fostering engagement and reinforcing community loyalty.

content.description:
In a climate where the thump of a letter on the doormat usually triggers a spike in cortisol, BBH London is turning the "unexpected bill" on its head for Tesco Mobile.

As the UK braces for the inevitable Q1 CPI hikes from major telcos, the “Helpful Network" is doubling down on its Frozen Prices proposition. The campaign subverts the visual language of debt and bureaucracy, transforming the dreaded white envelope into a brand superpower.

With data showing around a third of 30 - 49 year olds would struggle with an unexpected cost and are borrowing more than usual to get by1, the creative leans into the tension of "bill dread."

Laura Joseph, Chief Customer Officer said: “We wanted to make switching to Tesco Mobile the obvious choice. Thanks to our great network coverage with O2 and Clubcard Price deals, we have the perfect mix to protect customers from another Consumer Price Index (CPI) increase this winter. We’ll also promise to keep their price Frozen for the duration of their contract, thanks to Clubcard. The helpful network, when you need us.”

While competitors prepare to squeeze margins, BBH’s OOH and Audio executions pre-emptively reassure Clubcard customers that the only surprise coming from Tesco Mobile is... nothing at all.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Tesco Mobile's 'P.S. It’s Frozen' campaign subverts the anxiety associated with unexpected bills, particularly the dreaded white envelope, to highlight its fixed-price proposition and position itself as a 'Helpful Network' amidst rising living costs.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Financially sensitive UK consumers aged 30-49, who are concerned about unexpected costs, rising inflation, and telco price hikes, seeking stability and value.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Audio advertisements (e.g., radio, podcasts, streaming platforms) that narratively evoke the 'bill dread' experience and offer Tesco Mobile's 'Frozen Prices' as the solution.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive customer acquisition and switching by making Tesco Mobile's 'Frozen Prices' proposition the obvious and preferable choice for consumers.

content.description:

There is no drama, no escalation and no emotional payoff, just quiet observation. It is what it is; a credit card. Now all we have to do is try to remember it.

content.description:
‘Taste the Thrill’ is the global launch campaign for Doritos’ Formula 1 sponsorship, co-developed by PS21 and Goodby Silverstein & Partners. It transforms everyday snacking moments into high-octane F1 experiences, using G-force visual effects and authentic race commentary to bridge the gap between the living room and the cockpit. This project serves as a cornerstone of PepsiCo’s partnership with F1, using the "crunch" as a literal catalyst for adrenaline.

 A..

Agency: PS21

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign reimagines everyday Doritos snacking by transforming the 'crunch' into a literal catalyst for adrenaline, linking it to the high-octane thrill of Formula 1 races through G-force visuals and authentic race commentary. It aims to bridge the gap between casual snacking and immersive F1 experiences.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Global F1 enthusiasts and snack consumers, particularly young adults and thrill-seekers who appreciate immersive sports experiences and enjoy savory snacks.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Digital video content featuring G-force visual effects and authentic race commentary, likely distributed across social media platforms, digital media buys, and potentially F1's official digital channels as part of a global launch.
ai_enrichment.objective: To launch and amplify Doritos' global Formula 1 sponsorship, thereby strengthening brand association with excitement, high-octane experiences, and adrenaline.

content.description:
Created by Tombras and directed by Easy Pete’s Zach Tavel, the spot introduces a new antagonist whose grand plans are undone by poorly optimised corporate infrastructure. In the film, Tim’s vengeful simpleton attempts to airdrop a monster named Baby Breck into the city, only to discover the chaos cannot begin because the creature has not been onboarded and its harness is still awaiting finance approval.

True to the actor’s offbeat comedic style, the absurd scenario highlights how outdated business software can derail even the most ambitious schemes. The campaign launches with a 30-second Super Bowl LX spot, will air on Peacock during the Olympics, and continue throughout the year with additional ads following the supervillain.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Tim Robinson portrays a supervillain whose grand scheme to unleash a monster is comically thwarted by Rippling's inefficient corporate infrastructure, highlighting the real-world frustrations of outdated business software.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Businesses and organizations experiencing inefficiencies due to fragmented or outdated HR, IT, and finance software, specifically targeting decision-makers and employees who encounter these operational challenges.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film launched as a 30-second Super Bowl LX TV spot, followed by airings on Peacock during the Olympics, and continued throughout the year with additional television and digital ads featuring the supervillain.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness for Rippling's integrated business software by humorously demonstrating how inefficient corporate infrastructure can impede even ambitious plans, thereby positioning Rippling as the modern solution.

content.description:
The Week has launched a new TV campaign as the latest execution of its new global positioning ‘Because the news needs a curator’, developed in partnership with thirty6 (previously the Orange Panther Collective).

The TV ad drops as part of a multi-channel brand push for the global news subscriptions business, part of Future PLC.

‘Paperboy’ - written by Nigel Roberts (Creative Partner at thirty6) and directed by J Marlow at Outsider, uses sardonic wit and Marlow’s iconic offbeat directing to demonstrate how The Week uses the filter of their award winning editors to cut through the deluge of news to bring you only ‘the stories that matter’.

 

Brand: The WeekManaging Director, News: Richard CampbellVP Marketing: Chris RusheHead of Brand Marketing, UK News & Wealth: Sam PashleyBrand Director, News: Harry Haydon

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The 'Paperboy' campaign uses sardonic wit and offbeat direction to illustrate how The Week's award-winning editors curate the most important news, cutting through information overload.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Discerning news consumers who are overwhelmed by the volume of daily news and seek expertly curated, high-quality information.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero TV ad serving as the latest execution of a new global positioning, supported by a broader multi-channel brand push.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand awareness for The Week's new global positioning as a news curator and drive subscriptions by showcasing its unique value in filtering essential stories.

content.description:
Matin Kim inherently carries a fusion and twist of Europe and Korea. It’s part of what makes the brand unique — a new language of style.This campaign, titled “NEW LANGUAGE,” follows protagonists from different backgrounds who briefly meet for the first time. Despite speaking different languages, they connect naturally, experiencing a moment of connection on a new level — beyond the boundaries of any particular language or culture. It reflects Matin Kim’s style, a way of communication shaped by fashion itself.

 

Advertising Agency: Parcel Studio, Berlin, GermanyProduction Company: Shotview, Berlin, Germany

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign portrays fashion as a universal language that allows individuals from diverse backgrounds to connect beyond cultural and linguistic barriers, mirroring Matin Kim's unique East-meets-West aesthetic.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Fashion-forward individuals who appreciate unique, fusion styles and cultural connections, likely interested in contemporary design and a global aesthetic.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: An integrated campaign, likely featuring visual content such as a hero film or series of short videos, distributed across digital platforms (social media, brand website) and potentially print or OOH placements to showcase the narrative.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand awareness and establish Matin Kim's unique identity as a fashion brand that fosters universal connection through its distinct, fused style.

metadata.title: Happy Beautiful Year 大团圆，过靓年！

content.description:
The OG family is back, and this year, they are joined by the extended family in Watsons 大团圆，过靓年 #HappyBeautifulYear Everything is set for a heartwarming reunion, until chaos walks through the door!

content.description:
The heart of this commercial is sharing community stories. Minor League Baseball players aren’t just your hometown heroes on the field; they also contribute to your everyday lives. On and off the field, they embody what it means to be part of the community. In many small towns, the ballpark isn’t the only place where these athletes can represent the community.

This  student campaign titled 'Minor League. Major Community.' was published in United States in January, 2026. It was created for the brand: MiLB, . This Film medium campaign is related to the Hospitality, Tourism, Sports, and Travel and Tourism industries and contains 1 media asset. 

Directors: Kaya Lee and Lucy KiefferProducers: Kaya Lee and Lucy KiefferArt Direction and Copy: Kaya Lee and Lucy KiefferProfessor: Mark Allen

ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A commercial film dedicated to sharing authentic community stories featuring MiLB players.

content.description:
ESAN University invites young people to make education the tool that shapes their own path.

 

AGENCY: Fosbury LatamCEO: Jonathan DaveyChief Brand Officer & VP of Planning: Diana MandujanoChief Creative Officer: Andrés NarvarteExecutive Art Director: Manuel SauriJunior Art Director: Fernando BazánHead of Content: Camila AguirreSenior Brand Supervisor: Miluska VenancioBrand Supervisor: Lissette MenachoSenior Brand Consultant: Ruth HorruitinerBrand Consultant: Sofía BenitesCreative Copywriter: Oscar AlarcónPRODUCTION COMPANY: Moly Films

content.description: Pot Noodle’s infamous ‘slurp’ is back in a new tongue in cheek campaign that reaffirms the ‘King of Snacks’ brand platform, ‘Nothing Satisfies Like Pot Noodle’.Created with adam&eve\TBWA, the new work builds on the brand’s award-winning 2024 campaign, which originally featured an epic 12-second slurp before being re-edited with softer sounds following a widespread outcry.The new campaign makes the most of Pot Noodle’s divisive sonic brand asset with a 30-second film set in a cinema packed with horror movie fans. They’re expecting the scares to come from the big screen, but a Pot Noodle-loving moviegoer proves far more terrifying.The film launches alongside a series of out of home ads which lean into Pot Noodle’s reputation for cheeky advertising while reinforcing the satisfaction message. Featuring extreme close-ups of ecstatic faces, the suggestion, however, is that a rather different spot is being hit.Madeleine Boulton, Pot Noodle Brand Marketing Manager, said: “Before the internet comes at us, we're not advocating for people to eat noisy snacks in the cinema. However, our Pot Noodle fans appreciate a no‑nonsense, deeply satisfying way to tackle hunger whenever it strikes. This campaign is our tribute to that love, by celebrating our fans, their favourite flavours, and the unapologetic ways they choose to enjoy Pot Noodle, no matter what others might think.”Mark Shanley, Executive Creative Director, adam&eve\TBWA, added: “Have you bought food at the cinema lately? Wow! Expensive and not even very satisfying. Pot Noodle on the other hand is both cheap and satisfying. So we’ve had a lot of fun with that.”The work breaks on February 2 and runs until the end of April across cinemas, OOH video on demand and social channels.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign humorously reintroduces Pot Noodle's infamous and divisive 'slurp' sound, using it as a source of terror in a cinema-themed film to reaffirm the brand's 'Nothing Satisfies Like Pot Noodle' platform. It pairs this with cheeky out-of-home advertising reinforcing the satisfaction message.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General consumers of convenience food, particularly young adults and students, who appreciate humorous, tongue-in-cheek advertising and are familiar with the brand's quirky reputation.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 30-second hero film (cinema/TV/digital) depicting a horror movie scenario, complemented by a series of out-of-home (OOH) advertisements.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reinforce brand awareness and solidify Pot Noodle's core 'Nothing Satisfies Like Pot Noodle' satisfaction message, thereby strengthening its 'King of Snacks' brand platform.

content.description:
Agency:Publicidad Comercial MullenLowe Nicaragua

In the Dry Corridor of Nicaragua, climate change has made farming unpredictable. So, we created, The Climate Women: a community led climate alert system run by women farmers using simple tools. Low tech weather stations collect local data, which is processed and shared through WhatsApp and local radio. For the first time, women generate precise local forecasts for their own communities. By turning everyday technology into shared infrastructure, the project transforms climate uncertainty into actionable data, strengthening food security and resilience.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign created "The Climate Women," a community-led climate alert system where women farmers in Nicaragua's Dry Corridor use low-tech tools and everyday technology to generate and share precise local weather forecasts for their communities.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Women farmers in the Dry Corridor of Nicaragua, and their communities, who are directly affected by unpredictable farming conditions due to climate change.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Establishing low-tech weather stations to collect local data, processing this data, and disseminating local forecasts through WhatsApp and local radio.
ai_enrichment.objective: To strengthen food security and resilience in climate-vulnerable communities by transforming climate uncertainty into actionable, locally generated weather data.

content.description:
1. CONTEXT & PROBLEMATICOne in five Montréalers experience food insecurity, yet this crisis is just a number to the public. Moisson Montréal, the central food bank for Greater Montréal and the main organization dealing with  this issue, fulfills nearly one million requests for food aid each month. Since demand has increased 76%  over five years, the public has failed to grasp the emotional toll of making impossible choices.

The objective for Moisson Montréal was to move beyond conventional appeals to spark a broader, emotional conversation and make this quiet struggle real.

2. THE IDEAA simple, yet effective creative concept replaced standard-sized grocery carts with a tiny one, a physical metaphor for the limited options and purchasing power people facing food insecurity live with every day. The tiny cart was designed to be an emotional rallying point for the cause, transforming the issue from a statistic into an immediate, visceral experience. It was a simple, non-verbal piece of ambient media that used discomfort and surprise to trigger profound empathy.

 

Advertising Agency : LG2, Montréal, CanadaCCO : Nicolas BaldoviniCreative Director : Alexandre JourdainCopywriter : Vanessa Harbec, Roxanne PelletierArt Director : Patrick Rochon, Sarah LemireProject Manager : Katia Dupuy, Geneviève DemersStrategic Planner : Laurence Landry-Leduc, Philippe GoudreaultClient Service : Anne-Elizabeth De Longchamp, Claudia TrudeauPR : Marie-Ève Gionet

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign replaced standard-sized grocery carts with tiny ones, serving as a physical metaphor for the limited options and purchasing power experienced by those facing food insecurity.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General public in Montréal, particularly those unaware of the emotional toll and difficult choices faced by individuals experiencing food insecurity.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Placing tiny, symbolic grocery carts in public ambient spaces to physically represent the limited options and purchasing power.
ai_enrichment.objective: To spark a broader, emotional conversation about food insecurity and make the quiet struggle of those affected feel real to the public.

content.description:
Last night, in the heart of Hollywood, Apple TV celebrated the highly anticipated second season of “Monarch: Legacy of Monsters,” with a record-breaking aerial display that filled the sky with the biggest monsters from Legendary's Monsterverse.

Godzilla, Kong and the new “Titan X” were ready for their close-up as they took over Los Angeles in the Guinness World Record's tallest aerial display of a fictional character formed by drones — and, series stars Kurt Russell, Wyatt Russell, Anders Holm, Mari Yamamoto and Joe Tippett were on-hand to watch the Titans face-off

Set to a score developed by the show's composer, Leopold Ross, the 12-minute three dimensional drone show included 3,000 drones that flew nearly 500 feet in the sky (taller than the Statue of Liberty!) with a square footage of 1000’ x 1000’, or the size of three football fields, was stationed over the Hollywood Forever Cemetery, but due to it’s monstrous size it was viewable from three miles away at any direction.

While drone shows often appear as a sky spectacle, this 12-minute production plays with the Hollywood landscape to situate Godzilla, Kong, and the newly introduced Titan X amidst the landscape to feel like they’ve truly taken over the city. The show also integrates pyrodrones in the air and on the ground acting as visual effects for key moments like Godzilla blowing his atomic breath. The activation was created with creative agency, Heads in the Sky.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Apple TV+ launched the second season of “Monarch: Legacy of Monsters” with a Guinness World Record-breaking 3,000-drone aerial display in Hollywood, showcasing iconic monsters like Godzilla and Kong in a towering 3D show.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Fans of sci-fi monster movies and TV shows, particularly the Monsterverse franchise, existing and potential subscribers of Apple TV+, and individuals interested in unique large-scale public spectacles.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A live, record-setting 12-minute three-dimensional drone show in Hollywood featuring 3,000 drones forming fictional characters nearly 500 feet high, accompanied by an original score and attended by series stars, amplified via OOH Outdoor placement and likely PR/media coverage.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate significant buzz, media attention, and awareness for the highly anticipated second season of “Monarch: Legacy of Monsters” on Apple TV+, ultimately driving viewership and subscriptions.

content.description:
He keeps a treasure inside an old tin box, hidden behind his school notebooks: crumpled coins earned in small steps, small “yeses,” small sacrifices.

Every time other kids buy candy, he looks down and holds himself back. Not because he doesn’t want it, but because he’s carrying a dream bigger than himself.

He’s saving for one single pack of MIO, the way you prepare a surprise you’re not brave enough to name yet.

At night, he watches his mother quietly, her tired hands, her repeated gestures, that quiet strength holding the whole house together.

He holds the pack close to his chest, almost like a secret, and he dreams of a simple miracle: a chance hidden inside. A chance to win a gold necklace worth 10,000 dirhams, not to shine, but to finally tell her, with something that carries weight and light, what his child’s heart still struggles

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A young boy's heartfelt sacrifice and determination to save for a Mio pack, driven by the hope of winning a gold necklace to express his profound gratitude and love for his hardworking mother.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Families and mothers who value family bonds, selfless love, and the aspiration to provide for loved ones, likely in the Middle East region given the currency mention.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Emotional storytelling delivered through a hero film (e.g., online video, TV commercial) as part of a 360° campaign, supported by in-store promotions highlighting the prize, and digital activations encouraging sharing of personal stories of sacrifice and love.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive sales of Mio products through a compelling promotional offer, while building brand affinity by connecting with themes of selfless love, sacrifice, and hope.

content.description:
RITZ returns to the Super Bowl with Jon Hamm and Bowen Yang cast as self-described “salty” observers of a beachside party. Created by MARTIN* and directed by Dan Opsal via hungryman, the spot introduces ‘RITZ Island’, a coastal escape where buttery crackers reign and even the saltiest personalities struggle to resist joining in.

Perched in a treehouse overlooking the festivities, Jon and Bowen debate whether to abandon their post until Scarlett Johansson arrives by jet ski, urging them to loosen up. Playing on ‘saltiness’ as both attitude and flavour, the ad marks RITZ’s second Big Game appearance, building on last year’s debut rather than starting fresh.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign introduces 'RITZ Island', a vibrant, buttery cracker-filled escape where the irresistible deliciousness of Ritz crackers transforms even the most cynical and 'salty' personalities (represented by celebrities Jon Hamm and Bowen Yang) into joyful participants, playing on 'saltiness' as both an attitude and a flavor.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Broad audience of general consumers and snack lovers who watch the Super Bowl, likely adults aged 18-55, appreciative of humor and celebrity appearances, and those seeking enjoyable snack options.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A high-production value hero film aired during the Super Bowl broadcast, featuring celebrity talent (Jon Hamm, Bowen Yang, Scarlett Johansson), a narrative 'RITZ Island' concept, and likely supported by integrated digital and social media amplification.
ai_enrichment.objective: To significantly boost brand awareness and strengthen brand recall for Ritz crackers, reinforcing its presence and appeal within the competitive snack market during a major cultural event like the Super Bowl.

content.description:

content.description:
Eli Lilly and Company is partnering with Team USA for the 2026 Winter Olympic and Paralympic Games through its “Never Over” campaign, highlighting resilience in both health and sport. As an official partner, Lilly’s Milestones into Meaning programmed donates $5,000 to health-focused non-profits for every medal won or record broken, supporting greater health equity.

The “Never Over” theme connects the perseverance of elite athletes with scientific advancement in healthcare. Lilly is collaborating with Winter Olympians and Paralympians, including bobsledder Elana Meyers Taylor, to share stories of overcoming personal health challenges. Through Milestones into Meaning, every achievement by Team USA at Milano Cortina 2026 unlocks a donation to US-based health organizations, reinforcing the brand’s long-term commitment to community impact.

This initiative is part of a broader partnership spanning the 2024 Paris Games, the 2026 Milano Cortina Games and the 2028 Los Angeles Games. The campaign underscores the idea that just as athletes never stop training, Lilly never stops working to improve health, aiming to inspire healthier lifestyles while increasing access to care.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Eli Lilly's "Never Over" campaign partners with Team USA for the 2026 Winter Games, linking athletic resilience with health perseverance and scientific advancement, while activating the "Milestones into Meaning" program to donate to health non-profits for every medal or record.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: The general public, sports enthusiasts, patients and their families, and individuals interested in health equity, medical innovation, and corporate social responsibility, particularly those who follow the Olympic and Paralympic Games.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Strategic partnership with Team USA for the 2026 Winter Olympic and Paralympic Games, development and distribution of film content featuring Winter Olympians and Paralympians sharing personal health challenges, and a cause-related marketing program ("Milestones into Meaning") involving donations to health non-profits based on Team USA achievements.
ai_enrichment.objective: Enhance Eli Lilly's brand reputation and affinity by associating it with resilience, health advancement, and commitment to health equity through its partnership with Team USA and the "Milestones into Meaning" donation program.

metadata.title: Life's hard work. Switching bank accounts isn't.

content.description:
Life's hard work. Switching bank accounts isn't.

ELVIS have launched "Life's hard work. Switching bank accounts isn't." for the Current Account Switch Service.

The campaign targets financially stretched 18-44 year olds navigating life's biggest milestones, from starting university and moving home to buying property and starting families. The insight: at a time when major life moments demand all their energy, this audience doesn't have the mental bandwidth for more decision-making or time for additional life admin. Switching banks feels like hard work when it's actually effortless with the Current Account Switch Service.

Directed by Tim McNaughton through Arts & Sciences, two new 20" spots humorously prove that while life has plenty of genuinely difficult moments, from the awkward end of a job interview to wrestling with fitted sheets, switching your bank account won't be one of them. The Service automatically moves all regular payments including salary, rent and bills from your old account to your new one.

Dawn Lauder, Brand and Marketing Lead at Pay.UK, said: "We know that 18-44-year-olds are navigating some of life's biggest moments right now, and many are financially stretched. The last thing they need is another complicated piece of life admin. But the reality is that switching bank accounts with the Current Account Switch Service is genuinely simple, all your payments move automatically. This campaign cuts through with humour that feels true to their lives, showing them that while we can't make fitting a sheet any easier, we can make switching bank accounts effortless."

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses humor to contrast genuinely difficult life moments with the effortless process of switching bank accounts via the Current Account Switch Service.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Financially stretched 18-44 year olds navigating major life milestones, who lack the mental bandwidth for perceived complex tasks like switching banks.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Two 20-second humorous film spots directed by Tim McNaughton, part of an integrated campaign.
ai_enrichment.objective: To overcome the perception that switching banks is difficult and demonstrate the effortless nature of the Current Account Switch Service to encourage adoption.

metadata.title: SPORT IS EQUAL WHEN WE SEE ALL GAMES EQUAL

content.description:
SPORT IS EQUAL WHEN WE SEE ALL GAMES EQUAL

“Soccer is king”. In Vietnam and across much of ASEAN, sports like Wushu, Kabaddi, and Sepak takraw rarely receive any attention or media space. That’s why at SEA Games 33, where more than 50 sports came together, MILO gave away its iconic green space to let the lesser-known sports take the spotlight.For 30 years, MILO has stood for sports - with every green MILO pack fueling Vietnam’s next generation of athletes. Yet not every sport is seen equally. While some sports get the spotlight, many remain in the shadows.For the first time, Asian-rooted sports and more appeared on the familiar green pack many Vietnamese generations have grown up with.No scripts.No staged lines.Just genuine emotions from young athletes talking about the sport they love, and the pure joy of seeing themselves on a MILO pack.“I never imagined I’d be on a MILO milk pack,” said a young athlete, wide-eyed and proud.For MILO, this is more than an ad. It’s a statement, a promise: MILO See Games Equal, because every effort deserves to be seen.And this green space MILO is giving away today is only the beginning of our journey toward sports equality.

 

Brand: Néstle MILO VietnamAgency: Ogilvy VietnamCreative team: The Tao Nguyen - Art Director; Hai Linh Nguyen - Copywriter; Huy Vu Nguyen & Thy (Thi) Nguyen - Creative DirectorsAccount team: Hoang Anh Nguyen, Duc Canh Nguyen, Dung DaoPlanning team: Thanh Giang Tran, Minh Quan Dinh

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: MILO champions the equality of all sports by giving visibility to overlooked Asian-rooted and niche sports, featuring them on its iconic packaging and through authentic athlete stories.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Vietnamese youth and athletes, their parents, and the general public across Vietnam and ASEAN interested in sports and equitable representation.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Packaging redesign featuring diverse sports on MILO's iconic green packs, documentary-style film content showcasing athletes' genuine emotions, and leveraging the SEA Games 33 platform for visibility.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reinforce MILO's long-standing commitment to sports and athletes by championing equality across all sports, thereby strengthening brand image and connection with consumers.

content.description:
Business travel doesn't usually involve 200 mph pit stops and split-second decision-making, but for Engine, the all-in-one platform for business travel, and media agency Noble People, that's exactly the strategy.

Engine worked with NASCAR to develop "Road to DAYTONA," a three-part social docu-style content series leading up to and during the 2026 DAYTONA 500. It goes behind the scenes with Hendrick Motorsports, the most winning team in the race's history,  and features 2025 NASCAR Cup Series Champion Kyle Larson, Crew Chief Cliff Daniels, and Hauler Driver Lance Scott.

The series was produced by Engine and NASCAR, with creative concept, media strategy and execution led by Noble People.

About the episodesIn the first episode, "The Great American Race," that aired on Feb. 13th, Hendrick Motorsports  sets the stage for race weekend as the No. 5 Team breaks down what’s at stake and what it takes to win in DAYTONA.

The second episode, "Drop the Engine," released Feb. 19 speaks to the unseen prep and strategy to the belief and precision that lay the foundation before the season even begins.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Engine, a business travel platform, partnered with NASCAR to create 'The Road to DAYTONA,' a three-part social docu-series. It goes behind the scenes with Hendrick Motorsports to highlight precision, speed, and strategic decision-making, drawing a parallel to efficient business travel.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Business travel managers, corporate decision-makers responsible for travel, and frequent business travelers who value efficiency, strategic planning, and high performance.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A three-part docu-style content series released on social media channels, featuring behind-the-scenes access to Hendrick Motorsports and key NASCAR personalities, leading up to and during the 2026 DAYTONA 500.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand awareness and perception for Engine as an efficient, strategic, and high-performance platform for business travel.
