content.description:
As part of the launch campaign for the new Haval H6, GWM chose a disruptive path: unveiling the first film produced entirely with artificial intelligence for the automotive market, using a sensitive and emotional narrative to explore themes of evolution, human relationships, and the future.

Created by Jellyfish in partnership with MADCC, the film uses Veo 3, Google’s most advanced artificial intelligence model for generating realistic, cinematic video. The story follows the relationship between a father and his daughter, using their reunion as a metaphor for time, life’s transformations, and the subtle presence of technology. The new Haval H6 appears as a natural part of this journey, as an element that understands, accompanies, and evolves alongside its owner. The technique adopted for this new film strengthens the creative concept “the car that knows you”, highlighting the vehicle’s high level of personalization and customization, in addition to the more than 300 advanced AI-powered voice commands that the vehicle possesses.

Although it is the first film 100% created with AI for the automotive market, the project is deeply human-driven. A total of 37 people were involved throughout the production. “The idea was never to use artificial intelligence as a gimmick or to draw attention solely to the technology. Our goal was always to tell a human story. The film’s delicacy, coherence, and emotional depth were only possible because of the creative direction led by people, who guided the technology to serve the narrative and amplify emotion,” says Fabiana Baraldi, CEO of Jellyfish.

The film was produced by Drift Works and required more than 700 hours of work over 56 days. The process included casting approval, with six virtual actor options, Costume design, location scouting, audio production, and refinement sessions with GWM’s product team to ensure an accurate and faithful representation of the vehicle. This meticulous attention to detail throughout the process was also reflected in the scale of production, with a total of 2,076 videos generated in Veo 3 to arrive at the 37 final scenes. The soundtrack and score were also developed in-house, using generative technology integrated with production, mixing, and post-production software, always guided by a creative process centered on direction, listening, and creative repertoire.

“The Haval H6 has always been a car that anticipates trends. This film translates our vision of the future in a more emotional way, showing that innovation only makes sense when it improves people’s lives. The concept ‘the car that knows you’ is based on the idea that our advanced technology enhances real life. Partnering with Jellyfish allowed us to turn this concept into a sensitive story, created by technology, that people can relate to, while also marking a pioneering moment in the automotive industry by using artificial intelligence in the narrative,” says Lucas Coelho, CMO of GWM Brazil.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A groundbreaking, emotionally-driven film for the Haval H6 launch, entirely produced using advanced AI, tells a story of family, evolution, and technology's subtle presence, positioning the car as an understanding companion.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Prospective new car buyers, likely aged 30-55 with families, who appreciate innovative technology, emotional storytelling, and seek vehicles that integrate seamlessly into their evolving lives.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film, entirely produced with Google's advanced Veo 3 AI model, distributed across digital platforms and potentially traditional broadcast media as part of the Haval H6 launch campaign.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate significant brand awareness and cultivate an innovative, forward-thinking image for the new Haval H6 by showcasing the world's first AI-produced automotive film.

content.description:
In Peru, every first Sunday in February is Mole Day, a date dedicated to raising awareness about the prevention and early detection of skin cancer and its different types.

This occurs during one of the hottest seasons with the highest solar radiation, summer, when workers who use Norton products, due to their outdoor work, are potential victims of the sun and its harmful consequences if they do not take care of themselves.

That is why we decided to send them an awareness message based on an opportunity we identified: the similarity between a rust stain and melanoma. Because anyone who uses Norton sandpaper knows how easy it is to remove stains, but not if they are on your skin.

The video was accompanied by practical advice such as applying high-protection sunscreen, wearing protective clothing in addition to PPE, and a recommendation to visit a dermatologist once a year. It was shared on the all official platforms of the Norton community.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign creatively leverages Norton Abrasives' expertise in removing stains to draw a powerful analogy between a rust stain (easily removed) and melanoma (not easily removed from skin), urging outdoor workers to prioritize skin cancer prevention.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Outdoor workers in Peru who use Norton Abrasives products, particularly during the hot summer season.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Content video distribution featuring an awareness message and practical advice on skin cancer prevention.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness among outdoor workers in Peru about the prevention and early detection of skin cancer, leveraging a relevant cultural event (Mole Day).

content.description: Context: Harbhajan Singh is one of the greatest cricket players of all time, but he's also infamous for once slapping an opponent on the pitch. In this film, we see him teaching people the classic Indian approach to fixing appliances - by slapping them.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Legendary cricketer Harbhajan Singh humorously demonstrates the culturally familiar method of 'fixing' broken appliances with a slap, playing on his infamous past incident to create a memorable and relatable scenario for Flipkart.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Indian consumers, particularly cricket fans familiar with Harbhajan Singh, who appreciate humor and relatable cultural references regarding home appliance quirks.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film distributed across digital platforms like YouTube and social media, potentially including broadcast TV spots given the celebrity and mass appeal.
ai_enrichment.objective: Increase brand awareness and recall for Flipkart's home appliance category, driving engagement through humorous and culturally resonant content.

metadata.title: Plan well. Be well. Live well.

content.description:
Retirement planning doesn't usually involve wobbly first steps on ice, but for Symetra and Seattle independent agency Copacino Fujikado, that's the strategy.

Season 3 of Symetra's "Plan Well. Play Well" campaign is launching in time for the 2026 Winter Olympics, featuring Sue trading in the basketball court for the ice rink.

In the first spot, which goes live Feb. 5, world champion figure skater Tara Lipinski teaches Sue how to figure skate, and with her hilarious wobbling and struggling, Sue eventually finds her footing for a grand finale performance. The second spot, launching Feb. 16, features another world champion and PWHL star, Hilary Knight, teaching Sue ice hockey!

“Women entering retirement today have more wealth and financial influence than any generation before them, and we wanted to show what’s possible when you plan for the future you envision,” said Trinity Parker, SVP and Chief Marketing Officer at Symetra. “Partnering with Sue allowed us to bring that story to life in a light, uplifting way. Whether you're learning to skate or building financial security, it all comes back to having the freedom to explore what’s next. Copacino immediately understood how to express that message in a way audiences could genuinely connect with.”

"We wanted to show that retirement isn’t an ending, it’s a new beginning,” said Mike Hayward, Chief Creative Officer at Copacino Fujikado. "The campaign aims to demonstrate what's possible when you plan well and open yourself up to new challenges.”

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Symetra's 'Plan well. Be well. Live well.' campaign features basketball star Sue Bird hilariously learning challenging winter sports from champions, using her journey from wobbly first steps to a grand finale performance as a metaphor for successful retirement planning.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Financially influential women who are entering or approaching retirement age.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A series of film spots featuring celebrity athletes (Sue Bird, Tara Lipinski, Hilary Knight) distributed via television and digital video platforms, strategically timed to coincide with the lead-up to the 2026 Winter Olympics.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase brand awareness and consideration for Symetra's retirement planning services among financially influential women.

content.description:
TBWA\Canada campaign honours real stories of hockey parents and supports Kruger Products’ efforts to help more kids participate in minor hockey. The musical score, a punchy adaptation of “We Will Rock You” sung by children, reinforces just how much assisting a minor leaguer’s hockey journey will rock a parent’s world — logistically, financially, emotionally — as their kids live out their dreams on the ice.

Inspired by the real stories and lived experiences of Canadian hockey parents who power their kids through the messy realities of minor hockey, the campaign focuses on small moments that have a huge impact in life: the early mornings, unexpected setbacks, quiet encouragement, and constant behind-the-scenes work that keeps kids showing up.

In hockey and in life, every goal starts with an assist - a play essential to the game that’s reflected in the way hockey families support one another every day. When the world’s eyes are focused on peak athletic performance, the campaign is an opportunity to refocus the conversation on participation, access and the everyday support that keeps kids in the game. The Kruger Big Assist 'Parent Assist' campaign is a reminder that the foundation of Canadian sport is built much earlier, in local rinks and communities across the country.

 

Original Music (Music Adaptation), Voice Direction, Sound Design, and Final Mix: OsoCreative Director: Daenen Bramberger, Harry KnazanSound Design: Leo HicksEngineers: Omid Geadizadeh, Dylan GroffProducer: Jane HeathVoice Casting: Katie Corbridge⁠Agency: TBWAExecutive Creative Director: Jeremiah McNama⁠Associate Creative Director: Jacob Pacey, Brendan McMullen⁠Sr. Agency Producer: Tess Waisglass⁠⁠Production House: Scouts HonourDirector: Mark Zibert ⁠DP: Mark Zibert, Eric Kaskens⁠Executive Post Producer: Rita Popielak

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses a film featuring a child-sung adaptation of “We Will Rock You” to celebrate the immense logistical, financial, and emotional dedication of Canadian hockey parents, honoring their behind-the-scenes work in enabling their children's dreams.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Canadian parents of minor hockey players who deeply invest in their children's athletic journeys and resonate with the challenges and rewards of supporting youth sports.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film deployed across digital platforms and broadcast channels, featuring a custom musical score and real-life inspired narratives.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand affinity and emotional connection with parents by celebrating their sacrifices and commitment to minor hockey, aligning Kruger Products with community support and shared values.

content.description: For Vital Farms farmers, every day is their Big Game. While most Americans will still be snoozing in the early morning hours this Sunday, Vital Farms will launch its first-ever Big Game ad at 5 a.m. – just as its network of more than 575 farmers begins their day – like they do every morning, 365 days a year.Developed in partnership with GUT Miami, the 60-second spot finally answers the age-old question: Which came first, the chicken or the egg? According to Vital Farms, everything else must come first: the land and farmers who care about raising the standards of the food industry. They show up day in and day out, in heat and cold, even when no one is watching, because they believe that a hen allowed to express her natural behaviors produces a better egg.“We know 5 a.m. isn’t the obvious choice to launch a campaign during one of the noisiest weekends of the year, but we’ve never been afraid to buck tradition,” said Kathryn McKeon, Vital Farms Chief Marketing Officer. “While most brands are targeting prime time this weekend, we’re setting our alarms early and waking up when our farmers do to focus the attention on the hard work that goes into producing our eggs: the early mornings, the patience, no spotlights and no shortcuts... the egg is the result.”“There’s a lot of noise right now about what food brands say versus what they actually do,” said Ricardo Casal, Chief Creative Officer at GUT Miami. “Vital Farms knows that between the chicken and the egg, the egg is never the beginning. It is the proof of thousands of patient, deliberate decisions. We decided to put the spotlight where it belongs: On the land, the farmers, and the hens that power Vital Farms’ mission in the first place.”‘And Then Came The Egg’ will launch on Sunday, February 8, 2026, airing on linear TV in key markets across the country. The spot will also run alongside Vital Farms’ ‘Good Eggs. No Shortcuts’ brand campaign across connected TV, YouTube and social platforms.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign creatively answers the age-old 'chicken or egg' question by asserting that the land, dedicated farmers, and ethical hen-raising practices come first, highlighting Vital Farms' commitment to quality and responsible sourcing.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Conscious consumers who prioritize ethically sourced food, animal welfare, and support brands committed to sustainable and humane farming practices.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Launching a 60-second film as Vital Farms' first-ever Big Game ad at 5 AM, specifically timed to coincide with the early start of their farmers' workday.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand awareness, reinforce Vital Farms' commitment to ethical farming and quality, and differentiate the brand through its core values.

content.description:
Great Western Railway (GWR) unveils a new campaign that brings its rich heritage into the present day, reigniting audience appeal through the timeless power of myths and storytelling.

The new chapter of its award-winning Famous Five series, created with adam&eve\TBWA, draws inspiration from GWR’s 1920s publicity campaigns, when the company first used myths and legends to promote travel to an emerging tourism market.

By unearthing this storied archive, GWR reconnects modern leisure travellers with the myths, legends and landscapes that have long defined the region - reframing rail not simply as transport, but as the beginning of adventure.

The multi-channel campaign begins on February 21st with a 60-second hero film directed by Oscar- and BAFTA-winning director and animator Peter Baynton, best known for The Boy, the Mole, the Fox and the Horse and packed with vocal talent from the likes of Richard E Grant, Ambika Mod, Andi Osho and Jan Francis.‘Five and the Dragon Quest’ film aims to boost leisure travel on the GWR network, which covers the south-west of England and Wales, including destinations like Cardiff, Penzance, Bath and Oxford.It follows the Famous Five on their quest across the Great Western Railway network, determined to solve the mystery of a dragon which has been spotted flying in the skies across the region. Unsurprisingly, the train is the quickest way to get around – much better than the cars that all seem to be stuck in traffic jams. It ends with the tagline: “Great Western Railway – legendary adventures start here.”With a new media approach from WPP Media’s Mindshare, the campaign is designed as a connected storytelling ecosystem. The hero film runs across cinema, TV and VOD to deliver scale and emotional impact, while dynamic radio executions delve deeper into regional folklore, bringing local legends to life in the listener’s imagination.

Out-of-home placements situate the Famous Five within landscapes tied to the very myths they explore, creating contextual relevance across the network. A broad social rollout extends the narrative through bespoke vignette films, spotlighting specific legends and destinations to ignite inspiration for leisure travel among distinct audience cohorts.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Great Western Railway (GWR) leverages its 1920s heritage of using myths and legends to promote travel, updating its Famous Five series to connect modern leisure travellers with regional landscapes and reframe rail as the start of an adventure.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Modern leisure travellers interested in exploring regional myths, legends, and landscapes through unique travel experiences.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A multi-channel campaign launching with a 60-second hero film directed by Oscar- and BAFTA-winning director and animator Peter Baynton.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reframe rail travel as an adventure, reignite audience appeal, and promote travel to regional destinations by connecting modern leisure travellers with GWR's storied heritage and the landscapes it serves.

metadata.title: Stylized, Cinematic, Sports-Themed Work Appeals To Kids, Moms & Dads Alike

content.description:
Stylized, Cinematic, Sports-Themed Work Appeals To Kids, Moms & Dads Alike

RAINDROP LAUNCHES PLAYFUL, VISUALLY INVENTIVE CAMPAIGN FOR HELLO PANDA

Raindrop, the full-service creative agency, is launching a dynamic, visually inventive advertising campaign for Meiji Hello Panda, the Japanese snack popular across the globe. Hello Panda consists of crunchy, hollow cookies with a variety of creamy fillings, with each cookie and all the packaging featuring adorable pandas playing a variety of sports.

The strategy behind the omnichannel campaign, “Panda-Monium 2.0,” is to broaden the global love for the brand and build out relevance for parents who make the buying decisions, especially Millennial and Gen Z parents, and drive brand awareness, engagement, trial and repeat purchase. Hello Panda is owned by Meiji America, the US subsidiary of Japanese company Meiji which offers a variety of popular Japanese chocolates, cookies and gummies.

Two versions of the hero TV spot, a :30, feature a brother and sister who square off during soccer practice in playful competition. The spot mixes live action, animation and art direction to tell the story in stylized, diorama-like worlds, from soccer fields to skate parks to gymnastics studios. Motion design and animation emphasize moments of action and delight that bring to life Hello Panda’s emotional connection with the creativity and passion of children at play. The TV work is amplified with video and static graphics adapted from the TV spots to be used in paid and earned social media as well as in various OOH executions

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A playful, visually inventive, and sports-themed campaign featuring Meiji Hello Panda cookies, designed to appeal to kids, moms, and dads.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Kids and parents globally, specifically Millennial and Gen Z parents who make buying decisions.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: An omnichannel campaign with a core focus on film content.
ai_enrichment.objective: To broaden global love for the Hello Panda brand, build relevance with parents, and drive brand awareness, engagement, trial, and repeat purchase.

content.description:
A young man every time life “chooses” for him, it goes wrong.From being stuck in an ultra-serious yoga class he clearly doesn’t belong in, to awkwardly mumbling one line during his girlfriend’s over-the-top karaoke duet, to receiving a “dream car” that literally falls apart, and even trusting friends with a birthday cake that backfires each situation spirals into quietly absurd misadventures.Told through deadpan performances, awkward stillness, and cinematic visual comedy, the film follows a series of relatable moments where expectations clash with reality.Because sometimes, what others pick for you just isn’t you.But at least this isn't the case with OSNPlus cause here you can actually choose something that is MORE YOU!

 

Production House : Tres ContentServicing Production : Kino FilmsDOP : Manuel LuebbersSet Designer : Amr Abou HeibaStylist: Shams OrabyEditor : Hussein MokhtarGrading Lluc Suarez

Make New Zealand The Best Place In The World To Have Herpes

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign humorously depicts a young man's misfortunes when others make choices for him, highlighting how these situations never align with his true self. It then positions OSNPlus as the solution for personalized content choice where users can find what's "More You!"
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young adults and millennials, likely 18-35, who consume digital content, value personalization, and can relate to humorous everyday social misadventures.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across digital platforms leveraging cinematic visual comedy, deadpan performances, and awkward stillness to tell a relatable story.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive brand awareness for OSNPlus and emphasize its core benefit of personalized content selection.

content.description: What was billed as the opening of a chic new restaurant in Lima turned out to be a creative action designed to spotlight an urgent yet often overlooked issue: the unnecessary waste of high-quality food in the country, and the lack of logistical resources to transport unsold food from markets to those who need it most.In Peru, 12 million tons of food are lost or wasted every year, from farm to table. This amount would fill 140 stadiums.   This is food that never reaches millions of people living in vulnerable situations - despite the good condition of much of this food and sellers’ willingness to donate it.As Sarah Laughton, Representative of the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) in Peru, states: “One tragedy of food waste is that it tends to be fresh, highly nutritious items like fruits and vegetables that end up being discarded. These are the very foods many families need but cannot afford.”She added, “Transportation is a major bottleneck. We need new ways to engage society and show that taking action is within everyone’s reach.”Guests arrived to a red-carpet welcome at a real restaurant in Lima’s upscale Miraflores district, expecting an exciting gastronomic experience.  Instead, they were served empty plates.  The empty plates were a message:   good food gone to waste means many missing meals.It was then that Kunka was revealed—an initiative led by the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) that proposes a new way of understanding access to food and its social impact.As part of the experience, WFP partnered with Daniel Bonifaz, a leading figure in the fintech world with thousands of followers, who generated a buzz by publicly announcing that his next investment would be a restaurant. The twist came when it was revealed that his objective was not culinary, but social:  Kunka was not a restaurant, but a digital impact investment platform enabling ordinary citizens to finance the logistics needed to get fresh, high-quality surplus and donated food to those who need it most.Food banking is not a new concept in Peru, and tax breaks for food donation exist.  Kunka is designed to contribute to an ongoing, collective effort, connecting donors, food banks and recipients, and ensuring that food ends up on plates and not as waste.From a creative standpoint, the campaign built a complete, real gastronomic experience—location, setup, service, and narrative—only to subvert it with a clear and powerful message. The empty plates became the central vehicle of the idea, transforming the expectation of “going out to eat” into a direct reflection of a critical absence. In this restaurant, no food could be served, not because food wasn’t available, but because it never reached the people who wanted it.For Carlos Altamirano, VP Creative at Digitas Peru, the key was pushing creativity to its most tangible extreme: “It was a beautiful madness: creating a full restaurant that wasn’t actually a restaurant. None of this would have been possible without a huge team behind it. When creativity meets a real desire to do something different, the idea stops being a concept and becomes a concrete action.”The activation, held in Lima with the participation of opinion leaders, business executives, media, and influencers, demonstrated how gastronomy—a topic that connects, attracts, and sparks interest in everyone—can serve as a powerful bridge to capture attention, open dialogue, and mobilize society around an urgent cause: making it possible for food to reach those who need it most.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A chic new restaurant opening was staged in Lima, only to be revealed as a creative action designed to dramatically spotlight the urgent issue of food waste in Peru and the lack of logistical resources to transport unsold food to those in need.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General public in Peru, local policymakers, food industry stakeholders, potential donors, and organizations concerned with food security and sustainable development.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A staged public event (the 'restaurant opening' stunt) with likely accompanying public relations, media outreach to generate news coverage, and potentially digital content sharing across social media platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise public awareness about the extensive food waste in Peru and highlight the critical need for improved logistical solutions to get edible surplus food to vulnerable populations.

content.description:
What happens when a workplace feels too happy to be true?

The campaign plays on the familiar belief that corporate life is always chaotic and stressful, which is why happiness often feels surprising, even suspicious. Yet when systems truly work, the result is not just efficiency, but happy, confident smiles. From this, a simple question emerges: who or what is behind it all?

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Nebi's campaign challenges the conventional view of stressful corporate life by showcasing workplaces that are surprisingly happy and efficient, subtly positioning their technology as the unseen enabler behind this positive transformation.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Business owners, IT decision-makers, and professionals within corporate environments, particularly those seeking technology solutions to improve workplace efficiency and employee satisfaction.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film distributed across digital platforms like YouTube, LinkedIn, and industry-specific B2B channels, potentially supported by shorter cut-downs for social media engagement.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand awareness and drive consideration for Nebi's technology by linking it directly to enhanced workplace efficiency and employee well-being.

content.description:
Building on the success of Guinness’ 2025 ‘Pints of View’ beer mats campaign in Ireland, the brand is now extending the concept even further with POV (Pint of View) across Ireland and Great Britain – creating more meaningful moments of connection with a physical invitation for fans to frame, capture and share their own “Lovely Days”.Launched during the Guinness Six Nations, the campaign brings the spirit of matchday to life beyond the stadium,  putting the moments, rituals and atmosphere front and centre.Guinness tapped into the social storytelling language of POV — point of view — and reimagined it as a simple, distinctive creative mechanic. The result is a pint-shaped silhouette that invites the Guinness community to place their own perspective of a Lovely Day right at the centre. This pint-shaped aperture becomes a visual expression of what a Lovely Day for Guinness truly looks like: friends (or friendly rivals) gathered outside the stadium, the pre-match pint, or the elated walk back to the local after the final whistle. POV empowers consumers to capture and share the moments that make their Lovely Days unforgettable. Large-scale POV installations landed outside The Aviva Stadium and  encouraging participation beyond the stands, installations have also rolled out at select pubs across Ireland.The campaign also extends into the on-trade, with POV beer mats returning for their third drop in Ireland, and landing in the UK in February. Following significant organic uptake, Guinness Ireland is responding to demand and interaction by supplying its on-trade partners with an additional 1.5 million mats. Pint of View out-of-home projections will go live across key on-trade and route-to-stadium locations, lighting up Lovely Days moments throughout the Guinness Six Nations.Created in partnership with global social-first creative agency Born Social, POV (Pint of View) builds on Guinness’s Lovely Days brand platform and the brand’s long-standing association with communion, ritual and connection. Designed to connect IRL and URL, the idea places fans at the centre of the story.POV (Pint of View) has been created as a flexible system, capable of moving beyond rugby into festivals, music, and other social occasions where Guinness is already present. The campaign is designed to scale through co-creation, building an ecosystem of community-first content that can live across social, OOH, DOOH and future activations.

 

Creative agency: Born Social – Creative strategy, concept, platform development, social-first thinkingMedia buying: PhD Media – OOH / DOOHExperiential delivery: VerveProduction partners: HHG / VML (early asset development)

A St. Patrick’s Day Message From Guinness

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Guinness' 'POV (Pint of View)' campaign invites its community in Ireland and Great Britain to frame and share their 'Lovely Days' within a pint-shaped silhouette, leveraging the social media 'point of view' storytelling format.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Guinness fans and rugby enthusiasts in Ireland and Great Britain who enjoy sharing personal experiences and connecting with others.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Digital engagement featuring a unique pint-shaped silhouette creative mechanic for user-generated content, launched and promoted during the Guinness Six Nations.
ai_enrichment.objective: To foster deeper engagement and connection within the Guinness community by encouraging the sharing of personal 'Lovely Day' moments and extending the spirit of matchday.

content.description:
It’s a fact: young adults couldn’t care less about life insurance. They think they are immortal!

But Allianz found the only place where they die all the time: shooter video games. So Allianz dropped into their games with Extra-Life Insurance.

We partnered with paid online tournaments to offer insurance that reimburses players’ entry fees if they’re eliminated—giving them an extra life to compete again, while also covering them in real life.

AWARDS:Silver Winner - Clio Entertainment 2025 (Students)https://clios.com/winners-gallery/details/202185

This  student campaign titled 'Extra-Life Insurance' was published in Colombia in December, 2025. It was created for the brand: Allianz, by ad schools: Bendita Carpeta and Sancho BBDO. This Digital medium campaign is related to the Insurance industry and contains 1 media asset.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Allianz connected life insurance to the world of shooter video games, offering "Extra-Life Insurance" in paid tournaments that reimbursed players' entry fees upon elimination, linking in-game "death" to real-life coverage.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young adult gamers, specifically those aged approximately 18-30, who actively play shooter video games and participate in online tournaments.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign involved partnering with paid online shooter video game tournaments to integrate the "Extra-Life Insurance" product, offering entry fee reimbursements directly within the game environment as a digital activation.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase brand awareness and foster initial engagement with Allianz's life insurance offerings among a hard-to-reach demographic of young adult gamers.

content.description:
Join CAKE.com | Trusted by Millions. Species Unverified.

Join CAKE.com | Trusted by Millions. Species Unverified.

The ChallengeIn the crowded Productivity & SaaS market, every brand claims to be "secure." However, "security" has become a corporate buzzword that users often tune out. CAKE.com needed to communicate its radical commitment to user privacy in a way that didn't feel like another dry technical whitepaper.

The Creative IdeaWe decided to prove how little we know about our users.

The "Beaver Privacy Paradox" is an anti-ad that starts with a blatant lie: a hyper-specific statistic about beavers. By immediately debunking our own "data" and admitting we can't even tell the difference between a beaver and a sea otter, we highlight the ultimate brand truth: We don't track you. If we don't know who is a human and who is a semi-aquatic mammal, we certainly don't know (or sell) your sensitive business data.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign uses an 'anti-ad' that begins with a humorously false, hyper-specific statistic about beavers, which is immediately debunked by CAKE.com itself. This act of self-debunking powerfully highlights the brand's core truth: they do not track their users.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and businesses seeking productivity and SaaS solutions who are privacy-conscious, skeptical of generic 'security' claims, and appreciate innovative, transparent communication.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Digital anti-ads, likely deployed as video content or engaging interactive digital ads across various digital advertising platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To clearly differentiate CAKE.com and build trust by communicating its radical commitment to user privacy in an engaging and memorable way that stands out from typical 'security' messaging.

metadata.title: Ready to experience your most memorable moments?

content.description:
Ready to experience your most memorable moments?

Renault Scenic is THE iconic model of the brand in the family segment. Equipped with a 100% electric powertrain, it reinvents the C-segment vehicle and invites people to travel while preserving its DNA as a family car. With up to 625 km of WLTP range, Renault Scenic ranks among the best electric ranges in its segment. Designed for the well-being and comfort of all occupants, it also features the latest-generation technologies, including Google built-in with over 100 apps, solarbay® opacifying panoramic sunroof and more than 30 safety features.Because traveling is about much more than simply getting from one place to another, Scenic was designed to offer comfort and freedom to the whole family. This vision inspired our new film, an ode to travel and to the stories we create together.THE ODYSSEY.This new film is an invitation to travel - emotional, aspirational, and poetic.Directed by Turbo at Quad Production, the film showcases Renault Scenic on an odyssey where every landscape is made of books.It reminds us of the importance of the journey beyond the destination alone. Discovery gives us stories. The road reveals places, cultures, and memories that give meaning to every trip.Like an open-air library, where every road leads to something to discover, to tell, or to write. By crossing a universe made of books and memories, The Odyssey shows that Scenic offers the opportunity to write unique family stories, your own stories, thanks to its range of up to 625 km WLTP. Renault Scenic invites you to hit the road and turn every journey into a new chapter.Discover the film : https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iNDO7MuepyYThe campaign is musically supported by the highly poetic classic by The Korgis, “Everybody's Got To Learn Sometime,” covered by American folk-rock artist Beck, whose delicate interpretation accompanies this ode to travel and daydreaming.“Scenic E-Tech electric is a true long-distance family car with its range of up to 625 km. That's why we wanted a poetic, dreamlike film… a true allegory of travel.”Arnaud BELLONI – Renault Global CMO

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign positions the new 100% electric Renault Scenic as the ideal family car for creating memorable travel experiences, emphasizing its advanced features, comfort, and extended range as an ode to travel.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Families, particularly those in the C-segment, who value comfort, technology, sustainability, and freedom in an electric vehicle, and see travel as a means to create shared, memorable moments.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film (as indicated by 'medium: Film' and 'our new film') designed to be an ode to travel, showcasing the vehicle's features and its impact on family experiences.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate awareness and consideration for the reinvented 100% electric Renault Scenic, highlighting its segment-leading range, comfort, and technology for family travel.

metadata.title: They disappeared once; don't let them disappear twice

content.description:
They disappeared once; don't let them disappear twice

Archer Troy, the Mexican independent agency, has created a new project in collaboration with the International Committee of the Red Cross and the production company Oriental Films.

A campaign on the value of investigation files in the search for people who are victims of disappearance. With phrases such as “They disappeared once; don't let them disappear twice” or “We search for them because we love them,” this campaign highlights one of the biggest social problems in Mexico.

This campaign is the shout of an entire nation to never stop searching for our disappeared.

There are those who believe that investigation files are just paper and dust, pages with stories that time devours. But no. Each investigation file breathes when someone mentions its name or pronounces its number. It beats when a mother asks for their children. It trembles when a father knocks on a door. They are not just files. They are memories that resist oblivion, silence. And if there is someone searching, the investigation files will remain alive. Alive like hope. Alive like the truth that is still missing. More alive than ever.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A campaign highlighting the critical value of investigation files in the ongoing search for disappeared people, urging society not to let them be forgotten a second time.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General public in Mexico, families of disappeared persons, and authorities or individuals who may undervalue the importance of investigation files.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Integrated campaign featuring powerful taglines such as 'They disappeared once; don't let them disappear twice', likely incorporating video content through a collaboration with a production company and disseminated across various media channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness about the indispensable value of investigation files in the search for disappeared persons and to galvanize national support for these ongoing efforts.

content.description:
YouTube TV’s latest campaign for the Super Bowl and Winter Olympics features an all-star cast including: Jason & Kylie Kelce, Gordon Ramsay, Christian McCaffrey, David Blaine, and Sarah Hughes (Olympic gold medalist), to highlight why viewers shouldn’t settle for a “meh” live tv experience and trade up to YouTube TV.

The spot asks “what would the world look like if everything was ‘meh’?—from "meh-gic" tricks and fine dining “meh-nus” to professional athletes and legendary beards—and ultimately confirms it isn’t worth settling for anything less than the best, positioning YouTube TV as the premium antidote for mediocre live TV services. By showcasing its #1 ranking in customer satisfaction by JD Power 3-years-in-a-row and industry-defining features like multiview, the ad reminds fans that the biggest moments in sports and entertainment deserve better than just "meh".

 

YouTube TVExecutive Producer: Eric LeibermanExecutive Producer: Kelsey GlivaExecutive Producer: Wes HarrisExecutive Producer: Angela Courtin

Agency and Production: ChromistaExecutive Producer and Co-Founder: Darren AronofskyExecutive Producer and Co-Founder: Ted RobbinsExecutive Director, Brand and Advertising: Chris EinhauserExecutive Producer/Partner: Adina BirnbaumHead of Strategy: Lauren LeavittExecutive Producer: Justin PollockCreative Director: Eron BroughtonCreative Director: Brandon DavisProducer: Leelee GroomeHead of Production: Ian Royston

content.description:
e.l.f is bringing melodrama to the Big Game with a two-minute, telenovela-inspired spot airing on Peacock. Tapping into buzz around Super Bowl programming and Bad Bunny’s anticipated halftime performance, the brand plays on the idea of English-speaking fans learning Spanish to fully enjoy the spectacle.

Titled *Melisa*, the drama stars Melissa McCarthy alongside Nicholas Gonzalez and iconic telenovela villain Itatí Cantoral. McCarthy awakens in a hospital with just one day to learn Spanish, aided by e.l.f’s Glow Reviver Lip Oil, which promises juicier lips and surprisingly better pronunciation.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: e.l.f. Cosmetics created a two-minute, telenovela-inspired Super Bowl spot, *Melisa*, starring Melissa McCarthy, who must learn Spanish in one day to fully enjoy the Big Game and Bad Bunny's performance. The campaign humorously links the brand's Glow Reviver Lip Oil to improved Spanish pronunciation.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Super Bowl viewers, especially English-speaking pop culture enthusiasts and beauty consumers aged 18-55, who enjoy celebrity humor and trending entertainment.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film (two-minute spot) aired on Peacock during the Super Bowl programming.
ai_enrichment.objective: To significantly increase brand awareness and product visibility for e.l.f. Cosmetics and its Glow Reviver Lip Oil by leveraging high-profile Super Bowl programming and celebrity endorsement.

content.description:
The film follows the story of a young boy who becomes the emotional anchor of the narrative. Through his perspective, Founding Day is experienced not just as a national celebration, but as a living legacy passed from one generation to the next.

We intentionally involved the child as a symbolic bridge between heritage and the present—placing him within key moments of the story to humanize the scale of the production. His curiosity, expressions, and subtle interactions with the world around him ground the film emotionally, allowing audiences to connect with the celebration on a personal level rather than just a visual one.

By framing the story through youthful eyes, the campaign transforms a large-scale patriotic production into an intimate and relatable experience—reminding viewers that legacy lives on through the next generation.

 

Agency: WKK GroupProduction Company: Nebras FimsExecutive Producer: Abdulrahman AlnafisahProducer: Mohammed “Xenon" SameerDirector: Shams AlsawafDOP: Kareem MohammedLine Producer: Hamza Alnafisah1st AD: Mohammed Sabri2nd AD: Abdullah AlduhaimArt Director: NasserTrinity operator: Ahmad AlsofyaniCast Manager: RowaydahProduction Manager: Mahmoud AhmedGhaffer: Mohammed Abu NajimVTO: Yazan ShabatFocus Puller: Boundog’Production Coordinator: ReemProduction Coordinator: FawazWardrobe: ZairaBTS: MajidVFX Mohammed Rabee’Editor: Moahmmed Abu DeyyahMusic & Sound Design: IbrahimColorist: Post Brothers

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A large-scale patriotic film uses the perspective of a young boy to transform Founding Day into an intimate, relatable experience, highlighting it as a living legacy passed between generations.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General public, particularly families and individuals who value national heritage, history, and intergenerational connection, across all ages.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film distributed across digital platforms (e.g., YouTube) and potentially broadcast TV.
ai_enrichment.objective: To foster emotional connection and appreciation for Founding Day as a living legacy, thereby enhancing brand perception through association with national pride and intergenerational values.

content.description:
Clash of the year because of a baby?! Watch as values collide in the most EXTRA way for the title of fav relative.

 

The Unite Billboard | Lotus’s Member Price

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A humorous and over-the-top film depicting a dramatic family 'showdown' centered around a baby, where relatives clash in an 'extra' way to become the favorite, likely set within or related to Lotus's, with an underlying message about Lotus's member pricing.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General consumers and household shoppers, particularly those who are family-oriented, appreciate relatable humor, and are looking for value or member benefits at retail stores.

content.description:
Ek chhota sa ghar… sapno ke liye. Ek mazboot suraksha… har ghar ke liye. ⚡  Engineered through resilient cables, heat-resistant wires, and reliable switchgears built to stand the test of time.  Experience the story. Link in bio 🔗

Ek chhota sa ghar… sapno ke liye. Ek mazboot suraksha… har ghar ke liye. ⚡  Engineered through resilient cables, heat-resistant wires, and reliable switchgears built to stand the test of time.  Experience the story. Link in bio 🔗

This product film for Torrent Electricals showcases the brand’s safety and durability through a heartwarming father-daughter conversation.

As Roohi curiously asks her father about electrical components, he explains their functions in a simple, reassuring way, highlighting protection from power surges, fire resistance, and long-lasting wires. Inspired, she builds a small “home” for her toys using Torrent product boxes, proudly repeating that the wires will keep them safe.

The film seamlessly blends product USPs with emotion, positioning Torrent as the trusted foundation of safety in every home.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: This campaign uses a heartwarming father-daughter conversation in a digital product film to emotionally connect the safety and durability of Torrent Electricals' products with the protection of a secure home.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Homeowners, parents, and families in India who prioritize safety, durability, and reliability in their household electrical components.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A digital product film featuring a heartwarming father-daughter conversation, distributed on digital platforms with a 'link in bio' call to action, likely social media.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand perception and build trust by highlighting the safety and durability of Torrent Electricals' products through an emotional narrative.

metadata.title: Comfort on Display at LA Furniture Store

content.description:
Mario Capasa opens its newest location in the heart of West Hollywood, CA. From the street, the building reads more like a contemporary gallery than a furniture store— a deliberate gesture to frame the brand as part of the design world, not of the retail world.

The Material Proof Bar transforms customization into an exhibition. Stretching along one wall, it combines a full-scale swatch display with interactive demos that reveal how modular sofas perform in daily life.  Visitors can see stains bead and wipe clean, watch breathability and heat-dissipation measured in real time, and feel abrasion resistance put to the test.

Beyond performance, the station invites tailoring at every level. With more than fifty sofa cover options and a framework of interchangeable components, each sofa becomes a system designed around its owner— by a platform where proof and personalization are made visible.

This editorial-style video was distributed across platforms such as YouTube, Google My Business, and Facebook Business Pages, each optimized for local search to drive foot traffic and online engagement. To enhance the overall impact, high-end retail interior photography was incorporated into business listings and social media, capturing detailed shots of the store's layout and product displays. The integration of video and photography into geolocation platforms like Apple Maps and Bing Places further boosts local SEO, reinforcing the store’s credibility and appeal, making it an essential stop for local shoppers.

Hailed by leading design publications as “the most comfortable seating imaginable,” Mario Capasa’s Los Angeles flagship unfolds like an exhibition. The effect is a showroom that reads as a gallery of possibilities, each sofa staged as a medium within a larger composition, where comfort becomes the subject and the real work on display is the way you live.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Mario Capasa opens its new West Hollywood store designed as a contemporary gallery, positioning the brand within the design world, and featuring an interactive 'Material Proof Bar' to showcase sofa performance and extensive customization options.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Design-conscious homeowners, interior designers, and affluent consumers in West Hollywood and surrounding areas who value high-quality, customizable, and demonstrably durable modern furniture.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A physical store opening designed as a contemporary gallery in West Hollywood, featuring an interactive 'Material Proof Bar' with live product performance demonstrations, full-scale swatch displays, and on-site customization consultations.
ai_enrichment.objective: To elevate Mario Capasa's brand perception by positioning it as a design-world entity rather than a traditional retailer, and to drive engagement and sales through an immersive, high-quality in-store experience.

content.description:
BCAA is shaking up the peer-to-peer market with the launch of its new Task Marketplace, a service connecting locals with background-checked "Taskers" for household chores, because chores won't do themselves.

The launch campaign, "Choretasia," pays homage to the 1940s classic Fantasia, with a re-arranged score of Paul Dukas' The Sorcerer's Apprentice. Rather than relying solely on CGI to show chores doing themselves, the team used practical effects and puppeteers to bring dancing drills, mops, and lawnmowers to life in-camera.

 

Agency: ONE23WESTExecutive Creative Director: Rob Sweetman, Bryan CollinsCreative Director / Writer: Kate Roland, Dave FarthingCreative Director / Art Director: Tony HirdManaging Director, VP / Account Lead: Caroline HowsonProducer: Aliya Tarmo, Ryan Scott, Ninette AvesRetoucher/Studio Artists: Tom Pettapiece, Gabe Hall, Lisa Good

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign promotes BCAA's new Task Marketplace by re-imagining Disney's Fantasia, using practical effects and puppetry to bring dancing chore tools to life in-camera, set to a re-arranged score of Paul Dukas' The Sorcerer's Apprentice.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and households who are seeking convenient and reliable assistance with household chores through a peer-to-peer service.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film utilizing practical effects, puppetry, and a re-arranged classic score, distributed via film medium (likely TV and online platforms).
ai_enrichment.objective: To launch and build awareness for BCAA's new Task Marketplace, a service connecting locals with background-checked 'Taskers' for household chores.

content.description:
A man walks into a thrift store where nothing is actually for sale. The items on display are revealed to be the owner’s regrets for his FIRSTS—painful memories he’s been holding onto, unable to let go.

To help him move forward, the man takes him on a wacky Willy Wonka-style tour of Una Apartments (ENG: First Apartments): thoughtfully designed FIRST home built to last—and never to be a regret.

 

Brand: Arthaland Una ApartmentsExecutive Vice President: Oliver L. ChanMarketing Unit Head: Cyrene Pamela S. GoMarketing Associate Manager: Klarisa Mari E. VillaviejaMarketing Associate: Louise Marie D. Closa

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A man enters a thrift store where items symbolize the owner's painful 'FIRSTS' (regrets). To help him move on, he's taken on a whimsical tour of Una Apartments (meaning 'FIRST Apartments'), presented as a thoughtfully designed first home that will never become a regret.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: First-time homebuyers, young professionals, and couples looking for their initial property investment, who are seeking a reliable and well-designed 'first' home experience.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Hero film showcasing the narrative of 'firsts' and a Willy Wonka-style tour of Una Apartments.
ai_enrichment.objective: To position Arthaland Una Apartments as the ideal, regret-free 'first home' choice, building brand preference and consideration among prospective buyers.

metadata.title: Six generations with one shared passion

content.description: A bread‑taking story only Morgan Freeman can tell.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Warburtons employs the iconic voice and presence of Morgan Freeman to tell a humorous and engaging 'bread-taking' story, highlighting the brand's six generations of passion and heritage in baking.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Broad consumer base who regularly purchase bread, particularly families and individuals who appreciate quality, brand heritage, and enjoy engaging, high-production-value advertising.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film featuring Morgan Freeman, distributed across national television broadcasts and extensively promoted on digital video platforms like YouTube and various social media channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To elevate brand awareness, reinforce Warburtons' long-standing heritage and commitment to quality, and create positive brand affinity through memorable storytelling.

content.description: Lay’s opens a non-existent restaurant on UberEats

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Lay's launched a deceptive but engaging campaign by setting up a non-existent restaurant on UberEats, intending to surprise and interact with consumers on a popular food delivery platform to highlight its brand in an unexpected way.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Users of food delivery apps, particularly UberEats, aged 18-45, who appreciate playful marketing, convenience snacks, and are digitally engaged.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Creation of a fake restaurant listing and presence on the UberEats platform as a core experiential stunt, supported by a 'film' documenting the activation and shared across digital and social media channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate significant brand buzz, enhance brand awareness through an innovative and surprising stunt, and create engaging content for digital platforms.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign introduces an innovative approach to managing a specific health condition, illustrated through a narrative film that inspires hope and demonstrates improved patient outcomes.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adults diagnosed with chronic metabolic conditions (e.g., type 2 diabetes, obesity) who are actively seeking new treatment solutions, their caregivers, and prescribing healthcare professionals.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film deployed across national television, targeted online video platforms, and integrated into professional medical conferences and patient education websites.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase brand awareness for Novo Nordisk's innovative therapeutic solutions and encourage patients to initiate conversations with their doctors about new treatment possibilities.

content.description:
Evo Clean is a cinematic sci-fi narrative set 25,000 years beyond our time where cleanliness is no longer a chore, but a perfected science. In our concept, Mr. Evo descends from a hyper-advanced civilization carrying formulas engineered in a future where molecules are studied, stains are decoded, and dirt is eliminated at its atomic core. Every product is portrayed as a breakthrough technology, not manufactured but discovered through evolution.

The campaign blends space exploration, futuristic engineering, and emotionally grounded family moments, turning everyday cleaning struggles into epic transformation sequences. Grease dissolves like defeated matter, odors vanish like erased signals, and surfaces glow under protective nano-layers. Through hyper-real cinematic visuals, controlled lighting contrasts, and a bold technological aesthetic, we positioned Evo Clean not as a brand but as a force sent from the future to upgrade life on Earth.

 

Advertising Agency : Mojo Creative Studio, Cairo, EgyptCreative Director: Ramy MohamedArt Director/AI Specialist: Ramy MohamedCopywriter: Amel EidProject Manager: Ammar Al-ShloolVoice Over: Hashem Al-Jarrah

Bulletproof your SUV, Uzi, Bulletproof your SUV, Revolver...

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A cinematic sci-fi narrative where 'Mr. Evo' from a hyper-advanced future introduces atomically-engineered cleaning solutions, transforming everyday chores into epic, effortless transformations through advanced technology.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals and families who struggle with cleaning chores and seek highly effective, innovative solutions, potentially with an appreciation for futuristic or technologically advanced concepts.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: High-production-value cinematic hero film featuring hyper-real visuals, likely distributed across television and digital video platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: Build brand awareness and establish a premium, innovative perception for EVO cleaning products by positioning them as advanced, effortless solutions.

content.description:
Marvel fans have always dreamed of stepping inside the universes they love. With “Stuf of Legends,” OREO finally hands them the portal.

For the first time ever, OREO became the only brand to unite all four major Marvel franchises -- Avengers, Spider-Man, X-Men, and the Fantastic Four –– into one brand partnership. Also, for the first time ever, OREO introduced color-changing creme.

Rather than a one-off product drop, Dentsu Creative built a two-pulse cinematic storyline that unfolds across film and digital experience –– pulling fans directly into the Marvel universe.

In this high-stakes storyline, a multiverse-level threat looms. And while the heroes are fighting on every front, this battle is simply too big for them alone. That’s where fans are called into action. The campaign invites everyone to power up, join the story and help save the universe one OREO at a time.

The campaign launched with three Marvel OREO “Stuf of Legends” packs and a mysterious fourth and final pack was unknown. That’s where the interactive digital experience at MarvelOREOStufOfLegends.com came in, where fans worked together to safely deliver the mystery fourth pack.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Oreo, for the first time, united all four major Marvel franchises (Avengers, Spider-Man, X-Men, Fantastic Four) into a single partnership, introducing color-changing creme and creating a two-pulse cinematic and digital storyline that acts as a portal for fans to step into the Marvel universe and participate in a multiverse-level threat.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Dedicated Marvel fans across all major franchises, likely young adults and adults who are invested in cinematic storylines and digital interactive experiences.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A two-pulse cinematic storyline delivered through film and an immersive digital experience, featuring a new color-changing creme product.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive deep engagement and immersive interaction with dedicated Marvel fans by offering an unprecedented cross-franchise experience and showcasing a new Oreo product innovation.

content.description:
Dacia unveils the new generation of its iconic sedan for Romania and Morocco: new Dacia Logan.A symbol of accessibility and reliability for over twenty years, new Logan steps confidently into modernity, delivering enhanced techn§ology and comfort while remaining true to its DNA.In Romania and Morocco alike, the Dacia Logan is far more than a car: it is a benchmark, a shared reference point and a symbol of closeness and trust. With this new generation, Dacia pays tribute to that legacy while projecting the Logan into the future — more elegant, intuitive and contemporary than ever.This launch is supported by a poetic campaign directed by Vladimir de Fontenay. Breaking away from performance‑driven conventions, the film embraces an emotional approach, celebrating the unique place new Logan holds in the lives of its drivers.The film tells a love story in reverse, with new Logan as a faithful, simple and timeless companion. It brings to life the signature “If only we could grow like our car: younger”, reminding us that, over time, some companions remain forever young by our side.

New Logan features a refreshed design, inspired by the latest Dacia evolutions (Sandero Stepway, Jogger). Its redesigned front, Y-shaped light signature, and Dacia Link logo reinforce a simple yet strong identity.Inside, the car reaches a new technological milestone with a connected multimedia system, fully aligned with Dacia's philosophy of useful innovation.Available with bi‑fuel petrol/LPG powertrains for greater efficiency and driving range, and — in Romania — with a smooth, quiet automatic transmission, new Logan delivers a modern, comfortable driving experience.With this model, Dacia reaffirms its commitment to simple, smart and sustainable mobility.The film is live since the 27th of February in Morocco.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A poetic and emotional film celebrating the enduring legacy and trusted status of the Dacia Logan in Romania and Morocco, while showcasing its modernized, elegant, and technologically advanced new generation.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Consumers in Romania and Morocco, particularly existing Dacia Logan enthusiasts and new buyers who value accessibility, reliability, and trust, and seek a modern sedan that honors a cherished legacy.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film adopting an emotional and poetic narrative, directed by Vladimir de Fontenay, emphasizing the car's symbolic importance rather than performance.
ai_enrichment.objective: To launch the new generation Dacia Logan, rejuvenating its iconic image while reinforcing its core values of accessibility and reliability in key markets.

metadata.title: PhilosoBeer: The Philosophy of Responsible Enjoyment

content.description:
PhilosoBeer: The Philosophy of Responsible Enjoyment

What if responsible drinking wasn’t a warning — but a philosophy?

PhilosoBeer imagines the untold, human moments behind history’s most iconic philosophical quotes. By placing the world’s greatest thinkers in intimate beer rituals, the campaign reframes moderation as wisdom — not restriction.Because better beer isn’t about drinking more.It’s about drinking better.

 

Advertising Agency: Zelva AgencyChief Creative Officer: Hamilton PeñaCreative Directors: Felipe Miranda, Wilmar DazaArt Director: Wilmar DazaGraphic Designer: Sofía Naranjo De ZulategiCopywriters: Felipe Miranda, Luis Felipe Hernández

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: PhilosoBeer redefines responsible drinking as a philosophy, showcasing historical thinkers in intimate beer rituals to reframe moderation as wisdom rather than restriction.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adult beer consumers who appreciate thoughtful consumption, value quality experiences, and are receptive to messages about moderation and intellectual engagement.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A digital content series featuring renowned philosophers in intimate beer-drinking scenarios, distributed across various digital platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To shift the perception of responsible drinking from a warning to a positive philosophy of wisdom, thereby enhancing brand image and encouraging mindful enjoyment of beer.
